gmw_00007311.name: BRI 0952 0115
gmw_00007311.overview: BRI 0952 0115 is a doubly imaged optical quasar at a redshift of z=4.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594]. The system is gravitationally lensed by a faint, flattened early-type galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594].

sources.summary: BRI 0952 0115 is presented as a doubly imaged z=4.5 optical quasar with a flattened early-type lens galaxy whose spectroscopic lens redshift remains undetermined.

quotes.quote: BRI 0952 0115 (hereafter BRI0952) was discovered by mcmahonbri0952-92 as a doubly imaged z_s=4.5 optical quasar. The quasar is also detected at millimeter wavelengths omontbri0952-96 . keetonoptical-98 found that the lens is a flattened early type galaxy, and kochanekfundplane-00 estimated a lens redshift of z_l=0.41 0.05 based on fundamental plane fitting. Because the separation between the images is small ( 0.9 ) and the lens galaxy is faint 21.9 in F675W; keetonoptical-98 , the lens redshift has not been determined spectroscopically.

gmw_00007312.name: PMN J2004 1349
gmw_00007312.overview: PMN J2004 1349 is a two-image gravitational lens system featuring a radio-loud quasar source [arXiv:astro-ph/0511594]. The lensing galaxy is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0308093, arXiv:astro-ph/0511594] with a low bulge-to-disk mass ratio of 0.16 [arXiv:astro-ph/0308093].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the PMN J2004--1349 lens galaxy as a spiral, shows its mass quadrupole aligns with the disk, and derives a low bulge-to-disk mass ratio.

quotes.quote: We present Hubble Space Telescope and Magellan observations of the two-image quasar PMN J2004--1349, revealing that the lens galaxy is a spiral galaxy. One of the quasar images passes through a spiral arm of the galaxy and suffers 3 magnitudes of V band extinction. Using simple lens models, we show that the mass quadrupole is well-aligned with the observed galaxy disk. A more detailed model with components representing the bulge and disk gives a bulge-to-disk mass ratio of 0.16 0.05 . The addition of a spherical dark halo, tailored to produce an overall flat rotation curve, does not change this conclusion.

sources.summary: PMN J2004 1349 is presented as a two-image radio lens with a quasar source and spiral lens galaxy, with photometric and extinction-based constraints on lens redshift.

quotes.quote: PMN J2004 1349 (hereafter PMN2004) is a two-image lens discovered in a search for radio lenses in the southern sky winnpmn2004-01 . The radio spectral index of the images is typical for radio-loud quasars, so the source is considered to be a quasar despite the lack of an optical spectrum and a measured redshift winnpmn2004-01 . Based on photometry, winnpmn2004-01 suggested a lens redshift in the range 0.5<z_l<1.0 . Higher-resolution imaging by winnpmn2004-03 revealed a spiral lens galaxy (only the fifth one known) and showed that the color differences between the two images at optical and near-infrared wavelengths can be explained by differential extinction. The extinction analysis can be used to infer the lens redshift; it seems to imply somewhat low values ( 0.03 z_l 0.36 ), but that result depends on assumptions about the extinction curve.

gmw_00007313.name: J005244G1
gmw_00007313.overview: J005244G1 is a luminous elliptical galaxy located 32 kpc from a background quasar line of sight [arXiv:1102.4339]. Short-exposure SDSS imaging shows no evidence of interaction between J005244G1 and its neighboring emission-line galaxy, J005244G2 [arXiv:1102.4339].

sources.summary: J005244G1 is one of two possible galaxies associated with the absorber; it is a luminous elliptical 32 kpc from the quasar line of sight, with absorption spanning both systemic-velocity sides.

quotes.quote: J005244G1 J005244G2 In Figure fig:plotVr1 a we show that the absorption system detected in the spectrum of the background quasar maybe associated with two galaxies. G1 is a 2.86L^ _r elliptical galaxy located 32 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight and G2 is a 0.23L^ _r emission-line galaxy located 86 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight. In the short exposure SDSS image, there is no evidence of galaxy--galaxy interaction.

The rotation curve of G2, presented in Figure fig:plotVr1 b, is derived from absorption and exhibits a maximum projected rotation velocity of 143 . The mean absorption redshift is offset by 15 from the galaxy systemic velocity. The rotation curve of G2, presented in Figure fig:plotVr1 , is derived from and exhibits a maximum projected rotation velocity of 74 . Below the absorption profiles are shown with the systemic velocity of G1 is the velocity zeropoint. The mean absorption redshift is offset by + 82 from the galaxy systemic velocity. We find that the absorption spans both sides of the systemic velocity for both galaxies.

gmw_00007315.name: J081420G1
gmw_00007315.overview: J081420G1 is a moderately inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:1102.4339]. It has a maximum projected rotation velocity of 130 km/s and is located 51 kpc from a background quasar sightline [arXiv:1102.4339].

sources.summary: J081420G1 is described as a moderately inclined spiral 51 kpc from the quasar sightline, with a 130 km/s rotation curve and a single unsaturated absorber aligned with rotation.

quotes.quote: J081420G1 In Figure fig:plotVr2 a we show that the absorption system detected in the spectrum of the background quasar is associated with a 1.51L^ _r galaxy located 51 kpc away from the quasar line-of-sight. This spiral galaxy is moderately inclined at 40 degrees. The rotation curve of G1, presented in Figure fig:plotVr2 b, is derived from and exhibits a maximum projected rotation velocity of 130 . The mean absorption redshift is offset by + 105 from the galaxy systemic velocity. The absorption is an apparent single kinematic component having a velocity spread of roughly 200 .

gmw_00007316.name: J091119G1
gmw_00007316.overview: J091119G1 is an almost edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1102.4339] with an inclination of approximately 80 to 82 degrees [arXiv:1110.5877, arXiv:1102.4339]. It features a large-scale galactic wind [arXiv:1110.5877] and exhibits broad absorption spanning roughly 790 km/s [arXiv:1102.4339].

sources.summary: The paper describes J091119G1 as an edge-on, high-impact-parameter case whose broad absorption requires a large constant outflow speed and may trace cone edges or a stalling wind.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:J0911 shows the wind model for J091119G1 towards the quasar SDSSJ091119.16 + 031152.9. This sight-line has one with the highest impact parameter ( b=71 kpc). While the equivalent width is relatively small ( 0.8 ), the absorption is spread over a very wide range of velocities (from 300 to 300 ). In addition, the galaxy is seen almost perfectly edge-on ( i 80^ ).

As a result, our constant V_ out model requires a large wind speed of 500 . We also note that the \ profile shows little or no absorption around V_ sys , which indicates either the absorbing material traces the edges of the cone or that this sight-line caught the wind as it stalls, in which case our assumption of pure radial wind velocities break down.

sources.summary: J091119G1 is an almost edge-on spiral 72 kpc from the quasar sightline; its absorber is offset from systemic velocity and spans two broad regions over about 790 km/s.

quotes.quote: J091119G1 In Figure fig:plotVr2 we show that the absorption system detected in the spectrum of the background quasar is associated with an almost edge-on ( i= 82 ^ ) 1.38L^ _r spiral galaxy located 72 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight. The rotation curve of G1, presented in Figure fig:plotVr2 d, is derived from and exhibits a maximum projected rotation velocity of 230 . The mean absorption redshift is offset by + 66 from the galaxy systemic velocity. The absorption consists of two broad regions that span roughly 790 .

gmw_00007317.name: J092300G1

sources.summary: J092300G1 is an S0-like galaxy only 12 kpc from the quasar sightline, with a 110 km/s rotation curve and strong partially saturated absorption offset from systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: J092300G1 In Figure fig:plotVr3 a we show that the absorption system detected in the spectrum of the background quasar is associated with an 2.27L^ _r S0-like galaxy located only 12 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight. The galaxy has an inclination of 56 degrees. The dynamics of G1, presented in Figure fig:plotVr3 b, is derived from absorption and exhibits a maximum projected rotation velocity of 110 . sources.summary: J102847G1 is a spiral 90 kpc from the quasar sightline whose weak saturated absorption is offset from systemic velocity and lies mostly on one side of the galaxy.

gmw_00007320.name: J114518G1
gmw_00007320.overview: J114518G1 is a spiral galaxy with a low inclination of approximately 30 to 34 degrees [arXiv:1102.4339, arXiv:1110.5877]. It exhibits a high star formation rate, blue colors, an exponential profile [arXiv:1110.5877], and a maximum projected rotation velocity of 160 km/s [arXiv:1102.4339].

sources.summary: J114518G1 is a spiral 34 kpc from the quasar sightline, with a 160 km/s rotation curve and strong mostly saturated absorption primarily on one side of systemic velocity.

quotes.quote: J114518G1 In Figure fig:plotVr4 we show that the absorption system detected in the spectrum of the background quasar is associated with an 2.23L^ _r spiral galaxy located 34 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight. sources.summary: The paper treats J114518G1 as a wind pair because its low inclination allows wind interception, the model matches the kinematics at V_out about 125 km/s, and its SFR is high.

quotes.quote: The other ambiguous case is J114518G1 towards SDSSJ114518.47 + 451601.4. This sight-line has a low of 15^ . However, the wind modeling (Figure fig:J1145 ) reveals that the sight-line can very well intercept the wind given the low galaxy inclination ( i 30^ ) if the cone extends a bit beyond our canonical 30 deg to 40 deg. The wind model can account for the \ kinematics with an outflow speed of V_ out 125 . In addition, the galaxy SFR is high with SFR =2.59 , blue colors and an exponential profile, which are additional reasons to consider this pair as a `wind' pair.

The data suggest that G1 exhibits more of a global shear than rotation and has a maximum observed shear velocity of 67 . The mean absorption redshift is offset by 62 from the galaxy systemic velocity. The absorption consists of a large broad component that spans roughly 558 .

gmw_00007323.name: J225036G1
gmw_00007323.overview: J225036G1 is a highly inclined elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1102.4339, arXiv:1110.5877] located 54 kpc from a background quasar sightline [arXiv:1102.4339]. It exhibits a moderate star formation rate [arXiv:1110.5877] and a maximum projected rotation velocity of 240 km/s [arXiv:1102.4339].

sources.summary: J225036G1 is a luminous elliptical 54 kpc from the quasar sightline, with a 240 km/s rotation curve and strong mostly saturated absorption spanning both systemic-velocity sides.

quotes.quote: J225036G1 In Figure fig:plotVr7 a we show that the absorption system detected in the spectrum of the background quasar is associated with an 4.27L^ _r elliptical galaxy located 54 kpc from the quasar line-of-sight. sources.summary: The paper describes J225036G1 as a moderately star-forming, highly inclined galaxy whose wide absorption kinematics are consistent with a hollow cone and V_out about 200 km/s.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:J2250 shows the wind model for J225036G1 towards SDSSJ225036.72 + 000759.4. This galaxy has a moderate SFR of 1.36 , is highly inclined with i 70^ and the quasar impact parameter is b 50 . The radial wind speed V_ out is inferred to be 200 . Given this geometric configuration with the inclination approaching 90 ^ , the sight-line probes a wide range of velocities, a feature that is very consistent with the observed \ kinematics. Note also that the profile suggests that the cone is hollow, i.e. that the \ absorption arise on the edges on the cone, which is the reason why we used _ =15^ in this case.

gmw_00007326.name: UGC 06786
gmw_00007326.overview: UGC 06786, also known as NGC 3900, is a high-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:2208.04326] classified as SA()0^+ [arXiv:1108.3662]. It features a ring bounding the denser part of its disk, surrounded by a fainter spiral zone and highly elliptical outer isophotes that may indicate a warp or interaction [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: UGC 06786 is a high-surface-brightness rotation-curve example where compressed dark matter forces reductions in bulge and disk stellar mass-to-light ratios to fit the data.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:compression presents as examples the rotation curves of two high-surface-brightness galaxies, NGC 3198 and UGC 06786. The fits of Li2020 for these galaxies are satisfactory, given the steeply rising rotation curves of high-surface-brightness galaxies can accommodate the NFW model. However, computing the compression without reassessing the initial conditions make the total contributions overshoot the data (Fig. \ fig:compression ). The difference is more substantial at small radii, where the effect of the baryons is maximized. Consequently, the DM halo is no longer NFW in form.

For UGC 06786, both the bulge and disk contributions are decreased to leave room for the larger contribution of the compressed DM halo by adjusting their stellar mass-to-light ratios to lower values. The bottom panels plot their prior-compression (namely NFW) and post-compression halo density profiles. The compressed halos present much higher densities at small radii with respect to the initial ones.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3900's outer profile as III-d and describes spiral structure, rings, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3900 (III-d; SA()0 ^+ ) : A ring between 31 (3.9 kpc) and 47 (5.9 kpc) bounds the denser part of the disk, surrounded by a fainter zone with a spiral form. The ring shows up as a feature with local changes in gradient on the brightness profile. The isophotes remain highly elliptical beyond the break; for > 240 (30 kpc), they actually become more elliptical, which might hint at a warp or interaction affecting the outer disk. The broad, smooth transition zone between the inner and outer exponentials is similar to that seen for the Type III-d profiles of NGC 3489 and NGC 7177 in Paper I. It could indicate that we are seeing two co-extensive, superimposed components, though the high ellipticity of the isophotes implies that both components have a similar flattening.

gmw_00007327.name: 3C 212
gmw_00007327.overview: 3C 212 is a radio-loud red quasar located at a redshift of 1.049 [arXiv:astro-ph/0307397, arXiv:2305.05173]. It features a 216 kpc radio jet with multiple jet knots and outer lobe hot spots [arXiv:2011.03130, arXiv:2305.05173], alongside intrinsic X-ray and ultraviolet absorption systems [arXiv:astro-ph/0307397, arXiv:2305.05173].

sources.summary: The paper studies 3C 212 as a red, radio-loud quasar with X-ray absorption, intrinsic UV absorption, and possible weak extended X-ray emission tied to its radio lobes.

quotes.quote: The red quasar 3C 212 ( z=1.049 ) is one of the most distant and most luminous AGN which has shown evidence of an X-ray warm absorber. In order to further investigate this unusual quasar, we used /ACIS-S to observe 3C 212 for 19.5 ksec, resulting in a net detection of 4000 counts. The \ data confirm the presence of an excess absorbing column N_H 4 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 at the quasar redshift, but we find no compelling evidence for a warm absorber. Using both the \ and archival ROSAT PSPC data, we obtain very good fits for both a partially covered neutral absorber and a low-ionization ( U = 0.03 ) photo-ionized absorber. In the ultraviolet, 3C 212 shows a strong associated \ absorber.

3C 212 (Q0855+143) is a radio-loud quasar at redshift z = 1.049 . It is one of the prototype ``red quasars'' smith80 which have very faint optical counterparts, but are bright in the near infrared (1-2 m) rieke79 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 212 as a red quasar with a 216 kpc radio jet, bright-core radio structure, ALMA-constrained non-thermal radio emission, and moderate torus obscuration of the accretion disk.

quotes.quote: -3C 212 is a red quasar Aldcroft2003 with a radio jet of 216 kpc and 17 core contribution at 5 GHz. MERLIN observations at 6 cm and 15 cm Akujor1991 indicate multiple features including a bright core that best fitted with the fourth model in our fitting procedure (see Section sec:radio and Table tab:best_radio_fit ). To constrain the radio fit we used recent ALMA observations (see Table tab:submmdata ) which is dominated by the non-thermal radiation from the radio structure (Figure fig:sed ). Consistent with its classification as a red quasar our torus model indicates a combination of clumps and a homogeneous disk with moderate optical depth. These components result in moderate obscuration of the accretion disk emission.

sources.summary: SDSS J085841.44+140944.8 is a radio-loud red quasar with intrinsic X-ray absorption; ALMA resolves emission associated with jet knots and outer hot spots despite coarser resolution.

quotes.quote: SDSS J085841.44+140944.8 (3C 212), also part of the keenan21 sample, is a radio-loud red quasar at a redshift of 1.049 aldcroft03 , with an X-ray absorption system intrinsic to the quasar itself or its host galaxy aldcroft02 . The spatial resolution on the ALMA image is coarser than that of the 3.6 cm VLA map. Nevertheless, one can still identify the emission associated with the three jet knots in the north-east direction, as well as the two lobe hot spots farther out ( 40 projected kpc). The latter may be related to ongoing supply of energetic electrons and magnetic field to these regions e.g., machalski16 , but a multi-frequency analysis is required to confirm this scenario.

gmw_00007328.name: Arp 299-A
gmw_00007328.overview: Arp 299-A is an irregular galaxy [arXiv:1810.09897] and the eastern component of the interacting Arp 299 system [arXiv:1201.3220, arXiv:1810.09897]. Its core is a dust-enshrouded [arXiv:1201.3220], Compton-thick [arXiv:1611.01071] starburst [arXiv:1201.3220, arXiv:1611.01071] that may also host an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1611.01071].

sources.summary: The paper models Arp 299A's compact obscured nucleus, finding H2O/OH molecular lines, dense warm inner and outer components, inflowing gas, and a composite starburst/AGN power source.

quotes.quote: We probe the physical conditions in the core of Arp 299A and try to put constraints to the nature of its nuclear power source. We used Herschel Space Observatory far-infrared and submillimeter observations of H _ 2 O and OH rotational lines in Arp 299A to create a multi-component model of the galaxy. Nine H _ 2 O lines in absorption and eight in emission as well as four OH doublets in absorption and one in emission, are detected in Arp 299A.

The absorption in the ground state OH ^ 2 _ 3/2 ^ 2 _ 3/2 5 2 ^+- 3 2 ^- doublet at 119 m is found redshifted by 175 km s ^ 1 compared with other OH and H _ 2 O lines, suggesting a low excitation inflow. We find that at least two components are required in order to account for the excited molecular line spectrum. The inner component has a radius of 20-25 pc, a very high infrared surface brightness ( 3 10^ 13 L _ kpc ^ 2 ), warm dust ( T_ d >90 K), and a large H _ 2 column density (N _ H_ 2 >10^ 24 cm ^ 2 ).

The Compton-thick nature of the core makes it difficult to determine the nature of the buried power source, but the high surface brightness indicates that it is either an active galactic nucleus and/or a dense nuclear starburst. Our results are consistent with a composite source.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3690E as Arp 299's eastern irregular component, likely a retrograde-spiral remnant whose clusters trace gas density, extinction, and merger environment.

quotes.quote: The interacting system consists of a pair of two irregular galaxies: NGC 3690E NGC 3690E is commonly known as source A in the literature. in the eastern part, and NGC 3690W NGC 3690W is commonly known as sources B C. the western component. While the former is possibly the remnant of a retrograde spiral galaxy, the latter has a more disturbed morphology with multiple nuclei.

The cluster mass-galactocentric radius relation Because of its symmetrical morphological feature, the star cluster population of NGC 3690E was also used to check the tightness of the cluster mass-galactocentric radius relation. Does it only apply to the first, the third, and the fifth most massive clusters of each radial bin? Figure fig:mass-Rc shows the star cluster mass plotted against its galactocentric distance. The cluster mass decreases with an increasing galactocentric radius up to 2 kpc away from the galaxy nucleus, but after which it slowly increases until 4 kpc. While the high gas density in the nuclear regions of NGC 3690E likely explains the behaviour within 2 kpc, the steady increase beyond this point arises because of the extreme environments provided by the ongoing merging of the eastern component with its companion NGC 3690W.

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 299-A as a luminous infrared nuclear starburst with many compact radio sources, using VLBI monitoring to estimate its core-collapse supernova rate.

quotes.quote: The nuclear region of the Luminous Infra-red Galaxy Arp 299-A hosts a recent ( 10 Myr), intense burst of massive star formation which is expected to lead to numerous core-collapse supernovae (CCSNe). Previous VLBI observations, carried out with the EVN at 5.0 GHz and with the VLBA at 2.3 and 8.4 GHz, resulted in the detection of a large number of compact, bright, non-thermal sources in a region 150 pc in size. We aim at establishing the nature of all non-thermal, compact components in Arp 299-A, as well as estimating its core-collapse supernova rate.

The subject of this paper is Arp 299-A, the eastern component of the peculiar interacting system, Arp 299, in an early merging state Keel95 . Arp 299 is at a distance of 44.8 Mpc, for a resdhift of z=0.010811 as corrected to the Reference Frame defined by the 3K Microwave Background Radiation fixsen96 , and assuming H_0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ matter = 0.27 and _ vacuum = 0.73 . At the assumed distance, 1 \ corresponds to 217 pc. Arp 299 has an infrared luminosity L_ IR 6.7 10^ 11 L_ sanders03 , which is approaching the ULIRG category, and makes of Arp 299 the most luminous infra-red galaxy in the nearby 50 Mpc. HST and NICMOS observations have demonstrated that Arp 299-A is the most dust-enshrouded source in the system AH00 and is responsible for the largest fraction of the far-infrared luminosity CSG02 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3690B as a confirmed Compton-thick LINER, comparing its intrinsic hard continuum, steeper spectrum, low X-ray/[OIII] ratio, and FeK strength.

quotes.quote: Based on BeppoSAX data, sensitive above 10 keV up to 300 keV, two of the LINERs in the sample, NGC 3690B, and NGC 6240, show intrinsic continuum above 10 keV, typical of Compton-thick sources ptak_chandra_2003,ceca_enshrouded_2002, risaliti_distribution_1999,vignali_probing_1999 . Hereinafter we consider UGC 05101, NGC 3690B, and NGC 6240 objects as Compton-thick sources according to published literature results (hereinafter we refer to them as 'Confirmed Compton-thick '). Concerning the indirect Compton-thick diagnostics used above, two of the confirmed Compton-thick show flat spectra (UGC 05101 and NGC 6240) whereas NGC 3690B seems to have a somewhat steeper spectrum.

Moreover, the three confirmed Compton-thick show log(F_ X (2-10 keV)/F( [ OIII ] ))<0 , consistent with being Compton-thick object. Also, we have been able to determine the EW(FeK ) of the three confirmed Compton-thick in our sample, finding EW(FeK )=280 180 keV , EW(FeK )=230 110 keV and EW(FeK )=380 60 keV , for UGC 05101, NGC 3690B and NGC 6240 respectively (shown as black stars in Figs. cha3:fig:EWNH2 and cha3:fig:LHOIII_fek ).

gmw_00007329.name: UDFz-38537519
gmw_00007329.overview: UDFz-38537519 was initially reported as a faint galaxy at a redshift of approximately 7 [arXiv:0909.2437]. However, spectral energy distribution fitting suggests the object is more likely a low-mass interloper located at a redshift of approximately 1.5 [arXiv:0909.2437].

sources.summary: The paper revisits UDFz-38537519 as a reported faint z 7 galaxy, finding its colors near the selection boundary and favoring a low-redshift interloper interpretation.

quotes.quote: Moreover, in the right-hand panel of Fig 3 we show our SED fit to UDFz-38537519, the faintest z 7 galaxy reported by Oesch et al. (2009b). Adopting 2 upper limits for its non-detection in z_ 850 , this object is very close to the edge of the two-colour selection box employed by Oesch et al. (2009b), and our SED fitting analysis suggests that it is most likely a low-mass interloper at z 1.5 . This object illustrates the advantage of full SED fitting when dealing with objects close to the boundaries of colour-colour selection boxes in the presence of substantial photometric scatter.

gmw_00007330.name: AGC 749237
gmw_00007330.overview: AGC 749237 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits the largest single-dish line width among the SHIELD sample [arXiv:1609.05376]. Its rotation curve closely resembles those of larger dwarf and spiral galaxies, featuring a sharp velocity increase across its innermost parsecs before flattening at greater radial distances [arXiv:1609.05376].

sources.summary: The SHIELD analysis finds AGC 749237 has the sample rotation curve most like larger dwarfs, with a sharp inner velocity rise and the largest single-dish line width.

quotes.quote: AGC 749237 has the rotation curve most closely resembling those of larger dwarf and spiral galaxies (i.e., steeply rising with radius, then flat). There appears to be a sharp velocity increase across the innermost |sim 10 \ ( 560) parsecs of the galaxy, giving way to a flattened rotation curve at greater radial distance. The tapered and smoothed data have higher modeled deprojected rotation velocities because of a markedly different fitted inclination angle (by more than 20 ) than the other fits. It is important to note that AGC 749237 has the largest single-dish line width of any of the SHIELD galaxies. And yet, the various TRMs still show ambiguity in the final value of the circular rotation velocity of the source.

gmw_00007331.name: AGC 731457
gmw_00007331.overview: AGC 731457 is a dwarf galaxy characterized by a compact stellar component compared to its neutral gas [arXiv:1609.05376]. The galaxy features a centrally concentrated distribution with low-surface-brightness structures in its outer disk [arXiv:1609.05376].

sources.summary: The paper treats AGC 731457 as a difficult SHIELD dynamical case, with compact stars, extended low-surface-brightness H I, and inconsistent rotation-curve behavior across resolutions.

quotes.quote: AGC 731457 presents an especially difficult dynamical case (see Figures 731457.collage and 731457.slices ). The moment-zero map shows a centrally concentrated distribution, with low surface brightness structure in the outer disk. The stellar component is compact compared to the neutral gas.

The rotation curve of AGC 731457 is difficult to interpret. The tapered data favor a rising rotation curve at all radii, but the full resolution images are consistent with projected velocities equal to the velocity dispersion ( 10-15 ). This source is the second-most distant SHIELD galaxy except for AGC 749237 (see Table kin.props ). The solutions that we derive using this method should be interpreted with caution for each of the sources. However, unlike AGC 749237 (whose rotation curve is spatially resolved to a degree comparable with studies of more massive, closer galaxies), the rotation curve solutions for AGC 731457 appear to disagree even between maps of different resolution.

gmw_00007332.name: AGC 111977
gmw_00007332.overview: AGC 111977 is a dwarf galaxy that exhibits a clear major axis [arXiv:1609.05376]. Position-velocity analysis of the galaxy reveals a projected rotation signature of approximately 15 km/s [arXiv:1609.05376].

sources.summary: The SHIELD paper uses AGC 111977 as an example where position-velocity analysis recovers projected rotation, with a clear major axis and about 15 km/s rotation signature.

quotes.quote: AGC 111977 (see Figures 111977.collage and 111977.slices ) is a good example; the velocity field image suggests rotation along a clear major axis. The major axis P-V slice suggests that gas is moving at projected velocities between 180 \ and 210 , over an angular region spanning 45 . The projected rotation is apparent as a gradient in the velocity of the centroids of the gas along the minor axis slices; the same 15 \ of projected rotation is apparent.

gmw_00007333.name: 2MASX J22484405-4431507
gmw_00007333.overview: 2MASX J22484405-4431507 is the brightest cluster galaxy in the Abell S1063 cluster [arXiv:2001.04725]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a radio spectral index typical for a cluster AGN [arXiv:2001.04725].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J22484405-4431507 as the Abell S1063 BCG, measures its radio fluxes, and finds a cluster-AGN-like spectral index.

quotes.quote: Source A, namely rxj2248 17936 The ID number in CLASH catalog Postman2012CLASH . or 2MASX J22484405-4431507, is the BCG in AS1063. The integrated flux densities of the BCG are 8.5 1.1 mJy at 325 MHz, 2.1 0.2 mJy at 1.5 GHz, and 1.0 0.1 mJy at 3.0 GHz. This corresponds to a spectral index of about 1 , typical for a cluster AGN.

gmw_00007334.name: MG2016+112
gmw_00007334.overview: MG2016+112 is a massive elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.004 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412596]. It acts as a gravitational lens, producing multiple images of a background quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0412596].

sources.summary: The paper uses MG2016+112 as an LSD example: a massive elliptical lens at z=1.004 with Keck dispersion and modeled dark-matter and stellar mass profiles.

quotes.quote: The first target of the Lenses Structure Dynamics LSD survey, the lens system MG2016+112, is shown in Fig.1, as an example, consisting of a massive elliptical galaxy (G) at z =1.004, multiply imaging a quasar at z =3.273 in two images (A B). An 8.5--hr Keck ESI spectrum was obtained from which the stellar dispersion was determined ( =304 27 km/s). Given the mass enclosed by the images -- determined from the lens model -- one can calculate the velocity dispersion profile of the lens galaxy, with the inner dark-matter mass slope ( with _ dm ^ ) and stellar mass-to-light ratio ( M_*/L_B ) as free parameters in a two-component luminous plus dark-matter mass model (Treu Koopmans 2002). A useful simplification is to model the luminous plus dark-matter with a single density profile with slope ' . By comparing the model and the observed kinematic profiles, one can place likelihood constraints on the values of M_*/L_B , _ CDM and ' , as illustrated in Fig.1.

gmw_00007335.overview: Object 209 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate that exhibits a clear Lyman break [arXiv:1411.5691]. It has been detected in five infrared filters and is situated more than 2 arcseconds away from its nearest neighboring object [arXiv:1411.5691].

sources.summary: Object 209 is presented as a Pop III galaxy candidate with the clearest Lyman break, IR detections, Ly-enhanced model fit, and little nearby contamination.

quotes.quote: 209 As can be seen in Figure  fig:RF1 , the object 209 contains the clearest Ly break of the five objects we discuss in this paper. It has clear detections (S/N >5 ) in all five IR filters. In Figure  fig:RF2 , we plot observations versus the best-fit Pop III galaxy model and the best fitted model from CWW, Kinney and Gissel are compared. The clear Lyman-break is seen and also the steep decline in the flux of observations with wavelength. The strong Ly line, which enters the filters F105W, F110W and F125W, in the Pop III model causes it to be in better agreement with the observations compared to Gissel and CWW, Kinney.

However, as can be seen in Figure  fig:RF2 , there are Z > 0 Yggdrasil template fits closely resembling the Pop III model fits. Only the small peak described in Section  sec:popiiigalaxycandidates is not reproduced by the Z > 0 Yggdrasil templates, which instead find a lower photometric redshift solution. The nearest object to 209 is situated more than 2 '' away, as shown in Figure fig:RF_a209_3 .

gmw_00007336.overview: The object 1931 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate located behind the galaxy cluster MACS1931 [arXiv:1411.5691]. It exhibits extremely red colors, with spectral energy distribution modeling favoring a Population III galaxy solution at a redshift of z = 8.0 to 9.0 [arXiv:1411.5691].

sources.summary: Object 1931 is described as an extreme-color Pop III candidate behind MACS1931, with F110W/F125W detections and cross-validation favoring z=8-9 Pop III models.

quotes.quote: 1931 The object we have discovered behind the cluster MACS1931 has very extreme colors, even for a possible Pop III galaxy (see Figure  fig:colorcolordiagram ). In the thumbnail images (Figure  fig:RF1 ), this can be seen as a strong detection in F125W while the other filters have lower fluxes. This object has two 5 detections in F110W and F125W with the detection in F105W falling short at S/N 4.9 . The best-fit solutions are shown in Figure  fig:RF2 , along with the observed fluxes.

Whereas Pop III models do a reasonably good job at explaining the observed SED, comparison models fare much worse and are unable to simultaneously fit the bright flux in the F125W and the lower fluxes in adjacent filters. The object's cross-validation fit, seen in Figure  fig:RF2 , shows a very clear preference for a Pop III galaxy solution in the redshift interval z = 8.0-9.0 . However, caution must be expressed about the nature of this object because of its extreme colors, placing it outside the Pop III region in the color-color diagram.

gmw_00007337.name: NGC 4168
gmw_00007337.overview: NGC 4168 is an elliptical galaxy located within a galaxy group [arXiv:astro-ph/0611858]. The pressure map of its field is elongated to the north, which may indicate the direction of gas accretion [arXiv:astro-ph/0611858].

sources.summary: The paper reports pressure and entropy peaks in the NGC 4168 field, separates a background galaxy from the central galaxy, and notes limited counts prevent detailed group analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 4168, z=0.0077 The two peaks in the pressure and entropy maps correspond to the two bright galaxies in the XMM field of view. The pressure map does not show any sign of hydrodynamic interaction between those two galaxies and is elongated to the north, possibly indicating the direction of the gas accretion. Lack of counts precludes a detailed analysis of this group. The galaxy to the west (NGC 4164) turns out to be a background galaxy ( V_H=17500 , while the central galaxy in this group, NGC 4168, has a velocity of V_H=2784 ). While a discrimination of the background galaxy is trivially done by separating them in the velocity space, the important point is that we were able to separate the effects of projection from the interaction.

gmw_00007338.name: SDSS J094533.99+100950.1
gmw_00007338.overview: SDSS J094533.99+100950.1 is a weak-line quasar at a redshift of z = 1.66 [arXiv:0904.3517]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits weak ultraviolet emission lines [arXiv:1112.2228], practically no C IV emission [arXiv:0904.3517], and is X-ray weak compared to typical quasars [arXiv:1112.2228].

sources.summary: J0945+1009 is treated as a weak-featured quasar with weak UV lines, a highly blueshifted line, UV-deficient cold-disk-like SED, no Chandra detection, and X-ray weakness by factors of 8-12.

quotes.quote: J0945+1009 ( < 0.34 ) has weak \ and C iii ] emission lines. Its \ line is highly blueshifted ( 7000 km s ^ 1 ).

For one source in our sample, J0945+1009, the flux in the UV band is lower than for typical SDSS quasars (see the GALEX data points in the top-right panel of Fig. sed1_fig ). The UV deficiency of this source may be caused by Ly forest intervening absorption. However, Laor Davis (2011) argued that such intervening absorption is not significant ( 11 at most) for this source with z=1.66 . The near-infrared-to-UV SED of J0945+1009 can be well fitted with their local black-body model for a cold accretion disk.

Our main results are summarized as follows: All newly observed low-redshift , radio-quiet WLQ candidates are detected by , except for J0945+1009. Three sources (J0812+5225, J0945+1009, and J1252+2640) are \ weak by factors of 8--12 compared to typical quasars with similar optical/UV luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J094533.99+100950.1 as a z=1.66 weak-line quasar with almost no C IV emission, moderate Eddington ratio, and newly started quasar activity.

quotes.quote: The quasar SDSS J094533.99+100950.1, lying at z = 1.66 , has practically no C IV emission line, a red continuum very similar to the second steepest of the quasar composite spectra of Richards et al., is not strongly affected by absorption and the Mg II line, although relatively weak, is strong enough to measure the black hole mass. The Eddington ratio in this object is about 0.45, and the line properties are not consistent with the trends expected at high accretion rates. We propose that the most probable explanation of the line properties in this object, and perhaps in all weak emission line quasars, is that the quasar activity has just started. A disk wind is freshly launched so the low ionization lines which form close to the disk surface are already observed but the wind has not yet reached the regions where high ionization lines or narrow line components are formed.

gmw_00007339.name: SDSS J0926+3343
gmw_00007339.overview: SDSS J0926+3343 is a nearby dwarf galaxy located in the Local Volume [arXiv:1105.3034]. It is one of the most metal-poor dwarf galaxies in this region and features unusually blue outer colors [arXiv:1105.3034].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J0926+3343 as one of the two most metal-poor nearby dwarf galaxies, close to DDO 68 and unusually blue in its outer parts.

quotes.quote: The two most metal-poor dwarf galaxies in the Local Volume and adjacent regions, DDO 68 and SDSS J0926+3343, with the parameters 12+ (O/H) of 7.14 and 7.12, respectively, are representatives of this void galaxy population. Moreover, they are situated only at 1.6 Mpc from each other. Both of them are also unusual in the blue colours of their outer parts, indicating ages of the oldest visible stellar population of 1--3 Gyr DDO68_sdss,J0926 , which is in drastic contrast with the colours and ages of the absolute majority of other dwarf galaxies.

gmw_00007340.name: J1117+3927
gmw_00007340.overview: J1117+3927 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 3 [arXiv:1703.09653]. The active galactic nucleus hosts parsec-scale outflows with a low kinetic luminosity relative to its Eddington limit [arXiv:1703.09653].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1117+3927 as one of three z~3 BAL quasars with leaked emission, estimating parsec-scale outflow radii and low kinetic luminosity relative to Eddington.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present three z=3 quasars charaterizing close-to-black absorption troughs and spiky leaked emission lines, and demonstrate how our method is applied on them. As a result of a comprehensive search in the SDSS-III DR12 database (see Section 2 for details), these objects are among the most representative examples of this special class of BAL quasars.

Inserting these numbers into Equation (3), we find the following outflow radii (Table 1): (1) J1117+3927: 2.96 pc (Gaussian fit) or 25.62 pc (non-parametric); (2) J1140+6241: 3.50 pc (Gaussian fit) or 12.09 pc (non-parametric); (3) J1027+1939: 4.26 pc (Gaussian fit) or 6.46 pc (non-parametric). As mentioned in 3.3.3, due to the narrow absorptions, the FWHMs of directly measurement are significantly smaller than that of Gaussian fitting for J1117+3927 and J1140+6241. As a result, the R_ BLR deduced from FWHM of directly measurement is larger than that from Gaussian fitting. We take the R_ BLR deduced from FWHM of directly measurement as an upper limit.

Correspondingly, the lower limit of M is 1.4, 1.0 and 6.1 for J1117+3927, J1140+6241 and J1027+1939, and the lower limit of E_ k / is 0.05 , 0.02 , 0.07 , respectively. It is worth mentioning that Kurosawa09 predicted the quasar outflow efficiencies are as low as found in our work.

gmw_00007341.name: J1140+6241
gmw_00007341.overview: J1140+6241 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 3 [arXiv:1703.09653]. The active galaxy hosts a parsec-scale outflow with an estimated radius of 3.50 to 12.09 parsecs [arXiv:1703.09653].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1140+6241 as one of three z~3 BAL quasars with leaked emission, estimating parsec-scale outflow radii and low kinetic luminosity relative to Eddington.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present three z=3 quasars charaterizing close-to-black absorption troughs and spiky leaked emission lines, and demonstrate how our method is applied on them. As a result of a comprehensive search in the SDSS-III DR12 database (see Section 2 for details), these objects are among the most representative examples of this special class of BAL quasars.

Inserting these numbers into Equation (3), we find the following outflow radii (Table 1): (1) J1117+3927: 2.96 pc (Gaussian fit) or 25.62 pc (non-parametric); (2) J1140+6241: 3.50 pc (Gaussian fit) or 12.09 pc (non-parametric); (3) J1027+1939: 4.26 pc (Gaussian fit) or 6.46 pc (non-parametric). As mentioned in 3.3.3, due to the narrow absorptions, the FWHMs of directly measurement are significantly smaller than that of Gaussian fitting for J1117+3927 and J1140+6241. As a result, the R_ BLR deduced from FWHM of directly measurement is larger than that from Gaussian fitting. We take the R_ BLR deduced from FWHM of directly measurement as an upper limit.

Correspondingly, the lower limit of M is 1.4, 1.0 and 6.1 for J1117+3927, J1140+6241 and J1027+1939, and the lower limit of E_ k / is 0.05 , 0.02 , 0.07 , respectively. It is worth mentioning that Kurosawa09 predicted the quasar outflow efficiencies are as low as found in our work.

gmw_00007342.name: J1027+1939
gmw_00007342.overview: J1027+1939 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 3 [arXiv:1703.09653]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits close-to-black absorption troughs with spiky leaked emission lines and hosts parsec-scale outflows [arXiv:1703.09653].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1027+1939 as one of three z~3 BAL quasars with leaked emission, estimating parsec-scale outflow radii and low kinetic luminosity relative to Eddington.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present three z=3 quasars charaterizing close-to-black absorption troughs and spiky leaked emission lines, and demonstrate how our method is applied on them. As a result of a comprehensive search in the SDSS-III DR12 database (see Section 2 for details), these objects are among the most representative examples of this special class of BAL quasars.

Inserting these numbers into Equation (3), we find the following outflow radii (Table 1): (1) J1117+3927: 2.96 pc (Gaussian fit) or 25.62 pc (non-parametric); (2) J1140+6241: 3.50 pc (Gaussian fit) or 12.09 pc (non-parametric); (3) J1027+1939: 4.26 pc (Gaussian fit) or 6.46 pc (non-parametric). As mentioned in 3.3.3, due to the narrow absorptions, the FWHMs of directly measurement are significantly smaller than that of Gaussian fitting for J1117+3927 and J1140+6241. As a result, the R_ BLR deduced from FWHM of directly measurement is larger than that from Gaussian fitting. We take the R_ BLR deduced from FWHM of directly measurement as an upper limit.

Correspondingly, the lower limit of M is 1.4, 1.0 and 6.1 for J1117+3927, J1140+6241 and J1027+1939, and the lower limit of E_ k / is 0.05 , 0.02 , 0.07 , respectively. It is worth mentioning that Kurosawa09 predicted the quasar outflow efficiencies are as low as found in our work.

gmw_00007343.name: PJ308-21
gmw_00007343.overview: PJ308-21 is a quasar host galaxy at redshift z = 6.2342 [arXiv:1906.05308, arXiv:2402.13319] that is interacting with two companion sources in an early galaxy merger [arXiv:1906.05308, arXiv:2402.13319]. The system exhibits dust, gas, and young stars [arXiv:1906.05308], alongside a supermassive black hole that may drive a nuclear gas outflow [arXiv:2402.13319].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA and HST imaging of PJ308--21 as a z=6.2342 quasar-companion system, interpreting its resolved gas, dust, young stars, and velocities as an early galaxy merger.

quotes.quote: We present kpc--scale ALMA and HST imaging of the quasar PJ308--21 at z = 6.2342 , tracing dust, gas (via the 158 m line) and young stars. At a resolution of 0.3'' ( 1.7 kpc), the system is resolved over >4'' ( > 20 kpc). In particular, it features a main component, identified to be the quasar host galaxy, centered on the accreting supermassive black hole; and two other extended components on the West and East side, one redshifted and the other blueshifted relative to the quasar. The emission of the entire system stretches over > 1500 along the line of sight. All the components of the system are observed in dust, , and rest--frame UV emission.

A toy model of a single satellite galaxy that is tidally stripped by the interaction with the quasar host galaxy can account for the observed velocity and spatial extent of the two extended components. An outflow interpretation of the unique features in PJ308--21 is not supported by the data. PJ308--21 is thus one of the earliest galaxy mergers imaged at cosmic dawn.

sources.summary: The paper presents JWST/NIRSpec spectroscopy of quasar PJ308-21, describing its interacting companion sources, high-redshift optical spectrum, billion-solar-mass black hole, high Eddington ratio, and possible nuclear outflow.

quotes.quote: We present JWST/NIRSpec integral field data of the quasar PJ308-21 at z = 6.2342 . As shown by previous ALMA and HST imaging, the quasar has two companion sources, interacting with the quasar host galaxy. The high-resolution G395H/290LP NIRSpec spectrum covers the 2.87 - 5.27 \ m wavelength range and shows the rest-frame optical emission of the quasar with exquisite quality ( S/N 100 - 400 per spectral element). Based on the \ line from the broad line region, we obtain an estimate of the black hole mass M_ BH, H 2.7 10^ 9 \ M_ .

The high Eddington ratio of PJ308-21 _ Edd, H 0.67 ( _ Edd, H 0.96 ) is in line with the overall quasar population at z 6 . The relative strengths of the , \ and \ lines are consistent with the empirical "Eigenvector 1" correlations as observed for low redshift quasars. We find evidence for blueshifted \ 5007 emission with a velocity offset v_ O \ III = -1922 39 \ from the systemic velocity and a FWHM( ) = 2776^ +75 _ 74 . This may be the signature of outflowing gas from the nuclear region, despite the true values of v_ O \ III and FWHM( ) are likely more uncertain due to the blending with \ and \ lines.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes PJ308-21 as a z=6.23 quasar host in a companion system, with high-J CO evidence suggesting AGN/XDR excitation and less complete line coverage.

quotes.quote: This work is focused on the ISM characterization of two z>6 quasar host galaxies, PJ231-20 at z 6.59 , and PJ308-21 at z 6.24 , and the nearby companions which were serendipitously discovered with ALMA Decarli+2017, Neeleman+2019 . These companions are among the most star-forming galaxies known to date (with [ CII ] -based SFR 200-500 M_ yr^ 1 ) at z>6 that do not show evidence of AGN activity in the rest-frame optical/UV Decarli+2017, Mazzucchelli+2019 and in the X-rays Connor+2019, Connor+2020 . For these objects, the [ CII ] _ 158 m line and dust continuum have been clearly detected with previous ALMA observations Decarli+2017 , while higher-resolution [ CII ] _ 158 m follow-up observations have allowed study of their kinematics see Decarli+2019, Neeleman+2019 . The companion galaxies in the two systems, have a projected separation of 8.5 kpc and 14 kpc , with a velocity offset of +137 km s^ 1 and +591 km s^ 1 for PJ231-20 and PJ308-21, respectively.

While little information is available for the PJ308-21 companion, in the case of the quasar host we observe a high CO(15--14)/CO(1--0) luminosity ratio, possibly revealing the contribution of the AGN exciting this high- J CO ladder see e.g. Schleicher+2010, Gallerani+2014, Carniani+2019 . Finally, the observed CO(7--6)/CO(1--0) luminosity ratios in both PJ231-20 quasar host and the companion, and the upper limit available for PJ308-21 QSO, are lower than other z>6 quasars e.g. Venemans+2017a , while are consistent within uncertainties with average CO SLED model of the z 2 starburst galaxies in the ASPECS field.

gmw_00007344.name: PJ231-20 companion
gmw_00007344.overview: The PJ231-20 companion is a high-redshift galaxy located near a quasar host [arXiv:2105.09958]. It is a highly dust-obscured, starburst-like galaxy with no detected active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2105.09958, arXiv:1907.02607].

sources.summary: The paper describes the PJ231-20 companion as a high-redshift star-forming companion with detected CO lines, dust-obscured starburst-like SED, and no clear AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: This work is focused on the ISM characterization of two z>6 quasar host galaxies, PJ231-20 at z 6.59 , and PJ308-21 at z 6.24 , and the nearby companions which were serendipitously discovered with ALMA Decarli+2017, Neeleman+2019 . These companions are among the most star-forming galaxies known to date (with [ CII ] -based SFR 200-500 M_ yr^ 1 ) at z>6 that do not show evidence of AGN activity in the rest-frame optical/UV Decarli+2017, Mazzucchelli+2019 and in the X-rays Connor+2019, Connor+2020 . For these objects, the [ CII ] _ 158 m line and dust continuum have been clearly detected with previous ALMA observations Decarli+2017 , while higher-resolution [ CII ] _ 158 m follow-up observations have allowed study of their kinematics see Decarli+2019, Neeleman+2019 . The companion galaxies in the two systems, have a projected separation of 8.5 kpc and 14 kpc , with a velocity offset of +137 km s^ 1 and +591 km s^ 1 for PJ231-20 and PJ308-21, respectively.

On the other hand, the CO SLED of PJ231-20 companion galaxy is consistent within 1 with both the starbursts and AGN population, even taking into account the upper limits on CO(15--14)/CO(1--0) and CO(16--15)/CO(1--0) luminosity ratios. On the other hand, if we assume a dust temperature of 35 K , the CO SLED appears similar to the average CO SLED of starburst galaxies. However, Mazzucchelli+2019 do not detect the PJ231-20 companion in the rest-frame optical/UV wavelengths, revealing that this source is highly dust-enshrouded with a SED similar to Arp 220, that is both highly star-forming and highly dust-obscured ULIRG in the local Universe. In addition, Connor+2020 do not detect the PJ231-20 companion even in X-ray observations, thus ruling out the presence of an AGN in this source, at least with luminosity similar to those of optically selected quasars. These previous studies suggest that star formation should dominate the radiation field (and therefore the CO excitation) in this source.

sources.summary: PJ231c is treated as a z~6 quasar companion whose UV/optical limits fit an Arp 220-like, dust-obscured luminous infrared galaxy with star formation dominated by dust emission.

quotes.quote: The limits on rest--frame UV/optical brightness of PJ231c, J2100c and J0842c rule out all the galaxy templates considered here, with the exception of Arp 220. These companions have infrared luminosities in the range of (ultra-) luminous galaxies (e.g. 0.9--5 10 ^ 12 L _ ; Decarli17 ).

We compute the star formation rates for PJ231c, J2100c and J0842c assuming that their SEDs are equivalent to that of Arp 220, shifted in redshift and scaled as in Figure figSEDobs . We derive their star formation rates from the dust emission in the rest-frame infrared region (SFR _ IR ), assuming that the non-obscured SFR as negligible (see Section secAnalysisSFRUV ). The obtained values range between 120 and 700 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00007345.name: PJ308-21 companion galaxy
gmw_00007345.overview: The PJ308-21 companion galaxy is a high-redshift satellite galaxy located at a redshift of z > 6 [arXiv:2105.09958]. It exhibits extended carbon emission stretching over 25 kiloparsecs, which suggests the companion is being tidally stripped by its central quasar host [arXiv:2105.09958].

sources.summary: The paper describes the PJ308-21 companion galaxy as a high-redshift companion with extended [CII] emission suggestive of tidal stripping, though limited detections leave its AGN status uncertain.

quotes.quote: This work is focused on the ISM characterization of two z>6 quasar host galaxies, PJ231-20 at z 6.59 , and PJ308-21 at z 6.24 , and the nearby companions which were serendipitously discovered with ALMA Decarli+2017, Neeleman+2019 . These companions are among the most star-forming galaxies known to date (with [ CII ] -based SFR 200-500 M_ yr^ 1 ) at z>6 that do not show evidence of AGN activity in the rest-frame optical/UV Decarli+2017, Mazzucchelli+2019 and in the X-rays Connor+2019, Connor+2020 . For these objects, the [ CII ] _ 158 m line and dust continuum have been clearly detected with previous ALMA observations Decarli+2017 , while higher-resolution [ CII ] _ 158 m follow-up observations have allowed study of their kinematics see Decarli+2019, Neeleman+2019 . The companion galaxies in the two systems, have a projected separation of 8.5 kpc and 14 kpc , with a velocity offset of +137 km s^ 1 and +591 km s^ 1 for PJ231-20 and PJ308-21, respectively.

Remarkably, the [ CII ] _ 158 m emission of the companion galaxy in PJ308-21 system, stretches over about 25 kpc and >1500 km s^ 1 towards and beyond the quasar host suggesting that the satellite galaxy is tidally stripped by the interaction with the central quasar host galaxy Decarli+2017,Decarli+2019 . Furthermore, in both PJ231-20 and PJ308-21 systems, Ly nebular emission have been recently discovered Farina+2019 , possibly indicating that also PJ231-20 quasar recently underwent a merger event with the close companion galaxy.

While little information is available for the PJ308-21 companion, in the case of the quasar host we observe a high CO(15--14)/CO(1--0) luminosity ratio, possibly revealing the contribution of the AGN exciting this high- J CO ladder see e.g. Schleicher+2010, Gallerani+2014, Carniani+2019 . Finally, the observed CO(7--6)/CO(1--0) luminosity ratios in both PJ231-20 quasar host and the companion, and the upper limit available for PJ308-21 QSO, are lower than other z>6 quasars e.g. Venemans+2017a , while are consistent within uncertainties with average CO SLED model of the z 2 starburst galaxies in the ASPECS field.

gmw_00007346.name: LEDA 1245338
gmw_00007346.overview: LEDA 1245338 is a bright, red elliptical galaxy with a high stellar mass [arXiv:2307.02556]. It is classified as a "red and dead" galaxy due to its negligible recent star formation [arXiv:2307.02556].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 1245338, also SDSS J111927.73+030632.7, as the bright red elliptical host of AT2022aedm with high stellar mass and negligible recent star formation.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of AT2022aedm is LEDA 1245338 (or SDSS J111927.73+030632.7). This is a bright, red galaxy with M_r=-22.8 mag; Figure fig:host illustrates this with a colour image obtained from Pan-STARRS. An SDSS spectrum is also available (shown in Figure fig:spec ). Both the SDSS spectral fitting and Galaxy Zoo morphological analysis classify LEDA 1245338 as an elliptical galaxy.

We measure M_*=10^ 11.45 , consistent with SDSS results, and a specific SFR in the last 50 Myr of ( sSFR/yr ^ 1 ) = -11.69 . The SDSS analyses and our prospector results confirm that the host of AT2022aedm is a massive, `red and dead' galaxy.

gmw_00007347.name: spiral galaxy lens (RA=21:41:54.67, DEC= 00:01:12.2, J2000)
aliases_external: SWELLS spiral galaxy lens at RA=21:41:54.67 Dec=+00:01:12.2
gmw_00007347.overview: The spiral galaxy lens (RA=21:41:54.67, DEC= 00:01:12.2, J2000) is a disk-dominated galaxy with a disk inclination of 80 degrees [arXiv:1101.1622]. Located at a redshift of 0.1380, the galaxy has a measured velocity dispersion of 181 km/s and acts as a gravitational lens [arXiv:1101.1622].

sources.summary: The paper models a disk-dominated spiral lens at the stated coordinates, combining photometry, redshift, velocity dispersion, lensing, and kinematics to constrain its mass structure.

quotes.quote: In this paper, the second of the SWELLS series, we present a detailed and self-consistent mass model of the spiral galaxy lens (RA=21:41:54.67, DEC= 00:01:12.2, J2000), constrained by both kinematic and lensing data. As we will see, this galaxy is disk-dominated, with a disk inclination of 80^ ; this set-up approximately maximises the projected disk mass while allowing an accurate rotation curve to be measured.

The latest public SDSS-DR7 (Abazajian 2009) Petrosian magnitudes (uncorrected for extinction) for the lens galaxy are (u,g,,i,z)=(20.61,18.62,17.47,16.92,16.48) with errors (0.15,0.01,0.01,0.01,0.02) . The SDSS measured redshift for the lens galaxy is =0.1380 0.00015 , and the velocity dispersion is 181 14 . The spectrum also exhibits nebular emission lines at a background redshift of =0.7127 (Bolton 2008).

gmw_00007348.name: Q0454 + 039 57
gmw_00007348.overview: Q0454 + 039 57 is a faint, emission-detected galaxy located at an impact parameter of 18 kpc from a damped Ly-alpha absorber [arXiv:2305.11219]. Due to this low impact parameter and the high column density of the absorber, it likely traces gas within the galaxy's disk [arXiv:2305.11219].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q0454 + 039 57 as a single emission-detected galaxy 18 kpc from a damped Ly absorber, likely tracing disk gas despite lacking kinematic modelling.

quotes.quote: Absorbing gas associated with the galaxy disk Q0454 + 039 z_ abs = 0.8596 ; = 20.69 This damped Ly- absorber is associated with a single galaxy (Q0454 + 039 57) at an impact parameter of 18 kpc. The galaxy is detected in emission but is too faint to model with galpak . Given the high column density of the absorber and low impact parameter of b < 20 kpc, it is likely that the absorber traces gas in the galaxy disk. However, no kinematic nor photometric modelling is available to confirm this hypothesis.

gmw_00007349.name: Q0454 + 039 15
gmw_00007349.overview: Q0454 + 039 15 is a galaxy located at an impact parameter of 60 kpc from a z = 1.1532 absorber [arXiv:2305.11219]. The galaxy's projected minor axis is aligned with the quasar sightline, and the absorber is interpreted as tracing gas expelled from the system [arXiv:2305.11219].

sources.summary: Q0454 + 039 15 lies 60 kpc from the absorber with the sightline near its minor axis; the absorber is interpreted as likely expelled gas.

quotes.quote: Q0454 + 039 z_ abs = 1.1532 ; = 18.59 The galaxy at lowest impact parameter to the absorber (Q0454 + 039 15, b = 60 kpc) has a projected minor axis aligned with the QSO sightline ( = 62^ ; see the second row in fig:Maps4 ). Q0454 + 039 65 is another associated galaxy that is located 130 kpc from the absorber for which we do not have kinematic modelling. We find that the absorber is likely tracing expelled gas from Q0454 + 039 15. While the velocity of the absorber is the same sign as the galaxy velocity field, the v_ LOS 290 is multiple times the measured maximum rotational velocity.

gmw_00007350.name: Q0152 + 0023 20
gmw_00007350.overview: Q0152 + 0023 20 is a galaxy with an estimated stellar mass of 10 to the power of 8.1 solar masses [arXiv:2305.11219]. It is the nearest galaxy to a high column density absorber, with velocities suggesting the absorbing gas may be co-rotating [arXiv:2305.11219].

sources.summary: Q0152 + 0023 20 is the nearest galaxy to its absorber; velocities suggest co-rotation, but the absorber lies beyond the virial radius and may trace another low-mass galaxy.

quotes.quote: Importantly, the stellar mass of the galaxy Q0152 + 0023 20 associated with the absorber is (M_*/M_ ) = 8.1 0.1 and the estimated virial radius, R_ vir , is 70 kpc Puebla2017 . Despite the alignment in velocity, the absorber is beyond the halo of the associated galaxy and it is possible that a passive, low-mass galaxy (SFR < 0.19 \ , (M_*/M_ ) < 8.5 ) below the MUSE detection threshold nearer the QSO sightline hosts the dense absorber.

Absorbing gas of unclear origin Q0152 + 0023 z_ abs = 0.4818 ; = 19.78 We find four galaxies associated with this absorber, with impact parameters ranging from 120 to 190 kpc. For the nearest galaxy, Q0152 + 0023 20, to the QSO sightline, the velocity of the absorber is consistent with co-rotating gas. The absorber also aligns with the ionized gas map of another galaxy (Q0152 + 0023 13) 10 kpc away from Q0152 + 0023 20. This can be seen in the last row of fig:Maps2 . However, despite the consistency in velocities, the high column density absorber is outside the virial radius of both galaxies and hence, likely traces a quiescent, low-mass galaxy near the sightline.

gmw_00007351.name: Q0454 220 69
gmw_00007351.overview: Q0454 220 69 is a continuum-faint galaxy located within 10 kiloparsecs of an absorber [arXiv:2305.11219]. This nearby absorber appears to be counter-rotating with respect to the galaxy, although modeling is complicated by the galaxy's small size and contamination from a nearby quasar [arXiv:2305.11219].

sources.summary: Q0454 220 69 is a continuum-faint galaxy within 10 kpc of an absorber; the gas appears counter-rotating, though the fit is unreliable because of contamination.

quotes.quote: Q0454 220 z_ abs = 0.4744 ; = 19.45 The associated galaxy Q0454 220 69 has an impact parameter of < 10 kpc and we expect the absorber to be tracing gas in the galaxy disk. However, the absorber appears to be counter-rotating with a velocity of 100 from the systemic redshift of the galaxy (see first row in fig:Maps4 ). The modelled fit is unreliable because of the small galaxy size and nearby QSO contaminating the fit. A manual inspection of the [ O iii ] emission line in the MUSE cube reveals that the galaxy emission flux is orientated such that the side with positive velocity is towards the west. Hence, the absorber is indeed counter-rotating, but the derived kinematic properties from galpak are not reliable.

gmw_00007352.name: Q1130 1449 18
gmw_00007352.overview: Q1130 1449 18 is a single galaxy detected at the redshift of a specific absorber [arXiv:2305.11219]. At a low impact parameter of 18 kpc, the velocities of its ionized and neutral gas phases are consistent, though disk rotation cannot be separated from accreting gas [arXiv:2305.11219].

sources.summary: Q1130 1449 18 is the single galaxy detected at the absorber redshift; ionized and neutral gas velocities agree, but disk rotation and accretion cannot be separated.

quotes.quote: Q1130 1449 z_ abs = 0.1906 ; < 19.10 There is a single galaxy (Q1130 1449 18) detected at the redshift of this absorber. The velocity of the ionized and neutral gas phases are consistent (see the fourth row in fig:Maps5 ). However, the low impact parameter b = 18 kpc means we cannot separate gas rotating with the galaxy disk from accreting gas.

gmw_00007353.name: Q1130 1449 9
gmw_00007353.overview: The galaxy Q1130 1449 9 is located at an impact parameter of 75 kpc from a nearby absorber [arXiv:2305.11219]. It has an almost face-on inclination of 12 degrees, making the origin of the associated gas uncertain [arXiv:2305.11219].

sources.summary: Q1130 1449 9 is nearest its absorber at 75 kpc; opposite velocity signs and face-on inclination make the gas origin uncertain, possibly intragroup material.

quotes.quote: Q1130 1449 z_ abs = 0.3283 ; < 18.90 The galaxy Q1130 1449 9 is found nearest the absorber at b = 75 kpc. The absorber velocity has the opposite sign to the ionized gas velocity and is found near the projected minor axis ( = 55^ , first row in fig:Maps7 ). However, the measured inclination of i = 12^ suggests the galaxy is almost face-on and it becomes difficult to assign the absorber with any particular process. Instead, we may be tracing gas in the intragroup medium as there is another galaxy at an impact parameter of 90 kpc.

gmw_00007354.name: PKS 0226--038
gmw_00007354.overview: PKS 0226--038 is a luminous, jetted radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.06922 [arXiv:2303.13250]. The source exhibits mild variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474], a moderate accretion rate [arXiv:2303.13250], and an unresolved nucleus flanked by straddling components [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts PKS 0226--038 with low-redshift Population B composites, describing it as a luminous high-redshift jetted source with strong UV lines, near-solar metallicity, and moderate accretion.

quotes.quote: A typical RL source at high z PKS 0226--038 is a luminous jetted source at the cosmic noon ( z 2.06922; M_ B 28.0 , and Kellermann's radio loudness parameter , R_ K 10^3 ). Its optical and UV spectrum is markedly different from the Population B composite at low z : \ is weaker, \ stronger, and \ is almost as strong as \ (Fig. fig:pks0226 ). No diagnostic based on \ and \ is available in this case. The measured diagnostic ratios are reported in Table tab:pks0226 .
The Eddington ratio of PKS 0226-038 is 0.43 . It belongs to the spectral type B2, that include sources in the \ range 0.5 -- 1, and with moderate accretion rate.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0226 038 as a mildly variable quasar with 0.65 Jy integrated 5 GHz flux, an unresolved nucleus, straddling components, and 3.5 arcsec angular size.

quotes.quote: 0226 038 We measure a 5 GHz integrated flux density of 0.65 Jy, of which 0.58 Jy is in an unresolved nucleus and 0.07 Jy in additional components, straddling the former (Fig. 5). The overall angular size is 3.5 arcsec. As measured by Wall ( ), Parkes single dish data (0.55 Jy) are indicative of mild variability.

gmw_00007355.name: galaxy at z=0.1668
gmw_00007355.overview: The galaxy at z=0.1668 is a luminous spiral galaxy [arXiv:2010.02312]. It is associated with a circumgalactic medium that contains suggestive, but not conclusive, evidence of warm-hot gas [arXiv:2010.02312].

sources.summary: The paper studies a luminous spiral galaxy at z=0.1668, finding suggestive but inconclusive X-ray evidence that its CGM contains warm-hot gas traced by a broad absorber.

quotes.quote: The line symmetry, the b value and the absence of associated absorption strongly suggest that it is produced in collisionally ionized warm-hot gas with T (K) 6.41 and N_ H (cm^ 2 ) 20.5 (SA10; SA14), and it likely originates in the CGM associated with a galaxy at z=0.1668. It is a luminous spiral 4.4L^ galaxy within v < 100 km s^ 1 of the absorber redshift and with an impact parameter of 116 kpc. SA10 do not rule out the possibility that the absorption is produced in cooler gas with T (K)=5.8 (see table tab:Allmodels for all the possible solutions from SA10). The line broadening in that case could be because of non-thermal effects and the lack of may then be attributed to high oxygen abundance. We observed this unique and interesting system with to look for the presence of the warm-hot gas from the CGM of the galaxy at z=0.167 .
We show that there is suggestive, but not conclusive evidence for the warm-hot gas in the CGM of the galaxy.

gmw_00007356.name: J210020.73-212037.2
gmw_00007356.overview: J210020.73-212037.2 is an emission-line host galaxy located at a luminosity distance of 194.8 Mpc [arXiv:2107.12017]. It has a sub-solar metallicity of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.08 and exhibits negligible internal dust reddening [arXiv:2107.12017].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J210020.73-212037.2 as the likely SN 2019hcc host, confirms its matched redshift and negligible reddening, and derives sub-solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: The redshift was found to be z = 0.044 from the host galaxy emission lines as visible from the second spectrum, and then confirmed by the host galaxy spectrum taken at the end of the SN campaign. We assume a flat CDM universe with a Hubble constant of H_0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and _m = 0.3 and hence a luminosity distance of 194.8 Mpc. However, the second spectrum taken on MJD 58655 showed a prominent H profile implying the target was not a SLSN I, but rather a bright Type II. It had equatorial coordinates of RA: 21:00:20.930, DEC: -21:20:36.06, with the most likely host J210020.73-212037.2 in the WISEA catalogue at M_r = 19.3 mag WISEA , since the redshift of this host and that of SN 2019hcc are matched. The Milky Way extinction was taken from the all-sky Galactic dust-extinction survey Schlafly_2011 as Av = 0.19. Taking Rv = 3.1, this gives an E(B-V) of 0.06. Since there are no Na  i D absorption lines related to the host and the SN luminosity and colour evolution appear to be as expected in a SN II (see Section Light_Curve_Analysis ), the host galaxy reddening has been assumed negligible.
Averaging them we obtain a host galaxy metallicity of 12 + log(O/H)  = 8.08 0.05 , which is below solar abundance.

gmw_00007357.name: S5RG
gmw_00007357.overview: S5RG is a southern radio galaxy located in the A3718 cluster [arXiv:2302.03709]. It is associated with a faint radio arc and features a structure extending to the northwest, which may be a failed, faded, or line-of-sight-bent radio jet [arXiv:2302.03709].

sources.summary: The paper identifies S5RG as the southern radio galaxy in A3718, linked to a faint arc and a possible failed or line-of-sight-bent radio jet.

quotes.quote: The second structure is a southern radio galaxy associated with S5, which we will henceforth refer to as "S5RG", and the third is a faint radio arc connecting the two structures in projection. The radio arc is 30 kpc from the X-ray peak. An additional structure was clearly detected in both images extending from the radio galaxy S5RG toward the northwest of A3718. We suggest that this structure could be a radio jet that failed to expand or faded away due to energy losses. Another possibility is that the radio jet bends toward the Earth, making it undetectable.

gmw_00007358.name: bridge TDG
aliases_external: bridge TDG (centroid of Bridge TDG clumps 12, 13, 15, 16)
gmw_00007358.overview: The bridge TDG is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located within the residual bridge of the Arp 305 system [arXiv:0904.0670]. It is embedded in a massive neutral hydrogen plume and contains young stellar clumps where star formation likely began after separating from the host disks [arXiv:0904.0670].

sources.summary: The bridge TDG is a tidal-dwarf candidate in Arp 305’s residual bridge, detected in H-alpha and H I, containing young clumps whose star formation likely began after separation from the disks.

quotes.quote: For simplicity, we will adopt the name `bridge TDG' for the tidal dwarf candidate in the residual bridge. With SARA, we have detected \ emission from this feature confirming that it is at the same redshift as the galaxies (Figure 2). This structure is clearly detected in HI moo83 , further suggesting that it is part of the Arp 305 system.

The bridge TDG looks to be embedded in a massive HI plume stretching North from the primary moo83 . Four clumps in the bridge TDG (clumps 12, 13, 15, and 16) are visible in the GALEX image, but only two (clumps 12 and 13) are prominent in the \ and SDSS images. This suggests that the latter two are younger, have more O-stars, or less extinction. Our age and extinction estimates of these clumps are not precise enough to show such a difference (Table 5). The star formation in the bridge TDG most likely started after it was separated from the host disks.

gmw_00007359.name: J033226.49 274035.5
gmw_00007359.overview: J033226.49 274035.5 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.03 [arXiv:2304.06753]. Optical spectra classify the source as a type 1 active galactic nucleus, which is also detected in X-rays [arXiv:2304.06753].

sources.summary: The paper models J033226.49 274035.5 as a z=1.03 type 1 X-ray AGN, comparing SED fits with and without X-ray data to constrain AGN properties.

quotes.quote: Here, we demonstrate how the AGN module in Lightning can be used with and without X-ray data by fitting the SED of J033226.49 274035.5, an X-ray detected AGN in the Chandra Deep Field South at z=1.03 . For both fits, the UV-to-NIR photometry were retrieved from the 2013ApJS..207...24G CANDELS catalog, which covers the U band to Spitzer IRAC 8.0 m. From this data, we excluded the VLT/VIMOS U and HST/ACS F435W bands from our SED, due to potential contamination by broad-line emission, since J033226.49 274035.5 is classified from optical spectra as a type 1 AGN 2004ApJS..155..271S .

We modeled the resulting SED for J033226.49 274035.5 both with and without an X-ray model. In both cases, we used a stellar population with solar metallicity (i.e., Z=0.02 ) and stellar age bins spanning 0--10 Myr, 10--100 Myr, 0.1--1 Gyr, 1--5 Gyr, and 5--5.6 Gyr (the age of the universe at z=1.03 ). Both models also included the SKIRTOR UV-to-IR AGN model.

gmw_00007360.overview: The galaxy 1-113700 is a dusty starburst galaxy that shows no signs of interaction [arXiv:1609.02155]. It features a large ionized-gas extension that is interpreted as recycled-wind gas accretion [arXiv:1609.02155].

sources.summary: The paper studies MaNGA galaxy 1-113700, a dusty starburst with no interaction signs but a large ionized-gas extension interpreted as recycled-wind gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Designed to observe galaxies out to a maximum radius of 2.5 effective radius ( R_ e ), the MaNGA survey has observed a dusty, starbursting We define starbursts as galaxies with _ SFR > -1 M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 kennicutt12 galaxy that is on the upper end of the mass-metallicity relationship tremonti04 out to 6.3 R_ e through a fortuitous overestimation of R_ e MaNGA uses R_ e measurements from the NASA-Sloan Atlas, which estimated R_ e =7.4 for this galaxy. After masking out the bright, nearby stars, we used GALFIT peng02 to fit a single S ersic model to this galaxy, yielding R_ e =2.6 ; see 2 . This galaxy shows no signs of interaction and displays a large extension with no optical counterpart in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 7 (SDSS DR7; york00, abazajian09 ).

There is a large extension in the flux distribution that extends 8 ( 6.3 kpc) to the left (East) beyond the effective radius of the host galaxy, and does not correspond to any optical source in SDSS. This striking feature is elucidated in Fig. fig:spectra , where we present the flux contours superimposed on the SDSS band image, with the blue circle marking the R_ e of the host galaxy. Fig. fig:spectra d and Fig. fig:spectra e show the spectra centered on the emission line of the highlighted spaxels in the complex (which is 6.67 away from the center) and in the center of the host galaxy. Both spectra possess strong emission from the doublet, , and the doublet, confirming the authenticity of this extension and indicating the presence of ionized gas throughout the entire system.

Therefore the gas accretion scenario that best matches the properties of the extension is recycled wind material oppenheimer08, keres09b, oppenheimer10, hopkins14 , which is predicted to be a critical process in shaping the galaxy stellar mass function at low stellar masses ( <5 10^ 10 M_*/ M_ ; oppenheimer10, hopkins14 ).

gmw_00007361.name: J024009.10+010334.5
gmw_00007361.overview: J024009.10+010334.5 is a dwarf galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.02189]. This active nucleus harbors an intermediate-mass black hole with an estimated mass of approximately 10^5 to 10^5.75 solar masses [arXiv:2305.02189].

sources.summary: The paper uses J024009 as its known-IMBH test object, measuring emission lines and finding the ionizing-spectrum method recovers a roughly 10^5 solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: We find an IMBH dubbed J024009.10+010334.5 (hereafter J024009; 2011ApJ...739...28X ), harboring an AGN with a low BH mass of M_ BH =10^ 5.75 \ M_ that shows a [ Ne v ] 3426-line detection.

In summary, we find only one IMBH, J024009, for the testing purposes. The emission line fluxes of J024009 are measured in the same method explained in Section sec:flux_measurement . The extinction correction is applied to J024009 with H /H ratio, assuming electron temperature of T_ e = 8,000 K and deriving electron density n_ e with [ S ii ] ratio.
J024009 falls on the models of M_ BH =10^5 \ M_ suggesting that the BH mass of J024009 is M_ BH 10^5 \ M_ . Also an accretion rate is consistent with super-Eddington accretion within errorbars.

gmw_00007362.name: J0344
gmw_00007362.overview: J0344 is a dwarf galaxy that contains an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.02189]. Its ionizing spectrum suggests the presence of a massive black hole with an estimated mass between 10^5 and 10^6.5 solar masses undergoing sub-Eddington accretion [arXiv:2305.02189].

sources.summary: The paper treats J0344 as a dwarf-galaxy example whose ionizing spectrum implies a massive black hole of roughly 10^5 to 10^6.5 solar masses.

quotes.quote: In Figure corner_ID50 , we present the posterior probability distribution function (PDF) for the J0344 galaxy as an example, and determine the best-fit parameters and the associated errors.

J0344 is placed outside the regions covered by the models. However, J0344 is placed on or slightly above the orange dashed line connecting the data point of (M_ BH , m , ) = (10^5 M_ , 1, 1) and (10^ 6.5 M_ , 1, 0.1) . This suggest that J0344 is explained by BH accretion disk model with BH mass between 10^5 and 10^ 6.5 M_ with sub-Eddington mass accretion. This suggests that J0344 harbors a massive BH with a mass of M_ BH 10^5-10^ 6.5 \ M_ .

gmw_00007363.name: J1205
gmw_00007363.overview: J1205 is a dwarf galaxy that satisfies active galactic nuclei criteria [arXiv:2305.02189]. It exhibits temporal variability in its W1 and W2 photometry and hosts a black hole with an estimated mass of 10^4 to 10^5 solar masses [arXiv:2305.02189].

sources.summary: The paper notes J1205 satisfies an AGN criterion, shows WISE variability, and has EUV and broad-line black-hole mass estimates consistent with 10^4-10^5 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Moreover, four of the dwarf galaxies, J1205, J0344, J1222, and J024009 satisfy an AGN criterion proposed in . Some of the dwarf galaxies show temporal variability. J1222 is claimed to harbor an AGN on the basis of a presence of H broad line lasting for more than 10 years 2021MNRAS.504..543B,2021MNRAS.508.2556I . J1205 show temporal variability in W1 and W2 photometry in NEOWISE data 2014ApJ...792...30M, 2023ApJ...945..157H .

Figure fig:K03_and_galaxies indicates that the L_ EUV and _ EUV of SBS 0335-052E (J1205 and Tol 1214) agrees with the models with M_ BH =10^ 3-4 \ M_ ( M_ BH =10^ 4-5 \ M_ ) within the 2 level and therefore the BH mass is estimated to be M_ BH 10^ 3-4 \ M_ ( M_ BH 10^ 4-5 \ M_ ).
