quotes.quote: We closely follow this methodology to analyze the remaining 7 galaxies in this paper: MRG-M1341: a highly magnified 30 galaxy at z=1.59 ( is the linear gravitational magnification) ebeling2018, whitaker2019 (15 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M0138: a very bright, massive galaxy at z=1.95 with H_ F160W =17.3 newman2018 (6 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M2129 and MRG-M0150: massive quenched galaxies at z=2.1 and z=2.6 that are rotationally-supported and dispersion-dominated, respectively newman2015, toft2017 (5 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-P0918 and MRG-S1522: 1 Gyr old quiescent galaxies at z=2.36 and z=2.45 newman2018 (7 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-M0454: the most compact r_ eff 0.3 kpc and the highest redshift z=2.9 galaxy in the survey man2021 (12 orbits of WFC3/G141).

For those targets with ALMA 1.3 mm detections, MRG-M0138 and MRG-M2129, we carefully examine the distribution of the dust continuum emission. In both sources, the dust emission is unresolved in the 1-1 5 ALMA data whitaker2021 . While this resolution is significantly coarser than our spatially resolved bins, the fact that the emission is pointlike implies that there is some information about the spatial distribution of the 1.3 mm flux in the existing data. We performed a number of tests to determine the level of dust emission in each resolved bin that would be consistent with the existing ALMA data, jointly fitting for the 1.3 mm flux density of a series of unresolved point sources with positions fixed to the centers of each of our resolved bins. This analysis suggests that for both MRG-M0138 and MRG-M2129, the 1.3 mm emission is associated with the three central bins, though the distribution of flux among these bins is essentially unconstrained.

MRG-M0454, MRG-M2129, MRG-P0918 and MRG-M0150 age gradients are all mildly i.e., this non-axisymmetric trend is washed away upon azimuthally combining the data points and we only have a flat age gradient. non-axisymmetric, with one side of the galaxy older than the other side at similar distances. This could indicate that a merger or interaction with another galaxy played a role in their formation. For three galaxies, MRG-M0150, MRG-M0454 and MRG-P0918, the SFH on one side dips below the other side for lookback times of 100 Myr to 1 Gyr, leading to an older age. The asymmetrical features are not uncommon in spatially-resolved studies of quiescent galaxies e.g., Figures 8 and 4, respectively cheng2015, setton2020 . However, this non-axisymmetric trend vanishes if we azimuthally combine the data points given their distances from the center, consistent with setton2020 . The non-axisymmetric age gradient in MRG-M2129 is caused by the centrally concentrated 1.3 mm ALMA flux. One bin remains star-forming all along in MRG-M2129 in order to produce the significant measured 1.3 mm flux.

sources.summary: The paper discusses the red, singly imaged z=2.1472 compact galaxy in MACS J2129 as a strongly lensed fast-spinning disk and measures a predicted magnification of 3.7.

quotes.quote: Finally, MACS J2129 also hosts a particularly red and bright single strongly-lensed galaxy, West of the cluster center as shown in Fig. macs2129_big ( =322.3431122 , =-7.69202483 ). presented a detailed analysis of this z=2.1472 compact galaxy, spectroscopically confirmed thanks to VLT/X-Shooter observations, and which revealed to be a fast-spinning, rotationally supported disk galaxy. Thanks to their lensing mass model, measured a magnification of _ Toft+17 =4.2 0.6 . While this galaxy is not used in the mass model presented here as it is singly-imaged, we can measure its predicted magnification. We measure =3.7 0.2 , a slightly lower value than the one from , but in good agreement within error bars.

gmw_00007414.name: MRG-M0150
gmw_00007414.overview: MRG-M0150 is a massive, quenched galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.6 [arXiv:2203.04979]. It is a dispersion-dominated system that exhibits a mildly non-axisymmetric age gradient, where one side of the galaxy is older than the other at similar radii [arXiv:2203.04979].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MRG-M0150 as a z=2.6 dispersion-dominated quenched galaxy whose age gradient is mildly non-axisymmetric, with one side older at similar radius.

quotes.quote: We closely follow this methodology to analyze the remaining 7 galaxies in this paper: MRG-M1341: a highly magnified 30 galaxy at z=1.59 ( is the linear gravitational magnification) ebeling2018, whitaker2019 (15 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M0138: a very bright, massive galaxy at z=1.95 with H_ F160W =17.3 newman2018 (6 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M2129 and MRG-M0150: massive quenched galaxies at z=2.1 and z=2.6 that are rotationally-supported and dispersion-dominated, respectively newman2015, toft2017 (5 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-P0918 and MRG-S1522: 1 Gyr old quiescent galaxies at z=2.36 and z=2.45 newman2018 (7 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-M0454: the most compact r_ eff 0.3 kpc and the highest redshift z=2.9 galaxy in the survey man2021 (12 orbits of WFC3/G141).

MRG-M0454, MRG-M2129, MRG-P0918 and MRG-M0150 age gradients are all mildly i.e., this non-axisymmetric trend is washed away upon azimuthally combining the data points and we only have a flat age gradient. non-axisymmetric, with one side of the galaxy older than the other side at similar distances. This could indicate that a merger or interaction with another galaxy played a role in their formation. For three galaxies, MRG-M0150, MRG-M0454 and MRG-P0918, the SFH on one side dips below the other side for lookback times of 100 Myr to 1 Gyr, leading to an older age.

For all targets, we use the same priors for dust and metallicity for all bins, and for a half of the targets (MRG-P0918, MRG-M0150, MRG-M1341 and MRG-S1522) we get a metallicity posterior consistent with our flat prior.

gmw_00007415.name: MRG-P0918
gmw_00007415.overview: MRG-P0918 is a highly magnified, singly-imaged galaxy lensed by the massive galaxy cluster PSZ1 G295.24-21.55 [arXiv:2203.04979]. The galaxy exhibits a mildly non-axisymmetric age gradient, with one side of the system being older than the other [arXiv:2203.04979].

sources.summary: The paper presents a lensing model for MRG-P0918, a highly magnified singly imaged galaxy, and finds a mildly non-axisymmetric age gradient with one side older.

quotes.quote: The details of the previously unpublished lensing model for MRG-P0918 are presented in Appendix sec:lensing .

MRG-M0454, MRG-M2129, MRG-P0918 and MRG-M0150 age gradients are all mildly i.e., this non-axisymmetric trend is washed away upon azimuthally combining the data points and we only have a flat age gradient. non-axisymmetric, with one side of the galaxy older than the other side at similar distances. This could indicate that a merger or interaction with another galaxy played a role in their formation. For three galaxies, MRG-M0150, MRG-M0454 and MRG-P0918, the SFH on one side dips below the other side for lookback times of 100 Myr to 1 Gyr, leading to an older age.
MRG-P0918 is a highly magnified, singly-imaged galaxy lensed by the massive galaxy cluster PSZ1 G295.24-21.55 at z=0.61 planck2014 .

gmw_00007416.name: MRG-S1522
gmw_00007416.overview: MRG-S1522 is a quiescent galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.45 with an estimated age of 1 Gyr [arXiv:2203.04979]. It exhibits an age gradient featuring a younger inner core, with a star-formation history that indicates outside-in quenching [arXiv:2203.04979].

sources.summary: The paper finds MRG-S1522 is the clearest case of a younger inner core, with a significant age gradient and a star-formation history indicating outside-in quenching.

quotes.quote: We closely follow this methodology to analyze the remaining 7 galaxies in this paper: MRG-M1341: a highly magnified 30 galaxy at z=1.59 ( is the linear gravitational magnification) ebeling2018, whitaker2019 (15 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M0138: a very bright, massive galaxy at z=1.95 with H_ F160W =17.3 newman2018 (6 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M2129 and MRG-M0150: massive quenched galaxies at z=2.1 and z=2.6 that are rotationally-supported and dispersion-dominated, respectively newman2015, toft2017 (5 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-P0918 and MRG-S1522: 1 Gyr old quiescent galaxies at z=2.36 and z=2.45 newman2018 (7 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-M0454: the most compact r_ eff 0.3 kpc and the highest redshift z=2.9 galaxy in the survey man2021 (12 orbits of WFC3/G141).
MRG-S1522 is the only galaxy in the REQUIEM-2D sample which is clearly younger in the inner core ( /r_c 1) .

We detect a statistically significant age gradient in MRG-S1522, with the galaxy looking younger in the inner half-light radius. This result is consistent with gobat2017 , and it may imply non-uniform quenching wellons2015 . The recovered SFH of MRG-S1522 clearly indicates that this galaxy quenches in the outer bins first.

gmw_00007417.name: MRG-M0454
gmw_00007417.overview: MRG-M0454 is a highly compact, quenched galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.9 [arXiv:2203.04979]. It exhibits a mildly non-axisymmetric stellar age gradient and shows tentative, statistically insignificant signs of a metal-rich center [arXiv:2203.04979].

sources.summary: The paper describes MRG-M0454 as the survey's most compact and highest-redshift galaxy, with a mildly non-axisymmetric age gradient and tentative metal-rich center.

quotes.quote: We closely follow this methodology to analyze the remaining 7 galaxies in this paper: MRG-M1341: a highly magnified 30 galaxy at z=1.59 ( is the linear gravitational magnification) ebeling2018, whitaker2019 (15 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M0138: a very bright, massive galaxy at z=1.95 with H_ F160W =17.3 newman2018 (6 orbits of WFC3/G141), MRG-M2129 and MRG-M0150: massive quenched galaxies at z=2.1 and z=2.6 that are rotationally-supported and dispersion-dominated, respectively newman2015, toft2017 (5 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-P0918 and MRG-S1522: 1 Gyr old quiescent galaxies at z=2.36 and z=2.45 newman2018 (7 orbits of WFC3/G141 each), MRG-M0454: the most compact r_ eff 0.3 kpc and the highest redshift z=2.9 galaxy in the survey man2021 (12 orbits of WFC3/G141).

MRG-M0454, MRG-M2129, MRG-P0918 and MRG-M0150 age gradients are all mildly i.e., this non-axisymmetric trend is washed away upon azimuthally combining the data points and we only have a flat age gradient. non-axisymmetric, with one side of the galaxy older than the other side at similar distances. This could indicate that a merger or interaction with another galaxy played a role in their formation. For three galaxies, MRG-M0150, MRG-M0454 and MRG-P0918, the SFH on one side dips below the other side for lookback times of 100 Myr to 1 Gyr, leading to an older age.
While we do not detect any stellar metallicity gradients, there are slight hints of a relatively metal-rich center for MRG-M0454 and MRG-M1341, but our measurements are not statistically significant.

gmw_00007418.name: WISE J210257.88--620046.3
gmw_00007418.overview: WISE J210257.88--620046.3 is a galaxy located on the southern edge of the Odd Radio Circle ORC 1 [arXiv:2006.14805]. While its infrared colors suggest it is a star-forming galaxy, possibly a starburst or luminous infrared galaxy, its radio spectral index indicates it is more likely an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.14805].

sources.summary: The paper describes WISE J210257.88--620046.3 as a galaxy on ORC 1's southern edge, likely star-forming or luminous infrared, but with radio properties suggesting AGN activity.

quotes.quote: On the southern edge of the ring is a bright radio source (labelled ``S'' in Figure fig:wtfimages ) which is associated with a galaxy detected both by WISE (WISE J210257.88--620046.3) wise and by GALEX (GALEXASC J210257.91--620045.4) galex . The WISE colours indicate that this is a star-forming galaxy, and possibly a starburst or LIRG (luminous infrared galaxy). zou19 measure a photometric redshift of 0.228 for this galaxy. The radio observations listed in Table tab:orc1flux show that source S has a spectral index of 1.71 0.35 , making it more likely to be an active galactic nucleus (AGN) than a star-forming galaxy.

gmw_00007419.name: WISE J210258.15--620014.4
gmw_00007419.overview: WISE J210258.15--620014.4 is a faint, extended optical galaxy with an east-west extent of approximately 6 arcseconds [arXiv:2006.14805]. Optical colors suggest it is a quiescent galaxy, and it has a measured photometric redshift of 0.551 [arXiv:2006.14805].

sources.summary: The paper describes WISE J210258.15--620014.4 as a faint central object in ORC 1, probably a quiescent galaxy at photometric redshift 0.551 and possibly unrelated.

quotes.quote: At the centre of the ring is a faint optical object (WISE J210258.15--620014.4), labelled ``C'' in Figure fig:wtfimages , with no detectable radio emission in any of our observations. The enlarged DES gri band image in Figure fig:orc1-gri shows that this central object is extended E-W with a total extent of about 6 arcsec, (.f. the 1 arcsec resolution of DES) and is probably a galaxy. The WISE colours for the central object (W1--W2 = 0.081, W2--W3 < 2.045 ) do not enable us to distinguish between a star-forming galaxy or a quiescent galaxy, but the optical colours suggest a quiescent galaxy. zou19 measure a photometric redshift of 0.551 for this galaxy. At z=0.551, 6 arcsec corresponds to a diameter of 39 kpc, making it unlikely to be a normal star-forming galaxy at high redshift. The local space density of galaxies (6333 per sq deg at W1 < 15) mean that there is a 15 probability of there being a source brighter than 15 mag within 10 arcsec of the centre. We therefore consider it possible that this is an unrelated object.

gmw_00007420.name: source C
gmw_00007420.overview: Source C is a compact radio source coincident with an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2006.14805]. Optical imaging and color analysis confirm that it is a star-forming disk galaxy [arXiv:2006.14805].

sources.summary: The paper describes source C in ORC 2 as a compact radio source coincident with an edge-on, star-forming spiral galaxy that is probably unrelated to the ORC.

quotes.quote: Within the eastern limb of ORC 2 in the ASKAP image is a compact radio source which is coincident with the galaxy labelled C in Table tab:orc1-wise , which appears in the DES image to resemble an edge-on disk galaxy. Its colour of g-=0.43 confirms that it is a star-forming galaxy, and so we classify this galaxy as an edge-on spiral. e.g. masters11 . The space density of components in the EMU-PS at least as strong as source C is about 1000 per , so the probability of finding B within 1 arcmin of the ORC is about 90 , and so we consider it likely that this source is an unrelated object.

gmw_00007421.name: SDSS J155524.63+272634.3
gmw_00007421.overview: SDSS J155524.63+272634.3 is a red central galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 0.39 [arXiv:2006.14805]. It features a compact central radio source and has a detected WISE infrared counterpart [arXiv:2006.14805].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J155524.63+272634.3 as the red central galaxy/source in ORC 4, with a WISE counterpart and photometric redshift implying a large ring size if associated.

quotes.quote: ORC 4 differs from the other ORCs in having a central radio source, labelled ``G'' in Figure fig:wtfimages , which is listed in the NVSS catalogue nvss as NVSS J155524+272629. It is coincident with a red galaxy seen in both SDSS and Pan-Starrs images, and detected by WISE. The compact source has an unambiguous optical/IR counterpart in SDSS (SDSS J155524.63+272634.3) and WISE (WISEA J155524.65+272633.7) with a photometric redshift of 0.39 bilicki implying a linear size of the ring of 410 kpc 350 kpc .
If the central galaxy in ORC 4 is associated with the ring, then the ring is at a redshift of 0.39 and has a size of 410 350 kpc .

gmw_00007422.name: WNB 0313+683
gmw_00007422.overview: WNB 0313+683 is a giant double-lobe radio galaxy [arXiv:1210.6801]. It exhibits linearly polarized emission from its core and southern lobe, whereas its fainter northern lobe shows no detected polarized emission [arXiv:1210.6801].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WNB 0313+683 as a giant double-lobe radio galaxy in the field, detecting Stokes I emission, polarized south-lobe and core emission, and no polarized north-lobe emission.

quotes.quote: Faint Stokes I emission is also seen from the spiral galaxy IC 342 at ( , )_ J2000 (03^ h 47^ m ,68^ .0) and from the giant double radio galaxy WNB 0313+683 at ( , )_ J2000 (03^ h 18^ m ,68^ .3) .

The giant double-lobe radio galaxy WNB 0313+683, which shows prominent linearly polarized emission from the south lobe (at ( , )_ J2000 (03^ h 17^ m ,68^ .2) ) and the core (at ( , )_ J2000 (03^ h 18^ m ,68^ .3) ) in a range 17,-5 rad m ^ 2 consistent with values reported by Schoenmakers98 and Haverkorn03 . However, no polarized emission is detected from the north lobe, which is pointing away from the observer. Since the Stokes I map shows a sharp difference in brightness for the lobes (the north to south lobe mean brightness ratio is 0.2 ), the non-detection of polarized emission from the north lobe can be explained as a consequence of relativistic beaming and/or additional Faraday depolarization Heald09 .

gmw_00007423.name: GBT 1425+5235
gmw_00007423.overview: GBT 1425+5235 is an extremely low surface brightness dwarf galaxy characterized by an irregular structure [arXiv:1210.8333]. It is a gas-rich system with a large neutral hydrogen fraction and shows evidence of ongoing star formation [arXiv:1210.8333].

sources.summary: GBT 1425+5235 is a new HI source with an optical counterpart, unlikely to be Galactic, and interpreted as an extremely low surface brightness, gas-rich dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: We detect all known galaxies in the survey field with velocities in our detection range, along with two new sources, dubbed GBT 1425+5235 (hereafter G1425) and GBT 1355+5439 (hereafter G1355). While we use IAU-recommended nomenclature for the source names in this paper, the authors prefer the names ``Skipper" (G1425) and ``Gilligan" (G1355) for these objects, adopted in honor of Sherwood Schwartz, who passed away during the writing of this paper.

G1425 has a cataloged optical counterpart WHI J1425+52, identified by Whiting (2007) as Galactic nebulosity based on relatively shallow R-band imaging with a limiting surface brightness of 25.5. However, with a significant HI detection, a velocity of =736 , and a line width of 20=77 , G1425 is unlikely to be Galactic in origin. To examine G1425's optical properties in more detail, we used deeper ( _ g,lim 26.5 ) Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 7 (SDSS DR7) imaging (Abazajian 2009) of the field, shown in Figure newimages , to characterize the morphology and colors of the optical counterpart. In these properties, G1425 is similar to the extreme low surface brightness dwarf galaxies (Bothun 1998; van Zee 2001).

Of the two new sources we detect in the group, one (G1425) is optically identifiable using SDSS imaging as an extremely low surface brightness dwarf galaxy. G1425's properties make it comparable to the gas-rich HI dwarfs being found in blind HI surveys such as ALFALFA (Haynes 2011; Cannon 2011; Huang 2012), with large HI fractions, blue colors, irregular structure, and evidence for on-going star formation.

gmw_00007424.name: GBT 1355+5439
gmw_00007424.overview: GBT 1355+5439 is an isolated neutral hydrogen cloud that lacks any optical counterpart [arXiv:1210.8333]. It is distinct from the extended tidal plume of M101 and may represent a rare starless cloud or a primordial dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1210.8333].

sources.summary: GBT 1355+5439 has no optical counterpart, is isolated from M101's plume, and is discussed as a rare starless HI cloud or possible primordial dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: In contrast to G1425, around G1355 we find no plausible optical counterpart down to the _ g, lim 26.5 limiting surface brightness of the SDSS imaging (shown in Figure newimages ). The lack of any optical detection around G1355 makes classifying this source difficult. If instead of being a Galactic HVC, G1355 is at the M101 distance, it would have a mass of 10^7 , and with a velocity width of 20=41 \ it would be similar to the HI clouds found around the interacting galaxies M81/M82/NGC3077 (Chynoweth 2008).

In contrast, G1355 potentially represents a much rarer object --- a starless HI cloud with mass =10^7 . While such objects are seen in the tidal debris surrounding strongly interacting galaxies ( Chynoweth 2009), G1355 itself seems to be truly distinct and isolated from M101's extended (and likely tidal) HI plume. Isolated starless HI clouds like G1355 are extremely rare --- for example, less than 2 of the HI detected objects in the ALFALFA survey have no associated optical counterpart in SDSS or DSS2 imaging (Haynes 2011).

gmw_00007425.name: NGC 2835
gmw_00007425.overview: NGC 2835 is a highly inclined, star-forming Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:2209.13967, arXiv:2203.02522] that probably hosts a central LINER [arXiv:1902.07300]. It is the brightest member of a small galaxy group [arXiv:2012.00757], showing a disturbed neutral hydrogen profile that suggests prior interactions [arXiv:2212.10751].

sources.summary: For NGC 2835, the metallicity break lies far beyond the bar and is interpreted as likely driven by outer-disk gas accretion rather than bar mixing.

quotes.quote: For NGC 2835, a `steep-shallow' metallicity gradient break occurs at a distance 4 times the bar radius, which is more likely driven by gas accretion to the outskirt of the galaxy instead of the bar. We measure a global metallicity gradient in NGC 2835 of 0.0431 0.0009 dex/kpc from a single component fit (left column of Fig fig:Ograd3 ). The piecewise fit shows a steep-shallow metallicity profile in NGC 2835 with a residual distribution centered around zero throughout the galaxy (middle bottom panel of Fig fig:Ograd3 ). The observed HI distribution from Very Large Array shows a disturbed profile that is significantly deviated from the centre of NGC 2835 Condon_1987 , which supports the occurrence of prior interactions.

For NGC 2835, _ SFR remains elevated out to 8 kpc, far beyond the end of the bar and approaching the edge of the observations. This supports the scenario for gas accretion occurring in the outskirt of NGC 2835. For NGC 2835, the break far beyond the end of the bar in the metallicity gradient is not bar-driven but possibly the consequence of gas accretion to the outskirt of the galaxy Kewley_2010,Lopez_2015 . The break in the metallicity profile of NGC 2835 is at a distance that is 4 times greater than the R _ bar .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2835 as a PHANGS test case, measuring its metallicity semivariogram, ISM mixing scale, and the role of measurement error at small separations.

quotes.quote: A test case: NGC 2385 Figure fig:one_semivariogram shows the empirical semivariogram for one PHANGS galaxy, NGC 2835, highlighting the key features that can be observed. The semivariance is smallest between H ii regions that are separated by 0.1 0.1 kpc, and increases monotonically until a separation of 1.9 0.1 kpc is reached. Beyond this point, the semivariance is almost constant, showing variations on the 10 level. This indicates that in this galaxy, metallicity variations fail to be positively correlated for H ii regions that are separated by a distance larger than 2 kpc, setting an upper limit for the mixing scale of the ISM.

In the smallest separation bin, a semivariance of 7.79 10^ 4 is seen. This is comparable to the amount of variance expected to be produced by measurement errors of emission-line flux ratios alone: for this galaxy, the mean uncertainty in metallicity measurements reported in Kreckel+19 is 0.029 dex, which would add a semivariance of 4.35 10^ 4 to all spatial bins. From this, we can infer that the greatest source of fluctuations in the metallicity map for this galaxy on spatial scales below 200 pc is measurement error.

sources.summary: The paper applies its GMOS/IFU treatment to NGC 2835, finding that the processed central data cube reveals a probable LINER nuclear emission region otherwise hidden by instrumental effects.

quotes.quote: The treatment procedure applied to the data cube of the central region of the late-type galaxy NGC 2835 allowed the detection of a nuclear emitting region with emission-line ratios indicating a probable LINER, although the classifications of Transition Object or Seyfert galaxy cannot be discarded. Such a detection was not possible without the data treatment. This shows that the benefits provided by this treatment procedure are not merely cosmetic, but actually affect the quality of different types of analyses, like the determination of the fraction of galaxies, of different morphological types, with a central Seyfert/LINER/Transition object emission.

In order to show how this type of analysis can be considerably affected by instrumental artefacts, we used the data cube, from the DIVING^ 3D sample, of the central region of the SB(rs) galaxy NGC 2835, located at a distance of 10.36 Mpc (NASA Extragalactic Database - NED). There is not much information in the literature about this object. The data cube of this galaxy was treated with all the methodologies described before. We corrected the treated and non-treated data cubes of NGC 2835 for the interstellar extinction due to the Galaxy, using A_V = 0.274 mag sch11 and the extinction law of car89 , and passed them to the rest frame, using z = 0.002955 (NED).

Even so, based on the low number of Transition Objects detected by ho97 with [ N II ] 6584/H values compatible with [ N II ] 6584/H (1) _ treated and also on the fact that Seyfert nuclei are usually more luminous than the nucleus of NGC 2835, we conclude that this galaxy probably hosts a LINER, although the classifications of Transition Object or Seyfert cannot be ruled out.

sources.summary: The paper provides a first TRGB distance to NGC 2835, finding 12.22 Mpc from a halo PHANGS-HST field after previous Tully-Fisher distances varied widely.

quotes.quote: NGC 2835 is the brightest member of a small galaxy group in the southern hemisphere. Most previous distances were based on the Tully-Fisher (TF) relation 1977A&A....54..661T , though with a nearly factor of 3 spread in the reported values. The numerical action method (NAM, 2017ApJ...850..207S ) distance (see Section 4 for more details) to this target is 12.38 1.86 Mpc, which in the absence of a TRGB measurement represented the best distance estimate to this target. The PHANGS-HST parallel imaging for NGC 2835 lies far enough out into the halo of the galaxy that we do not need to trim the field for analysis due to the lack of a substantial population of young stars. From the entire field, we determine m_ TRGB = 26.47 0.14 mag, which results in our adopted distance of D = 12.22 0.94 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2835 as an Sc galaxy with specified inclination, position angle, optical radius, distance, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 2835 is a Sc galaxy (morphological type code T = 5.0 0.4). The inclination angle of NGC 2835 is i = 51 Ryder1995 , the position angle of the major axis PA = 1 Lang2020 . The optical radius is 3.30 arcmin or 198.2 arcsec RC3 . At the distance of d = 12.22 Mpc Anand2021 , the physical optical radius is R_ 25 = 11.74 kpc. The stellar mass is M _ = 1.0 10 ^ 10 M _ Leroy2021 .

sources.summary: The paper compares recent and TF distances for NGC2835, notes its LGG172 membership, and adopts 9.82 Mpc using an SBF distance to NGC2784.

quotes.quote: NGC2835. -- Different distance values to NGC2835 were used recently: 10.76 Mpc (Elmegreen Salzer 1999) and 6.03 Mpc (Larsen Richtler 2000). The TF distance to NGC2835 is 9.80 Mpc (Russel 2002). The galaxy NGC2835 is a member of the LGG172 group (Garcia 1993). The distance to NGC2784 (a member of the LGG172 group) has been determined with the SBF method and is equal to 9.82 Mpc (Tonry et al. 2001). This value is adopted as distance to NGC2835.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2835 as a highly inclined star-forming TYPHOON spiral with a reported shallow metallicity gradient and possible outer flattening.

quotes.quote: The TYPHOON Survey The six galaxies in this study --- NGC 1566, NGC 2835, NGC 3521, NGC 5068, NGC 5236, and NGC 7793 --- are drawn from the TYPHOON survey. The H signal to noise image of each galaxy are shown in Figure fig:galaxies . The TYPHOON data are constructed using a Progressive Integral Step Method (PrISM), establishing a 3D data cube where each spatial pixel (spaxel) occupies a spectrum for that pixel. A long-slit spectrum is captured and then the slit is moved along spatially, generating multiple slices to form into a cube. Details about the TYPHOON Program and long-slit data reduction techniques will be presented in a forthcoming paper (M. Seibert et al., in preparation). Here, we provide a short summary, focusing on aspects immediately relevant to this study. The TYPHOON galaxies (PI and Team Leader, B.F. Madore) were observed using the Wide-Field CCD imaging spectrograph on the 2.5m du Pont telescope, located at the Las Campanas Observatory in Chile. The WFCCD has a field of view of 25' ; the TYPHOON 3D datacubes are constructed using a custom long-slit (18' × 1.65; 0.5 square arcminute). The slit was positioned as either north-south or east-west, moved after each 600s to scan the data in the perpendicular direction of each galaxy. This was performed until the entire optical disk of each galaxy had been observed. The observations were taken over the course of observing runs from 2011 through 2018. Observations were performed only when the seeing was less than the slit width of 1 '' 65. By choosing nearby galaxies ( z 0.005 ), TYPHOON, and the galaxies in this study, is able to achieve seeing-limited resolutions of up to 5 pc. Standard spectro-photometric flux calibrations were taken each observing night. The data are reduced using standard long-slit data reduction techniques, summarized in ho17 and to be described in detail in a forthcoming paper (M. Seibert et al., in preparation). We briefly summarize here the most important details. The wavelength calibration has a typical root-mean-square value 0.05 \ for the entire data set. Flux calibration is accurate to 2 at the spaxel scale over the range of 4500--7500 . The reduced long-slit 2D spectra are tiled together to form the 3D data cube. The final reduced data cube covers a wavelength range of 3650--8150 , with spectral and spatial samplings of 1.5 \ and 1.65'', respectively. The instrumental dispersion is approximately 3.5 , corresponding to a spectral resolution R 850 at 7000 . We typically reach a 3 surface brightness sensitivity for H of 4.2 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 . We note that as all main results in this paper (metallicity, ionization, and pressure diagnostics) are inferred from line ratios, the results depend only on the relative flux calibration and are insensitive to the absolute flux calibration. The TYPHOON Galaxies The six galaxies selected for this study are all the spiral galaxies from the TYPHOON survey that have undergone the full data reduction pipeline at the time of writing, and additionally, were not previously published by ho17, ho18 . A summary of properties of the galaxies are listed in Table tab:1 along with the spatial resolution of the observations and the number of \ regions in each galaxy. NGC 1566 The most distant galaxy in the sample at 17.9 Mpc, NGC 1566 is a face-on spiral galaxy with an intermediate-strength bar type, classified as a SAB(s)bc. NGC 1566 has a nuclear gas disk which presents as a star-forming ring smajic15 and an outer pseudo-ring that winds anti-parallel to the bar ends buta15 . NGC 1566 hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nuclei (AGN) that does not show evidence of an outflow nor AGN feedback combes14 . The moderate to loosely wound arms are consistent with being formed through bar-driven spiral density waves shabani18 . The largest \ regions in NGC 1566 of 600 pc (Figure fig:size ) are consistent with the maximum size of star-forming complexes of 508 179 pc as traced by the young stellar population grasha17b . NGC 2835 NGC 2835 is a highly inclined star-forming galaxy with a reported shallow radial metallicity gradient of 0.096 dex/kpc using the dopita16 metallicity calibration kreckel19 .
NGC 2835 also hints at evidence of a flattening in the metallicity profile at distances greater than 10 kpc, albeit with significant scatter.

gmw_00007426.name: CIG 85
gmw_00007426.overview: CIG 85 is an isolated, gas-rich galaxy with a low stellar mass that is classified as an irregular galaxy [arXiv:1209.0786, arXiv:2312.16661]. It exhibits a highly disturbed optical disk, clumpy and asymmetric neutral hydrogen distribution, and enhanced star formation likely triggered by a recent minor merger [arXiv:1209.0786, arXiv:2312.16661].

sources.summary: The paper presents CIG 85 as an isolated asymmetric galaxy whose clumpy H I, disturbed optical disk, enhanced star formation, and projected remnants point to a recent minor merger.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The kinematic centre of the galaxy is at 02h 03m 20.3s, +22d 02m 39.8s

quotes.quote: We present the results from the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) interferometric \ and 20 cm radio continuum observations of CIG 85, an isolated asymmetric galaxy from the AMIGA (Analysis of the Interstellar Medium of Isolated GAlaxies (http://amiga.iaa.es) sample. Despite being an isolated galaxy, CIG 85 showed an appreciable optical and H i spectral asymmetry and therefore was an excellant candidate for resolved H i studies to understand the reasons giving rise to asymmetries in isolated galaxies. The galaxy was imaged in \ and 20 cm radio continuum using the black GMRT. For a detailed discussion of the results we also made use of multi--wavelength data from archival SDSS, GALEX and H imaging. We find the \ in CIG 85 to have a clumpy, asymmetric distribution which in the NW part is correlated with optical tail like features, but the \ velocity field displays a relatively regular rotation pattern.

Thus evaluating all the observational evidence, we come to a conclusion that CIG 85 is most likely a case of disturbed spiral galaxy which now appears to have the morphology of an irregular galaxy. Though it is currently isolated from major companions, the observational evidence is consistent with \ asymmetries, highly disturbed optical disk and recent increase in star formation having been caused by a minor merger, remnants of which are now projected in front of the optical disk. If this is correct, the companion will be fully accreted by CIG 85 in the near future.

sources.summary: CIG 85 is identified as a gas-rich, low-stellar-mass outlier with disturbed optical and HI morphologies; the paper notes possible recent minor mergers and inclination bias.

quotes.quote: By evaluating the deviations of the j sample galaxies from the line of best fit, for each of the stellar and gas relations, and setting a maximum scatter of 2 _i from each relation (where _i is the standard deviation of the scatters around the line of best fit for component i ), we identify two outliers in each of the panels: CIG 188 232 for the \ distribution, and CIG 85 744 for the \ distribution. Could the outlier galaxies either have an excess in their gas content (for CIG 188 232) or a deficit in their stellar mass (for CIG 85 744)? Regarding CIG 85 744, fig:mhi-ms shows that these galaxies have significantly higher gas masses for their low stellar masses -- they are, in fact, among the the galaxies with the lowest stellar masses. CIG 85 and 744 are respectively classified as an irregular and a late-type spiral, with CIG 744 hosting an AGN in its centre Hernandez-Ibarra2013 .

Furthermore, CIG 85 presents highly disturbed optical and \ morphologies, leading Sengupta2012 to argue that the galaxy may have undergone minor mergers in the recent past. The particularly low inclination of CIG 85 ( 16 ) could also lead to an overestimation of its rotation velocity, pushing the galaxy upwards in the j plane. The two galaxies discussed in sec:jvsm:gs (CIG 85 744) to have low stellar masses with respect to their angular momentum appear to be among the few ``stable'' discs in the isolated j sample, located near the region populated by mainly dwarf galaxies.

gmw_00007427.name: CIG 188
gmw_00007427.overview: CIG 188 is a disk galaxy characterized by an average gas mass [arXiv:2312.16661]. It is a stellar-component outlier that exhibits very low rotation velocities, with a maximum velocity of only 40 km/s [arXiv:2312.16661].

sources.summary: CIG 188 is a stellar-component outlier with average gas mass but very low rotation velocities, making its deviation from the angular-momentum plane likely due to slow rotation.

quotes.quote: By evaluating the deviations of the j sample galaxies from the line of best fit, for each of the stellar and gas relations, and setting a maximum scatter of 2 _i from each relation (where _i is the standard deviation of the scatters around the line of best fit for component i ), we identify two outliers in each of the panels: CIG 188 232 for the \ distribution, and CIG 85 744 for the \ distribution. Could the outlier galaxies either have an excess in their gas content (for CIG 188 232) or a deficit in their stellar mass (for CIG 85 744)? We particularly note that CIG 188 presents an average gas mass, while CIG 232 shows a high gas content with respect to its stellar mass.

Furthermore, an inspection of the rotation curves of these galaxies in fig:rotcur (and also in fig:btf discussed in the next section) shows that CIG 188 presents very low rotation velocities, with a maximum as low as 40 . These suggest that the deviation of CIG 232 from the \ plane could be caused by an excess in its \ mass, while that of CIG 188 is likely due to its slow rotation. We identify seven galaxies in this category, including CIG 188, which was previously found to have abnormally low \ values.

gmw_00007428.name: CIG 232
gmw_00007428.overview: CIG 232, also known as UGC 4256, is a spiral galaxy characterized by a single, bright, tail-like spiral arm overlaying two dimmer symmetric arms [arXiv:1511.06365]. The galaxy is a gas-rich outlier with a high neutral hydrogen content and low-density gas in its outer regions [arXiv:2312.16661].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 4256 as a complex spiral where a bright tail-like arm and dimmer symmetric arms lead to method-dependent pitch measurements.

quotes.quote: Interesting Galaxy: UGC 4256 Occasionally there exists a galaxy with different spirals of different pitch angles. Figure fig:InterestingGalaxy shows one such galaxy. UGC 4256 consists of a single, bright, tail-like spiral with a pitch angle of about 45 . The bright spiral overlays two dimmer symmetric spirals with pitch angles of about 30 . This image was found on the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). When Spirality measures the galaxy as a whole, it fits the single bright spiral to a template of 45.1 3.8 .

UGC 4256 This galaxy is discussed in Section subsection:InterestingGalaxy and shown in Figure fig:InterestingGalaxy . The two methods' disagreement on the pitch angle of this galaxy stems from 2DFFT's assumption of 2-arm symmetry. The disagreement is resolved when Spirality is directed to measure only the 2-arm component.

sources.summary: CIG 232 is discussed as a gas-component outlier whose high HI content and unrecovered low-density outer gas likely explain its angular-momentum-plane deviation.

quotes.quote: By evaluating the deviations of the j sample galaxies from the line of best fit, for each of the stellar and gas relations, and setting a maximum scatter of 2 _i from each relation (where _i is the standard deviation of the scatters around the line of best fit for component i ), we identify two outliers in each of the panels: CIG 188 232 for the \ distribution, and CIG 85 744 for the \ distribution. Could the outlier galaxies either have an excess in their gas content (for CIG 188 232) or a deficit in their stellar mass (for CIG 85 744)? We particularly note that CIG 188 presents an average gas mass, while CIG 232 shows a high gas content with respect to its stellar mass. These suggest that the deviation of CIG 232 from the \ plane could be caused by an excess in its \ mass, while that of CIG 188 is likely due to its slow rotation. Since the \ masses presented here are measured from single-dish observations see Jones2018 , this implies that CIG 232 contains a significant amount of low-density gas in its outer regions that is not seen in its kinematic maps.

gmw_00007429.name: Source C
gmw_00007429.overview: Source C is an active galactic nucleus coincident with the optical galaxy WEIN 047 and identified as an IRAS source [arXiv:astro-ph/0202255]. It is a radio source with an asymmetric elongation and a steep spectral index of 1.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0202255].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source C with the optical galaxy WEIN 047 and favors it over 3C 129.1 as the likely Crosspiece fossil-plasma origin.

quotes.quote: It is identified in the NVSS catalogue as a 3.2 mJy point source. In our 330 MHz map it is slightly extended, and has an integrated flux of 27.8 mJy. Combining these two fluxes we calculate a spectral index = 1.5 . This source is coincident with the optical galaxy WEIN 047, and is also an IRAS source.

Source C as the origin of the Crosspiece's radio plasma would not have this difficulty, because it could have passed the present position of the Crosspiece relatively recently. The source itself is asymmetric with an elongation pointing directly towards the Crosspiece. Although it has a somewhat steeper than normal spectrum for an AGN ( 1.5 ), it is coincident with a known galaxy. Therefore we consider source C a more likely origin of the radio plasma revived by 3C 129's bow shock.

gmw_00007430.name: Carina
gmw_00007430.overview: Carina is a dwarf galaxy located within the Local Group [arXiv:0901.4975]. Although neutral hydrogen clouds have been reported nearby, there is no detected HI gas within the galaxy's optical radius, making a physical association with these clouds unlikely [arXiv:0901.4975].

sources.summary: The paper finds that reported HI clouds near Carina sit outside its optical and tidal radii, making a physical association with the dwarf unlikely.

quotes.quote: Three clouds were reported near the Carina galaxy by BCS06. Of the three clouds, two are near the optical edge of the galaxy at a distance of about 80 ^ (2.3 kpc) from the optical center, and have velocities close to the optical velocity of Carina. There is no HI within the optical radius of the galaxy and the clouds lie outside the tidal radius, so it is unlikley that the gas and the dwarf are physically associated (BCS06).

To summarize our findings for the non-detections of previously known Local Group dwarfs, we confirm the non-detections or improve HI mass limits with HIPASS for Leo I and Leo II knapp78 , Cetus, Carina, Sextans (BCS06 BCS06 ), and the core of the Sagittarius stream koribalski94 and find that the gas in the vicinity of Tucana is unlikely to be associated.

gmw_00007431.name: Tucana
gmw_00007431.overview: Tucana is an optical dwarf galaxy located near the Magellanic Stream [arXiv:0901.4975]. It is considered a neutral hydrogen non-detection because the nearby gas cloud is offset from the galaxy in both velocity and position [arXiv:0901.4975].

sources.summary: The paper treats Tucana as an HI non-detection because nearby gas is offset in velocity and position and lies close to the Magellanic Stream.

quotes.quote: HI in the general direction of Tucana was first detected by who claimed it was associated with the Magellanic Stream. BCS06 also detect this cloud at a velocity of about 130 km s ^ 1 , and offset from central position of the dwarf by 18 ^ . The optical velocity for Tucana has been found by to be 182 km s ^ 1 , so the difference in velocity between the HI cloud and the optical dwarf is 52 km s ^ 1 .

Figure ambig shows the average velocity along the line of sight with the integrated intensity contours overlaid for the vicinity of the Tucana Dwarf. The optical position of the dwarf is marked with a plus sign. Due to its proximity to the Magellanic Stream and the offset velocity of the cloud near the Tucana dwarf, we consider it a non-detection.

gmw_00007432.name: Sculptor Dwarf
gmw_00007432.overview: The Sculptor Dwarf is a classical dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:2006.16706] that lacks recent star formation [arXiv:0901.4975]. It is characterized by a high mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:2006.16706] and a sharp metal-rich cutoff in its stellar population, which is best explained by ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1310.0814].

sources.summary: The paper selects Sculptor as a classical dwarf spheroidal benchmark for CTA branon dark-matter searches, citing its dark-matter suitability and simulating 300-hour observations.

quotes.quote: We will adopt two astrophysical targets as benchmarks, one for each site, namely the Draco and Sculptor dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies. This kind of objects has been shown to be particularly relevant for DM searches given their high mass-to-light ratios, relative proximity and low level of expected gamma-ray background induced by ordinary astrophysical phenomena . In particular, Draco and Sculptor are both very well studied objects and are among the brightest ones in terms of expected DM flux .

For each selected target we simulate CTA observations with total exposures of 300 h per dSph. This exposure is the expected amount of time dedicated to observations of dSphs in the first 3 years of scientific operation of CTA . Note that the coordinates in table tab:simu refer to the target position, which slight differs from the center of the CTA field of view when adopting the wobble strategy for the observation. In our case, the pointing has an offset of 0.5 degrees with respect to the source position .

sources.summary: The paper labels the Sculptor Dwarf HI detection ambiguous because nearby clouds match its velocity but are offset and confused with Magellanic Stream-region gas.

quotes.quote: One of these is the Sculptor Dwarf, near which two HI clouds were discovered by . The velocity of the HI ( 105 km s ^ 1 ) and the optical velocity of the dwarf galaxy (102 km s ^ 1 ) agree. Despite this, the Sculptor dwarf is in the same direction as the Magellanic Stream and another complex of HI clouds in the general direction of the Sculptor Group putman03 , and numerous clouds near that velocity are found in this region that could be mistaken for gas associated with the Sculptor dwarf (see Figure ambig ). For these reasons, as well as the offset of the clouds from the optical center and the lack of recent star formation in this dwarf, we consider the Sculptor detection ambiguous.

Sculptor and Fornax are the most distant dwarf galaxies in Table Table 3 and are also labeled as ambiguous detections indicating there are clouds in the vicinity of these galaxies which may or may not be associated (see Figures ambig and hidist ). Since in both cases the HI clouds are offset from the optical center of the dwarf, even if the gas is associated with the dwarf galaxies it appears to have been partially removed.

sources.summary: The paper finds Sculptor among the luminous dSphs with a sharp MDF cutoff; rapid gas removal can explain it, and ram-pressure stripping best fits Sculptor.

quotes.quote: None of the models is a great fit to the MDFs of the four most luminous MW dSphs in our sample (Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II) . The observed MDFs approach a wall in [ Fe/H ] at the metal-rich end. None of the three models we presented so far can explain the sharpness of the wall. All three models overpredict the number of the most metal-rich stars.
Rapid, efficient removal of gas can explain the sharp, metal-rich cut-offs we observed for Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II .
However, the Ram Pressure Stripping Model is the best fit of all four models only for Sculptor.

gmw_00007433.name: Fornax dwarf
gmw_00007433.overview: The Fornax dwarf spheroidal galaxy is a Local Group member with a metal-rich stellar population cutoff that suggests rapid gas removal [arXiv:0901.4975, arXiv:1310.0814]. An associated neutral hydrogen cloud is offset from its optical center, indicating the gas may have been partially stripped [arXiv:0901.4975].

sources.summary: The paper considers the Fornax dwarf HI cloud ambiguous, citing Galactic contamination, offset from the optical center, and possible partial gas stripping.

quotes.quote: Another ambiguous case is that of the Fornax dwarf. The Fornax dwarf has an optical velocity of 53 km s ^ 1 . The cloud in question suffers from contamination from Galactic HI emission in the HIPASS data and is offset 28 ^ from the optical center of Fornax. In the analysis by BCS06 the removal of the Milky Way's spectrum was incomplete, and it was unclear if the cloud was part of the Milky Way, a cloud of separate origin, or associated with the Fornax dwarf. We confirm that the origin of the cloud is unclear from the HIPASS data, which is shown in its environment in Figure ambig .

Sculptor and Fornax are the most distant dwarf galaxies in Table Table 3 and are also labeled as ambiguous detections indicating there are clouds in the vicinity of these galaxies which may or may not be associated (see Figures ambig and hidist ). Since in both cases the HI clouds are offset from the optical center of the dwarf, even if the gas is associated with the dwarf galaxies it appears to have been partially removed.

sources.summary: The paper finds Fornax has a sharply cut-off MDF poorly fit by simple models; rapid gas removal can explain the cutoff, though Fornax prefers the Accretion Model.

quotes.quote: None of the models is a great fit to the MDFs of the four most luminous MW dSphs in our sample (Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II) . The observed MDFs approach a wall in [ Fe/H ] at the metal-rich end. None of the three models we presented so far can explain the sharpness of the wall. All three models overpredict the number of the most metal-rich stars.
Rapid, efficient removal of gas can explain the sharp, metal-rich cut-offs we observed for Fornax, Leo I, Sculptor, and Leo II .
Fornax, Leo I, and Leo II prefer the Accretion Model, although the difference between the two models for Leo II is tiny.

gmw_00007434.name: Leo T
gmw_00007434.overview: Leo T is a low-luminosity dwarf galaxy in the Local Group that is characterized by a recent history of star formation [arXiv:0901.4975]. It contains a compact neutral hydrogen cloud with a total mass estimated at several 10^5 solar masses [arXiv:0901.4975].

sources.summary: The paper highlights Leo T as the only newly discovered SDSS dwarf with HI, measuring a compact cloud and finding a total HI mass of several 10^5 solar masses.

quotes.quote: Leo T is one of the newly discovered SDSS dwarf galaxies and is a particularly interesting object due to its low luminosity, recent history of star formation, and gas content. The HIPASS data show a compact HI cloud in the direction of the Leo T dwarf which was first reported by , and confirmed by with synthesis data. Leo T has a velocity of about 35 km s ^ 1 , and in the HIPASS data a maximum velocity of about 46 km s ^ 1 . The lower velocity cutoff and the total width in velocity are uncertain due to Galactic interference.

Our reanalysis of the HI cloud as it appears in the HIPASS data reduced to recover extended emission indicates Leo T has a total HI mass of about 4.3 10 ^ 5 M _ assuming a distance of 420 kpc. The HI mass of Leo T was also checked with new data from the Galactic Arecibo L-band Feed Array (GALFA) HI Survey, which has a velocity resolution of 0.74 (e.g., Stanimirovic et al. 2006 stanimirovic06 ), and found to be 4.8 10 ^ 5 M _ . found a total HI mass of 2.8 10 ^ 5 M _ and a peak HI column density of 7 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 .

gmw_00007435.name: NGC 4394
gmw_00007435.overview: NGC 4394 is a barred, ringed spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0107100] classified as a LINER [arXiv:1204.6212] that contains a recently formed, vertically thin bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0504646]. The galaxy exhibits low star formation along its bar [arXiv:2106.13099], with young star groupings concentrated primarily in its ring region [arXiv:astro-ph/0107100].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4394 as a barred ringed spiral near M85 whose young star groupings are concentrated in its ring rather than its spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 4394 NGC 4394 is a barred and ringed spiral galaxy, with integrated B magnitude 11.53 mag and with D_ 25 =3.6 (RC3). Its proximity to M85, just 6 East, and their similar radial velocities suggest that NGC 4394 may belong to the M85 subgroup of the Virgo Cluster. Thus we assume as distance of this galaxy the same (about 16 Mpc) deduced by Ferrarese et al. (2000) for the M85 subcluster basing on Cepheid's indicators. The inclination and the position angle are taken from Chapelon et al. (1999) and are 25 ^ and 103 ^ , respectively.

The distribution of the YSGs, shown in Fig. 3, is clearly concentrated in the ring region and just few YSGs are detected along the spiral arms. This is supported by the radial distribution of the surface luminosity density, _B , defined as the total B luminosity of the YSGs found in an annulus divided by its area (see Fig. 5).

sources.summary: The paper adopts an activity-region size for NGC 4394, classifies it as a LINER, and finds a positive gradient with low bulge nebular metallicity.

quotes.quote: These influence regions have been determined from Fig. actividad except for NGC 2681, NGC 2859 and NGC 4394 for which we have considered the sizes reported by V eron-Cetty V eron (2006) and references therein.
For LINERs, we find a positive gradient for three galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 4314 and NGC 4394) and none of them has a negative gradient.

For the following run2 galaxies the nebular metallicities lie clearly below the stellar metallicity values: NGC 1169, NGC 2962 (in agreement with Marino et al. 2011) and NGC 4394. Only NGC 4394 is classified as LINER. This lower metallicity gas (w..t. the stellar metallicity) is located in the bulge region, similar values for the metallicity of both components are found elsewhere.

sources.summary: NGC 4394 is identified as a quiescent barred galaxy with low molecular gas and low star formation, but not the reduced SFE seen in NGC 4548 and NGC 5850.

quotes.quote: All apertures along the bar of NGC 4394 have _ mol <10 M_ pc ^ 2 .
In general, three galaxies in our sample (NGC 4394, NGC 4548, and NGC 5850) show low levels of SF along the bar major-axis.

Of the three quiescent bars that we resolve in this work, with _ SFR 10^ 2.25 M_ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 , NGC 4548 and NGC 5850 have a factor of 2 lower SFEs, while NGC 4394 (<SFE> =0.64 0.07 Gyr ^ 1 ) does not.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4394 as having a recently formed, vertically thin bar, with low bar velocity dispersion and radial profiles matching expected young-bar behavior.

quotes.quote: One can say with a certain degree of confidence that NGC 1326, NGC 4394 and NGC 5383 harbor recently formed bars. Their results resemble what we would expect based on Fig(s). 5(a) and 6(a) (for NGC 1326 and NGC 5383) and 6(b) (for NGC 4394). Moreover, _ z, bar < 40 Km/s and _z < 10 Km/s (although we do not have this parameter for NGC 5383), that in fact point to these bars as being still a vertically thin structure, belonging to the plane of the disk.

gmw_00007436.name: UM675
gmw_00007436.overview: UM675 is a luminous active galactic nucleus classified as a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2107.06496], though it has also been described as radio-quiet [arXiv:2107.06496, arXiv:1407.5998]. The quasar hosts a variable mini-broad absorption line system [arXiv:2107.06496, arXiv:1407.5998] whose carbon and nitrogen absorption profiles have weakened over several years in the rest frame [arXiv:1407.5998].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UM675 as a radio-loud quasar and models its mini-BAL and narrow absorption systems across four spectra, finding variability, partial coverage, and overlapping absorbing clouds.

quotes.quote: We illustrate and test this method by fitting models to a ``mini-broad'' (mini-BAL) and six NAL profiles in four spectra of the quasar UM675 taken over a rest-frame interval of 4.24 years. Our fitting results are consistent with past results for the mini-BAL system in this quasar by ham97b . We also measure covering factors ( ) for two narrow components in the C 4 and N 5 mini-BALs and their overlap covering factor with the broad component.

We classify UM675 as a radio-loud quasar with a radio-loudness parameter of R = 351, using an optical magnitude ( V = 17.4) and a radio flux ( f_ = 122 mJy at 4.85 GHz) from gri94 , while it was classified as a radio-quiet quasar in ham95,ham97b . luminous quasar UM675 at \ = 2.147, sar88 detected three C 4 NALs at \ 1.7666, 1.9288, and 2.0083. ham95 also discovered C 4 , N 5 , and \ mini-BALs with FWHM 500 with a high metallicity ( Z > 2 Z_ ) at \ 2.134 corresponding to an offset velocity of \ 1500 \ from the quasar emission redshift.

The mini-BAL system is optically thin because the Lyman-continuum edge is absent. The system also has a wide variety of absorption lines with a full range of ionization from Ne 8 (ionization potential IP = 239 eV) to C 3 (IP = 48 eV) bea91,ham95 , which suggests that the gas density and/or radial distance from the flux source span a factor of 100 or 10, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper discusses UM675 as a radio-quiet quasar with a variable mini-BAL absorber whose C IV and N V profiles weakened over several rest-frame years.

quotes.quote: UM675 ( = 2.15 ), mini-BAL system at 1,900 \ ( 2.13 ): Time variability of the C 4 and N 5 doublets over 2.9 yrs in the quasar rest frame was discovered in the mini-BAL absorber at \ 2.13 (i.e., \ 1900 ) in this radio-quiet quasar ham95 . Additional observations indicated that the different ions can have different coverage fractions: \ 0.45 for C 4 , \ 0.83 for , and \ 0.6 for O 6 ham97a . We collected two spectra taken with Keck/HIRES and Subaru/HDS in 1994 and 2005, respectively. As shown in Figure fig:varplo_UM675 , N 5 and C 4 got weaker at a > 5 significance over 3.5 years in the quasar rest frame. \ also showed the same trend.
Summary: The C 4 and N 5 profiles have weakened on timescales of several years in the quasar rest frame. This places constraints on density ( n_e 3.3 10 ^3 ), and thus distance ( 4.0 kpc).

gmw_00007437.name: HE0151 4326
gmw_00007437.overview: HE0151 4326 is a quasar located at a redshift of 2.78 [arXiv:1407.5998]. It hosts a mini-broad absorption line system spanning more than 5000 km/s in velocity space, which has shown an increase in absorption strength over a two-year rest-frame period [arXiv:1407.5998].

sources.summary: The paper identifies multiple mini-BAL absorption systems in HE0151 4326, with later-epoch strengthening and Si IV emergence consistent with lower ionization.

quotes.quote: HE0151 4326 ( = 2.78 ), mini-BAL system at 11,300 and 8,900 \ ( 2.64 and 2.67): This system has not yet been studied in detail as an intrinsic absorption system. There are two distinct absorption systems at \ 8900 \ (corresponding to v 0 -- 1000 \ in Figure fig:varplo_Q0151 ), with C 4 and Si 4 detected, and at \ 11300 \ ( v 2800 to 1800 ), detected in C 4 . In addition to these two systems, there is likely to be a weaker system at \ 6400 \ ( v 1500 -- 3000 ) with detected C 4 . This mini-BAL system spans more than 5000 \ in velocity space, although it does not satisfy the criterion for being a BAL (which requires that the observed spectrum falls at least 10 below the model of continuum plus emission lines over a contiguous velocity interval of at least 2000 ).

Summary: The C 4 profile for this absorption complex, which spans 5000 , has increased in strength between observations separate by 2 years in the quasar rest frame. Si 4 has also appeared in one component. This is consistent with a lower degree of ionization at the later time, and places constraints on densities ( n_e 3.9 10 ^3 ), and thus distances ( 6.3 kpc) for the \ 11300 \ and 8900 \ systems.

gmw_00007438.name: HE1341 1020
gmw_00007438.overview: HE1341 1020 is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.134 [arXiv:1407.5998]. It hosts a mini-broad absorption line system that exhibits C IV and N V absorption variability over a rest-frame timescale of two years [arXiv:1407.5998].

sources.summary: The paper reports that HE1341 1020 has a mini-BAL system with C IV and N V variability over two rest-frame years, consistent with coverage or ionization changes.

quotes.quote: HE1341 1020 ( = 2.134 ), mini-BAL system at 1,300 \ ( 2.12 ): We found this mini-BAL system with broad absorption features of C 4 and N 5 in the catalog of nar07 . As far as we know, this system has not been previously studied in detail as an intrinsic absorption system. We collected two epochs of observations with VLT/UVES in 2001 and 2007, which are shown in Figure fig:varplo_Q1341 . With the two available epochs we find that both lines show time variability on a timescale of 2 yrs in the quasar rest frame. Both the C 4 and the N 5 are stronger at the later epoch at more than 5 significance level, and they are stronger over the entire profile.

Summary: The C 4 and N 5 profiles have varied on a timescale of 2 years in the quasar rest frame, with both ions becoming stronger over the full range in velocity. This is certainly consistent with a change in coverage fraction, but an ionization change also cannot be ruled out with only two ions available as constraints. If an ionization change is the cause of variability, we can place constraints on density ( n_e 3.0 10 ^3 ), and distance ( 3.5 kpc).

gmw_00007439.name: Q2343+125
gmw_00007439.overview: Q2343+125 is a radio-quiet quasar [arXiv:1407.5998] that stands out as an exceptionally bright source [arXiv:1810.10655]. It features a high-velocity mini-broad absorption line system [arXiv:1407.5998] and is located near another quasar, FSzP1170, which may contribute to its observed flux [arXiv:1810.10655].

sources.summary: The paper discusses Q2343+125 as a radio-quiet quasar with a high-velocity mini-BAL; later C IV monitoring shows only low-significance variability.

quotes.quote: We place constraints on the electron density ( n_e 1.6 10 ^4 ) and the absorber's distance from the flux source ( 4.0 kpc) Q2343+125 ( = 2.515 ), mini-BAL system at 24,400 \ ( 2.24 ): Previously, ham97b detected C 4 , N 5 , and Si 4 mini-BALs at \ 2.24 (corresponding to \ 24400 ) in this radio-quiet quasar spectrum.

These lines showed both time variability (the lines weakened and then strengthened again by a factor of 4 on a timescale of 0.3 yrs in the quasar rest frame) and partial coverage ( \ 0.2 for C 4 ). We collected additional spectra with VLT/UVES at three different epochs from 2002 to 2007. Because of the different wavelength coverage of the different observations, only C 4 has multiple observations (Figure fig:varplo_Q2343 ). \ and N 5 are blended with the \ forest.

The \ measurements for C 4 1548 were 3.29 0.69 , 2.04 0.44 , and 2.69 0.63 \ for observations 1, 2, and 3, respectively, indicating only negligible variability at less than the 2 level. We do not detect any Si 4 features, while ham97b found a weak Si 4 mini-BAL. Several narrow components inside the broader absorption profiles of C 4 ham97b are not apparent in our lower- \ spectra. Summary: In this \ 24,400 \ mini-BAL, C 4 gets slightly weaker, and then stronger, with changes taking place in only 124 and 403 days in the quasar rest frame, but in < 2 level.

sources.summary: The paper reports the system at z_abs=2.431 toward Q 2343+125 has metal absorption over 250 km/s, Zn metallicity [Zn/H]=0.89, weak H2 detection, and the smallest molecular fraction observed.

quotes.quote: The profile of the metal lines is spread over more than 250 km s ^ 1 from z_ abs = 2.4283 to 2.4313 but the strongest component is centered at z_ abs = 2.43127 corresponding to the red edge of the above redshift range. From Voigt--profile fitting to the H i Lyman-- , and lines, we find that the damped Lyman- line is centered at z_ abs = 2.431 and the column density is log N (H i ) = 20.40 0.07 , consistent with previous measurement by D'Odorico et al. (2002; log N (H i ) = 20.35 0.05 ). We use Zn as the reference species for metallicity measurement and find [ Zn/H ] = 0.89 0.08.

Absorption from the J = 1 and probably from the J = 0 rotational levels of H _2 is detected in this system at z_ abs = 2.43127 (see Fig. q2343h2 ). The optically thin H _2 absorption lines are very weak, i.e. close to but above the 3 detection limit.

The total H _2 column density, integrated over the J = 0 and 1 levels, is estimated to be log N (H _2 ) = 13.69 0.09 (12.97 0.04 and 13.60 0.10 for J = 0 and 1 respectively). This leads to the smallest molecular fraction observed up to now, log f = 6.41_ 0.16 ^ +0.16 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Q2343 + 125 as an unusually bright source whose flux may be affected by nearby QSO FSzP1170 and recommends detailed follow-up.

quotes.quote: Q2343 + 125 - This source definitely stands out amongst this sample, being nearly 3 times brighter than the next brightest source. Interestingly, there is another QSO, FSzP1170, located only 5 arcsec north of Q2343 + 125 at a redshift whose velocity difference corresponds to 670 km s ^ 1 , but that is undetected in our maps. Given the close proximity of FSzP1170 to Q2343 + 125 and the limited angular resolution of our NOEMA data (the angular separation makes the second QSO right on the border of our synthesized beam, and prominent sidelobes in the north-south direction nearly overlap with the position of FSzP1170), it could be that this secondary QSO is contributing to the high flux seen in Q2343 + 125. Future observations should follow up Q2343 + 125 in more detail in order to better understand this source. While the field does appear to contain a companion galaxy, we find that it does not contain a detectable CO(3--2) line.

gmw_00007440.name: SDSS J084133.15+200525.8
gmw_00007440.overview: SDSS J084133.15+200525.8 is a FeLoBAL quasar hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.06977]. Between 2005 and 2011, the quasar exhibited a large change in flux driven by a weakening of its absorption troughs [arXiv:1604.06977].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J084133.15+200525.8 as a FeLoBAL quasar with dramatic absorption weakening, luminosity estimates, and an adopted high black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:j0841 , we plot the SDSS and rescaled BOSS spectra of J0841, from epochs 53705 and 55567 (2005 and 2011) respectively. We observe a large change in flux due to an absorption decrease between the two spectra. Black Hole Mass Estimate For J0841, the value of L_ bol from is suspect because it is based on a partial detection of \ in the SDSS spectrum, whereas the BOSS spectrum covers the whole \ region and the luminosity at 2900 \ as suggested by \ calibrations, see e.g., , . Using F_ 2900 =5.5 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ^ 1 , D_L=5.8 10^ 28 cm, and a 2900 \ bolometric correction of BC_ 2900 =5 1 gtr06 , we find L_ bol =(BC_ ) F_ 4 D_L^2= (3.36 0.69) 10^ 47 .

Due to strong absorption within \ emission line, a clean black hole mass measurement is not possible from this object's BOSS spectrum. For our calculations, we adopt M_ BH = 6 10^9 M_ and thus L_ Edd =3.8 10^ 47 erg s ^ 1 with Eddington ratio of L_ bol /L_ Edd = 0.45 .

Centred on that redshift, there are overlapping troughs of \ (from both ground and excited states) which weaken dramatically between our two spectral epochs. Also weakening are troughs of \ + , , , , , , and .

gmw_00007441.name: SDSS J123103.70+392903.6
gmw_00007441.overview: SDSS J123103.70+392903.6 is a variable broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 1.0227 [arXiv:1604.06977]. The object exhibits strong continuum flux variability [arXiv:1604.06977] and showed a weakening of its absorption lines between 2005 and 2011 [arXiv:1507.07247].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J123103.70+392903.6 as a variable FeLoBAL quasar whose continuum brightened while BAL trough behavior constrained the absorbing regions.

quotes.quote: For the quasar SDSS J123103.70+392903.6 (hereafter J1231), we adopt the Principal Component Analysis redshift z_ PCA = 1.0227 0.0004 from the SDSS DR12 quasar catalog (DR12Q, Paris et al. \ 2015, in preparation).

Quasar J1231 Figure fig:f_j1231 is a plot of the spectra of J1231 from MJDs 53466 and 55591; there is obvious strong flux variability. also report this variability; however, they report a lack of detectable strong variability in other spectra taken by them within about one rest-frame year of the MJD 55591 spectrum. Figure fig:f_j1231n is a plot of the same spectra normalized at wavelengths 7200-8400 \ observed. Power Law Fitting and Analysis No spectrophotometric correction is needed for the BOSS spectra of this object, as its fiber was positioned to optimize throughput at the same central wavelength as for the standard stars.

When our two spectra of J1231 are multiplicatively normalized by a constant factor equal to the ratio of their fluxes in the rest-frame 3600 - 4200 \ region, the profile of the low-velocity half of the Mg ii BAL is the same in both spectra, and the spectra longward of 3100 \ are identical as well. That indicates identical brightenings of regions not covered by the Mg ii BAL (seen in the bottom of the BAL trough) and regions covered by it (which contribute most of the flux seen at wavelengths outside of absorption troughs). If instead only a part of the source uncovered by Mg ii had brightened, we would expect the second-epoch spectrum to be well fit by the first-epoch spectrum plus a smooth power-law component, which is not the case (see the cyan curve in Figure fig:f_j1231a ).

sources.summary: The paper finds J123103 weakens in absorption from 2005 to 2011, with significant Fe II and Ni II changes but no 2011 to 2013 variability.

quotes.quote: Over the 2.9 yr between the DR7 and DR12 epochs, we see decreases in absorption line strength using our 8 criterion between 2130 and 2690 , a region where the Fe ii (UV1,2,3), Fe ii ^* (UV62,63), and Ni ii ^* (UV12,13) multiplets occur (see Fig. 1). This source varied with a maximum significance level of 11 . Similar decreases in absorption line strength are observed near Fe iii ^* (UV48) and Fe ii ^* (UV78,Opt6), although these do not meet our 8 threshold. The BAL extending from 2650 to 2775 \ could consist of absorption from both Mg ii and Fe ii ^* (UV62,63), and we see variability only in the high-velocity portion of the BAL. We conclude that there is no significant absorption line variability from 2011 to 2013, a time-scale of 1.0 yr, however J123103 does exhibit absorption line weakening from 2005 to 2011 at the 8 level.

gmw_00007442.name: FBQS J140806.2+305448
gmw_00007442.overview: FBQS J140806.2+305448 is a quasar at a redshift of z=0.848 [arXiv:1604.06977] that transitioned from a heavily absorbing iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar to a modestly absorbing one [arXiv:1501.00091]. This transition was marked by a decrease in magnesium II absorption and the disappearance of iron II absorption troughs [arXiv:1604.06977, arXiv:1501.00091].

sources.summary: The paper reports continued absorption weakening in FBQS J140806.2+305448, with the later spectrum showing Mg II absorption, Fe II emission, and decreasing equivalent width.

quotes.quote: For the quasar FBQS J140806.2+305448 (hereafter J1408), we adopt the value of z=0.848 used in , for consistency.

Quasar J1408 The dramatic absorption variability in a time span less than 5 rest-frame years of this quasar was first discussed in . We present the continued decrease of this object's absorption in Figure fig:f_j1408 . As the absorption has conntiued to decrease, the underlying power-law spectrum of the quasar is quite obvious in these latest epochs.

Trough Identification In the later BOSS spectrum of J1408, the only absorption present is from Mg ii ; Fe ii is seen in emission, not absorption. We also confirm the finding of that H absorption was present in J1408 up to and including the SDSS epoch. Equivalent Width The equivalent width of \ absorption decreases for about 18.92 0.25 \ from 2006 to 2010 while \ equivalent width decreases at the same time for about 17.24 0.39 .

sources.summary: The paper reports FBQS J140806.2+305448 changed from a spectacular OFeLoBAL quasar to a modest LoBAL, with Mg absorption weakening, Fe troughs disappearing, and modeled BAL gas close to the black hole.

quotes.quote: FBQS J140806.2+305448 was a spectacular OFeLoBAL quasar with a covering fraction of Fe 2 absorption of 75 percent, but now, it is only a modestly absorbing LoBAL quasar. Hall et al. (2011) have found there was no significant absorption variability in early observations, one rest-frame year prior to the KECK/ESI observation in March 2000; however, the Mg 2 trough outflowing at 12,000 decreased by a factor of two, and Fe 2 troughs at the same velocity disappeared over the next 7 rest-frame years. The SDSS DR10 spectrum shows a complete lack of Fe 2 troughs (see also the HET spectrum in Hall et al. 2011).

Based on the size of that region and the time scale over which the absorption changes, Hall et al. (2011) constrained the BAL structure of FBQS J140806.2+305448 to a transverse velocity between 2600 and 22,000 and a line-of-sight velocity of 12,000 ; thus, the moving BAL structure was considered to be located approximately 1.7 to 14 pc from the black hole, nearly ten to ninety times farther than the H broad-line region.

For FBQS J140806.2+305448, a model with U = 10^ 1 , n _e = 10^ 11  cm^ 3 , N _H = 10^ 23  cm^ 2 and FWHM = 2000 exhibits absorption features that are quite consistent with the observations, with the exception of the absorption between the troughs of Mg 2 and Fe 2 Opt8.

gmw_00007443.name: Swift J2058.4+0516
gmw_00007443.overview: Swift J2058.4+0516 is a distant active galactic nucleus that hosted a tidal disruption event [arXiv:2202.11265]. This event is considered a candidate for the birth of a relativistic jet and exhibits correlated X-ray and optical variability [arXiv:2202.11265].

sources.summary: The paper models Swift J2058.4+0516 as a distant relativistic TDE candidate, deriving a central black-hole mass and linking X-ray and optical variability.

quotes.quote: China In the manuscript, central black hole (BH) mass is estimated in the distant TDE (tidal disruption event) of Swift J2058.4+0516 as the second candidate of relativistic jet birth related to TDE. Swift J2058.4+0516 have quite different BH masses estimated through different indirect methods in the literature. Therefore, it is necessary and interesting to determine the central BH mass in Swift J2058.4+0516 by one another independent method. Here, based on the theoretical TDE model applied to describe the long-term time-dependent X-ray variabilities of Swift J2058.4+0516, the central BH mass can be well determined to be around 1.05_ 0.29 ^ +0.39 10^5 M_ , after kind considerations of suggested intrinsic beaming effects from such relativistic jet tightly related to TDE. Moreover, the Swift J2058.4+0516 is an unique object in the space of BH masses versus energy transfer efficiencies of the reported TDE candidates, providing interesting clues to detect and/or anticipate candidates of relativistic TDE to make the birth of relativistic jets. galaxies:active - galaxies:nuclei - galaxies:individual: transients:tidal disruption events \ (= Swift J20:58:19.90+05:13:32) has been reported as a Tidal Disruption Event (TDE) candidate in ck12 by the long-term X-ray variabilities discovered by the Burst Alert Telescope bb05 on the Swift satellite gc04 , and then by the long-term X-ray variabilities discussed and reported in pc15 and in wh20 , and also reported as the second candidate of relativistic TDE to make the birth of relativistic jets similar as the results in Swift J1644+57 \ well discussed in bur11, zb11, bz12, mb16, eb18 .

Therefore, significant correlation between X-ray and optical light curves can be confirmed in Swift J2058.4+0516, providing further evidence to support that it is also an efficient way to detect the TDE by X-ray properties in Swift J2058.4+0516, although most TDEs are commonly detected and reported by optical variability properties.

gmw_00007444.name: Andromeda XXVII
gmw_00007444.overview: Andromeda XXVII is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of M31 that contains a single old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:1710.10286]. The spatial distribution of its red giant branch and RR Lyrae stars indicates that the system is undergoing complete tidal disruption [arXiv:1710.10286].

sources.summary: The paper presents Andromeda XXVII as an M31 satellite with 90 variables, an old metal-poor population, and strong evidence for tidal disruption and line-of-sight structure.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: center coordinates of And XXVII R.A.= 00^ h 37^ m 27^ s , decl.=+45 ^ 23 13 ; J2000

quotes.quote: Clementini We present B and V time series photometry of the M31 satellite galaxy Andromeda XXVII (And XXVII) that we observed with the Large Binocular Cameras of the Large Binocular Telescope. In the field of And XXVII we have discovered a total of 90 variables: 89 RR Lyrae stars and 1 Anomalous Cepheid. The average period of the fundamental mode RR Lyrae stars (RRab) P _ ab =0.59 d ( =0.05 d) and the period-amplitude diagram place And XXVII in the class of Oosterhoff I/Intermediate objects. Combining information from the color-magnitude diagram (CMD) and the variable stars we find evidence for a single old and metal poor stellar population with [ Fe/H ] 1.8 dex and t 13 Gyr in And XXVII. The spatial distribution of RR Lyrae and red giant branch (RGB) stars gives clear indication that And XXVII is a completely disrupted system.

These authors claimed that And XXVII is in the process of being tidally disrupted by M31 and derived only a lower limit of 757 45 kpc for its heliocentric distance due to the difficulty in measuring the magnitude of the galaxy horizontal branch (HB). iba2013 later found that And XXVII is a member of the thin plane of satellites identified in M31 Great Plane of Andromeda, GPoA, following the definition of paw2013 . The heliocentric distance of And XXVII was revised using tip of the Red Giant Branch (RGB) stars by con12 who estimated a value of 1255^ +42 _ 474 kpc that would place the galaxy outside the M31 complex. The RGB tip of And XXVII is very scarcely populated likely due to the disrupting nature of the galaxy.

gmw_00007445.name: IKN
gmw_00007445.overview: IKN is a faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the M 81 group of galaxies [arXiv:1506.04155]. It hosts five old globular clusters and exhibits a complex star-formation history likely triggered by tidal interactions within its group [arXiv:1506.04155].

sources.summary: The paper presents IKN as a faint dwarf spheroidal with unusually numerous old globular clusters, high cluster-to-field-star mass fraction, broad metallicity evidence, and a likely tidally influenced complex star-formation history.
