quotes.quote: IKN is a faint dwarf spheroidal (dSph) in the M 81 group of galaxies. It contains five old GCs, which makes it the galaxy with the highest known specific frequency ( S_N = 126 ). We estimate the photometric age, metallicity and spatial distribution of the poorly studied IKN GCs.

IKN GCs' VIKs colours are consistent with old ages ( 8 Gyr) and a metallicity distribution with a higher mean than typical for such a dSph ( [ Fe/H ] 1.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.6 dex). Their photometric masses range ( 0.5 < M_ GC <4 10^5M_ ) implies an unusually high mass ratio between GCs and field stars, of 10.6 . Mixture model analysis of the RGB field stars' metallicity suggests that 72 of the stars may have formed together with the GCs.

The evidence towards i) broad and high metallicity distribution of the field IKN RGB stars and its GCs, ii) high fraction and iii) spatial alignment of IKN GCs, supports a scenario for tidally triggered complex IKN's SFH in the context of interactions with galaxies in the M 81 group.

gmw_00007446.name: NGC 5426/7
gmw_00007446.overview: NGC 5426/7 is a weakly interacting binary system composed of two Sc-class spiral galaxies of similar sizes and masses [arXiv:1107.3280]. The system exhibits distorted spiral arms, slightly distorted ionized gas kinematics in NGC 5427, and coherent magnetic spiral patterns that closely follow the optical morphology [arXiv:1107.3280].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5426/7 as a weakly interacting spiral binary whose distorted arms, gas kinematics, dust geometry, and polarimetric data match an early tidal encounter.

quotes.quote: NGC 5426/7 Both galaxies in this binary system are spirals of the same Hubble class Sc, with similar sizes ( 20 kpc), and masses ( 3 10^ 10 M ; Fuentes-Carrera et al. ). The evidence for weak gravitational interaction are the thin straight strands of the outer arms of NGC 5426 that overlap the southern spiral arms of NGC 5427 and the straight arm segment in the SW part of the spiral structure of NGC 5427 (Fig. f:n5426tp ). The velocity field of ionized gas in NGC 5427 is also slightly distorted (Fuentes-Carrera et al. ).

This scenario of weak tidal interaction fully agrees with the radio polarimetric data at 4.86 GHz. The total radio intensity closely follows the optical morphology (Fig. f:n5426tp ). Both galaxies have coherent magnetic spiral patterns that closely follow the optical spiral arms.

gmw_00007447.name: NGC 876/7
gmw_00007447.overview: NGC 876/7 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of a larger, late-type spiral galaxy and a small, highly inclined companion [arXiv:1107.3280]. The larger galaxy features a galactic bar and slightly disturbed optical spiral arms, while a low-surface-brightness radio bridge connects the two components [arXiv:1107.3280].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 876/7 as an early-interaction pair with a disturbed barred spiral, a compact inclined companion, and a low-surface-brightness radio bridge.

quotes.quote: NGC 876/7 The larger component of this pair of galaxies -- NGC 877 -- is a late-type spiral with prominent and slightly disturbed optical spiral arms (Fig. f:n877tp ). Bright H emitting regions are distributed along the arms but are not condensed in the central part of the disk (Hattori et al. ). The companion is a small, highly inclined galaxy ( 78 ) at a projected separation of about 50 kpc. The total radio emission at 4.86 GHz of both the galaxies corresponds well to optical and H disks and even reveals a bridge of low-surface brightness emission (at 180 Jy level) between the components (Fig. f:n877tp ). The strong and elongated radio structure in the main body of NGC 877 is a galactic bar, from which regular magnetic fields stretch out to the N and S.

gmw_00007448.name: NGC 6907/8
gmw_00007448.overview: NGC 6907/8 is an interacting system where the small lenticular galaxy NGC 6908 is superimposed on the northeastern arm of the dominant spiral galaxy NGC 6907 [arXiv:1107.3280]. This minor merger has distorted NGC 6907, shifting its elliptical bulge and creating an elongated eastern arm and a triangular HI gas stream [arXiv:1107.3280].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6907/8 as a minor-merger system where a lenticular companion distorts NGC 6907, producing an elongated arm and triangular HI stream.

quotes.quote: NGC 6907/8 NGC 6908 is a small lenticular galaxy superimposed on the NE arm of the dominant galaxy NGC 6907. It is most clearly seen in near-IR images (Madore et al. ). The spiral structure of NGC 6907 is distorted: its eastern arm is much longer and wraps around the entire galaxy (Fig. f:n6907tp ). The bulge of NGC 6907 has an elliptical shape, and is shifted off the base of the arms. The analysis of distortions in the HI velocity field suggests that the first collision between galaxies occurred 3.4 0.6 10^7 yr ago, leaving behind a triangular HI gas stream to the NE from the current position of NGC 6908 (Scarano et al. ).

gmw_00007449.name: Taffy2
gmw_00007449.overview: Taffy2 is a pair of post-collision spiral galaxies consisting of UGC 813 and UGC 816 [arXiv:1107.3280]. The system experienced a suspected head-on collision approximately 50 million years ago and features a radio bridge [arXiv:1107.3280].

sources.summary: The paper describes Taffy2 as a post-collision spiral pair with a radio bridge, regular magnetic field in UGC 816, and less clear fields in its companion.

quotes.quote: Taffy2 This pair of post-collision spiral galaxies (UGC 813 and UGC 816) is similar to the Taffy system and shows a radio bridge at 1.40 GHz (Condon et al. ). The suspected head-on collision was also a recent one that occurred about 5 10^7 yr ago. We call this system the Taffy2. Our re-reduced data at 4.86 GHz show that, besides the radio bridge, the regular field is discerned in the barred disk of UGC 816 and resembles a typical spiral structure (Fig. f:taffy2tp ). The configuration of B vectors is not as clear in the second galaxy, which could be caused by its high inclination (72 ).

gmw_00007450.name: B2 1811+31
gmw_00007450.overview: B2 1811+31 is an intermediate-frequency peaked BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z = 0.117 [arXiv:2108.05460]. In October 2020, the blazar exhibited elevated optical activity alongside a strong detection of very-high-energy gamma-ray emission [arXiv:2108.05460].

sources.summary: The paper reports VERITAS observations of the blazar B2 1811+31 after elevated Fermi-LAT, MAGIC, and optical activity, yielding a strong VHE detection and Crab-level flux estimate.

quotes.quote: B2 1811+31 is an IBL at redshift z = 0.117 that showed an elevated MeV-GeV flux ( 11x brighter) and harder gamma-ray spectrum ( _ LAT 1.4 vs. 2.1) during Fermi LAT observations in Oct. 2020 (ATel 14060). Following the discovery of VHE emission by MAGIC (Oct. 4-10; ATel 14090), and reports of enhanced optical activity (ATel 14103), VERITAS observed the blazar from Oct. 15-19, 2020. A preliminary analysis of these data ( 5 h) yields a strong detection ( 8 standard deviations, ) and a soft photon spectrum ( = 4.1 0.5).

A sky map of the significance observed near B2 1811+31 is shown in Figure results_panel1 . The VERITAS light curve is consistent with a constant flux F( > 250 GeV) = (1.10 0.18_ stat ) 10^ 11 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . This is approximately 6 of the Crab Nebula flux (Crab) above the same threshold, and is similar to the flux reported by MAGIC.

gmw_00007451.name: SDSS J012415.53 003318.4
gmw_00007451.overview: SDSS J012415.53 003318.4 is a broad-absorption line quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1604.07410]. It is identified as a candidate for broad-absorption line acceleration, although its absorption trough narrowed between later observation epochs [arXiv:1604.07410].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J012415.53 003318.4 as a BAL acceleration candidate, with velocity-shift signatures partly weakened by profile narrowing between later epochs.

quotes.quote: SDSS J012415.53 003318.4 (Figure fig:accel2 ) is one of our two acceleration candidates; the comparisons between Epochs 1 and 2 (middle panel of Figure fig:accel2 ) and Epochs 1 and 3 (Figure fig:all_candidates ) formally meet our requirements for acceleration candidates, although Epochs 2 and 3 (right panel of Figure fig:accel2 ) do not. In addition, the lower panels of the comparison plots, showing the sigma deviation between the two spectra, reveal a trend expected from a velocity shift; the ratio of the deviation at the higher-velocity end of the BAL is systematically negative, while at the lower-velocity end of the BAL it is systematically positive. This offset is remedied by applying the velocity shift to the first epoch --- the red error bars show the sigma deviation after the shift is applied; the velocity shift removes the trend. Unfortunately, Epochs 2 and 3 (right panel of Figure fig:accel2 ) do not formally meet the ^2 requirements to be considered unambiguous acceleration because the trough visibly narrowed between these two epochs, resulting in a statistically unacceptable match between the two spectra.

gmw_00007452.name: SDSS J013656.31 004623.8
gmw_00007452.overview: SDSS J013656.31 004623.8 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1604.07410]. The galaxy is a strong candidate for broad-absorption line acceleration, exhibiting systematic velocity-shift trends and nonconstant acceleration over time [arXiv:1604.07410].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J013656.31 004623.8 as the strongest BAL acceleration candidate, showing systematic velocity-shift trends and nonconstant acceleration across epochs.

quotes.quote: The \ BAL in SDSS J013656.31 004623.8 (Figure fig:accel3 ) is perhaps our strongest acceleration candidate. We again see a visible shift between Epochs 1 and 2 and Epochs 1 and 3, although the test between Epochs 2 and 3 (right panel of Figure fig:accel3 ) does not formally meet the ^2 requirements (the p value is too small in the shifted version). This is because the uncertainties are small in the BOSS and TDSS spectra, and there is a very slight narrowing of the BAL profile as well as some isolated, deviant pixels with small uncertainties. However, in this particular system, we see a systematic negative/positive trend in the sigma deviation between all three pairs of spectra (in contrast, the BAL in J012415.53 003318.4 only showed this trend between two of the three pairs of spectra), and this trend is removed when the velocity shift is applied in all three cases. We again measure a smaller magnitude of acceleration between Epochs 2 and 3 than between Epochs 1 and 2, indicating that the magnitude of the acceleration is not constant over time.

gmw_00007453.name: SDSS J091425.73+504854.9
gmw_00007453.overview: SDSS J091425.73+504854.9 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1604.07410]. It exhibits a shift toward smaller velocities in its highest-velocity absorption trough, which indicates a deceleration of the broad-absorption line outflow [arXiv:1604.07410].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J091425.73+504854.9 as a BAL deceleration case, with the strongest evidence between the SDSS and BOSS epochs.

quotes.quote: SDSS J091425.73+504854.9 (Figure fig:accel4 ) is the only case where we detect a shift toward smaller velocities, or deceleration of the BAL. The upper-left panel of Figure fig:accel4 reveals that this target has several absorption features in the \ line region; the trough in question is the highest-velocity trough, as indicated by the vertical dashed lines. We do not measure significant acceleration in any of the other lower-velocity BAL troughs. Due to an increase in trough strength between Epochs 2 and 3 and the relatively short time span between these two epochs, the measurement of acceleration between these two epochs is formally an upper limit; i.e., the acceleration between these two epochs is not detected at a 3 significance.

The average acceleration measured between Epochs 1 and 3 is actually smaller in magnitude than the average acceleration measured between Epochs 1 and 2 (which is a longer timescale that includes the first epoch); this result suggests that the change between Epochs 2 and 3 represents positive acceleration rather than a continuing deceleration. The deceleration observed between Epochs 1 and 2 (SDSS and BOSS) formally passes all of our requirements, and we see again that the deviation between these two spectra possesses the same systematic trend we would expect from deceleration; hence, we still consider the first two epochs of this target to be a fairly good indication of deceleration, though admittedly less solid than the acceleration seen in our first two candidates.

gmw_00007454.name: WISE J044232.92+322734.9
gmw_00007454.overview: WISE J044232.92+322734.9 is a high-redshift galaxy with a preliminary photometric redshift of 1.132 [arXiv:2010.00808]. Its infrared colors suggest it is a star-forming galaxy, and its estimated infrared luminosity indicates it may be an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:2010.00808].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISE J044232.92+322734.9 as a likely star-forming galaxy at photometric redshift 1.132, possibly a ULIRG/main-sequence SFG, though its WISE-only SED makes the properties preliminary.

quotes.quote: We uncovered a source, namely the WISE source J044232.92+322734.9 (hereafter J044232.92), which is detected in the W1--W3 bands of WISE , but undetected at 22  m (W4), and whose WISE infrared (IR) colours suggest that it is a star-forming galaxy (SFG).

The redshift was derived to be z_ phot =1.132^ +0.280 _ 0.165 , while, for example, the stellar mass, IR (8--1 000  m) luminosity, and star formation rate were derived to be M_ =4.6^ +4.7 _ 2.5 10^ 11  M _ , L_ IR =2.8^ +5.7 _ 1.5 10^ 12  L _ , and SFR =191^ +580 _ 146   M _  yr ^ 1 (or 281^ +569 _ 155   M _  yr ^ 1 when estimated from the IR luminosity). The derived value of L_ IR suggests that J044232.92 could be an ultraluminous IR galaxy, and we found that it is consistent with a main sequence SFG at a redshift of 1.132.

Indeed, the SED of J044232.92 could not be well constrained using the currently available data ( WISE only), and hence the derived redshift of the source and its physical properties should be taken as preliminary estimates. Further observations, in particular high-resolution (sub-)millimetre and radio continuum imaging, are needed to better constrain the redshift and physical properties of J044232.92 and to see if the source really is a galaxy seen through a Galactic dust clump, in particular an SMG population member at z 1.1 .

gmw_00007455.name: SDSS J162034.99+240726.5
gmw_00007455.overview: SDSS J162034.99+240726.5 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0665 [arXiv:2206.00049]. While pre-transient observations classified it as a star-forming galaxy, a tidal disruption event temporarily shifted its nuclear activity to a composite or active galactic nucleus state [arXiv:2206.00049].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J162034.99+240726.5 as AT 2017gge's spiral star-forming host and argues the TDE temporarily shifted its nuclear activity toward composite/AGN-like line ratios.

quotes.quote: The transient is located within 0 .1 (0.1 kpc) from the center of the host galaxy, specifically at the coordinates RA(J2000) = 16:20:34.99 and DEC(J2000)= +24:07:26.5. The host galaxy has been identified to be SDSS J162034.99+240726.5, a spiral galaxy at redshift z=0.0665 , classified as a star-forming galaxy from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) pre-transient optical spectrum.

Finally, the line ratios obtained from the X-shooter spectrum (taken at 1698 days) are log( [ N II ] /H )= 0.36 0.03, log( [ O III ] /H )= 0.08 0.03, and log( [ S II ] /H )= 0.54 0.04 and place the galaxy inside the composite and the starforming regions in the BPT diagrams of Figure fig:BPT (green star in the left and right panels, respectively), indicating a further variation in the host galaxy activity, which come back to values in agreement with what derived from the pre-transient spectrum of SDSS.

The occurrence of the TDE have an impact on the activity of the host galaxy which passes from a quiescent starforming galaxy to a composite/AGN in the BPT diagram, to come back to quiescence after 1698 days.

gmw_00007456.name: J1538+0855
gmw_00007456.overview: J1538+0855 is a hyperluminous quasar at redshift z=3.6 that hosts both broad-line and narrow-line region outflows [arXiv:2205.06832, arXiv:1802.03423]. The system exhibits variable, ultra-fast broad absorption line outflows [arXiv:2205.06832] and is surrounded by a metal-enriched circumgalactic medium nebula [arXiv:2205.06832].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1538+0855 as a hyperluminous z=3.6 quasar with variable multi-component CIV BAL ultra-fast outflows and an extended metal-enriched CGM nebula.

quotes.quote: We report on the variability of a multi-component broad absorption line (BAL) system observed in the hyper-luminous quasar J1538+0855 at z=3.6. Observations from SDSS, VLT, LBT and Subaru telescopes taken at five different epochs, spanning 17 yr in the observed frame, are presented. We detect three (A, B, C) CIV variable troughs exhibiting extreme velocities ( 40,000-54,000 km s ^ 1 ) similar to the ultra-fast outflows (UFOs) typically observed in the X-ray spectra. The A component of the BAL UFO ( 0.17 ) shows strength variations, while B ( 0.15 ) and C ( 0.13 ) components show changes both in shape and strength, appearing and disappearing at different epochs.

identified a hyperluminous AGN in the WISE/SDSS selected Hyperluminous QSO (WISSH) survey, J1538 + 0855 at z 3.567, with BAL features exhibiting a maximum velocity of 0.16 . This object has been extensively studied at rest-frame UV/optical wavelengths, exhibiting pervasive signs of outflows at all scales from pc up to tens of kpc ( Vietri2018 , Travascio2020 ). Vietri2018 reported the presence of broad-line (pc-scale) and narrow-line (kpc-scale) region outflows showing similar kinetic powers, possibly revealing the same outflow in two different gas phases. Travascio2020 reported the presence of blueshifted broad emission component of the Ly providing evidence for outflowing gas reaching distance of 20-30 kpc from the QSO and, for the first time, the detection of a bright 75 kpc wide nebula in the C IV emission line, thereby revealing a metal-enriched component of the CGM around J1538+0855.

sources.summary: J1538+0855 is highlighted as the only WISSH quasar with both NLR and BLR winds, including blueshifted [OIII] and CIV outflows with comparable kinetic powers in different gas phases.

quotes.quote: In the case of J1538+0855, we measure a broad (FWHM _ [ OIII ] ^ broad = 1980 ), blueshifted (v _ [ OIII ] ^ broad = 1,200 ) component with a REW _ [ OIII ] ^ broad = 7.7 , making us highly confident to associate this spectral feature with blueshifted [ OIII ] emission.

Remarkably, in one case, i.e. J1538+0855, the outflows associated with CIV and [ OIII ] have consistent \ values (see Appendix sec:app_1538 ). This would suggest that we are possibly revealing the same outflow in two different gas phases at increasing distance from the AGN (in an energy conserving scenario). This further supports the idea that this object represents a hybrid showing a mixture of the distinctive properties of the two populations of Weak and \ QSOs.

We present here a detailed analysis of the source J1538+0855 since is the only object exhibiting both NLR and BLR winds. As reported in Sect. sec:ekin we measured the CIV outflow properties based on the so-called outflow component. We estimate the gas mass of the outflow M _ ion = 590 M _ , the maximum velocity of the outflow as representative of the bulk velocity of the outflow v _ CIV ^ max = 13,400 , the ionized gas mass rate M _ ion = 24 M _ yr ^ 1 and the kinetic power E _ kin = 1.4 10 ^ 45 .

gmw_00007457.name: UGC 8839
gmw_00007457.overview: UGC 8839 is a low-surface-brightness spiral [arXiv:2105.00587] or dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:1605.01638] with an extended neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1605.01638]. The galaxy is nearly devoid of star formation except for a large native complex located in its extreme outer disk [arXiv:2105.00587].

sources.summary: UGC 8839 is described as a low-surface-brightness spiral whose extreme outer-disc star-formation complex appears native, linked to a dark-matter-dominated stable disk and possible three-body interaction.

quotes.quote: The low surface brightness (LSB) spiral UGC 8839 is nearly devoid of star formation aside from a large region complex located in the extreme outer disc. In order to understand the origin and nature of this complex, we compare new and archival broad images of UGC 8839 to similar data for four other spiral galaxies. We conclude that the extreme off-axis star formation in UGC 8839 is likely due to a hyper-stable disk that is dark matter dominated at all radii, with the Toomre parameter reaching a minimum only in the extreme outer disc. Using analysis strategies designed to be particularly insensitive to the pitfalls of low-surface brightness objects and small number statistics, we determine that the presence of this complex in UGC 8839 is not exceptional when the region luminosity function is modelled by a power law, suggesting that it is a native structure and not a merging satellite.

However, we find that the entire population of regions in UGC 8839 shows a preference for larger galactocentric radii when compared to regions in the other galaxies in our sample. UGC 8839 dramatically highlights the relationship between the baryonic/dark matter ratio and disk stability. A three-body interaction, similar to a scaled-down version of the interaction suspected to be responsible for Malin 1, is consistent with the extreme outer disk star formation that we see in the extended disk of UGC 8839.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 08839 as a dwarf irregular with an extended HI disc, weak H emission, solid-body rotation with a slight warp, and dark-matter-dominated decompositions.

quotes.quote: UGC 08839 UGC 08839 is a dwarf irregular galaxy with an extended HI disc ( D_ HI / D_ 25 = 5). The HI integrated intensity map in Fig.  fig:u08839sum from new VLA observations shows a slight central depression surrounded by two peaks in intensity. Very little H emission was detected in the narrowband image, so UGC 08839 was not targeted for SparsePak observations. The velocity field of UGC 08839 shows solid body rotation in with a slight warp at the outer edges where the isovelocity contours turn over. This warp is modeled as a decrese in inclination angle beyond a radius of 130 arcsec.

gmw_00007458.name: UGC 5633
gmw_00007458.overview: UGC 5633 is a low surface brightness spiral galaxy that features a strong central bar [arXiv:2105.00587]. This bar is both stellar and gaseous, with its southern end exhibiting diffuse emission that contributes to the galaxy's overall emission [arXiv:2105.00587].

sources.summary: UGC 5633 is described as a strongly barred LSB spiral with bar-associated emission; its bar complicated central photometry and contributes a small diffuse-emission component.

quotes.quote: UGC 5633: This is a strongly barred galaxy, a rare characteristic for LSB spirals, yet with a central surface brightness of _0(g)= 22.9 UGC 5633 clearly falls within the LSB category. In images we see some emission associated with this bar, suggesting that it is a gaseous as well as a stellar bar.
The southern end of the bar in UGC 5633 is even evident in the images as diffuse emission, although it only contributes about 5 to the total diffuse emission.

gmw_00007459.name: UGC 6151
gmw_00007459.overview: UGC 6151 is a spiral galaxy with spiral arms traced by star-forming regions [arXiv:2105.00587]. Although its galaxy-wide luminosity is modest, a star-forming region at the exact center of its disc likely drives its starburst classification [arXiv:2105.00587].

sources.summary: UGC 6151 is described as having spiral arms traced by star-forming regions and a central region likely responsible for its SDSS starburst classification despite modest galaxy-wide luminosity.

quotes.quote: UGC 6151: The image for this object shows spirals arms traced out by the regions. One of the regions is located at the exact centre of the disc. This central region is likely the cause of the SDSS DR15 spectral classification of UGC 6151 as a starburst galaxy; in fact the galaxy-wide luminosity is not particularly high (see targets ).

gmw_00007460.name: UGC 6181
gmw_00007460.overview: UGC 6181 is a marginal low surface brightness galaxy that hosts numerous star-forming regions at the edge of its disc [arXiv:2105.00587]. It lies at a projected distance of 640 kpc from UGC 6151, though it shows no obvious signs of interaction [arXiv:2105.00587].

sources.summary: UGC 6181 is presented as a marginal LSB galaxy with edge-disc star-forming regions, lying 640 kpc from UGC 6151 without obvious signs of interaction.

quotes.quote: UGC 6181: This galaxy is a marginal LSB, with _0(g)= 22.2 . It hosts numerous star-forming regions at the edge of the disc. The projected distance between UGC 6181 and UGC 6151 is 640 kpc, close enough that they may be bound, although there are no obvious signs of interaction.

gmw_00007461.name: SDSS J0228-0901
gmw_00007461.overview: SDSS J0228-0901 is an isolated dwarf Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:2306.03404]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus powered by an intermediate-mass black hole, which drives a spatially extended gas outflow [arXiv:2306.03404].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J0228-0901 as an isolated dwarf Seyfert hosting an intermediate-mass black hole and a spatially extended AGN-driven outflow whose velocity can exceed the halo escape speed.

quotes.quote: The galaxy SDSS J0228-0901 is first identified as an AGN in 2007ApJ...670...92G . As listed in Table tab:target_info , its black-hole mass of (3.7 0.3 ) 10^5 M_ is measured through a single-epoch broad line width by 2018ApJ...863....1C . In order to search for and study outflows in this galaxy hosting such an intermediate-mass black hole, we carry out the MUSE IFU observations (PID: 0104.C-0181(C), PI: F. Bian).
As indicated by panel (), the central regions of SDSS J0228-0901 are classified as Seyfert. As shown later in this study, the Seyfert regions overlap more or less with the outflow regions.

The V_ 80 /V_ esc of SDSS J0228-0901 is 1.09 0.04 , larger than most massive galaxies. These results suggest that the AGN-driven outflow in dwarf galaxies is able to escape from the gravitational potential of the dark matter halo.

gmw_00007462.name: source F4
gmw_00007462.overview: Source F4 is a newly detected remnant radio galaxy candidate located in the Abell 2256 cluster [arXiv:2405.09384]. It exhibits a steep, curved radio spectrum at low frequencies, suggesting it is a very old remnant with an estimated age exceeding 400 Myr [arXiv:2405.09384].

sources.summary: Source F4 is identified as a newly detected, steep-spectrum remnant radio galaxy candidate in Abell 2256, likely older than 400 Myr with a possible host MCG+13-12-020.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, a new source was detected below the F complex which complicates the scenario once more. We have named this source F4. The spectrum of F4 remains curved below 100 MHz, with a spectral index of _ 23 ^ 144 =-1.9 0.1 , indicating that we have not yet found the break frequency of this source, but constrain it to be <23 MHz.

Given the steep spectrum of the source at such low frequencies, it is likely that source F4 is a very old remnant radio galaxy with an age of >400 Myr. The dense and turbulent intracluster medium possibly quenched the expansion of source F4, limiting adiabatic losses and allowing the low-frequency detection of such an old source Murgia2011 .

For source F4 the simple continuous injection model does not fully work, because it would imply an injection index of _ inj =0.5-1.9 0.1=-1.4 0.1 , which is much steeper than typical injection indices ( >-1 ). Thus we are likely observing the exponential steepening of the spectrum of F4, implying relativistic particles are not continuously injected.

gmw_00007463.name: NGC 2410
gmw_00007463.overview: NGC 2410 is a Seyfert 2 active galaxy with a narrow-line region radius of 6.3 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1408.5888]. Its global emission is dominated by a combination of star formation and active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1408.5888].

sources.summary: NGC 2410 has line emission dominated by star-formation plus Seyfert 2 AGN activity; its global spectrum appears star-forming, and its AGN narrow-line region radius is measured.

quotes.quote: 100 percent of the mixing sequence spaxels in NGC 2410 and NGC 6394 have EW_ H > 3 ; confirming that the emission from these galaxies is dominated by a combination of star-formation and Seyfert 2 AGN activity.

Although both NGC 6394 and NGC 2410 are clearly classified as Seyfert 2 hosts within a 4 kpc nuclear aperture, their globally integrated spectra place them beneath the Ke01 theoretical upper bound to pure star-formation in both the \ and \ vs. \ diagnostic diagrams.
The AGN NLRs of NGC 6394 and NGC 2410 have radii of 6.3 kpc (see Table table:table1 ).

gmw_00007464.name: NGC 6394
gmw_00007464.overview: NGC 6394 is a galaxy with line emission dominated by a combination of star formation and Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1408.5888]. Although its globally integrated spectrum appears star-forming, its nuclear region hosts an active galactic nucleus with a narrow-line region radius of 6.3 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1408.5888].

sources.summary: NGC 6394 has line emission dominated by star-formation plus Seyfert 2 AGN activity; its global spectrum appears star-forming, and its AGN narrow-line region radius is measured.

quotes.quote: 100 percent of the mixing sequence spaxels in NGC 2410 and NGC 6394 have EW_ H > 3 ; confirming that the emission from these galaxies is dominated by a combination of star-formation and Seyfert 2 AGN activity.

Although both NGC 6394 and NGC 2410 are clearly classified as Seyfert 2 hosts within a 4 kpc nuclear aperture, their globally integrated spectra place them beneath the Ke01 theoretical upper bound to pure star-formation in both the \ and \ vs. \ diagnostic diagrams.
The AGN NLRs of NGC 6394 and NGC 2410 have radii of 6.3 kpc (see Table table:table1 ).

gmw_00007465.name: IC 0540
gmw_00007465.overview: IC 0540 is classified as a Seyfert-LINER active galaxy [arXiv:1408.5888]. It has a low rate of contamination from diffuse ionized gas or post-AGB stars, and its narrow-line region has a minimum estimated size of 0.47 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1408.5888].

sources.summary: IC 0540 is a Seyfert-LINER CALIFA galaxy with low DIG/pAGB contamination but no clean AGN-free spaxels; the paper derives a lower limit on its narrow-line region size.

quotes.quote: In IC 0540, more than 90 percent of the spaxels have EW_ H > 3 ; indicating that the possible rate of contamination from DIG/pAGB emission is low.

We cannot numerically calibrate the mixing sequences of NGC 6762 and IC 0540 to calculate AGN fractions because these galaxies do not have any spaxels which can be conclusively identified as having no contamination from AGN activity.
We can use the largest radius at which we observe , , \ and \ as a lower limit on the size of the NLR for IC 0540, which we find to be 0.47 kpc.

gmw_00007466.name: M18
gmw_00007466.overview: M18 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.929 [arXiv:1209.1484]. Rest-frame ultraviolet spectroscopy shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity, and the galaxy has a weak potential CO emission line with a strict upper limit on its molecular gas luminosity [arXiv:1209.1484].

sources.summary: The paper treats M18 as a non-lensed z=2.929 LBG with weak CO evidence and an upper limit on its molecular gas luminosity.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies of this study (M23 and M18) were originally optically selected ( U_ n , G, R with R < 25.5 ) by Steidel et al. (2003) in the GOODS-N field. Rest-frame ultraviolet (UV) spectroscopy - which was also used to ascertain the absence of AGN signatures (i.e. strong high-ionization emission lines) - provided redshifts, z_ spec = 3.216 and z_ spec = 2.929 , respectively, determined from Ly- (among other) absorption lines.

We searched for CO emission at the position of the LBG targets (M23, RA: 189.2612457, DEC: 62.2405968 and M18, RA: 189.1837769, DEC: 62.2196693) and detected possible emission lines at S/N = 4.0 and 3.3 for M23 and M18, respectively. The first is exactly at phase center while the latter reaches 3.6 if we allow for a small offset of 1.4 '' (only 20 of the synthesized beam). In both cases this peak emission is within the velocity range allowed by the 1 optical redshift uncertainties of 600 km s ^ 1 and 1000 km s ^ 1 respectively, for M23 and M18.
For M18, we only place a 4 upper limit of < 2.2 10 ^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 (assuming a FWHM of 400 km s ^ 1 ), as the detected signal is weak, with a spurious probability of 3 10^ 2 .

gmw_00007467.name: LAE1
gmw_00007467.overview: LAE1 was initially identified in the COSMOS field as a bright Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy candidate at a redshift of approximately 7.7 [arXiv:1402.3604]. However, subsequent spectroscopic observations failed to detect the expected Lyman-alpha line emission, ruling out the candidate at a high confidence level [arXiv:1402.3604].

sources.summary: The paper follows up LAE1, the brightest z=7.7 KRUG12 LAE candidate, and rules out its expected Ly emission as likely artifact or noise.

quotes.quote: The two targets of our study are among the brightest LAE candidates at z 7.7 (12.1 and 8.6 10 ^ 18 erg/s/cm ^ 2 , respectively, measured in UNB filters assuming negligible continuum). These targets were initially selected and published by KRUG12 and throughout this work we refer to these as LAE1 (brightest) and LAE2 (second brightest), respectively. Both LAEs were detected with an ultra narrow band (UNB) filter in the COSMOS field SCOVILLE07 using NEWFIRM AUTRY03 .

We observed the two LAE candidates ( = 10 ^ h 00 ^ m 46 ^ s .94, = +02 ^ 08 48.84 and = 10 ^ h 00 ^ m 20 ^ s .52, = +02 ^ 18 50.04 ) with the MOSFIRE MCLEAN12 spectrograph on the Keck-I telescope on the nights of January 15 16, 2013. Each candidate was observed with a separate mask created using the MOSFIRE Automatic GUI-based Mask Application (MAGMA http://www2.keck.hawaii.edu/inst/mosfire/magma.html , version 1.1) and aligned using bright 2MASS stars. The conditions were photometric on both nights, with an average seeing around 1.0 . The observations were carried out in Y band ( 9710 - 11250 ) using the YJ grating and a 0.7 slit width resulting in a resolution R 3270 . We used 180s exposures with 16 Multiple Correlated Double Samples. The telescope was nodded by 1.25 with respect to the mask center position between exposures. The total integration times were 46 180 s = 8280 s = 2.3 h for LAE1 and 40 180 s = 7200 s = 2.0 h for LAE2, respectively. Before creating the mask, we verified that the 2MASS, COSMOS, and NEWFIRM astrometric systems agreed to within measurable errors ( 0.1 ). During the observations we make sure that the masks were properly aligned by using either alignment stars and/or bright filler targets. In addition several bright sources with known fluxes and morphologies from the zCOSMOS-bright spectroscopic survey LILLY09 were placed on the mask to verify slit losses (estimated to be 40-50 ). We observed 12 and 4 of these galaxies in the LAE1 and LAE2 masks, respectively. The comparison of the expected spatial position from MAGMA to the final spatial position on the reduced 2D spectra indicates that the alignment was better than 0.2 during the observations. We used the public MOSFIRE python data reduction pipeline N. Konidaris, https://code.google.com/p/mosfire/ for sky subtraction, wavelength calibration, and co-addition of the single exposures. The pipeline performs an A--B / B--A subtraction and co-adds the single exposures using a sigma-clipped noise weighted mean after shifting them to a common pixel frame and masking bad pixels. The atmospheric OH sky lines are used for wavelength calibration. The final 2D spectra have a spatial resolution of 0.18 per pixel and a spectral resolution of 1.09 per pixel. Figure fig:Slit shows the final 2D spectra (degraded to R 1500 ) together with the slit positions on sky. We measured an RMS noise of 5-10 10^ 19 erg/s/cm ^ 2 (4.4 resolution element) in the 10545-10565 wavelength region, in good agreement with the estimated noise from the the MOSFIRE exposure time calculator ETC version 2.3 by G. C. Rudie, http://www2.keck.hawaii.edu/inst/mosfire/etc.html , corrected for our estimated slit losses. Absolute flux was measured using the white dwarf spectrophotometric standard star GD71. The standard star was observed during the same nights with identical settings and reduced in the same way as the science exposures. We present the sensitivity curve for the MOSFIRE Y band in Figure fig:Sensitivity together with the line fluxes of the two targets derived from UNB filters. This shows that we would have clearly detected the two LAEs as it is further discussed below. Tests and Simulations: Establishing our detection limits Assuming the observed fluxes given in KRUG12 at 10560 and based on our measured noise and our seeing of 1 , we expect to detect the two sources at a signal-to-noise of 12.4 and 8.2, respectively, with a line width of 200km/s (e.g., HU10 ). Even with a seeing as bad as 2 , the expected signal-to-noise is still 8.8 and 5.8, respectively. To verify the SNR calculation and lack of detection, we simulated the expected 2D spectra by adding lines to the reduced 2D spectra. For these simulations we assumed the total measured flux was distributed over a truncated gaussian with a rest-frame FWHM ranging from 0.5 - 3.0 (observed from stacked spectra it is 1.5 , e.g., HU10 ). For the spatial extent we assume a gaussian with FWHM of 1 corresponding to our seeing. The following results of our simulation are not sensitive to the actual spatial extent. We find that the total flux of such a line would have to be less than 2 - 4 10^ 18 erg/s/cm ^ 2 for not to be visible in our data (for the range in rest-frame line FWHM). Vice versa, to miss LAE1 (LAE2) in our data, we would require a rest-frame FWHM of more than 10 (7 ). Figure fig:Slit shows the simulated spectra rescaled to R=1500 assuming the line fluxes measured by KRUG12 , a line rest-frame FWHM of 1.5 , and spatial extent of 1 . This shows a clear expected detection of both Ly- lines. No detection of Ly in LAE1 and LAE2 Our firm non-detection of line emission in the targeted LAEs yields an upper limit in Ly line flux of 2 - 4 10^ 18 erg/s/cm ^ 2 . We therefore rule these candidates out on a 7 and 5 level, respectively.

gmw_00007468.name: LAE2
gmw_00007468.overview: LAE2 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate located in the COSMOS field [arXiv:1402.3604]. It was initially identified as the second-brightest Lyman-alpha emitting candidate at a redshift of approximately 7.7, though subsequent spectroscopic observations failed to detect its expected Lyman-alpha line emission [arXiv:1402.3604].

sources.summary: The paper follows up LAE2, the second-brightest z=7.7 KRUG12 LAE candidate, and rules out its expected Ly emission as likely artifact or noise.

gmw_00007469.name: Columba I
gmw_00007469.overview: Columba I is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy that exhibits a large, clear metallicity spread [arXiv:1805.07350]. This chemical variation and its relatively large size support its classification as a galaxy rather than a globular cluster [arXiv:1805.07350].

sources.summary: Columba I has a measured metallicity spread supporting a galaxy classification, but its distance and orbital pole make former LMC membership unlikely.

quotes.quote: Also for Columba I we measure a clear metallicity spread. The orbital pole of Phoenix II is well constrained and close to that of the LMC, suggesting a prior association. The uncertainty on the orbital poles of the other systems are presently very large, so that an association cannot be excluded, apart from Columba I.

The measurement for Col I (0.71 dex) is more robust since the two brightest certain members have a clearly different metallicity, [ Fe/H ] = 3.36 and -2.22. The large metallicity spread appears to indicate that Col I is a galaxy; this hypothesis is further supported by its rather large size Carlin_17 , that makes it unlikely to be a globular cluster.

Since Col I is located outside the main track of LMC debris and has an unfitting large distance for its location, we deem it as very unlikely that it is a former member of the LMC cohort of satellites.

sources.summary: The paper measures Columba I proper motion from high-probability members and finds consistency with a VLT/FLAMES spectroscopy-based DR2 measurement.

quotes.quote: Columba I has 5 members with high probability and 2 with medium probability. However, both medium probability members are below the stellar locus and are therefore likely non-members. If we calculate the proper motion with the 5 high probability members, we get _ = 0.08 0.21 mas yr ^ 1 , _ = -0.11 0.28 mas yr ^ 1 , which is still consistent to our results with the mixture model in Table table:pm . Fritz2018arXiv180507350F presents spectroscopic and astrometric data of Columba I using DR2 and VLT/FLAMES spectroscopy. They find 8 potential members, considering 4 as confident members and 4 as potential members.

gmw_00007470.name: Eridanus III
gmw_00007470.overview: Eridanus III is a stellar system that has been classified as a star cluster [arXiv:1806.02345]. Its proper motion overlaps with models of Large Magellanic Cloud satellite accretion and suggests it may be co-orbiting in the Vast Polar Structure [arXiv:1806.02345].

sources.summary: The paper reports a low-significance Eridanus III proper-motion detection based mainly on one bright RGB star and two BHB stars.

quotes.quote: Eridanus III has the lowest significance with what we consider a `detection'. It has 4 members with high probability (including 2 BHBs) and 2 with medium probability. The confidence of our detection is mainly based on the agreement in proper motion space for the brightest RGB and 2 BHB stars and the constrained satellite parameters in the posterior distribution. Interestingly, the proper motion overlaps with the Kallivayalil2018ApJ...867...19K LMC satellite accretion models and as a satellite co-orbiting in the VPOS Pawlowski2015MNRAS.453.1047P . Of the satellites with new measurements it is the only satellite considered to be a star cluster Luque2018MNRAS.478.2006L, Conn2018ApJ...852...68C .

gmw_00007471.name: Pictor I
gmw_00007471.overview: Pictor I is a metal-poor satellite system that is most likely a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1806.02345, arXiv:2112.04689]. It contains seven high-probability red giant branch member stars, which form a tight cluster in proper motion space [arXiv:1806.02345].

sources.summary: The paper clarifies the Pictor I name, reports seven high-probability RGB members, and identifies a tight proper-motion signal for a metal-poor satellite.

quotes.quote: Pictor I We note that in both discovery papers, Bechtol2015ApJ...807...50B and Koposov2015ApJ...805..130K , this satellite (DES J0443.8-5017) was incorrectly referred to as Pictoris I. This mistake was due to latin case. Pictoris is the latin genitive of Pictor, and is used to refer to stars in constellations. has 7 RGB members with high probability. The 5 brighter stars form a tight cluster in proper motion space, indicating that this is the likely signal of Pictor I. All stars are found above the stellar locus suggesting that this is a metal-poor satellite.

sources.summary: The paper groups Pictoris I with Phoenix II, noting DES discovery, unavailable velocity or metallicity data, large dwarf-like structural parameters, and no significant mass segregation.

quotes.quote: Phoenix II and Pictoris I were discovered by bechtoletal2015 and koposovetal2015 in Dark Energy Survey (DES) data. No velocity or metallicity information is available for these systems. The structural parameters that have been determined for both systems by mutlupakdiletal2018 and munozetal2018 make them larger than normal star clusters and are more typical for dwarf galaxies. Since we also do not detect significant mass segregation in both systems we conclude that they are most likely dwarf galaxies, although given the large relaxation times, a star cluster nature is not completely ruled out either by our data.

gmw_00007472.overview: The active galaxy 0215+015 is a highly luminous BL Lac object [arXiv:astro-ph/9910269] and a gamma-ray-detected blazar [arXiv:2206.13665]. It exhibits extreme radio variability over time [arXiv:2206.13665], high optical and infrared polarization [arXiv:2206.13665, arXiv:astro-ph/9910269], and a bent radio jet near its core [arXiv:2206.13665].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0215+015 as a polarized, gamma-ray-detected hybrid blazar whose radio jet bends near the core and whose apparent hotspot coincides with X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: PKS 0215+015 PKS 0215+015 shows large optical polarization 17 ; Wills1992 and Fermi LAT gamma-ray detection Abdollahi2020 . Radio observations reveal an inverted spectrum peaking above 10 GHz and extreme variability over time Torniainen2005 . VLBA observations display a one-sided jet in the west-east direction. While the lower resolution VLA images (figure  fig:0215+015_vla ) show lobe directions consistent with that of the VLBA jet, the VLA A-array image at C-band (see the top-left panel of figure  fig:0215+015_vla ) shows a bent jet that is pointed in the north-south direction near the core and is bent towards the east farther away. The apparent hotspot-like feature is resolved out in the highest resolution image, although the X-ray emission coincides with this brightness feature.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0215+015 as a luminous BL Lac with high polarization and tentative infrared color-magnitude correlations suggesting spectral flattening when bright.

quotes.quote: 0215+015, z_ abs =1.686 It is one of the most luminous ( B=14.5 19.5 Pettini et al. 1983) known BL Lac objects and polarized at P_ IR =17.4 1.1 in H band (Holmes et al. 1984) and P_ opt =20 (Angel Stockman 1980). There are only a few infrared data in the literature, which show a positive correlation between color index and magnitude suggesting the spectrum flattens when the source brightens: (H-K) = 0.08J - 0.26 with = 0.97 and p = 1.2 ; (J-K) = 0.16H - 0.36 with = 0.89 and p = 4.5 . Those color-magnitude correlations were obtained based on 4 points, so they should be confirmed with more observations. For color indices, there is also an indication of correlations between (J-K) and (J-H) and (H-K) as well with p < 5 (see Fig. 2).

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0217+0144 as a quasar and reports resolved radio structure, including opposite emission regions around the compact central component.

quotes.quote: J0217+0144 : quasar at z=1.715 . The source is slightly resolved in the NW-SE direction in the L band. At higher resolution, in the C and X bands, two opposite regions of emission with respect to the central compact component are detected (see Fig. fig:J0217+0144 ).

gmw_00007473.overview: The flat-spectrum radio source 2223-052 is a violently variable quasar located at a redshift of z = 1.404 [arXiv:2101.08291, arXiv:astro-ph/9910269]. It is a merging system featuring a host galaxy with a tidal arm and a merging companion galaxy [arXiv:2101.08291].

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C446 as a quasar host merger with a host tidal feature, a merging-galaxy component, blended velocities, and possible overlap between the galaxies.

quotes.quote: 3C446 is a quasar at z = 1.404. For this target, we identify two regions, ``N component A tidal feature" is a region belonging to the quasar host galaxy, resembling a tidal feature that is most likely induced by the merger. We measure an FWHM of 395.14 2.0 for this region. ``E-W component B" belongs to the merging galaxy, a portion of it resembles a tidal feature, counter to the tidal arm of ``N component A tidal feature." For this region, we measure a line FWHM of 558.5 63 however, it appears to be a blend of two velocity components.

It is unclear where the nucleus of the merging galaxies resides. It could be that it has already merged with that of the quasar host galaxy. The two galaxies appear to be offset by at least 500 in velocity, and there is a possibility that a portion of the merging galaxy lies on top of the quasar host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper finds that 3C 446 varied strongly in flux density and spectral index, had mostly simultaneous multi-frequency lightcurves, and showed red-noise, random-walk-like periodograms.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a time series analysis of the long-term radio lightcurves of four blazars: 3C 279, 3C 345, 3C 446, and BL Lacertae. We exploit the data base of the University of Michigan Radio Astronomy Observatory (UMRAO) monitoring program which provides densely sampled lightcurves spanning 32 years in time in three frequency bands located at 4.8, 8, and 14.5 GHz.

3C 446 During the entire observing time this source shows strong variability in both flux density -- with values ranging from 3 Jy to 10 Jy (at 14.5 GHz) -- and spectral index -- with values fluctuating between 0.5 and 0.3. According to our criteria ( ssect_timelag ) we divided the lightcurves into three phases, ranging from 1980 to 1986 (phase A), 1986 to 1995 (phase B), and from 1995 onward (phase C). Whereas in phases A and B the lightcurves of all three frequencies are consistent with being simultaneous, we find a time lag of =0.67^ +0.5 _ 0.51 years ( 3 confidence interval) for the pair 4.8/14.5 GHz. The periodograms of all three lightcurves are consistent with being pure red-noise spectra.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2223-052 as 3C446, a prototype violently variable quasar with optical outbursts, polarization, and an infrared outburst.

quotes.quote: 2223-052, 3C446 , ( z_ em =1.404) The flat spectrum radio source is the prototype of the class of violently variable quasars (Bregman et al. 1986b). During the optical outbursts, the spectral index shows complex dependence on the brightness, sometimes the slope of the optical continuous is steeper (Sandage et al. 1966 ) and sometimes flatter (Visvanathan 1973) than during quiescent states. On occasions, week emission lines have been observed in the optical region, the intensities of which do not change with the spectral flux (Sandage et al 1966, see also Garilli Tagliferri 1986). Polarizations of P_ opt =4 17.3 (Impey Tapia 1990), P_ IR =16 (Impey et al. 1982) and P_ 10 GHz =4 (Mead et al. 1990) and an optical variation of m=5.0 (Branly et al. 1996) are reported. The infrared light curves shows a clear outburst, after which the brightness decrease rapidly by about 4 mag in H.

gmw_00007474.name: NGC 4123
gmw_00007474.overview: NGC 4123 is a modest-sized barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0008205, arXiv:astro-ph/0703615] featuring a fast-rotating bar with straight dust lanes [arXiv:astro-ph/0008205, arXiv:astro-ph/0008204]. The galaxy contains an untruncated stellar disk with star-forming regions located well outside the main disk radius [arXiv:astro-ph/0008204].

sources.summary: The paper models gas flow in the barred spiral NGC 4123, finding a high-mass stellar disk and fast bar while deriving a dark halo less concentrated than cosmological simulations predict.

quotes.quote: We use fluid-dynamical models of gas flow in the barred galaxy NGC 4123 to constrain the dynamical properties of the galaxy: disk , bar pattern speed, and the central density and scale radius of the dark halo. We derive a realistic barred potential directly from the light distribution. For each model we assume a value of the stellar \ and a bar pattern speed \ and add a dark halo to fit the rotation curve. We then compute the gas flow velocities with a 2-D gas dynamical code, and compare the model flow patterns to a 2-D velocity field derived from Fabry-Perot observations. The strong shocks and non-circular motions in the observed gas flow require a high stellar \ and a fast-rotating bar.

These results contradict some recent claimed ``universal'' galaxy disk/halo relations, since NGC 4123 is of modest size (rotation curve maximum 145 , and V_ flat =130 ) yet is quite disk-dominated. The dark halo of NGC 4123 is less concentrated than favored by current models of dark halos based on cosmological simulations.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4123 as a barred disk galaxy with strong non-circular motions, an extended untruncated stellar disk, bar shocks, and a modeled disk-halo mass decomposition.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present photometric and kinematic observations of NGC 4123, a barred galaxy of modest size ( V_ rot = 130 , L = 0.7 L_* ), which reveal strong non-circular motions. The bar has straight dust lanes and an inner Lindblad resonance. The disk of NGC 4123 has no sign of truncation out to 10 scale lengths, and star-forming regions are found well outside R_ 25 . A Fabry-Perot \ velocity field shows velocity jumps of >100 \ at the location of the dust lanes within the bar, indicating shocks in the gas flow. VLA observations yield the velocity field of the \ disk.

Axisymmetric mass models yield good fits to the rotation curve outside the bar regionfor disk I band \ of 2.25 or less, and dark halos with either isothermal or power-law profiles can fit the data well.

sources.summary: NGC 4123 provides the observed SBc-bar dust-lane benchmark: its color map, HST profile, and inferred extinction are used to scale the model dust attenuation.

quotes.quote: It is therefore crucial to select systems with well constrained PAs when applying the TW method to real late-type galaxies. wei01 presented a B-I color map of the SBc galaxy NGC 4123 that clearly delineates the dust lanes on the leading edges of its bar. They found that B-I = 2.3 in the dust lanes. With the extinction law of rl85 this colour implies an average extinction in the dust lanes of A_V = 2.7 magnitude. We checked this value using an HST archival WFPC2 F450W band image of NGC 4123 (SNAP-9042 PI: Smartt) to compare a surface brightness profile across the dust lane (i.e. parallel to the bar minor axis) in the HST image to a similarly placed slit in model TW1.
The model profile is scaled ad hoc to match the peak in the NGC 4123 data: the resulting profile fits the observed one remarkably well.

gmw_00007475.name: z_ abs = 2.06 sub-DLA towards the QSO J2123 0050
aliases_local: z_ abs = 2.06 sub-DLA towards the QSO J2123 0050
aliases_external: z_abs=2.06 sub-DLA sightline toward SDSS J212329.46-005052.9
gmw_00007475.overview: The high-redshift galaxy absorber at redshift 2.06, located along the line of sight to the quasar J2123-0050, exhibits a complex multi-phase structure with neutral, singly, and multiply ionized species [arXiv:1007.0014]. The background quasar itself is marginally resolved in CO emission and is accompanied by two bright, spatially aligned companion galaxies [arXiv:2304.02041].

sources.original_name: z_ abs = 2.06 sub-DLA towards the QSO J2123 0050
sources.summary: The z=2.06 sub-DLA toward QSO J2123 0050 is described as a high-redshift, multi-phase galaxy absorber with complex kinematics and significant ionization corrections.

quotes.quote: We present a high resolution (FWHM =2.7 km s ^ 1 ), high S/N echelle spectrum for the z_ em = 2.26 QSO J2123 0050 and determine elemental abundances for the z_ abs = 2.06 sub-DLA in its line of sight. This high redshift sub-DLA has a complex kinematic structure and harbours detections of neutral ( S i , C i ), singly (e.g. C ii , S ii ) and multiply ionized (e.g. C iv , Si iv ) species as well as molecular H _2 and HD. The plethora of detected transitions in various ionization stages is indicative of a complex multi-phase structure present in this high redshift galaxy.

We demonstrate that the ionization corrections in this sub-DLA are significant (up to 0.7 dex). For example, if no ionization correction is applied, a super-solar metallicity is derived ( [ S/H ] = +0.36 ), whereas a single phase ionization correction reduces this to [ S/H ] = 0.19 .
After ionization corrections are applied the ISM of this galaxy appears to be similar to some of the sightlines through the Milky Way.

sources.summary: The paper targets the luminous z=2.279 quasar SDSS J212329.47--005052.9, using spectroastrometry to constrain its BLR size, kinematics, and black-hole mass limit.

quotes.quote: We observed the luminous quasar SDSS J212329.47--005052.9 at z=2.279 with the aim of resolving its 100 as H broad emission-line region (BLR), and present the first SA constraints on the size and kinematic structure of the BLR. Using a novel pipeline to extract the SA signal and reliable uncertainties, we achieved a centroiding precision of 100 as , or >2000 smaller than the K band AO-corrected PSF, yielding a tentative 3.2 detection of an SA signal from the BLR. Modeling the BLR emission as arising from an inclined rotating disk with a mixture of coherent and random motions we constrain r_ BLR =454^ +565 _ 162 as ( 3.71^ +4.65 _ 1.28 pc ), providing a 95 confidence upper limit on the black hole mass 1.8 10^9 M _ .

The chosen quasar SDSS J212329.47--005052.9 (abbreviated as J2123--0050 in the following) is among the brightest quasars known at this redshift with a bolometric luminosity of = 8.4e47 Hamann11,Rakshit20_SDSS_DR14 and magnitudes of r_ AB = 16.4 Abazajian09_SDSS_DR7 and K = 13.9 Schneider10_SDSS_DR7 . From the SDSS data release 14 Rakshit20_SDSS_DR14 , we extract a redshift of z = 2.274 for J2123--0050, while we estimate a redshift of z = 2.279 based on the line centroid of the emission line in the combined spectra (see Figure fig:spectrum ), which we will henceforth take to be the systemic redshift.

sources.summary: Q2123 0050 is marginally resolved in CO, has two aligned bright CO companions, and its blue-side companion shows extended CO across 60 kpc.

quotes.quote: Q2123 0050 : This QSO is marginally resolved in CO(4-3), but there are also two bright companions (CO emitters) well aligned with the QSO both spatially and in velocity. Both of them are located at a projected distance of 56 kpc from the QSO. Fig. fig:spectrum2 shows that the blue-side companion (i.e., the companion with lower CO velocities) reveals extended CO emission across 60 kpc in the EW direction. The flux of the tapered spectrum of the blue-side companion (0.63 0.09 Jy ) is roughly 62 higher than that of the untapered spectrum (0.39 0.04 Jy ). The continuum image taken with the ALMA 12m array reveals two unresolved point sources associated with the QSO and red-side companion.

gmw_00007476.overview: The active galaxy 1511+0518 is classified as a Seyfert I galaxy [arXiv:1111.3710] and a compact symmetric object [arXiv:1111.3710, arXiv:2312.13418] with a young radio structure [arXiv:2312.13418]. It is a Compton-thick source [arXiv:2002.12870] exhibiting high intrinsic X-ray absorption [arXiv:2312.13418] and an unusually high mid-infrared luminosity [arXiv:2002.12870].

sources.summary: The paper treats 1511 + 0518 as a CSO with an unusually hard X-ray photon index and a possible Compton-thick interpretation, but the data cannot distinguish competing models.

quotes.quote: 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518 The absorbed power-law model applied to two CSOs, 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518, resulted in an unusually hard photon index of = 0.8 0.3 and = 1.0 0.2 respectively. Typically, such a low value indicates a strong nuclear obscuration able to cover completely the source of the primary X-ray radiation. The observed spectrum is then dominated by a Compton reflection hump (and an accompanying neutral iron line) from a distant material, presumably a molecular torus, resulting in an artificially low photon index. AGN with these properties are called `Compton thick', and the amount of the hydrogen absorbing column required to distort the X-ray spectrum in this manner is N_H 10^ 24 e.g., comastri2004 .

In 1511 + 0518 the Compton thick model results in Cstat/d.o.f. = 198.2/443, N_H^z = 3.8^ +4.0 _ 1.3 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , = 3.8^ +0.3 _ 0.4 and L_X 3 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . On the other hand, we do not see any evidence for the presence of a neutral iron line in the spectra of 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518. The line is unconstrained in 1511 + 0518.

Unfortunately, the quality of our present Chandra data is not sufficient to discriminate between the Compton thick absorption and X-ray lobe emission scenarios for X-ray emission in 2021 + 614 and 1511 + 0518 (only 54 and 49 counts collected in 5 ksec and 2 ksec \ observations, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper confirms J1511+0518 as a Seyfert-associated CSO, resolving two hot spots and a central core and measuring marginal hotspot motion in VLBI data.

quotes.quote: CSO source J1511+0518 is associated with a Seyfert I galaxy at a redshift of 0.084 (1 mas = 1.527 pc; Chavushyan et al. 2001). It displays typical CSO morphological and spectral characteristics: the source is resolved into two hot spots A and C separated by 4.8 mas (or 7.3 pc) in East-West direction at 2.3 and 5.0 GHz, while the higher-resolution VLBI images at 8.4 and 15.4 GHz reveal a central component B between the two lobes Figure fig:j1511 : the current paper; Paper II; OD08 . A and C show convex spectra with a peak at about 8 and 5 GHz, respectively. B exhibits a rising spectrum that probably breaks at 15 GHz and identifies B as the core of the CSO Paper II; OD08 .
The hot spot A shows a marginal proper motion of 7.0 3.3 as yr ^ 1 (or 0.038 0.018 ) to the East. It is in agreement with the previous report of hotspot-hotspot expansion velocity of 0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper models J1511+0518 as a young, compact, X-ray-obscured CSO/AGN whose SED is matched by equipartition radio-lobe models with low UV luminosity and high ISM density.

quotes.quote: With this picture in mind, we model the broad-band spectral energy distributions (SEDs) of three objects: J1511+0518, OQ+208 (also known as 1404+286 or Mrk 668), and 2021+614, to quantitatively explore the validity of the above expectation. These objects were selected from a sample of compact symmetric objects CSOs, a subset of AGNs known for their compact, young radio structures, see e.g., ODea98,ODea21,Kiehlmann23a,Kiehlmann23b,Readhead23 considered in based on their distinctive X-ray spectral features derived based on extensive observations by , XMM- Newton , and NuSTAR see further denoted as S19 and S23 respectively Sobolewska19b,Sobolewska23 . Key features are a high intrinsic X-ray absorption (equivalent hydrogen column densities, N_ H > 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ), as well as a narrow fluorescent Fe K line and the broad Compton reflection component indicating a reflection from a toroidal obscurer.

J1511+0518 The SED and best model are shown in Fig. fig:SED_J1511 . Models that could match the observed SED in both the radio and the X-ray domains, were the equipartition models U_e/U_B 1 (i.e., _e = _B = 0.75 ), with relatively low UV luminosities, L_ UV (1.0 - 3.5) L_ IR , and a relatively high ISM density, within the n_ int n_ high range, as presented in Figure fig:SED_J1511 , and summarized in Table tab:SEDres . Models with lower ISM densities, n_0 < n_ int , or departing from the equipartition condition, U_e > U_B , generally over-predicted the X-ray flux, unless the disk UV luminosity was set to be very low, L_ UV L_ IR , the possibility which we deem as unlikely.

sources.summary: 1511+0518 is identified as a Compton-thick compact radio galaxy with quasar-like WISE colors and unusually high MIR luminosity relative to its intrinsic X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: Only in a few cases, subjected to deeper exposures (1404+286, 1511+0518 and 2021+614), was there a detection of the neutral fluorescence iron line and/or hydrogen column densities in excess of 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , indicating the disk coronal emission is seen through a Compton-thick circumnuclear tori Guainazzi04,Siemiginowska16,Sobolewska19a,Sobolewska19b .

Interestingly enough, all the objects in the sample confirmed as Compton-thick, based on the X-ray spectroscopy 1404+286, 1511+0518 and 2021+614; see respectively Siemiginowska16,Sobolewska19a,Sobolewska19b , are characterized by the WISE colors as being consistent with a quasar identification, as in fact expected.

On the related note, we comment that in the case of z<0.4 young radio galaxies, the only ones with L_ 12 m exceeding 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , are the three Compton-thick objects 1404+286, 1511+0518 and 2021+614, and the peculiar 1345+125, which can also be classified as Ultraluminous Infrared Galaxies see Rodriguez09 . Those four sources appear in fact over-luminous in MIR for their given intrinsic (absorption-corrected) X-ray luminosities when compared to the general local AGN population, as well as other local GPS/CSOs (see in this context the right panel in Figure fig:redshift ).

gmw_00007477.name: DF4
gmw_00007477.overview: DF4 is a low-surface-brightness galaxy classified as an ultra-diffuse galaxy [arXiv:1610.01609]. It appears empty in high-resolution Hubble Space Telescope imaging and exhibits a boomerang-like shape that may indicate it is undergoing tidal stripping [arXiv:1610.01609].

sources.summary: DF4 is one of four unresolved Dragonfly low-surface-brightness galaxies classified as UDGs; it appears empty in HST imaging and may have a boomerang-like, tidally stripped morphology.

quotes.quote: However, the remaining four (DF4, DF5, DF6 and DF7) are, at first inspection, undetected in our HST imaging. The differences between the resolved and unresolved galaxies is illustrated in Figure ptsourcefig . We select DF3 and DF4 as example galaxies, as they have nearly identical central surface brightness and size (Table paramstable ). Despite this, when we examine the images at full HST resolution and the associated distributions of point sources detected with DOLPHOT (from Danieli et al.), DF3 emerges as a clear overdensity while DF4 appears to be an empty image. These two galaxies are representative of the differences between the two subsets of galaxies. Only after binning and smoothing can we infer the presence of large, diffuse objects in the images of DF4-DF7 although DF5-DF7 are better visually described as large scale background variations than obvious objects (see Figure hstfig ).

The CFHT images of the four galaxies are shown in Figure cutoutscfht . All four objects are clearly detected, and a comparison between Figures cutoutsdf and cutoutscfht shows that their morphologies are consistent between the two datasets.
One galaxy (DF4) has a boomerang-like shape, and is possibly undergoing tidal stripping.

gmw_00007478.name: DF5
gmw_00007478.overview: DF5 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy that is the largest and roundest of its local sample [arXiv:1610.01609]. Neutral hydrogen detections confirm that DF5 lies in the background of M101, making its physical association with the NGC 5485 group feasible [arXiv:1911.09694].

sources.summary: DF5 is part of the unresolved Dragonfly UDG set near NGC 5485; it is clearly detected in CFHT imaging and described as the largest and roundest of the sample.

quotes.quote: However, the remaining four (DF4, DF5, DF6 and DF7) are, at first inspection, undetected in our HST imaging. The differences between the resolved and unresolved galaxies is illustrated in Figure ptsourcefig . We select DF3 and DF4 as example galaxies, as they have nearly identical central surface brightness and size (Table paramstable ). Despite this, when we examine the images at full HST resolution and the associated distributions of point sources detected with DOLPHOT (from Danieli et al.), DF3 emerges as a clear overdensity while DF4 appears to be an empty image. These two galaxies are representative of the differences between the two subsets of galaxies. Only after binning and smoothing can we infer the presence of large, diffuse objects in the images of DF4-DF7 although DF5-DF7 are better visually described as large scale background variations than obvious objects (see Figure hstfig ).

The CFHT images of the four galaxies are shown in Figure cutoutscfht . All four objects are clearly detected, and a comparison between Figures cutoutsdf and cutoutscfht shows that their morphologies are consistent between the two datasets.
The largest, DF5, is the roundest of the sample; the remaining two (DF6 and DF7) are elongated.

sources.summary: The paper detects HI from DF-5, confirms it lies behind M101, and argues that its velocity and projected separation make association with NGC 5485 feasible.

quotes.quote: We estimate kinematic distances, D_ HI , for DF-5, DwB, dw1408+56, and Dw26 using of our detections and assuming H_ 0 =70 Mpc ^ 1 . The value of D_ HI for DF-5 is consistent with the lower limits on D_ opt from M16 and C19.

DF-5 was suggested to be a member of the NGC 5485 group since HST imaging does not resolve any stars (M16), and our HI detection confirms that it is in the background of M101 with V_ sys =1424 . While this differs by 500 from that of the group Galaxygroups2massTully , the small projected separation between DF-5 and NGC 5485 implies that a physical association is nonetheless feasible.
We find that the remaining 3 LSB dwarfs with HI detections (DF-5, DwB, and dw1408+56) are likely members of the background NGC 5485 group.

gmw_00007479.name: DF6
gmw_00007479.overview: DF6 is an extreme low-surface-brightness galaxy that was originally identified as an unresolved Dragonfly object [arXiv:1610.01609]. Although it is not significantly detected in Hubble Space Telescope imaging, observations with the Canada-France-Hawaii Telescope confirm its detection and reveal an elongated morphology [arXiv:1610.01609].

sources.summary: DF6 is one of the unresolved Dragonfly galaxies classified as an extreme low-surface-brightness UDG; HST raises detection concerns, but CFHT confirms it and the paper describes it as elongated.

quotes.quote: Only after binning and smoothing can we infer the presence of large, diffuse objects in the images of DF4-DF7 although DF5-DF7 are better visually described as large scale background variations than obvious objects (see Figure hstfig ). Figure cutoutsdf displays greyscale images of all four unresolved galaxies (as observed with Dragonfly); color images of the central 100 arcsec of each field, created from HST F606W and F814W data, are shown in Figure cutoutshst . CFHT t The fact that these four galaxies do not resolve into stars raises the question of whether our detection algorithm picked up noise peaks or artifacts in the Dragonfly data. This is particularly a concern for DF6 and DF7, which are not significantly detected with HST even after binning and smoothing.

The CFHT images of the four galaxies are shown in Figure cutoutscfht . All four objects are clearly detected, and a comparison between Figures cutoutsdf and cutoutscfht shows that their morphologies are consistent between the two datasets.
The largest, DF5, is the roundest of the sample; the remaining two (DF6 and DF7) are elongated.

gmw_00007480.name: DF7
gmw_00007480.overview: DF7 is an extreme low-surface-brightness galaxy that was originally identified as an unresolved Dragonfly object [arXiv:1610.01609]. Although difficult to detect in some space-based imaging, its existence is confirmed by ground-based observations that reveal an elongated morphology [arXiv:1610.01609].

sources.summary: DF7 is one of the unresolved Dragonfly galaxies classified as an extreme low-surface-brightness UDG; HST raises detection concerns, but CFHT confirms it and the paper describes it as elongated.

quotes.quote: Only after binning and smoothing can we infer the presence of large, diffuse objects in the images of DF4-DF7 although DF5-DF7 are better visually described as large scale background variations than obvious objects (see Figure hstfig ). Figure cutoutsdf displays greyscale images of all four unresolved galaxies (as observed with Dragonfly); color images of the central 100 arcsec of each field, created from HST F606W and F814W data, are shown in Figure cutoutshst . CFHT t The fact that these four galaxies do not resolve into stars raises the question of whether our detection algorithm picked up noise peaks or artifacts in the Dragonfly data. This is particularly a concern for DF6 and DF7, which are not significantly detected with HST even after binning and smoothing.

The CFHT images of the four galaxies are shown in Figure cutoutscfht . All four objects are clearly detected, and a comparison between Figures cutoutsdf and cutoutscfht shows that their morphologies are consistent between the two datasets.
The largest, DF5, is the roundest of the sample; the remaining two (DF6 and DF7) are elongated.

gmw_00007481.name: PLCKG287+32-2013
gmw_00007481.overview: PLCKG287+32-2013 is one of the brightest known galaxy candidates at a redshift of approximately 7 [arXiv:2009.00020]. The galaxy is experiencing an ongoing burst of star formation and exhibits strong oxygen and hydrogen emission lines [arXiv:2009.00020].

sources.summary: PLCKG287+32-2013 is highlighted as a bright z~7 candidate with elevated Spitzer 3.6 micron flux, strong inferred [OIII]+H emission, and an ongoing burst of star formation.

quotes.quote: We find that PLCKG287+32-2013, one of the brightest z 7 candidates known (F160W mag 24.9), has strong evidence for strong [ OIII ] +H- emission (EW 1000 ), as suggested by its elevated Spitzer [ 3.6 ] flux. We believe that this galaxy is experiencing an ongoing burst of star formation.

The PLCKG287+32-2013 IRAC color corresponds to rest-frame equivalent widths of 1000 . \ Compared to galaxies observed at z=0.2-0.9 where [ OIII ] +H- equivalent widths are commonly observed to be <100 and extreme emission line galaxies (EELGs) are defined to be anything above that Mainali2020,Amorin2014 , this is a relatively high EW, and in line with the extreme emitters discovered by (1000-4000 \ at z 7 ) and those discovered by (500-2000 \ at z 2-3 ).

gmw_00007482.name: ACT0102-49-2391
gmw_00007482.overview: ACT0102-49-2391 is a high-redshift galaxy located at an estimated redshift of z ~ 6.6 to 6.9 [arXiv:2009.00020]. It exhibits an elevated Spitzer 3.6 micron flux and blue IRAC colors, which suggest the presence of strong nebular emission lines [arXiv:2009.00020].

sources.summary: ACT0102-49-2391 is treated as a lower-luminosity analogue of PLCKG287+32-2013, with elevated Spitzer 3.6 micron flux at z~6.6-6.9 suggesting strong nebular emission.

quotes.quote: Objects With Potentially Strong Emission Lines Of the objects highlighted in this work, the ones with blue IRAC [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colors at z=6.8-7.0 include ACT0102-49-2391, Abell1758-1942, PLCKG287+32-698, PLCKG287+32-777, and PLCKG287+32-2013. Of these, we focus on three objects in PLCKG287+32: 2013, 698, and 777, as well as ACT0102-40-2391. We show our analysis of these objects with BAGPIPES in Figure fig:bagpipes1 : SEDs fit to the median and 1 in mass-weighted age template (note that we are now using mass-weighted age, defined in Equation eqn:mwage instead of formation age as it is more easily constrained), and the median 1 in mass-weighted age SFHs in corresponding colors, as well as all allowed SFHs in gray.

With a lower S/N detection, the range of solutions and SFHs for ACT0102-49-2391 are much dramatic. Additionally, we highlight these objects in Figure fig:irac_colors . Of the four galaxies, PLCKG287+32-2013 has the highest S/N [ 3.6 ] detection ( 9 ). EA z Y yields z = 7.3 0.3 , while BAGPIPES finds z = 6.8^ +0.2 _ 0.1 . As can be seen in Figure fig:irac_colors , both objects show more extreme (blue) IRAC colors than most of the sample. In combination with the fact that they are consistent with the expected color decrease at z 6.6-6.9 for young, star-forming galaxies, we believe these are potentially strong [ OIII ] +H- emitters.
We find a similar object, ACT0102-49-2391, which although less luminous, also reveals an elevated [ 3.6 ] flux, and falls around z 6.6-6.9 , again suggesting strong nebular emission.

gmw_00007483.name: PLCKG287+32-698
gmw_00007483.overview: PLCKG287+32-698 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z ~ 6.8 to 7.0 [arXiv:2009.00020]. It is one of the multiple images in a quadruply lensed system and exhibits a blue Spitzer IRAC color [arXiv:2009.00020].

sources.summary: PLCKG287+32-698 is discussed as one image of a quadruply lensed z~6.8-7 system, with blue IRAC color and physical properties matching PLCKG287+32-777.

quotes.quote: Objects With Potentially Strong Emission Lines Of the objects highlighted in this work, the ones with blue IRAC [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colors at z=6.8-7.0 include ACT0102-49-2391, Abell1758-1942, PLCKG287+32-698, PLCKG287+32-777, and PLCKG287+32-2013. Of these, we focus on three objects in PLCKG287+32: 2013, 698, and 777, as well as ACT0102-40-2391. We show our analysis of these objects with BAGPIPES in Figure fig:bagpipes1 : SEDs fit to the median and 1 in mass-weighted age template (note that we are now using mass-weighted age, defined in Equation eqn:mwage instead of formation age as it is more easily constrained), and the median 1 in mass-weighted age SFHs in corresponding colors, as well as all allowed SFHs in gray.

Two of these objects (PLCKG287+32-698 and 777) are multiple images in a quadruply imaged system, first discovered by . In addition to finding the same redshift for both images analyzed here, we find convincingly similar physical properties with both Methods, as can be seen in Figures fig:histograms1 and fig:histograms2 ( M_* 10^ 8-9 M_ , sSFR 10 Gyr ^ 1 ). We note the third lensed image in this system, PLCKG287+32-2235, is included in our catalog but is not significantly detected in Spitzer .

gmw_00007484.name: PLCKG287+32-777
gmw_00007484.overview: PLCKG287+32-777 is a lensed high-redshift galaxy candidate that exists as one of multiple images in a quadruply imaged system [arXiv:2009.00020]. Gemini spectroscopic observations of the galaxy detected no Lyman-alpha or carbon IV emission lines [arXiv:2009.00020].

sources.summary: PLCKG287+32-777 is a lensed high-redshift candidate with physical properties matching PLCKG287+32-698; Gemini spectra did not detect prominent Ly-alpha or CIV emission.

quotes.quote: Two of these objects (PLCKG287+32-698 and 777) are multiple images in a quadruply imaged system, first discovered by . In addition to finding the same redshift for both images analyzed here, we find convincingly similar physical properties with both Methods, as can be seen in Figures fig:histograms1 and fig:histograms2 ( M_* 10^ 8-9 M_ , sSFR 10 Gyr ^ 1 ). We note the third lensed image in this system, PLCKG287+32-2235, is included in our catalog but is not significantly detected in Spitzer .

Gemini Observations of PLCKG287+32-777 We take this opportunity to note that PLCKG287+32-777 was spectroscopically observed with Gemini South (GS-2018A-Q-901; PI: Zitrin), and we publish here the first results from these observations. It was first observed for 2 hrs with Flamingos-2 (R3000 Grism + J-band filter). A 4-pixel ( 0.72 ) wide longslit was used, leading to an average resolving power of R 1300 , with a nod size of 1.5 .
