No prominent emission line was detected in either observation for either image, suggesting that Ly- may not be easily escaping this galaxy and the presence of extremely strong CIV emission is likely ruled out.

gmw_00007485.name: Abell1763-1434
gmw_00007485.overview: Abell1763-1434 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2009.00020] located at a redshift of approximately 8 [arXiv:1905.09295]. It is a relatively massive galaxy [arXiv:1905.09295] that hosts an evolved stellar population [arXiv:2009.00020, arXiv:1905.09295], with its earliest star formation potentially beginning less than 100 million years after the Big Bang [arXiv:2009.00020, arXiv:1905.09295].

sources.summary: Abell1763-1434 is emphasized as a high-redshift candidate preferring an evolved, maximally old stellar population, with IRAC colors consistent with a Balmer break or dust.

quotes.quote: Objects Preferring Maximally Old Formation Age In addition to galaxies with potentially strong nebular emission, there are a subset of galaxies in our sample show a strong preference with Method A and some preference with Method B, for a maximally old solution, with the earliest possible formation time, including ACT0102-49-2551, RXC0911+17-143, MACS0553-33-219, and Abell1763-1434. Here we focus on Abell1763-1434 and RXC0911+17-143, both of which were initially introduced by . The former has since received deeper data ( 30 hours, from 5 hours in Strait20 ).

In the top panel of Figure fig:bagpipes2 , we show our analysis with BAGPIPES of Abell1763-1434, where we plot again median 1 mass-weighted age templates. In all cases, these templates represent relatively evolved stellar populations, reflected by the SFHs on the right panel which ``turn on" relatively early. The oldest solution prefers a gently declining SFH, while the others are relatively constant. There are some solutions that prefer a high [ OIII ] +H- EW (i.e., a young, high sSFR template rather than an evolved one with a Balmer break), which falls in IRAC ch2 at the redshift of this galaxy ( z 8.4 ), but overwhelmingly, an evolved stellar population is preferred.
We find two objects, Abell1763-1434 and RXC0911+17-143, that show evidence for an evolved stellar population, i.e., that they formed very early ( <100 Myr after the Big Bang).

sources.summary: The paper presents Abell1763-1434 as a relatively massive z about 8 galaxy with an evolved stellar population, high stellar mass and age estimates, and first star formation possibly within 100 Myr of the Big Bang.

quotes.quote: We find that Abell1763-1434 is a relatively massive galaxy with an evolved stellar population. Using the assumptions outlined in calcs , we find a median intrinsic stellar mass of 4.3 ^ +2.4 _ 2.1 10^9 M_ and median age of 510^ +60 _ 190 Myr. The distribution of the time since the Big Bang until the onset of star formation in that galaxy is shown in the rightmost panel of Figure mcmc , and implies that the oldest stars in this galaxy started forming <100 Myr after the Big Bang. Abell1763-1434 prefers the oldest possible solution the large majority of the time: 73 of solutions prefer first star formation < 100 Myr after the Big Bang, though we cannot exclude the possibility that abnormally strong nebular emission, large dust content, or some combination thereof could serve to decrease the estimated age.

gmw_00007486.name: RXC0911+17-143
gmw_00007486.overview: RXC0911+17-143 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy with photometry that suggests an early formation time [arXiv:2009.00020]. Its elevated IRAC fluxes provide evidence of a Balmer break, indicating an evolved stellar population or a dusty solution [arXiv:2009.00020].

sources.summary: RXC0911+17-143 is highlighted as a high-redshift candidate with evidence for an evolved stellar population, Balmer-break-like IRAC colors, and early formation.

quotes.quote: Objects Preferring Maximally Old Formation Age In addition to galaxies with potentially strong nebular emission, there are a subset of galaxies in our sample show a strong preference with Method A and some preference with Method B, for a maximally old solution, with the earliest possible formation time, including ACT0102-49-2551, RXC0911+17-143, MACS0553-33-219, and Abell1763-1434. Here we focus on Abell1763-1434 and RXC0911+17-143, both of which were initially introduced by . The former has since received deeper data ( 30 hours, from 5 hours in Strait20 ).

A similar story can be told for RXC0911+17-143, shown on the bottom panel of Figure fig:bagpipes2 : this time with both IRAC fluxes elevated, there is evidence of a Balmer break, although the detections are lower S/N compared to Abell1763. The SFHs reflect a similar preference for a declining or constant, but relatively stagnant SFH for this object. When these objects are compared to the rest of our sample in Figure fig:irac_colors , both are consistent with an evolved and/or dusty solution.
We find two objects, Abell1763-1434 and RXC0911+17-143, that show evidence for an evolved stellar population, i.e., that they formed very early ( <100 Myr after the Big Bang).

gmw_00007487.name: Q0953+4749
gmw_00007487.overview: The object Q0953+4749 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835]. It exhibits extended narrow Lyman-alpha emission located mostly to its south, which is redshifted by 1000 km/s relative to the quasar itself [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended narrow emission south of Q0953+4749, redshifted from the quasar, but notes the data cube is too noisy for detailed morphology or dynamics.

quotes.quote: Only the QSO \ and Ly emission lines are included in the spectral window and both are affected heavily by absorption lines in the \ forest. Hence no attempt is made to estimate the QSO redshift from the data cube. The on-band image in Fig. fig:qlya_qso_lya for Q0953+4749 corresponds to 6656--6682 . We find extended narrow \ emission at 6670 mostly towards the south of the QSO. This wavelength is redshifted by 1000 km s ^ 1 relative to the QSO redshift.

gmw_00007488.name: Q1347+112
gmw_00007488.overview: The quasar Q1347+112 is a high-redshift object [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835]. Narrow-band imaging indicates that faint extended emission appears to be present near the quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835].

sources.summary: The paper finds likely faint extended emission near Q1347+112, but warns that a bad CCD column and sky-line residuals prevent further analysis.

quotes.quote: The narrow-band image for Q1347+112 corresponds to 4466--4482 . \ emission from this QSO appears in a region of the CCD affected by a bad column, which after QSO subtraction causes a distinct structure extending over the field in a narrow-band image. This can be seen to the north-west of the QSO center in Fig. fig:qlya_qso_lya . The emission line coincides with a sky emission line from which sky-subtraction residuals can be interpreted as narrow emission lines. Nonetheless, faint extended emission appears to be present, but further analysis is not possible.

gmw_00007489.name: Q1451+112
gmw_00007489.overview: Q1451+112 is a high-redshift quasar with an estimated redshift of approximately 3.25 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835]. Spectral analysis reveals a faint, narrow emission-line region that extends two arcseconds to the northwest and west of the quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835].

sources.summary: The paper derives uncertain redshifts for Q1451+112 and reports a faint narrow emission-line region extending northwest and west, with limited structural analysis.

quotes.quote: The narrow-band image for Q1451+112 corresponds to 5163--5173 . The broad QSO \ emission line is strongly affected by absorption lines in the \ forest. An estimate of the QSO redshift is difficult because the red wing of the O i 1302 line is affected by residuals from the 5577 sky line. However, we adopt z=3.261 estimated from this emission line. A lower redshift of z=3.2468 is inferred from the C iv 1549 line.

After QSO spectral subtraction a faint narrow emission line region is found extending to 2 \ to the north-west and west of the QSO as seen in Fig. fig:qlya_qso_lya . This narrow emission line has z=3.2530 implying a velocity difference of +700 km s ^ 1 relative to C iv from the QSO broad line region but a blue shift of --600 km s ^ 1 relative to the O i line. Further analysis of the structure of the emission line region is not possible with the signal-to-noise level in the individual spaxels.

gmw_00007490.name: Q1759+7539
aliases_local: the z_ abs = 2.62 damped system toward GB1759+7539
gmw_00007490.overview: Q1759+7539 is a bright, radio-loud quasar with a redshift of approximately 3.05 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835]. An extended Lyman-alpha emission structure is located southwest of the quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835], and a partially ionized damped absorption system lies along its line of sight at a redshift of 2.62 [arXiv:astro-ph/0202140].

sources.original_name: the z_ abs = 2.62 damped system toward GB1759+7539
sources.summary: The paper treats the z=2.62 damped system toward GB1759+7539 as a photoionization case study, finding metal-line evidence for neutral and partially ionized gas components.

quotes.quote: We present a case study of the z_ abs = 2.62 damped system toward GB1759+7539 ( z_ em = 3.05 , V 17). This sightline has been previously analysed by , , and , but none of these studies focused on the ionization state of the damped system. Here, we present observations of a series of metal-line transitions which indicate this damped system is partially ionized.

We investigate the ionization state of the damped system at z=2.62 toward GB1759+7539 through an analysis of ionic ratios sensitive to photoionization: / , / , / , / . Approximately half of the metals arise in a mostly neutral velocity component with H I/H > 0.9 , based on / < 0.013 . In contrast, the remaining half exhibits / 0.3 indicative of a partially ionized medium with H I/H 0.5 . These conclusions are supported by the observed / , / , and / \ ratios.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q1759+7539 as the sample’s only radio-loud quasar, with bright optical emission and an extended Ly emission structure southwest of the QSO.

quotes.quote: The narrow-band image for Q1759+7539 corresponds to 4919--4933 . This object is the only RLQ in the sample with a core-dominated, flat-spectrum radio emission hook96 , and like Q1425+606 it is also very bright in the optical. A Gaussian fit to the QSO C iv emission line gives z=3.0486 0.0008 , where the error includes the wavelength calibration error. The \ emission line gives a similar result; other emission lines are fainter or do not allow for a good determination of the redshift because absorption lines affect the line profiles. The on--off band image in Fig. fig:qlya_qso_lya shows the clear presence of an extended structure to the south-west of the QSO center.

sources.summary: The paper adopts HI=20.8 for Q1759 + 75, presents velocity and column-density measurements, notes the bright QSO and accurate abundances, but excludes Zn because of sky-line blending.

quotes.quote: Q1759 + 75, z = 2.625 This system and the adopted HI = 20.8 0.1 are taken from an ongoing survey by I. Hook. Figure vpQ1759 presents the velocity plots and Table Q1759-clm gives the measurements. The QSO is very bright and was observed at FWHM 6 resolution. We expect all of the measured abundances are very accurate with the exception of Zn^+ where the ZnII 2026 profile is blended with sky lines. Although the strongest feature appears unblended, we have chosen not to include Zn in the abundance analysis.

gmw_00007491.name: Q1802+5616
gmw_00007491.overview: Q1802+5616 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835]. Observations of the quasar have revealed no detected emission-line nebula at its redshift, which may be due to its faint continuum emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835].

sources.summary: The paper reports no detected emission-line nebula around Q1802+5616, attributing the non-detection to the quasar’s faint continuum and sky-line residuals.

quotes.quote: The narrow-band image for Q1802+5616 corresponds to 6270--6277 . No \ emission line nebula is found at the QSO redshift, but this is possibly because the QSO itself if fainter than the others in this sample. The QSOs continuum emission is 40 times fainter than for the two bright QSOs in our sample. Thus if any correlation between the QSO and EELR flux is present, the narrow \ EELR should have a total flux of 3 10^ 17 \ which is below the detection limit in this data cube. This flux has been estimated applying a global scaling relation between the QSO emission and the EELR flux as detailed in Sect. sect:qlya_scaling .
Because the QSO \ emission line falls close to the strong sky emission line at 6300 and is affected by strong residuals, this object is not considered further here.

gmw_00007492.name: Q2233+131
gmw_00007492.overview: The quasar Q2233+131, also identified as SDSS J223619.19+132620.3, hosts a nearby emission-line object located 1 arcsecond to its north-east [arXiv:astro-ph/0603835, arXiv:2305.05173]. Additionally, two nearby submillimeter galaxies have been detected in its vicinity, though their physical association remains unconfirmed [arXiv:2305.05173].

sources.summary: The paper cannot determine Q2233+131’s redshift from the cube, relies on literature, and finds a nearby emission-line object after QSO subtraction.

quotes.quote: The narrow-band image for Q2233+131 corresponds to 5223--5230 . Only the broad \ emission line lies within the spectral range in the data cube, and this line only is insufficient to estimate the QSO redshift. The broad \ line is affected by both \ forest lines and a strong Si II 1260 absorption line associated with a DLA system see christensen04 . Since no adequate redshift could be determined from the data cube, we rely on the literature value for the QSO redshift. After QSO subtraction we find an emission line object 1 \ to the north-east of the QSO.

sources.summary: SDSS J223619.19+132620.3 has two nearby submillimeter galaxies in band-8 observations, both seen in continuum, but lacking redshifts needed to confirm physical companionship.

quotes.quote: Band 8 observations of SDSS J223619.19+132620.3 revealed two nearby submm galaxies ogura20 . One of them was about 4 away from the quasar, while the other was about 10 away. Both sources are also seen in the respective continuum image in Fig. fig:companions . Since no data in other wavelengths nor spectroscopic redshifts for these objects were available, it was not possible to confirm whether they were physical companions.

gmw_00007493.name: Sunburst
gmw_00007493.overview: The Sunburst is a strongly lensed galaxy at redshift z=2.37 that is split into four bright arcs by a foreground cluster [arXiv:2106.10280, arXiv:2301.04672]. This system contains at least 13 star-forming knots, including a young massive star cluster that emits Lyman continuum radiation [arXiv:2106.10280, arXiv:2301.04672].

sources.summary: The paper studies the strongly lensed Sunburst LyC galaxy at z=2.37, deriving delensed size, mass, star-cluster contribution, ionizing escape fractions, and star-formation properties.

quotes.quote: We investigate the strongly lensed ( 10-100 ) Lyman continuum (LyC) galaxy, dubbed Sunburst , at z=2.37 , taking advantage of a new accurate model of the lens. A characterization of the intrinsic (delensed) properties of the system yields a size of 3 sq.kpc, a luminosity of M _ UV = -20.3 , and a stellar mass of M 10^ 9 ; 16 of the ultraviolet light is located in a 3 Myr old gravitationally bound young massive star cluster (YMC), with an effective radius of 8 pc (corresponding to 1 milliarcsec without lensing) and a dynamical mass of 10^ 7 \ (similar to the stellar mass) -- from which LyC radiation is detected ( < 912 ). The star formation rate and stellar mass surface densities for the YMC are Log _ 10 ( _ SFR [ M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 ] ) 3.7 and Log _ 10 ( _ M [ M _ pc ^ 2 ] ) 4.1 , with sSFR > 330 Gyr ^ 1 , consistent with the values observed in local young massive star clusters.

The resulting relative escape fraction of the ionizing radiation emerging from the entire galaxy is higher than 6-12 , whilst it is 46-93 if inferred from the YMC multiple line of sights. At least 12 additional unresolved star-forming knots with radii spanning the interval 3-20 pc (the majority of them likely gravitationally bound star clusters) are identified in the galaxy. A significant fraction (40-60 ) of the ultraviolet light of the entire galaxy is located in such bound star clusters. The star formation rate surface density of the Sunburst galaxy (Log _ 10 ( _ SFR ) = 0.5_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 ) is consistent with the high inferred , as observed in local galaxies experiencing extreme gas physical conditions.

sources.summary: The paper describes Sunburst as a z=2.37 strongly lensed high-redshift galaxy split into four bright arcs, with at least 13 star-forming knots and over 50 multiple images.

quotes.quote: The Sunburst is a galaxy at z =2.37, strongly lensed by the Planck cluster PSZ1 G311.65-18.48 at z =0.44, initially reported by . The strong gravitational lensing effect deflects the light from the background high- z Sunburst galaxy into four bright arcs. These bright arcs harbor at least 13 star-forming knots, which likely are stellar clusters. There are more than 50 multiple images of this system Pignataro2021 , whose physical properties are studied in detail in .

gmw_00007494.name: NGC 3191
gmw_00007494.overview: NGC 3191 is a massive, barred Sbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1708.03856, arXiv:2304.10416] that has hosted multiple supernovae [arXiv:1708.03856]. It exhibits a recent burst of star formation [arXiv:1708.03856] and kinematic deviations in its outer regions, which suggest a gravitational interaction with the companion galaxy MCG+08-19-017 [arXiv:1708.03856].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3191 as the spiral host of SN 2017egm, finding substantial stellar mass, subsolar average metallicity, recent star formation, and interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: In this work we present IFU data of a large part of the SLSN-2017egm host galaxy, NGC 3191, which is the closest SLSN-I discovered to date and has hosted another two SNe in the past, among them a SLSN-Type II. NGC 3191 is a large spiral galaxy with an integrated stellar mass of M_*=10^ 10.7 M_ . The galaxy has an average metallicity of 0.6 Z _ , with a typical negative gradient distribution towards the external regions, as it is usually found in bright large spirals.

Star-formation in this galaxy seems to have been at a low level for several 100 Myrs, after which it experienced a new recent burst in SF. Evidence for this is found in the brightest H II regions. Their location, in the apparent direction toward the companion galaxy MCG+08-19-017, could suggest a gravitational interaction which triggered the recent star-formation. Kinematic analysis of NGC 3191 shows some deviations in the external regions of the galaxy with a ``twist'' in the velocity field that could very well be explained by an interaction within the last tens of Myrs.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3191 as the massive barred Sbc host of SN 2017egm, studies emission-line metallicity at the SN site, and finds a likely metal-rich environment.

quotes.quote: The supernova (SN) host, NGC 3191, is a barred Sbc galaxy with a stellar mass of a few 10^ 10 M _ , much higher than in typical SLSNe-I . The results of various metallicity diagnostics at the SN position are controversial, with Z 0.6 Z _ or Z 1.3 -2.6 Z _ .

The prominent host emission lines include H i , [ N ii ] , [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] , and [ S ii ] . For H , [ O ii ] , and [ O iii ] , we applied a Gaussian fit to the continuum-subtracted line profile. A double Gaussian function is used to fit the [ S ii ] doublets, while a triple Gaussian fit is performed to the profile of H and [ N ii ] 6548, 6584. The flux ratios of strong emission lines can be used to determine the metallicity of the galaxy according to empirical relations established between oxygen abundance and the ratios.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 3191 as a massive nearby spiral SLSN host with high metallicity, substantial star formation, and radio-to-UV galaxy photometry.

quotes.quote: At redshift z=0.03 , the recently-discovered SN 2017egm is the nearest Type I superluminous supernova (SLSN) to date, and first near the center of a massive spiral galaxy (NGC 3191). Using SDSS spectra of NGC 3191, we find a metallicity 2 \ at the nucleus and 1.3 \ for a star forming region at a radial offset similar to SN 2017egm.

NGC 3191 also exhibits resolved emission at 1.4 GHz in data from the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty centimeters survey FIRST; bec1995 , pointing to a star formation origin, with an inferred value of SFR 13 M _ yr ^ 1 (using the relation of yc02 ).

We therefore conclude that NGC 3191 has a total star formation rate of 15 M _ yr ^ 1 , of which about 70 is dust obscured, and that the X-ray emission detected with XRT is likely not dominated by SN 2017egm itself. We further fit the broad-band spectral energy distribution of NGC 3191 using prospector leja2017 to determine additional galaxy parameters (Figure fig:host ).

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3191 as SN 2017egm host, finding a massive, metal-rich spiral with no line-of-sight satellite and an unusually low-EW, solar-metallicity SN environment.

quotes.quote: However, the most nearby SLSN to date, SN 2017egm, occurred in the spiral galaxy NGC 3191, which has a relatively high stellar mass and correspondingly high metallicity. In this paper, we present detailed analysis of the nearby environment of SN 2017egm using MaNGA IFU data, which provides spectral data on kiloparsec scales. From the velocity map we find no evidence that SN 2017egm occurred within some intervening satellite galaxy, and at the SN position most metallicity diagnostics yield a solar and above solar metallicity ( 12+ (O/H) 8.8-9.1 ). Additionally we measure a small H equivalent width (EW) at the SN position of just 34 , which is one of the lowest EWs measured at any SLSN or Gamma-Ray Burst position, and indicative of the progenitor star being comparatively old. We also compare the observed properties of NGC 3191 with other SLSN host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3191 as the large spiral host of SN 2017egm, close to the nucleus, and argues its spiral-arm dust may explain a weak polarization signal.

quotes.quote: Here we present multi-colour polarimetric observations of the superluminous Type I SN 2017egm in the galaxy NGC 3191 (see Figure fig:image ), acquired with the Liverpool Telescope (LT) RINGO3 instrument. SN 2017egm was discovered by Gaia, as Gaia 17biu, on 2017 May 23 2017TNSTR.591....1D . With a redshift of z = 0.03 for the host galaxy Quoted from the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database - https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ , SN 2017egm is the nearest hydrogen-poor Type I SLSN yet discovered, occurring in a large spiral galaxy in close proximity to the nucleus (see Fig. fig:image ).

If we are to believe the slight preference for polarization signal, in the blue and green channels, being found in the Stokes q parameter, i.e. 0^ , this might be consistent with the apparent orientation of the spiral arm containing SN 2017egm in the galaxy NGC 3191 appearing to lie approximately North-South, with the SN located almost due East of the host galaxy nucleus (see Fig. fig:image ). Given the expectation that the ISP arising from dust grains in other galaxies should run parallel to the magnetic field lines running along spiral arms 1987MNRAS.224..299S , the slight preference for positive Stokes q may reflect an ISP contribution arising in spiral arm of NGC 3191 and the observed polarization is not intrinsic to SN 2017egm itself.

gmw_00007495.name: PSO J006+39
gmw_00007495.overview: PSO J006+39 is a high-redshift quasar located at z=6.621 [arXiv:1901.02615, arXiv:1905.02920]. It hosts a supermassive black hole in a rapid growth phase [arXiv:1901.02615] and exhibits an extremely blue rest-frame optical and ultraviolet continuum [arXiv:1905.02920].

sources.summary: The paper presents a new NIR spectrum of z=6.621 quasar PSO J006+39, deriving a low SMBH mass, very blue continuum, super-Eddington SED, and supersolar BLR metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of a new near-infrared (NIR) spectrum of a recently discovered z=6.621 quasar PSO J006+39 in an attempt to explore the early growth of supermassive black holes (SMBHs). This NIR (rest-frame ultraviolet, UV) spectrum shows blue continuum slope and rich metal emission lines in addition to Ly line. We utilize the Mg II line width and the rest frame luminosity L_ 3000 to find the mass of SMBH ( M_ BH ) to be 10^8 M_ , making this one of the lowest mass quasars at high redshift. The power-law slope index ( _ ) of the continuum emission is 2.94 0.03 , significantly bluer than the slope of _ =-7/3 predicted from standard thin disc models.

All plausible SED models require that the source is super-Eddington ( L_ bol /L_ Edd 9 ), so the apparently small disc may simply be the inner funnel of a puffed up flow, and clearly the SMBH in this quasar is in a rapid growth phase. We also utilize the rest-frame UV emission lines to probe the chemical abundance in the broad line region (BLR) of this quasar. We find that this quasar has super solar metallicity through photoionization model calculations.
2017ApJ...849...91M (hereafter 2017ApJ...849...91M ) analysed the continuum slope of 15 quasars at z 6.5 , including our target J006.1240+39.2219 (hereafter PSO J006+39) in this study.

sources.summary: PSO J 006+39 is modeled as a z=6.621 super-Eddington quasar with an unusually blue optical/UV continuum requiring an extremely small outer disc radius.

quotes.quote: This is the recently discovered quasar PSO J 006+39 , at z=6.621 tang2017 , with a black hole mass measured from the width of the Mg _ II line of (1.4 - 1.7) 10^8 M_ tang2019 . This paper also showed that the rest frame optical/UV continuum was extremely blue, with _ =0.94 (i.e, photon index =0.06 ), significantly bluer than the slope of the standard disc of _ =1/3 (i.e., =2/3 ), and significantly bluer than the majority of quasars at either high z ( tang2019 and references therein) or more locally xie2016 .

We then follow and allow r_ out to be a free parameter. The results are shown in table tab:pso06 and Fig. fig:eeu_p006 . The spectrum is well reproduced by the model with m =2.2 and r_ out =140 for spin 0, and for m =4.4 and r_ out =145 for spin 0.9. These values of r_ out are consistent with the result of as the difference between NT based emissivities and the slim disc emissivity is not large in this regime. Thus our fits confirm the requirement for an extremely small disc outer radius in this super-Eddington object.

gmw_00007496.name: BCG
aliases_external: brightest cluster galaxy in XMMU J2235.3-2557
gmw_00007496.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of the XMM2235 cluster is located within two arcseconds of the system's X-ray emission peak [arXiv:0910.1716]. This massive galaxy has a stellar mass of approximately 9e11 solar masses, and its major axis aligns with the elongation of the core-galaxy distribution [arXiv:0910.1716].

sources.summary: The BCG in XMM2235 lies within two arcseconds of the X-ray peak, aligns with the core-galaxy distribution, and is the most massive of the four central red galaxies.

quotes.quote: The peak of the X-ray emission is within 2 \ of the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) and is elongated, from the southwest to the northeast, following the distribution of the red cluster galaxies in the core and the major axis of the BCG.

Stellar masses range between 5 10^ 10 and 5 10^ 11 M _ apart from the BCG. The four red galaxies in the core (within 100 kpc radius) all exceed 10^ 11 M _ with the BCG being by far the most massive of the four, with M_ *BCG 9 10^ 11 M _ .
The X-ray emission has a regular morphology and is clearly elongated in the same way as the distribution of red passive galaxies in the core as well as the major axis of the BCG.

gmw_00007497.name: NGC 5337
gmw_00007497.overview: NGC 5337 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1908.08580, arXiv:2312.00253] located at an estimated distance of 53.2 Mpc [arXiv:1908.08580, arXiv:2312.00253]. It hosted the long-lasting Type IIn supernova SN 2015da [arXiv:1908.08580, arXiv:2312.00253], which occurred in an environment with a nearly solar metallicity [arXiv:1908.08580].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5337 as the SBab spiral host of SN 2015da, giving its photometry, velocity, distance, and local metallicity environment near the supernova.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA=13:52:23.024, Dec= +39 :41:14.16 - J2000 - for the center of the host

quotes.quote: NGC 5337 is a SBab galaxy, with a total corrected Apparent magnitude corrected for foreground Galactic extinction in the direction of NGC 5337 A_ B,MW =0.052 mag ; 2011ApJ...737..103S , internal extinction A_ B,int =0.35 mag ; 1995A&A...296...64B and k--correction. apparent B- band magnitude of 12.94 0.29 mag and mean heliocentric radial velocity ( cz ) V_r=2127 2 (as reported in the HyperLeda database http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ ). In the following, we assume a luminosity distance of 53.2 13.2 Mpc (corresponding to a distance modulus of 33.63 0.54 mag ), which is the most recent redshift--independent distance estimate for NGC 5337 2016AJ....152...50T . We note, however, a large discrepancy among different distances reported in the literature for NGC 5337 (in particular among redshift-dependent distances reported in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database -- NED https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu/ ).

We computed the de-projected normalized distances ( r_ SN /R_ 25 and r_ HII /R_ 25 ) from the center of NGC 5337 following 2009A&A...508.1259H their Equations 1 and 2; see also 2013A&A...558A.143T . Using the coordinates of the SN and the host center, the inclination and major axis position angle (PA) reported in the HyperLeda database, we inferred de-projected distances r_ SN /R_ 25 =0.35 and r_ HII /R_ 25 =0.47 (17 38 and 23 65 offset from the center of NGC 5337) for the SN and the H II region, respectively. Assuming a metallicity gradient of 0.47 R/R_ 25 i.e., the average of the metallicity gradients reported in for a sample of galaxies 2004A&A...425..849P , we extrapolated the oxygen abundance at the galactocentric distance of , obtaining an averaged nearly solar value of 12+ [ O/H ] =8.48 dex assuming a solar metallicity of 12+ [ O/H ] =8.69 dex ; 2009ARA&A..47..481A .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5337 as the barred spiral host of SN 2015da, adopting its distance, redshift, reddening, metallicity, and a line-of-sight gas velocity offset.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we discuss the long-lasting superluminous Type IIn event SN 2005da (also known as PSN J13522411+3941286), discovered in the barred spiral NGC 5337 (see Fig. fig:finder ). The photometric and spectroscopic evolution have already been discussed in detail by T20 hereafter tar20 , and we refer the reader to that paper for background information about its discovery, host galaxy and environment, and early-time evolution. To be consistent with T20, we adopt the same values for the distance d=53.2 Mpc, host-galaxy redshift z=0.0071 , distance modulus m-M=33.63 mag, line-of-sight reddening E(B-V)=0.98 mag (combined 0.01 mag Galactic and 0.97 mag host-galaxy reddening), and metallicity 0.6 Z _ .

First, we needed a slightly different redshift than that of the host galaxy in order to match the observed Na i D lines to their expected laboratory wavelengths (shown by the light-blue bars in Fig. fig:na1d ). As noted in the Introduction, the host galaxy NGC 5337 has a centroid velocity that gives z=0.0071 , but the redshift used to correct the spectra in Fig. fig:na1d is z=0.0067 . In other words, host-galaxy interstellar gas along the line of sight to SN 2015da is blueshifted by 120 km s ^ 1 compared to the centroid velocity of the host, with the difference most likely due to galactic rotation at the location of SN 2015da. This is useful when interpreting the velocities of narrow components throughout the paper. For all remaining spectra in the paper, we correct the redshift using z=0.0067 .

gmw_00007498.name: Draco
gmw_00007498.overview: The Draco dwarf spheroidal galaxy is a well-studied benchmark target for dark matter searches [arXiv:2006.16706]. It is characterized by a high mass-to-light ratio, relative proximity, and a low expected background of astrophysical gamma rays [arXiv:2006.16706].

sources.summary: The paper selects Draco as a classical dwarf spheroidal benchmark for CTA branon dark-matter searches, citing its dark-matter suitability and simulating 300-hour observations.

quotes.quote: We will adopt two astrophysical targets as benchmarks, one for each site, namely the Draco and Sculptor dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxies. This kind of objects has been shown to be particularly relevant for DM searches given their high mass-to-light ratios, relative proximity and low level of expected gamma-ray background induced by ordinary astrophysical phenomena . In particular, Draco and Sculptor are both very well studied objects and are among the brightest ones in terms of expected DM flux .

For each selected target we simulate CTA observations with total exposures of 300 h per dSph. This exposure is the expected amount of time dedicated to observations of dSphs in the first 3 years of scientific operation of CTA . Note that the coordinates in table tab:simu refer to the target position, which slight differs from the center of the CTA field of view when adopting the wobble strategy for the observation. In our case, the pointing has an offset of 0.5 degrees with respect to the source position .

gmw_00007499.name: NGC 6395
gmw_00007499.overview: NGC 6395 is a nearby grand Scd spiral galaxy located in the Draco constellation [arXiv:2107.13439]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy near the site of the supernova SN 2020cxd reveal a slightly sub-solar gas metallicity [arXiv:2107.13439].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6395 as the nearby grand Scd spiral host of SN 2020cxd, gives distance estimates, and measures slightly subsolar gas metallicity near the explosion.

quotes.quote: SN 2020cxd is positioned in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 6395 which has a measured redshift of z=0.003883 . According to the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu https://ned.ipac.caltech.edu catalog, the peculiar motion corrected distance for a standard cosmology ( _M=0.3 , _ =0.7 , and h=0.7 ) is 23 Mpc, whereas the most recent Tully-Fisher measurement reported on the same site is 20 Mpc.

Host galaxy NGC 6395 is a nearby grand Scd spiral galaxy in the Draco constellation, which has not been reported to host any SN before. Simbad reports an AB magnitude of 13 in the band, but there is no SDSS spectrum of this galaxy and we have not been able to find any estimates of the metallicity of NGC 6395. We therefore made use of some of the spectroscopic observations of the SN to also measure some of the stronger emission lines from the nearby regions as a way to probe the metallicity of the gas close to the site of the explosion.

We measure a slightly sub-solar metallicity in most indicators, for example we can adopt the N2 scale of the calibration using the flux ratio between [ N II ] 6583 and H . We found that 12+ log (O/H) =8.50 0.15 . We also employed another metallicity diagnostic of using [ N II ] , H and [ S II ] lines, in which 12+ log(O/H) =8.40 0.10 .

gmw_00007500.name: UGC 148
gmw_00007500.overview: UGC 148 is an infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. Its multiwavelength fluxes, including data at 60, 100, 170, and 450 microns, are well-fitted by a two-component dust model [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 148 has uncertain 850 micron flux because of tilted-sky and noisy-bolometer issues, but its multiwavelength fluxes fit a two-component dust model.

quotes.quote: UGC 148 . Data points for this object are not well-fitted by the two-component dust model (Section sed-fits ), probably due to the 850 flux having been underestimated -- the 850 S/N contours shown in Figure egmaps for this object show evidence of a residual tilted sky plane (the sky is more positive on one side of the map than the other), suggesting that sky removal techniques may have been inadequate in this case and that therefore the source flux may have been under- (or over-) estimated. Also, since the E-NE part of the galaxy is coincident with noisy bolometers in the 850 map the flux-measurement aperture was drawn to avoid this region, so the 850 flux may be underestimated. However, an additional data point at 170 (ISO) is available from the literature (Stickel et al. 2000, 2004). Using the 60, 100, 170 and 450 fluxes we find that the data points are well-fitted by the two-component dust model (we take an average of all 170 fluxes available, see Section sed-fits ), and these results are listed in Table 450tab and the SED is shown in Figure 2compSEDfig .

gmw_00007501.name: NGC 803
gmw_00007501.overview: NGC 803 is an infrared galaxy with reliable submillimetre peaks [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. Its four-point spectral energy distribution allows for two distinct two-component fits, each characterized by a different warm dust temperature [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper reports reliable submillimetre peaks for NGC 803 and notes that its four-point SED admits two good two-component fits with different warm dust temperatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 803 . None of the submillimetre peaks are associated with noisy bolometers. Due to the high ratio of S_ 25 /S_ 60 good two-component SED fits (Section sed-fits ) to the 4 data points (60, 100, 450 and 850 ) can be achieved for two quite different values of the warm component temperature. In addition to the parameters listed in Table 450tab a good fit is also found with the following parameters: T_ w =60 K, T_ =19 K, N_ N_ w =2597 , log M_ d2 =6.99 and log L_ fir =9.48 .

gmw_00007502.name: NGC 3270
gmw_00007502.overview: NGC 3270 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits central submillimetre emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. This emission is located near the main optical galaxy, specifically where the galaxy bulge ends and the inter-arm region begins [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper treats much of NGC 3270’s off-galaxy submillimetre emission as noise, but finds central emission near the optical galaxy where the bulge gives way to inter-arm regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 3270 . All the submillimetre emission away from the main optical extent, and the emission to the N of the galaxy, is associated with noisy bolometers. This observation also suffered from a tilted sky, which may explain much of the submillimetre emission in the N part of the map. However, the emission towards the centre of the map, coinciding with the main optical galaxy, is not associated with any noisy bolometers. This emission seems to occur where the galaxy bulge ends and the inter-arm region begins, as was found for NGC 3627 by Sievers et al. (1994).

gmw_00007503.name: NGC 3815
gmw_00007503.overview: NGC 3815 is an interacting galaxy that exhibits extended optical emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. It features arm-like submillimetre structures extending to the north and south, which align with faint optical features [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper finds possible arm-like submillimetre structures around NGC 3815 aligned with faint optical features, interpreting the extended emission as plausibly linked to an interacting system.

quotes.quote: NGC 3815 . The submillimetre emission to the NE and W of the galaxy is associated with noisy bolometers. However, the arm-like submillimetre structures seen extending from the galaxy to the N and S are not . Both of these `arms' extend in the direction of faint optical features seen in the DSS and 2MASS images. The optical images also show evidence of extended optical emission around the main optical extent (just visible to the NE in Figure egmaps ), but 2MASS (JHK) images show a band of emission stretching E-W between two nearby galaxies on either side of NGC 3815. It seems clear that some kind of interaction is taking place in this system, and therefore it is perhaps not unlikely that there might also be significantly extended submillimetre emission.

gmw_00007504.name: NGC 3987
gmw_00007504.overview: NGC 3987 is an edge-on spiral galaxy that features an optical dust lane [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. Its submillimetre emission follows this dust lane but is observed to be slightly offset from it [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3987 as an edge-on galaxy whose submillimetre emission follows, but is slightly offset from, its prominent optical dust lane.

quotes.quote: NGC 3987 . This edge-on galaxy has a prominent dust lane in the optical. Though the submillimetre emission follows the dust lane it is seen slightly offset. A similar result was found for another edge-on spiral by Stevens et al. (2005), who conclude this effect is simply an effect of the inclination of the galaxy on the sky.

gmw_00007505.name: UGC 7115
gmw_00007505.overview: UGC 7115 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768] identified as a star-forming candidate [arXiv:2202.08593]. Its radio emission is dominated by a large-scale, edge-darkened Fanaroff-Riley Class I structure powered by jets that extends approximately 300 kpc [arXiv:2202.08593].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 07115 as a star-forming candidate but notes its radio output is dominated by a large FR I jet-powered structure about 300 kpc across.

quotes.quote: The seven SF candidates are all detected by LOFAR. They display different radio morphologies: two (NGC 2918 and NGC 3837) are compact sources, with measured sizes of 8.0 and 10.6 , respectively (deconvolved sizes 3 kpc), three (NGC 3805, NGC 3919, and NGC 5525) are diffuse, low-brightness sources with sizes between 6 and 10 kpc (see Fig. diffuse ), while UGC 07115 is an extended ( 300 kpc) edge-darkened source. By excluding UGC 07115, whose radio emission is dominated by a large-scale structure powered by jets, we obtain a range between 0.1 and 0.5 M _ yr ^ 1 , with the exception of NGC 2789, for which this estimate is 8 M _ yr ^ 1 .

UGC 07115, the SF candidate with the large-scale radio structure, is located to the right of the boundary between SF- and AGN-powered sources (by considering only its core emission, log L_ 150 =22.85 , this galaxy falls on the boundary between SF and AGN).

sources.summary: The paper reports mostly non-noisy submillimetre emission for UGC 7115, but warns that tilted-sky effects and radio-source synchrotron contamination may dominate its 850 micron flux.

quotes.quote: UGC 7115 . With the exception of the submillimetre emission to the SE, none of the submillimetre emission in this map is associated with noisy bolometers. However, we found this SCUBA observation to have a tilted sky. We estimate that as much as 80 of the 850 flux from this elliptical may be due to synchrotron contamination from a radio source associated with the galaxy (Vlahakis et al., in prep.).

gmw_00007506.name: UGC 8902
gmw_00007506.overview: UGC 8902 is an Sb-like spiral galaxy spanning approximately 36 kpc [arXiv:1103.2910]. It has several physical companion galaxies nearby [arXiv:1103.2910], and its submillimetre emission is offset toward the south-southeast where faint optical condensations are located [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper describes 008902 as a 36 kpc Sb-like spiral near a bright star, with an emission-line spectrum and several nearby physical companions.

quotes.quote: 008902 (7559 km s ^ 1 ): This object, classified in NED as Sb:, has a 70 .50 major axis (from RC3 at 25 mag arcsec ^ 1 ). With a plate scale of 30.60 kpc arcmin ^ 1 , this makes it a 36 kpc object. The SDSS finding chart shows a spiral galaxy very close to a bright star, and a number of galaxies of similar size nearby. The galaxy itself shows H and [ SII ] in emission on an absorption spectrum, whereas SDSS J135906.56+153434.2, a diffuse irregular neighbour one arcmin to the north-east at 7260 30 km s ^ 1 , shows a blueward-sloping continuum with strong Balmer, [ NII ] , [ SII ] , and [ OIII ] emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper rejects several UGC 8902 submillimetre features as noisy but identifies a real southern peak, consistent with emission offset toward faint optical condensations.

quotes.quote: UGC 8902 . The region of submillimetre emission to the E, far SW and far S are all associated with noisy bolometers. However, the 4 submillimetre peak lying to the S/SE beyond the main optical extent, at a similar declination to the small galaxy to the SE, is not associated with any noisy bolometers. This submillimetre emission is consistent with the fact that the overall emission associated with the galaxy is offset to the S/SE with respect to the optical. We also note that this region in the optical contains a number of faint condensations in the direction of the small galaxy to the SE.

gmw_00007507.name: IC 979
gmw_00007507.overview: IC 979 is an infrared galaxy that has been detected at both 850 and 450 microns [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. These submillimeter observations were conducted under poor weather conditions, resulting in relatively low signal-to-noise detections [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper reports low-significance 850 micron and 450 micron detections of IC 979, with calibration and weather problems preventing a reliable two-component dust fit.

quotes.quote: IC 979 . Although this galaxy is detected with relatively low S/N at 850 it is also detected at 450 . We allocate a higher 450 calibration error (25 ) for this source, since there were no good 450 calibrator observations that night (calibration was achieved by taking the mean results from a number of calibrators observed that and the previous night). Note also that no two-component fit could be made to the data since the 450 data point is higher than the 100 value, possibly due to the problems with calibrating the 450 data but more likely due to an underestimate of the 100 flux. We note this object was observed in poor weather.

gmw_00007508.name: UGC 10205
gmw_00007508.overview: UGC 10205 is an active galactic nucleus host [arXiv:1912.06451] featuring an outer equatorial ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0401057] and submillimetre emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. The galaxy exhibits an asymmetric, boxy envelope likely produced by the tidal disruption of a captured satellite [arXiv:astro-ph/0401057].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 10205 as a thick-boxy-bulge galaxy with an outer equatorial ring, asymmetric boxy envelope, multi-component gas, and evidence for satellite accretion.

quotes.quote: UGC 10205 (VV 624) (Fig. 10205 ) is already studied in detail by Reshetnikov Evstigneeva ( ) and Vega et al. ( ). Both note that the bright spots in this galaxy (first mentioned by Vorontsov-Velyaminov ) belong to an outer equatorial (edge-on) ring. Other interpretations of this structure as a merger remnant (Vorontsov-Velyaminov ) or polar ring (Whitmore et al. , van Driel et al. ), seem to be very unlikely.

Reshetnikov Evstigneeva ( ) point also to the asymmetric weak envelope surrounding the galaxy. However, they do not mention its boxy structure at certain contour levels. The asymmetry of the boxy envelope is especially remarkable in the outer parts of the galaxy.

They support this conclusion by the observation that the star-formation rate is fairly high compared to normal disk galaxies. In addition, they run a numerical simulation of a capture and tidal disruption of a satellite galaxy by UGC 10205 producing the observed asymmetric envelope structure (Reshetnikov Evstigneeva ).

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 10205 as an active case study with irregularity in different radial zones, inner gas-kinematic twisting, and a twisted H-emitting lane along the major axis.

quotes.quote: UGC 10205 (Current AGN activity) The maximum values for \ and \ fall outside the regular ranges but in different regions for UGC 10205. \ tends to be irregular in the middle to outer parts of the studied area (radii 10, 11, 12, 13, and 14, or 19 4, 21 3, 23 3, 25 2, and 27 2) while \ is irregular in the inner parts (radii 1, 2, and 3, or 1 9, 3 9, and 5 8). There is a degree of twisting present in the inner portions of the gas kinematics, but not the stellar kinematics (which could indicate triaxiality with respect to kinematics in the same manner as isophotal twist), which would explain the increased values of \ in the center. Indeed, there appears to be a lane of H emitting gas present along the major axis of the galaxy, with a twist near the center (see Fig. fig:obscases ), which would explain these elevated \ values. The elevated values of \ toward the edge of the galaxy may be a reaction to the decreased amounts of warm gas, as with NGC 6146.

sources.summary: The paper coadds two UGC 10205 observations, finds real submillimetre emission on the optical galaxy and southern peak, and flags northern and western peaks as noisier.

quotes.quote: UGC 10205 . The 850 map in Figure egmaps is a coadd of two observations. Since emission at the optical galaxy position is very clear in one observation (both at 850 and 450 ) and not in the other observation, and since we find no explanation for this, we simply coadd the two observations (Section obs ). The submillimetre emission coincident with the main optical galaxy extent, and also the peak to the S, are not associated with any noisy bolometers. Peaks to the N and W of the galaxy lie in a region of the map which is slightly noisy. We note that this observation was carried out in less than ideal weather.

gmw_00007509.name: NGC 6103
gmw_00007509.overview: NGC 6103 is an infrared galaxy that exhibits submillimetre emission to its south [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. This southern emission region spatially overlaps with several faint features visible in both optical and 2MASS images [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 6103 has southern submillimetre emission not tied to noisy bolometers, spatially overlapping faint optical and 2MASS features despite imperfect weather.

quotes.quote: NGC 6103 . The submillimetre emission to the S of the galaxy is not associated with any noisy bolometers. This region contains a number of faint features seen in optical (DSS) and 2MASS images. We note that this object was observed in less than ideal weather.

gmw_00007510.name: NGC 6126
gmw_00007510.overview: NGC 6126 is an optical galaxy with a submillimetre source that is offset to its south [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. This submillimetre source is coincident with a small optical object and a possible companion galaxy visible in DSS and 2MASS images [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 6126’s submillimetre source as a point source offset south of the optical galaxy, near a possible companion and a non-noisy southern optical object.

quotes.quote: NGC 6126 . The submillimetre source (which we measured as a point source) is offset to the S of the optical extent of the galaxy. We note that, at minimum contrast, a small satellite/companion object can be seen in this region in the DSS and 2MASS images. The 3 peak to the S of the map is not associated with any noisy bolometers and is coincident with a small object visible in the optical. This observation, however, was carried out in poor weather.

gmw_00007511.name: NGC 6155
gmw_00007511.overview: NGC 6155 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits extended 850-micron submillimetre emission to its south and southeast [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. This emission is coincident with optical condensations and aligns with one of the galaxy's spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended 850 micron emission south and southeast of NGC 6155, coincident with optical condensations and aligned with a spiral arm, with 450 micron flux more confined.

quotes.quote: NGC 6155 The submillimetre map shows extended emission to the S and SE of the galaxy at 850 , coincident with a number of small galaxies/condensations in the optical. M arquez et al. (1999) find one of the spiral arms in this galaxy is directed towards the SE. None of the submillimetre peaks in this map are associated with noisy bolometers. A large aperture was used to measure all the flux associated with this object, and these results are listed in Table fluxtab . However at 450 any flux appears confined to the main optical extent (though the map at 450 is very noisy), and thus the flux measurement at 450 was made using a smaller aperture.

gmw_00007512.name: NGC 6190
gmw_00007512.overview: NGC 6190 is a spiral galaxy that has been studied using submillimeter observations [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. Its 850-micron emission appears to trace the structure of its outer spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper cautions that NGC 6190 has noisy data and unreliable fluxes, but says the reliable parts of its 850 micron emission trace the outer spiral arm structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 6190 . Some of the data for this object was very noisy and unusable. Consequently the remaining data may not be reliable. The submillimetre emission to the W of the galaxy lies in a region where there is a noisy bolometer in the 850 flux map. Thus the apertures used also unavoidably encompass some noisy bolometers, particularly at 450 , and results for this object should be used with caution. However, the rest of the 850 emission in the map is not associated with any noisy bolometers, so while the flux measurements may be unreliable this does not apply to the emission extent, which appears to follow the outer spiral arm structure.

gmw_00007513.name: NGC 7081
gmw_00007513.overview: NGC 7081 is a spiral galaxy with a central region that is clearly visible in optical DSS images [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. Deeper optical imaging reveals that the galaxy possesses very knotty and irregular faint extended spiral arms that coincide with peaks of submillimetre emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7081’s submillimetre peaks reliable and aligned with faint, knotty spiral arms seen in deeper optical images beyond the central DSS appearance.

quotes.quote: NGC 7081 . The submillimetre emission to the E and W of this galaxy are not associated with any noisy bolometers. The emission to the N and SE is coincident with regions of the map which are only slightly noisy, and since this observation was carried out in very good weather it is unlikely that any residual features of this noise remain in the S/N map (Section snmaps ). Though from the optical DSS image only the central region of the galaxy (coincident with the main submillimetre peak) is clearly visible, there is evidence that this spiral has a very faint extended spiral arm structure. This is confirmed by optical images from SuperCOSMOS which clearly show very knotty and irregular faint spiral arms coincident with the peaks of submillimetre emission to the N and W of the galaxy.

gmw_00007514.name: NGC 7463
gmw_00007514.overview: NGC 7463 is a galaxy that forms part of a triple system alongside NGC 7464 and NGC 7465 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. Submillimeter observations reveal emission from NGC 7463, which is connected to NGC 7464 by a bridge of submillimeter emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7463 as part of a triple system, with clear 850 micron emission and a submillimetre bridge to NGC 7464 while NGC 7465 is IRAS-resolved.

quotes.quote: NGC 7463 . This galaxy is part of a triple system with NGC 7464 (to the S of NGC 7463) and NGC 7465 (not shown in Figure egmaps ). At 850 we clearly detect emission from both NGC 7463 and NGC 7464, which seem to be joined by a bridge of submillimetre emission. The flux listed in Table fluxtab is for NGC 7463 alone, measured in an aperture corresponding to its main optical extent. Unfortunately a very noisy bolometer to the SE prevents us measuring the flux from the eastern half of NGC 7464, but excluding this region we measure a flux for the pair of 0.051 0.012 Jy , though obviously a lower limit. An source is associated with NGC 7465, which is resolved from the other members of the system at 60 (HIRES; Aumann, Fowler Melnyk 1990).

gmw_00007515.name: NGC 7722
gmw_00007515.overview: NGC 7722 is characterized by a high ratio of cold-to-warm dust [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768]. The galaxy features an optical dust lane extending over most of its northeastern half, which is closely followed by 850 micron submillimeter emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0510768].

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 7722 has a high cold-to-warm dust ratio, a prominent optical dust lane, and 850 micron emission that follows that lane.

quotes.quote: NGC 7722 . Along with a very high ratio of cold-to-warm dust this object also has a very prominent dust lane, extending over most of the NE `half' of the galaxy. The 850 emission clearly follows the dust lane evident in the optical. The 2 submillimetre peak to the NW of the galaxy is not associated with any noisy bolometers.

gmw_00007516.name: GB 1310+487
gmw_00007516.overview: GB 1310+487 is a gamma- and radio-loud narrow-line active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.638 [arXiv:1401.2151, arXiv:1702.04736]. Its light shines through an unrelated foreground galaxy at z=0.500 and is likely amplified by gravitational lensing [arXiv:1401.2151].

sources.summary: The paper studies GB 1310 + 487 as a redshift 0.638 gamma/radio-loud narrow-line AGN with strong flares, blazar-like SED, foreground-galaxy lensing, and ambiguous blazar versus radio-galaxy nature.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: radio VLBI position see http://astrogeo.org/vlbi/solutions/rfc_2012b _ J2000 = 13 12 43 353644 0.22 mas, _ J2000 = +48 28 30 94047 0.16 mas

quotes.quote: The radio source GB 1310 + 487 [ also known as GB6 B1310 + 4844 The correct B1950 source name, if its declination is expressed as three digits, is 1310 + 487, while 1310 + 484 is an unrelated nearby radio source. , and CGRaBS J1312 + 4828, listed in the Fermi ray source catalogues as 1FGL J1312.4 + 4827 2010ApJS..188..405A and 2FGL J1312.8 + 4828 2012ApJS..199...31N , radio VLBI position see http://astrogeo.org/vlbi/solutions/rfc_2012b _ J2000 = 13 12 43 353644 0.22 mas, _ J2000 = +48 28 30 94047 0.16 mas 2002ApJS..141...13B ] is a flat-spectrum radio source. It was unremarkable among other faint ray detected blazars (the E>100 MeV flux during the first 11 months of the Fermi mission was 3 10^ 8 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , as reported in the 1FGL catalogue; 2010ApJS..188..405A ) until it appeared in the daily Fermi sky with a flux of 1.0 10^ 6 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on 2009 November 18 UT dates are used through the text. 2009ATel.2306....1S . observations reported two days later confirmed the high-flux state of the source Bulgarelli2009:ATel1310 . Follow-up observations in the near-IR 2009ATel.2311....1C and optical 2009ATel.2320....1I also found GB 1310 + 487 in a high state compared to historical records.

The ray/radio-loud narrow-line active galactic nucleus (AGN) is located at redshift z=0.638 . It shines through an unrelated foreground galaxy at z=0.500 . The AGN light is probably amplified by gravitational lensing.

sources.summary: The paper models GB 1310+487 as a gamma-ray-loud radio source whose emission region lies near the BLR outer edge, with changing magnetic-to-electron energy across flare states.

quotes.quote: We employ a single-zone leptonic jet model, with synchrotron, synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) and external Compton (EC) process, to reproduce the quasi-simultaneous multi-wavelength spectral energy distributions in active and quiescent states of the narrow-line gamma-ray-loud radio source GB 1310+487. In the case of the EC process , the external seed photons from both broad line region (BLR) and dust torus are considered by assuming that the gamma-ray emission region is located at the outside boundary of the BLR and inside the dust torus.

We find that the location of the gamma-ray emitting region of GB 1310+487 can be tightly constrained at the outer edge of the BLR (the dissipation distance of the ray emission region from central black hole r_ diss a few times of R_ BLR ). The ratio of magnetic energy and emitting-electron energy in the radiation blob ( _ B =L_ B /L_ e ) is gradually increased from Flare 1, Flare 2 to Post-Flare, where the magnetic energy increase and matter energy decrease. These results suggest that the conversion of the magnetic field and the matter (radiation electrons) energy and the location of the ray emission region (or ambient photon field) may play an important role in different radiation states of GB 1310+487.

gmw_00007517.name: SDSS J141918.81+394035.8
gmw_00007517.overview: SDSS J141918.81+394035.8 is a low-mass blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2007.00563, arXiv:2107.04703]. It exhibits a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:2107.04703, arXiv:1808.08964] and low metallicity, with ionization properties typical of a normal star-forming dwarf [arXiv:2107.04703].

sources.summary: The paper describes the FIRST J1419+3940 host as a low-mass, high-star-formation dwarf galaxy whose properties are plausible but somewhat atypical for a neutron-star merger origin.

quotes.quote: We examined what information the host galaxy of FIRST J1419+3940 carries of its origin. FIRST J1419+3940 was detected in a dwarf galaxy, SDSS J141918.81+394035.8 2018ApJS..235...42A . The galaxy has an estimated stellar mass of 2 10^7 M _ , and a star formation rate of 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 .

Therefore, while most neutron star mergers will occur in massive galaxies, the observed host galaxy could have been the host of a neutron star merger. We note that other caveats remain, however. First, as the fiducial neutron star merger delay time of 100 Myr is longer than the typical duration of star formation epochs in dwarf galaxies, the host galaxy's unusually high star formation rate is likely unrelated to the binary's formation. Second, the transient's spatial offset from the galactic center is smaller than typically expected from neutron star mergers.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J141918.80+394035.9 as a blue compact dwarf host with low stellar mass, high specific star formation, low metallicity, and normal star-forming ionization properties.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of the transient, SDSS J141918.80+394035.9, is a blue compact dwarf and is clearly detected in SDSS survey imaging see 2018ApJ...866L..22L . The spectra are shown in Figure fig:spectrum . We extracted line fluxes by fitting a Gaussian profile to each emission line, and obtained estimates of the gas-phase oxygen abundance using both strong-line methods (all 13 diagnostics enabled within pymcz , Bianco+2015 ) as well as a T_e based ``direct'' measurement based on the [ OIII ] 4363 auroral line Izotov+2005 . Other bulk measurements (including mass and star-formation rate, SFR, estimates) were obtained from the NASA-SDSS Atlas (NSA; 2011AJ....142...31B ). We infer a stellar mass of M_* = 3 10^ 7 M_ , specific SFR (sSFR) of 2.4 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 , and an oxygen abundance of 12+log [ O/H ] = 8.10 ^ +0.06 _ 0.05 ( T_e method; strong-line methods give consistent estimates.)

Similarly, the right panel of Figure fig:host plots two strong-line emission ratios (as in 1981PASP...93....5B ); the host lies near one end of the diagram due to its low metallicity but is ionization properties are otherwise consistent with a normal star-forming dwarf.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J141918.81+394035.8 as a small star-forming dwarf host of FIRST J141918.9+394036, with low mass, high sSFR, and GRB-like host properties.

quotes.quote: FIRST J141918.9+394036 is likely associated with the small star-forming galaxy SDSS J141918.81+394035.8 at a redshift z=0.01957 \ ( d=87 Mpc), which implies a peak luminosity L_ 3 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . Joint consideration of the event light curve, host galaxy, lack of a counterpart gamma-ray burst, and volumetric rate suggests that FIRST J141918.9+394036 is the afterglow of an off-axis (``orphan'') long GRB.

They find that SDSS J141918.81+394035.8 has a stellar mass M/M_ =7.24 0.11 , a star formation rate SFR /(M_ yr^ 1 ) =-1.2^ +0.16 _ 0.2 , and specific star formation rate sSFR /yr^ 1 =-8.4 0.2 (95 confidence intervals). The low mass and high specific star formation rate make this galaxy similar to the typical host galaxies of long GRBs and superluminous supernovae 2016A&A...593A.115J .

gmw_00007518.name: ASPECS-LP.02
gmw_00007518.overview: ASPECS-LP.02 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.317 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It is detected in CO(2-1) emission, MUSE continuum, and continuum at both 1 mm and 5 cm [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.02 is a CO(2-1) emitter at z=1.317 detected in MUSE and radio/submillimeter continuum, though a skyline complicates line measurements.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.02: CO(2-1) at z=1.317 . Detected in both \ and continuum in MUSE. This source is also detected in continuum at 1 mm and 5 cm UDF16 Dunlop2017, Rujopakarn2016 . \ is severely affected by a sky-line complicating the redshift and line-flux measurement. We remeasure the cataloged redshift for this source, which is used to compute the velocity offset with CO(2-1) ( tab:CO-line-iden ).

gmw_00007519.name: ASPECS-LP.03
gmw_00007519.overview: ASPECS-LP.03 is a faint high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.454 [arXiv:1903.09167]. This redshift is supported by the detection of CO(3-2) emission and a lack of lower-redshift spectral features in its MUSE spectrum [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.03 is a faint CO(3-2) source at z=2.454; photometry and MUSE non-detection of lower-redshift features support that identification.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.03: CO(3-2) at z=2.454 . Photometric redshift indicates z=2-3 Skelton2014, Rafelski2015 , perfectly in agreement with the detection of CO(3-2) at z=2.45 . The source is faint ( m_ F775W > 27 mag) and an extraction of the MUSE spectrum yields essentially no continuum signal (see fig:MUSE-spectra-1 ). This supports a redshift solution between z=2-3 , where no bright emission lines lie in the MUSE spectral range (see sec:muse ). Beside there being little continuum in the spectrum, there are no spectral features (in particular emission lines) indicative of a lower redshift ( \ at z=1.30 ) or higher redshift ( \ at z= 3.60 ) solution.

gmw_00007520.name: ASPECS-LP.04
gmw_00007520.overview: ASPECS-LP.04 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.414 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It is identified as a CO(2-1) emitter that is detected in the continuum at both 1 mm and 5 cm [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.04 is identified as a z=1.414 CO(2-1) emitter with weak MUSE continuum and continuum detections at 1 mm and 5 cm.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.04: CO(2-1) at z=1.414 . MUSE spectrum shows \ and (weak) continuum. Detected in continuum at 1 mm and 5 cm UDF6 Dunlop2017, Rujopakarn2016 .

gmw_00007521.name: ASPECS-LP.09
gmw_00007521.overview: ASPECS-LP.09 is a gas-rich dusty star-forming galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2.69 [arXiv:2108.05596, arXiv:1903.09167]. It contains a rotating molecular gas disk [arXiv:2108.05596] and hosts an active galactic nucleus that dominates its near-infrared and X-ray emission [arXiv:2108.05596, arXiv:astro-ph/0701409].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF1 as a compact DSFG whose dusty star formation is traced by ALMA, radio, and CO gas; it is gas-rich and expected to be quenched by AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: UDF1 (J033244.01-274635.2) UDF1 is a DSFG at z_ spec =2.698 (see Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ).

In the B7 ALMA map the source is resolved: we measure a circularized radius _ ALMA 0.46 kpc, where we expect the dusty star-formation to be located. Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R find the 6 GHz radio flux to be compatible with the star formation activity of the ALMA source, confirming the outcome we obtained from the analysis of galaxy SED in Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P . The radio emission, that samples also the mildly obscured and un-obscured star-formation of the galaxy, extends over a larger region: _ VLA 2.7 kpc FWHM; Rujopakarn2016:2016ApJ...833...12R . From the CO(3-2) line luminosity we estimate the molecular hydrogen content of the galaxy to be M _ H_2 =(2.6 0.7) 10^ 10 M _ . From its velocity and velocity dispersion maps we claim that the galaxy cold gas component is probably a faced-on rotating disk that extends over a radius 3 kpc (see Tab. tab:COemission ).

Gas and dust rich, with a typical depletion timescale _ depl 75 Myr, we think that UDF1 very compact and intense star formation must have triggered a rapid growth of the central SMBH, whose power in the X-ray clearly overwhelms the host galaxy one Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P . We expect this galaxy to overlap the galaxy main-sequence in a few hundreds of Myr and then to be quenched by the central AGN.

sources.summary: Source 1 is a z=2.69 Chandra-detected infrared-luminous source with strong PAH emission, a featureless SED, and an AGN plus ongoing star-formation interpretation.

quotes.quote: To meet these criteria, we selected a z=2.69 \ Chandra detected source with 24 \ flux density of 0.13 \ mJy, hereafter Source 1, and a z=1.09 object with a 16 \ flux density of 0.15 \ mJy and no X-ray detection, hereafter Source 2. The infrared luminosity of these sources is approximately 10^ 13 \ and 3 10^ 11 , respectively, based on the \ templates. The Chandra \ detection of Source 1 is only in the soft-band (0.5-2 keV), which does not immediately rule out star-formation as the origin of the X-ray emission.

Source 1 at z=2.69 , on the other hand, does not show this ``bump.'' In fact, its SED appears relatively featureless at all wavelengths shortward of 6.2 , extending into the NIR and optical. This featureless SED is consistent with the expectation for an X-ray source at high redshift. Source 1, therefore, is likely to be a composite object containing an AGN which dominates the emission in the near-IR, swamping the 1.6 \ bump, together with significant ongoing star formation which is responsible for the PAH emission in the rest-frame MIR.

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.09 is a z=2.698 CO(3-2) source with matching UV absorption and possible association with a nearby source at similar redshift.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.09: CO(3-2) at z=2.698 . Photometric redshift indicates z=2-3 Skelton2014, Rafelski2015 , perfectly in agreement with the detection of CO(3-2) at z=2.69 . The source is faint ( m_ F775W > 27 mag), yet, UV absorption features at z=2.695 , matching the expected redshift of CO(3-2) at z=2.698 , are found in the MUSE spectrum at the position of the source (see fig:MUSE-spectra-3 ). The features arise in a source (MUSE 6941) 0 8 to the north ( 6.5 kpc at z 2.7 ). Given the potential proximity of the two sources, both spatially and spectrally, ASPECS-LP.09 could be part of a pair of galaxies with the source to the north.

sources.summary: The paper identifies .09 as the brightest 1.3 mm source in the UDF1 field, explains its edge-of-mosaic coverage and unconstraining Band 6 line limits, and uses its 1.3 mm continuum for SED fitting.

quotes.quote: The fourth is .09, which is the brightest source at 1.3 mm in the field UDF1; Dunlop2017 . This source was detected towards the edge of the Band 3 mosaic (at 40 of the primary beam peak sensitivity; hereafter PB response) and is at the extreme edge of the Band 6 mosaic. The CO(7--6) and (2--1) lines lie at 6 of the PB response at 218 GHz and the source falls outside the continuum map (below 10 PB response). We do include this source in this paper, but note that the upper limits on the lines in Band 6 are essentially unconstraining. For the SED fitting ( sec:multi-wavel-data ) we use the continuum measurement at 1.3 mm.

gmw_00007522.name: ASPECS-LP.10
gmw_00007522.overview: ASPECS-LP.10 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.037 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It features a close star-forming companion galaxy at the same redshift [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.10 is the lowest-redshift CO detection, with a close star-forming companion and MUSE absorption/emission features used for deblending.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.10: CO(2-1) at z=1.037 . The lowest redshift detection. Features a close star-forming companion at the same redshift. The MUSE spectrum shows continuum with both absorption and emission line features ( ). We reextract the spectrum with a new segmentation map to recompute the redshift and to minimize blending of the \ flux from the close companion at slightly different redshift.

gmw_00007523.name: ASPECS-LP.11
gmw_00007523.overview: ASPECS-LP.11 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.096 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It is part of a known overdensity in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field and exhibits a rich star-forming spectrum with both absorption and emission features [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.11 is a z=1.096 CO(2-1) source in the HUDF overdensity, with MUSE continuum plus absorption and emission features.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.11: CO(2-1) at z=1.096 . Part of the overdensity in the HUDF at the same redshift. MUSE reveals a rich star-forming spectrum with stellar continuum and both absorption and emission ( , ) features.

gmw_00007524.name: ASPECS-LP.12
gmw_00007524.overview: ASPECS-LP.12 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.574 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus detected in the X-ray band and contains a CO(3-2) line emission [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.12 is a z=2.574 CO(3-2) galaxy and X-ray AGN whose CO identification is confirmed by reanalyzed MUSE absorption features.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.12: CO(3-2) at z=2.574 . Detected in 1 mm continuum (C4; Aravena2016 ) and an X-ray AGN 680 Luo2017 . The source contains a CO line at 96.76 GHz. The optical counterpart shows red colors in HST and features a blue component towards the north. The source is considered to be a single galaxy in most photometric catalogs e.g., Skelton2014, Rafelski2015 .

A reanalysis of the MUSE spectrum revealed weak absorption features that, when cross correlated with an absorption line template, correspond a redshift z=2.5738 . Assuming that the CO line is CO(3-2) instead, this independently matches the redshift from ASPECS-LP exactly ( z=2.5738 ).

gmw_00007525.name: ASPECS-LP.13
gmw_00007525.overview: ASPECS-LP.13 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.601 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It represents a CO(4-3) detection and is likely to have a dust content [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.13 is the highest-redshift CO detection, a likely dusty z=3.601 CO(4-3) source supported by photometric redshifts and MUSE constraints.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.13: CO(4-3) at z=3.601 . Highest redshift CO detection. It is an F435W dropout and the photometric redshifts for this source consistently suggest that it lies in the z=3-4 range Skelton2014, Straatman2016 , with z_ BPZ =3.67_ 0.24 ^ +0.74 Rafelski2015 . These all suggest a detection of CO(4-3) at z=3.601 .

Furthermore, the source is likely to have a significant dust content in which case no \ emission may be expected at all. Nevertheless, while at m_ F775W = 26.4 , the spectrum does not reveal emission or absorption lines compatible with a solution for CO(2-1) at z=1.30 or CO(3-2) at z=2.45 , which suggests a higher redshift solution is appropriate for ASPECS-LP.13 (in agreement with the photo-z). In summary, the combined evidence of the photometric redshifts indicating z 3.5 and the lack of a lower redshift solution from MUSE makes the case for the detection of CO(4-3) at z=3.601 in ASPECS-LP.13.

gmw_00007526.name: ASPECS-LP.14
gmw_00007526.overview: ASPECS-LP.14 is a galaxy at a redshift of approximately 1.09 [arXiv:astro-ph/0701409, arXiv:1903.09167] located within an overdensity in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field [arXiv:1903.09167]. It is classified as a stellar-dominated luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0701409] and exhibits a rich spectrum containing continuum, absorption, and emission lines [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: Source 2 is a z=1.09 infrared-luminous source without X-ray detection, observed with IRS and showing a stellar-dominated LIRG SED with a rest-frame 1.6 micron peak.

quotes.quote: To meet these criteria, we selected a z=2.69 \ Chandra detected source with 24 \ flux density of 0.13 \ mJy, hereafter Source 1, and a z=1.09 object with a 16 \ flux density of 0.15 \ mJy and no X-ray detection, hereafter Source 2. The infrared luminosity of these sources is approximately 10^ 13 \ and 3 10^ 11 , respectively, based on the \ templates.
For the z=1.09 \ target, Source 2, we obtained a spectrum using the second order of the Long-Low module (LL-2, 14-21 \ ).

The extended SED shows that Source 2, at z=1.09 , appears indeed to be a typical, stellar-dominated LIRG, as predicted by its other properties. A clear emission peak is seen in the IRAC photometry at 1.6 \ in the rest-frame.

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.14 is a z=1.098 CO(2-1) source in the HUDF overdensity with a rich MUSE continuum and line spectrum.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.14: CO(2-1) at z=1.098 . Part of an overdensity in the HUDF. MUSE reveals a rich spectrum with continuum, absorption and a range of emission lines (among which , \ and Balmer lines).

gmw_00007527.name: ASPECS-LP.16
gmw_00007527.overview: ASPECS-LP.16 is a high-redshift disk galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.294 [arXiv:1903.09167]. Its MUSE spectrum reveals a stellar continuum with both absorption and emission lines [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP.16 is a disk-like z=1.294 CO(2-1) galaxy whose MUSE spectrum shows continuum, absorption, and emission lines.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP.16: CO(2-1) at z=1.294 . Shows a disk-like morphology. MUSE spectrum reveals a stellar continuum with absorption, as well as emission lines ( \ and ).

gmw_00007528.name: ASPECS-LP-MP.01
gmw_00007528.overview: ASPECS-LP-MP.01 is a high-redshift emission-line galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.096 [arXiv:1903.09167]. It is part of a galaxy overdensity in the Hubble Ultra Deep Field and exhibits a stellar continuum with both absorption and emission lines [arXiv:1903.09167].

sources.summary: ASPECS-LP-MP.01 is an additional z=1.096 CO(2-1) emitter in the HUDF overdensity with MUSE continuum, absorption, and emission-line features.

quotes.quote: ASPECS-LP-MP.01: CO(2-1) at z=1.096 . Part of an overdensity in the HUDF at the same redshift. MUSE spectrum shows stellar continuum with absorption, as well as emission lines ( ).

gmw_00007529.name: GS6
gmw_00007529.overview: GS6 is a high-redshift galaxy characterized by active star formation [arXiv:1910.08742]. Its spectral energy distribution requires the addition of a starburst component, placing the galaxy well above the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:1910.08742].

sources.summary: GS6 is one of the most active star-forming galaxies in the sample; its SED requires a burst, and it remains well above the main sequence.
