quotes.quote: Conversely, for two galaxies (UDF3 and GS6), less than 10 of the dust emission can be explained from the rest-frame UV to NIR fit. We found that a burst must be added to fit the full SED of these galaxies with any of our attenuation laws. We see in Section sec:sfr-mstar that both are the most active galaxies of our sample in star formation.
Another one (GS6) has an SFR 3.5 times higher than the MS. In our analysis, GS6 and GS7 appear less extreme but still well above the MS for a stellar mass estimated with the stellar continuum.

gmw_00007530.name: UDF13
gmw_00007530.overview: UDF13 is a dusty star-forming galaxy at a redshift of 2.497 [arXiv:2108.05596]. It exhibits a smooth, isolated optical morphology [arXiv:2108.05596], hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2108.05596], and has a compact millimeter emission region [arXiv:2108.05596].

sources.summary: The paper describes UDF13 as a z=2.497 DSFG with compact millimeter emission, smooth isolated optical morphology, an X-ray AGN, and SED results placing it on the main sequence.

quotes.quote: UDF13 (J033235.07-274647.6) UDF13 is a DSFG at redshift z _ spec =2.497 0.008 .

We measure an ALMA B7 continuum flux of S _ B7 =910 170 Jy and give an upper limit on the ALMA size, i.e. <0.65 kpc (Tab. tab:continuum ). We note that the optical radius ( _ H 1.2 kpc; Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ) is more extended than the upper limit on the millimeter size. In the UV/optical rest-frame (Fig. fig:cont ) UDF13 appears as an isolated object with a smooth (i.e. undisturbed) morphology. UDF13 has a X-ray counterpart (IDX 655) in the 7 Ms Chandra catalog by Luo2017:2017ApJS..228....2L with a 2-10 keV luminosity of 1.3 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . Luo et al. classify it as AGN (Tab. tab:galaxy_properties ).

The analysis presented in Pantoni2021:2021MNRAS.504..928P classifies UDF13 a main-sequence galaxy, confirming the outcome by Elbaz2018:2018A&A...616A.110E . Quite old (age 900 Myr) when compared to the other sources of the sample, UDF13 forms stars at a rate SFR 100 M _ yr ^ 1 , with a sSFR 1.7 Gyr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: UDF13 differs from the sample trend: its infrared emission and SED can be fit without a burst or extra attenuation, and it lies below the main sequence.

quotes.quote: In one case (UDF13) the IR emission is over-predicted from the fit of the stellar continuum alone, with an excess of 30 . In section sec:fit we show that both the stellar and full SEDs of UDF13 can be fitted without adding a burst, for any attenuation law. No extra attenuation is needed to explain L_ dust measured from Herschel and ALMA data.
UDF13 is still well below the MS and UDF3 very close to starbursting. Once again, UDF13 departs from the general trend.

gmw_00007531.name: J1029+2623
gmw_00007531.overview: J1029+2623 is a gravitationally lensed quasar that features three distinct images [arXiv:1602.01462]. Monitoring data from the system reveals a dominant time-delay mode of approximately 735 days [arXiv:1602.01462].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes J1029+2623 as a three-image gravitationally lensed quasar, using merged B+C monitoring data from 2007-2012 and confirming a dominant time-delay mode near 735 days.

quotes.quote: J1029+2623 discovered the gravitationally lensed quasar J1029+2623 whose estimated time delay is the second largest yet observed. Though J1029+2623 has three images ( A , B , and C ), merged B and C because they overlap significantly and thus their light curves are difficult to disentangle. They published its data ( A , B+C ) with 279 epochs monitored at the Fred Lawrence Whipple Observatory from January 2007 to June 2012. The time delay estimate obtained by analyzing the combined image can be different from that obtained by analyzing images B or C separately.
We confirm a dominant mode near 735 days and invisibly small modes near -2,000 and 1,800 days via L_ prof ( ) . Since the mode near 735 days overwhelms the other modes, we focus on the dominant mode.

gmw_00007532.name: Coma P
gmw_00007532.overview: Coma P is a gas-rich dwarf galaxy that formed more than 10 billion years ago [arXiv:2005.09633]. It is a strong candidate for a fly-by interaction with M64 approximately 1 billion years ago, which triggered the formation of its young stars [arXiv:2005.09633].

sources.summary: The paper presents Coma P as a gas-rich interacting companion candidate whose fly-by may have supplied M64's counter-rotating gas, with old RGB stars and young stars supporting recent interaction.

quotes.quote: More recently, bru19 suggested another scenario that M64 had experienced a fly-by interaction with a gas-rich galaxy rather than a merger. They suggested that a gas-rich progenitor of Coma P had a high speed fly-by interaction with M64 approximately 1 Gyr ago, and some of the gas stripped from Coma P resulted in the counter-rotating gas ring in the outer disk of M64. They also pointed out that young stars in Coma P seen in the CMD might have formed during the interaction of Coma P with M64.

The presence of old RGB stars in both M64 (in this study) and Coma P bru19 implies that both galaxies were formed more than 10 Gyrs ago. The presence of young stars in Coma P and the central region of M64 strongly indicates a recent interaction between the two galaxies. The counter-rotating gas ring with clumpy structure in M64 must have an external origin: either via accretion or fly-by interaction of dwarf gas-rich satellites, or cosmological cold-gas filaments. Coma P is a strong candidate for providing the counter-rotating gas to M64 through fly-by interaction about 1 Gyr ago.

gmw_00007533.name: host galaxy (HG) of GRB 221009A
aliases_external: host galaxy of GRB 221009A / GRB 221009A sightline
gmw_00007533.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 221009A is a star-forming disk galaxy viewed from a nearly edge-on orientation [arXiv:2302.07761, arXiv:2307.08313]. It has a modest stellar mass, and the gamma-ray burst occurred close to its nuclear region with a small offset [arXiv:2302.07761, arXiv:2307.08313].

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 221009A host as an edge-on disc-like galaxy near the burst, with typical long-GRB host luminosity, size, mass, colors, and environment.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of GRB 221009A is visible in our late time \ observations. It appears to be an edge-on system with the burst close to the nuclear regions, but with a notable offset ( 0.25^ 0.65 kpc) from the nucleus of the host.

Our model also provides us with an estimate of the galaxy size. We determine an effective radius of R_e = 2.45 0.20 kpc for the F160W filter. In addition, using a mass-to-light ratio derived from the star-forming galaxies of the COSMOS2015 catalog Laigle2016 at z_ GRB 0.1 and the F160W magnitude corrected for extinction, we estimate a stellar mass of = 9.00^ 0.47 _ +0.23 .

For F160W, our model returned a index of n = 1.71 0.18 and an axis ratio of b/a = 0.22 0.01 , in agreement with the apparent edge-on, disc-like morphology observed in the WFC3/IR images. This morphology seems slightly more unusual amongst GRB hosts lyman17 , although some local events do appear in such galaxies. These diagnostics suggest that the host of GRB 221009A is not especially unusual amongst the hosts of either long or short-duration GRBs.

sources.summary: The paper models HG as an edge-on star-forming disk host of GRB 221009A, using HST geometry, GRB position, stellar mass, and star-formation information for photon-ALP conversion.

quotes.quote: This geometry is remarkable because it corresponds to a rare edge-on orientation of this disk galaxy. We compare it with a nearby (8.4 Mpc) edge-on spiral NGC 891, for which a/b 0.24 NGC891a-b , and the disk plane is inclined to the line of sight by less than 1^ NGC891field-2019 . In what follows, we assume that HG, having even smaller a/b , is seen precisely in the disk plane. Further, HST images JWST-HST allow one to determine the projected position of GRB 221009A in HG.

According to JWST-HST , HG is a typical star-forming galaxy for its redshift, as determined by comparison with other starburst galaxies in general HST-starforming and host galaxies of long GRBs in particular HST-hosts observed by HST. The HG stellar mass M_ is modest, estimated as _ 10 (M_ /M_ )=9.00^ +0.23 _ 0.47 JWST-HST . Construction of the toy field model Further observations with longer exposures, after the GRB 221009A afterglow fades, would bring more information about HG; however, given the distance, there is no hope to obtain direct observational constraints on the magnetic field in the galaxy. First observations, already available, give sufficient information to construct a toy field model which can be used to estimate the probability of the photon-ALP conversion.

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 221009A host galaxy magnetic field for ALP constraints, using homogeneous, Milky-Way-like, and HST-informed configurations for a near-edge-on disk host.

quotes.quote: Based on the observation by LHAASO, we examine the ALP-photon oscillation effect in the host galaxy of GRB 221009A and Milky Way. Considering the uncertain magnetic field configuration in the host galaxy, we set constraints on the ALP parameters with three different models: a homogeneous magnetic field model, a model same as the Galactic magnetic field model, and a model constructed from the HST observations of the host halo. The CL_s method is applied to set the constraints in this study. We find that the constraints derived with three host galaxy magnetic field models are comparable.

Fortunately, the recent observations of Hubble Space Telescope (HST) have revealed some important properties of the HG . The HST observations show that the disc-like HG is viewed close to edge-on. GRB 221009A is found to be close to the nuclear regions of the HG with an offset of 0.25'' . Based on these results, an improved HG magnetic field model is constructed in Ref. .

gmw_00007534.name: CXOJ 123716.7+621733
gmw_00007534.overview: CXOJ 123716.7+621733 is an active galactic nucleus and quasar located at a redshift of z=1.146 [arXiv:astro-ph/0404364, arXiv:1504.03329]. The system contains a compact radio core [arXiv:1301.1253] and exhibits a strong, potentially broad iron K line in its X-ray spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/0404364, arXiv:1504.03329].

sources.summary: The paper reports a broad Fe K line in CXOJ 123716.7+621733 at z=1.146, using Chandra data and optical/radio properties to identify the source as an AGN of uncertain optical type.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a strong iron K line in the hard X--ray selected source CXOJ 123716.7+621733 in the Chandra Deep Field North survey at z= 1.146. The analysis is made possible by the very deep exposure 2 Ms and low background of the ACIS detector. The line profile seems to be inconsistent with a narrow feature.

The spectroscopic redshift ( z=1.146 ) is based on a single OII 3727 line (Barger et al. 2002). The compact optical morphology, the hard X--ray spectrum and the presence of a radio source coincident with the X--ray position (Richards 2000) leaves no doubt about the AGN nature of this object. On the basis of the available optical spectrum it is not possible to determine if the object is a Type 1 or a Type 2 AGN.

sources.summary: The source is a 0.35 mJy radio object with an optical counterpart at z=1.146, compact-plus-extended MERLIN-VLA structure, Chandra detections, and a compact VLBI core.

quotes.quote: VLA J123716+621733 is a 0.35mJy radio source with an I=22.2 optical counterpart at a redshift of 1.146 cowie04 . The MERLIN-VLA survey detected this radio source, finding a compact component with a 0.6 arcsec one-sided extension to the south-west muxlow05 . The infrared-bright object was also detected in the deep Chandra survey, in both the hard and soft bands. Our VLBI detects a compact core containing about half of the VLA flux density.

sources.summary: XID 390N is described as a z=1.146 quasar with a relatively strong Fe K line and possible second redshifted iron line from the accretion disk or an outflow.

quotes.quote: XID 390N : This z=1.146 quasar has a relatively strong iron K line ( EW=0.38^ +0.13 _ 0.14  keV, rest frame, =50  eV fixed), which could be broad, as leaving free to vary in the fit, we obtain <556  eV. Clear residuals suggest the presence of another emission line at E 5.9  keV (rest-frame) with EW 0.28  keV, which could be a relativistically redshifted iron line from the inner parts of the SMBH accretion disc or an outflow (e.g., fabian2000 ). Although very interesting, more investigation on the emission lines of this quasar is beyond the scopes of this paper.

gmw_00007535.name: NGC 5903
gmw_00007535.overview: NGC 5903 is an E2 elliptical galaxy that forms a pair with NGC 5898 [arXiv:0711.3607]. It exhibits a small rotation, a central alpha-element deficiency [arXiv:0711.3607], and is identified as an extreme high mass-deficit core galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603439].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5903 as an E2 elliptical paired with NGC 5898, showing small rotation, dust, X-ray emission, and central alpha-element deficiency with higher central metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 5903 (morphological type E2 - RC3) forms a pair with the elliptical galaxy NGC 5898; both galaxies are the brightest members of a small group composed of NGC 5903, NGC 5898 and ESO 514-G 003 maia89 . NGC 5903 presents a small rotation around the semi-major axis spar85 . Dust is observed in a small region ( 100 pc) ferr99 . NGC 5903 presents X-ray emission with no evidence of ionized gas osull01 . The effective radius of NGC 5903 is Re=37 car93a .

The central regions of NGC 6868 and NGC 5903 present a deficiency of alpha elements with respect to the external parts, which suggests different star-formation histories in both regions. With respect to the metallicity, the central regions of both galaxies have higher [ Z/Z_ ] values than the external parts. The range in metallicity spanned by the sampled regions of NGC 6868 appears to be larger than in NGC 5903.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5903 as the most extreme high mass-deficit core galaxy in its comparison, noting that its inferred black-hole and mass-deficit ratios depend strongly on published velocity dispersions.

quotes.quote: A few galaxies in Figure fig:mdefobs have significantly larger mass deficits; the most extreme object is NGC 5903 with M_ def /M_ 4.5 .

In the case of NGC 5903, for which M_ was computed from the M_ relation, we note that published velocity dispersions for this galaxy range from 192 km s ^ 1 smith-00 to 245 km s ^ 1 davies-87 ; the corresponding range in the inferred black hole mass is 1.36 10^8 M_ /M_ 4.45 10^8 and the range in M_ def /M_ is 1.9 M_ def /M_ 6.2 .
(The former point is only relevant to galaxies -- unlike NGC 5903 -- for which M_ has actually been measured.)

gmw_00007536.name: IC 2487
gmw_00007536.overview: IC 2487 is a disc galaxy located approximately 54.8 Mpc away [arXiv:2008.07177]. It is either isolated or situated within a sparse group, exhibiting a warped structure and a lopsided neutral hydrogen distribution [arXiv:2008.07177].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 2487 as a case study, modeling its observed warp and lopsided H I distribution as effects of a dark-matter wake from dynamical friction.

quotes.quote: We quantify the effects that the tidal field, which is generated by the density wake of DF, have on the galaxy IC 2487 (also known as CIG 340). This galaxy is a disc galaxy located at a distance of 54.8  Mpc FL:2013 and is either an isolated or lies in a sparse group. We select an isolated galaxy The nearest neighbour to the IC 2487 galaxy is at 0.6 Mpc in projection, which (in combination with typical line-of-sight velocities) is equivalent to 2.9 Gyr of motion Scott:2014 . in order to reduce other possible phenomena responsible for lopsidedness or warp production.

For quantifying the effect of tidal forces in the galaxy, we, first, model the ability of IC 2487 to generate a DM density wake by gravitational scattering of DM particles. The generation of this wake depends on the gravitational potential of the galaxy ( x ) which can be decomposed into a stellar and a DM components. Secondly, we model the lopsidedness in the HI distribution and velocity field of the IC 2487 galaxy using measurements of the 21-cm line emission.

As a case study, we selected the isolated galaxy IC 2487 to estimate the properties of the background dark matter distribution in order to be able to reproduce the observed lopsidedness, warp and other HI distribution features. We estimated that the necessary local density of the surrounding dark matter field is 10^4 - 10^5 M_ kpc^ 3 and the necessary velocity dispersion of dark matter particles should be 80 km s^ 1 .

gmw_00007537.name: 4FGL J0258.1+2030
gmw_00007537.overview: The active galactic nucleus 4FGL J0258.1+2030 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2302.10080, arXiv:2308.16699]. It features a heavily reddened, featureless power-law spectrum with a redshift lower limit of z > 0.3 [arXiv:2302.10080].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4FGL J0258.1+2030 as a BLL candidate associated with two IceCube events; its reddened, featureless power-law spectrum gives no emission lines and only a redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: 4FGL J0258.1+2030 : This source was proposed as a BLL in the BZCAT catalogue BZCAT and is quoted as a plausible association with two neutrino events: IceCube-191231A GCN26620 and IceCube-211125A GCN31126 . The spectrum is heavily reddened (E(B-V)=1.1) and appears featureless (see Fig.  fig:spectra ). The continuum is described by a power-law shape ( 0.8), which confirms the BLL classification of the target. No emission lines with EW > 0.8  are found and from the absence of the absorption lines of the host galaxy, we can set a redshift lower limit z  >  0.3, consistent with the previous result of shaw2013 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4FGL J0258.1+2030 as an AGN source in the IC-211125A region, includes it in follow-up coverage, computes TeV upper limits, and generates an SED.

quotes.quote: Two sources in the ROI were identified as potential counterparts to the neutrino event: the bright optical transient AT2021afpi, which was detected by MASTER and classified as a classical nova , and the AGN source 4FGL J0258.1+2030. During the first night of observations on November 29th, 2021 pointed at the position of the Nova source with a 0.5 ^ ``wobble" offset In wobble observation mode the source direction has an offset in declination with respect to the camera center. . In the following four nights, a manual pointing pattern without wobble offsets was defined to cover both sources. However, due to bad weather conditions and resulting unstable trigger rates, only 5 hours of observations were analyzed.
Upper limits were then computed in the energy range between 0.42 and 100 TeV centered on the AGN position, as shown in fig:ic211125Amap .
Additionally, both and conducted observations during the neutrino alert , and a SED was generated on the 4FGL J0258.1+2030 source position (see fig:ic211125Ased ) .

gmw_00007538.name: PKS 0829+046
gmw_00007538.overview: PKS 0829+046 is a gamma-ray luminous BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2308.16699, arXiv:1812.10614] that exhibits rapid optical variability [arXiv:0711.1158] and intra-night flux variations [arXiv:1812.10614]. Its radio jet is misaligned with its kiloparsec-scale radio structure [arXiv:0711.1158], and the source was recently associated with a neutrino flare [arXiv:2308.16699].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0829+046 as a BL Lac source followed after an IceCube flare, with no VHE detection but optical and X-ray detections and no SED variation.

quotes.quote: PKS 0829+046 is a BL Lac type object located at RA: 08h 31m 48.88s, Dec: +04 ^ 29' 39.09''. The neutrino flare occurred on December 22nd, 2021, lasting 7.9 days. It had a pre-trial significance just above the trigger threshold (3.03 ) and a false alert rate of 0.055/year.

However, the source was not detected at VHE, and an upper limit on the integral flux was computed above an energy threshold of 224 GeV. Similarly, the telescope did not detect the source. Nevertheless, during the IceCube alert , the source was detected in the optical band by ATOM and in the X-ray band by . The flux and upper limit data points are presented in fig:gfupks0829_sed . Comparisons with archival data reveal no significant variation in the SED associated with the neutrino flare.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0829+046/OJ 049 as a radio-loud blazar with misaligned radio structures, stable rich VLBI polarization geometry, and evolving polarized jet knots.

quotes.quote: 0829+046 0829+046 or OJ 049 is a ray loud blazar Dondi95 which shows rapid and large optical variability LillerLiller75 . The 1.4 GHz VLA images of AntonucciUlv85 and Giroletti04a show a two-sided structure with an extended and curved region of emission to the southeast. Previous VLBI images show a VLBI jet extending to the northeast Jorstad01 , clearly misaligned with the kpc-scale radio structure. Our VLBI images (Fig.  fig:0829 ) reveal the rich polarization structure of the VLBI jet, whose inferred B field geometry has remained roughly constant over about five years. The predominant jet B field is longitudinal to the jet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies this BL Lac as gamma-ray luminous, detects significant INOV on all three monitored nights, and attributes it plausibly to non-thermal synchrotron emission.

quotes.quote: 3FGL J0831.9+0430: 3FGL J0831.9+0430 or PKS 0829+046 is a ray luminous BL Lac object and it is observed at three different epochs. A significant INOV is detected during all three nights (Figure fig_inov3 ) and this is probably the first report of the intra-night flux variations from this source. It is important to note that the source was ray quiescent during our monitoring campaign. However, its IR-optical-UV spectrum is dominated by the non-thermal synchrotron emission 2011MNRAS.414.2674G and hence the observed INOV could be originated from it. The long term KAIT monitoring reveals a moderate activity in the optical band during our observing run.

gmw_00007539.name: 1ES0229+200
gmw_00007539.overview: 1ES0229+200 is a high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2308.16699]. In August 2022, IceCube detected a short neutrino flare lasting 1.2 days from this source, though subsequent observations showed no spectral variation compared to archival data [arXiv:2308.16699].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1ES0229+200 as a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac with a short IceCube flare, follow-up observations, quality-limited data, and no significant spectral variation.

quotes.quote: 1ES0229+200 is a high-frequency peaked BL Lac (HBL) located at RA: 02h 32m 49s, Dec: +20d 17m 1s . On August 25th, 2022, IceCube detected a short neutrino flare from this source, lasting 1.2 days. The flare had a pre-trial significance of 3.09 and a false alert rate of 0.050/year.

Observations by were carried out over five nights starting on August 27th, 2022, resulting in approximately 13.5 hours of data. However, the observations were affected by unstable weather conditions and only 8 hours are passing quality criteria. During the neutrino alert , the source was observed in the optical band by ATOM, in the X-ray band by , and in the high-energy gamma-ray band by . The flux and upper limit data points are presented in fig:gfu1es0229_sed . Comparisons with archival data reveal no significant variation in the spectral distribution associated with the neutrino flare.

gmw_00007540.name: SHOC 486
gmw_00007540.overview: SHOC 486 is a metal-deficient Green Pea analogue and Wolf-Rayet galaxy [arXiv:1705.07958]. It exhibits a somewhat spherical morphology with a dense central region that hosts compact X-ray emission [arXiv:1705.07958].

sources.summary: The paper observes SHOC 486 as a metal-deficient Green Pea analogue and Wolf-Rayet galaxy, detecting compact X-ray emission consistent with the L_X-SFR-metallicity relation.

quotes.quote: We were awarded joint Chandra / HST observations of the two candidates with the shortest required exposure times: SHOC 486 and SDSS J084220.94+115000.2. The remaining sample candidates required observation times 100 ks. classified, with certainty, that SHOC 486 is a Wolf-Rayet galaxy -- a galaxy that exhibits evidence of Wolf-Rayet stars in its integrated spectra.

We find X-ray luminosities of L_X(0.5-8 keV )=1.4^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and 3.6^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 within the entire defined ellipses for SHOC 486 and J0842+1150, respectively. Based on the associated SFR and metallicity for each galaxy, we use the L_X -SFR--metallicity relation of to predict X-ray luminosities of 4.0^ +4.7 _ 2.1 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and 5.0^ +6.0 _ 2.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. The predicted values have 1 uncertainties based on the dispersion of the L_X -SFR--metallicity relation and the uncertainties in SFR and metallicity. The measured X-ray luminosities are in agreement with these predictions.
From HST images (Figure fig:hst_xrayContours ), we see that SHOC 486 is somewhat spherical in its morphology and contains a dense central region, from which the X-rays originate.

gmw_00007541.name: SDSS J084220.94+115000.2
gmw_00007541.overview: SDSS J084220.94+115000.2 is a metal-deficient luminous compact galaxy that serves as a Green Pea analogue [arXiv:1705.07958]. It exhibits an apparent interaction between two star-forming clumps that host its X-ray emission [arXiv:1705.07958].

sources.summary: The paper observes SDSS J084220.94+115000.2 as a Green Pea analogue, measuring X-ray luminosity and identifying interacting star-forming clumps that host the X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2017 X-ray observations of two metal-deficient luminous compact galaxies (LCG) (SHOC 486 and SDSS J084220.94+115000.2) with properties similar to the so-called Green Pea galaxies were obtained using the Chandra X-ray Observatory .

We find X-ray luminosities of L_X(0.5-8 keV )=1.4^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and 3.6^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 within the entire defined ellipses for SHOC 486 and J0842+1150, respectively. Based on the associated SFR and metallicity for each galaxy, we use the L_X -SFR--metallicity relation of to predict X-ray luminosities of 4.0^ +4.7 _ 2.1 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 and 5.0^ +6.0 _ 2.7 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. The predicted values have 1 uncertainties based on the dispersion of the L_X -SFR--metallicity relation and the uncertainties in SFR and metallicity. The measured X-ray luminosities are in agreement with these predictions.

We can see from Figure fig:hst_xrayContours that the two Green Pea analogs exhibit drastically different morphologies. J0842+1150 shows an apparent interaction between two X-ray emitting clumps of clusters of star forming regions, whereas SHOC 486 is a much more simple, spherical shape showing a single region of X-ray emission.

gmw_00007542.name: J1610+1811
gmw_00007542.overview: J1610+1811 is a high-redshift quasar that features a luminous candidate jet [arXiv:1901.00057]. The galaxy exhibits X-ray emission along the direction extending from its radio core to an associated radio lobe [arXiv:1901.00057].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1610+1811 as a new high-redshift quasar with significant X-ray emission along the radio-core-to-lobe direction and a luminous candidate jet.

quotes.quote: J1610+1811 Figure fig:J1610 plots the 0.5 to 7 keV X-ray data from our observation of J1610+1811. There is significant X-ray emission along the dashed line that extends from the quasar radio core to an extended radio lobe. The 8 counts associated with the jet correspond to a flux of 10 ^ 14 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . If interpreted as isotropic emission, the luminosity would be 9 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 . Those numbers could be a factor of 2 larger or smaller due to the detection being just above threshold.

gmw_00007543.name: TXS 0952-217
gmw_00007543.overview: TXS 0952-217 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of 2.95 [arXiv:2405.09884]. It features a compact steep spectrum radio source and an ionized gas outflow that extends far beyond its radio structure [arXiv:2405.09884].

sources.summary: The paper studies TXS 0952-217, a z=2.95 high-redshift radio galaxy with a 21 kpc bipolar red emission component and an outflow extending beyond its compact radio source.

quotes.quote: In this work we present the results obtained for the central regions of the \ nebula in TXS 0952-217, an HzRG at a redshift of 2.95 Brookes_2008 located at RA=09:54:28.9, Dec=-21:56:53.6.

When observed with this method, the red component shows a bipolar morphology, extending 2 7 ( 21 kpc) along the NW-SE direction, and on both sides of the AGN position, while the main component is more compact ( 1 8 or 14 kpc).

The radio source associated with TXS 0952-217 is unresolved, with a limit on its deconvolved size of 10 kpc; with this and its steep radio spectrum, it fulfills the criteria for compact steep spectrum (CSS) sources (see, e.g., odea21 ). CSS sources, along with gigahertz peaked spectrum radio sources, have been associated with the earlier evolutionary phases of RGs (see Fanti_95 , Collier_2018 ). The MUSE observations indicate that the outflow of ionized gas extends far beyond the radio structure. This is unusual, given that the expansion speed of the radio structure is expected to be higher than that of the outflow, in particular in the early phases of expansion where velocities as high as 0.1 have been observed in CSS (e.g., polatidis03 ), while the projected outflow speed is just 500 .

gmw_00007544.name: AGC 111946
gmw_00007544.overview: AGC 111946 is a dwarf galaxy with a resolved stellar population that shows evidence of recent star formation [arXiv:1402.3723]. It has been identified as a member of the NGC 672 galaxy group [arXiv:1402.3723].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

The Environment Around AGC 110482, AGC 111164, AGC 111946, AGC 111977, AGC 112521: The NGC 672 and NGC 784 Groups There are six SHIELD galaxies located in region (4) in Figure  fig:all_groups and the final plot of Figure  fig:subgroups . With the exception of AGC 748778 (discussed in the previous subsection), all of the SHIELD systems have been previously associated with two galaxy groups, namely the NGC 672 and NGC 784 groups e.g., Karachentsev2004 . Specifically, AGC 110482, AGC 111946, and AGC 112521 have been identified as part of the NGC 672 group. The heliocentric radial velocities of the 3 SHIELD galaxies range from 274 367 km s ^ 1 compared with the higher velocity of 429 km s ^ 1 for NGC 672.

gmw_00007545.name: AGC 174585
gmw_00007545.overview: AGC 174585 is a dwarf galaxy with a distance measured between 5 and 12 Mpc using the tip of the red giant branch method [arXiv:1402.3723]. The galaxy exhibits recent star formation and is located 0.9 Mpc away from the 14+19 association, making a physical connection unlikely [arXiv:1402.3723].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

The Environment Around AGC 174585 AGC 174585 is located in region (3) in Figure  fig:all_groups and the third plot of Figure  fig:subgroups . This system does not have any close association with other systems, but lies 0.9 Mpc from the previously identified association, 14+19 , which includes DDO 47, KK 65, UGC 4115, and UGC 3755 Tully2006 . The \ velocities of the members of the 14 + 19 range from 272 341 km s ^ 1 , compared with a velocity of 356 km s ^ 1 for AGC 174585. Given the large physical separation, it is unlikely that AGC 174585 is part of the 14+19 association.

gmw_00007546.name: AGC 174605
gmw_00007546.overview: AGC 174605 is a dwarf galaxy characterized by a late morphological type, low luminosity, and gas content [arXiv:1402.3723]. It is an isolated system with no known galaxies within a 1 Mpc radius, and its closest neighbors are located 3.2 Mpc away [arXiv:1402.3723].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

Truly Isolated: AGC 174605, AGC 182595, AGC 748778, AGC 749237 AGC 749237 is located in region (1), AGC 174605 and AGC 182595 are located in region (3), and AGC 748778 is located in region (4) in Figure  fig:all_groups . No known galaxies were identified within a 1 Mpc radius of these four galaxies. The four SHIELD galaxies have properties (i.e., late morphological type, low luminosity, significant gas content) consistent with other known isolated galaxies Karachentsev2011 . The closest systems identified are as follows: AGC 174605: Closest neighbors are the pair of galaxies DDO 47 and KK 65 at a distance of 3.2 Mpc that are part of the 14+19 association. AGC 182585: Closest neighbor is the galaxy LSBCD564 0 at a distance of 1.3 Mpc. AGC 748778: Closest neighbor is the galaxy UGC 00685 at a distance of 2.4 Mpc. AGC 749237: Closest neighbor is the galaxy IC 3308 at a distance of just over 1 Mpc. Note that three of these systems are at distances greater than 10 Mpc. Thus, their isolation may be due to our incomplete knowledge of the galaxy population at these larger distances.

gmw_00007547.name: AGC 731457
gmw_00007547.overview: AGC 731457 is a dwarf galaxy with a distance measured between 5 and 12 Mpc [arXiv:1402.3723]. It is located 0.9 Mpc away from the DDO 83 system, with its nearest identified neighbor situated 0.5 Mpc away [arXiv:1402.3723].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for this SHIELD dwarf galaxy, finds recent star formation from its CMD, and uses the distance to reassess its local environment.

quotes.quote: Here, we measure the distances to the 12 SHIELD galaxies to be between 5-12 Mpc by applying the tip of the red giant method to the resolved stellar populations imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope. Based on these distances, the \ masses in the sample range from 4 10^6 to 6 10^7 , with a median \ mass of 1 10^7 . The TRGB distances are up to 73 farther than flow-model estimates in the ALFALFA catalog.

AGC 731457 lies 0.9 Mpc from another system, DDO 83. AGC 749241 is situated in a more populated volume, forming part of 1.6 Mpc linear structure with six other galaxies. Similarly to the NGC 672 and NGC 784 groups, these galaxies are likely bound, lying along a dark matter filament. The nearest neighbor identified is 0.5 Mpc away. Four galaxies, AGC 174605, AGC 182595, AGC 748778, and AGC 749237 appear to be truly isolated with no known neighbors identified within a radius of 1 Mpc, although three of these galaxies are at relatively large distances (i.e., 10 Mpc) where our knowledge of the galaxy population is incomplete.

gmw_00007548.name: 4C +07.04
gmw_00007548.overview: The radio source 4C +07.04 is a low-redshift active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1702.04788]. Observations with the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope showed that the source is unresolved, and no associated 21-cm or OH absorption was detected [arXiv:1702.04788].

sources.summary: The paper targets this low-redshift radio source for associated 21-cm and OH absorption, reports the GMRT setup and imaging quality, and finds no absorption detection.

quotes.quote: 4C +07.04 (0114+074) : The \ band was observed for a total of 1.05 hours. Five antennas (14, 20, 22, 23 30) were non-functioning, with antennas 19 and 26 also being flagged due to a severe bandpass ripple, leaving 300 baseline pairs. Self calibration of the phases produced a superior image in which the source was unresolved by the 5.6 4.3 synthesised beam. The OH band was observed for a total of 1.27 hours and after flagging of the non-functioning antennas (14,20,22,23,30), 300 baseline pairs remained. Again, a far superior image was obtained through self calibration of the phases, giving a synthesised beam of 4.1 3.5 . The extracted spectrum shows a strong emission feature, which we believe to be an artifact (see Sect. sec:ef ).

We have undertaken a survey for associated \ 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in six z 0.2-0.4 radio sources with the GMRT. Despite selecting targets which have ionising photon rates lower than the critical value, above which the neutral gas is believed to be completely ionised, and reaching the sensitivities of previous detections, we do not detect either transition. Given that the OH absorption strength is expected to be 10^ 4 times that of the \ 21-cm absorption cdbw07 , the non-detection of this radical is not surprising, especially in the knowledge that our targets are not as reddened as originally believed.

gmw_00007549.name: UM 355
gmw_00007549.overview: UM 355 is a low-redshift radio source [arXiv:1702.04788]. Observations of the galaxy with the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope detected no associated 21-cm or OH 18-cm absorption [arXiv:1702.04788].

sources.summary: The paper targets this low-redshift radio source for associated 21-cm and OH absorption, reports the GMRT setup and imaging quality, and finds no absorption detection.

quotes.quote: UM 355 (0137+012) : \ was searched for a total of 1.05 hours. After flagging non-functioning antenna 20, antennas 23, 28, 29 and 30 were removed, due to less than ideal phase calibration. Although all of the remaining phases were well behaved, self calibration of this source could not produce a satisfactory image. The calibration was then obtained from the phase calibrator LBQS 0056--0009, and the data from the unflagged 351 baseline pairs. The synthesised beam was 5.3 3.4 giving the partially resolved main component and a separate feature (see Sect. sec:rs ).

We have undertaken a survey for associated \ 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in six z 0.2-0.4 radio sources with the GMRT. Despite selecting targets which have ionising photon rates lower than the critical value, above which the neutral gas is believed to be completely ionised, and reaching the sensitivities of previous detections, we do not detect either transition. Given that the OH absorption strength is expected to be 10^ 4 times that of the \ 21-cm absorption cdbw07 , the non-detection of this radical is not surprising, especially in the knowledge that our targets are not as reddened as originally believed.

gmw_00007550.name: 4C +06.21
gmw_00007550.overview: 4C +06.21 is a low-redshift radio source [arXiv:1702.04788]. Observations with the Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope did not detect associated 21-cm or OH 18-cm absorption in the source [arXiv:1702.04788].

sources.summary: The paper targets this low-redshift radio source for associated 21-cm and OH absorption, reports the GMRT setup and imaging quality, and finds no absorption detection.

quotes.quote: 4C +06.21 (0454+066) : \ was searched for a total of 0.66 hours. In addition to antenna 20, antenna 4 was found to be non-functioning with antenna 26 giving extremely noisy spectra. After flagging these, 351 baselines pairs remained from which the image was produced, using 3C 120 for phase calibration. The source was unresolved by the 4.3 3.2 synthesised beam. For the OH band, antenna 20 was non-functioning and after the removal of the badly behaving antenna 12, 378 baseline pairs remained.

We have undertaken a survey for associated \ 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in six z 0.2-0.4 radio sources with the GMRT. Despite selecting targets which have ionising photon rates lower than the critical value, above which the neutral gas is believed to be completely ionised, and reaching the sensitivities of previous detections, we do not detect either transition. Given that the OH absorption strength is expected to be 10^ 4 times that of the \ 21-cm absorption cdbw07 , the non-detection of this radical is not surprising, especially in the knowledge that our targets are not as reddened as originally believed.

gmw_00007551.name: 4C +04.49
gmw_00007551.overview: 4C +04.49 is a low-redshift radio source [arXiv:1702.04788]. Radio imaging of the source reveals a double structure, but searches for associated 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption have yielded no detections [arXiv:1702.04788].

sources.summary: The paper targets this low-redshift radio source for associated 21-cm and OH absorption, reports the GMRT setup and imaging quality, and finds no absorption detection.

quotes.quote: 4C +04.49 (1456+044) : \ was searched for a total of 0.47 hours. After removing non-functioning antennas (4 and 26), the non-functioning baseline pair 2--8 was also removed, leaving 377 pairs. Self calibration of the phases proved unsatisfactory and so the nearby 3C 298 (1416+067) was used. Severe RFI below 1016 MHz required removal of the first 70 remaining channels, leaving 420. The 4.9 3.4 beam reveals a double source (see Sect. sec:rs ).

We have undertaken a survey for associated \ 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in six z 0.2-0.4 radio sources with the GMRT. Despite selecting targets which have ionising photon rates lower than the critical value, above which the neutral gas is believed to be completely ionised, and reaching the sensitivities of previous detections, we do not detect either transition. Given that the OH absorption strength is expected to be 10^ 4 times that of the \ 21-cm absorption cdbw07 , the non-detection of this radical is not surprising, especially in the knowledge that our targets are not as reddened as originally believed.

gmw_00007552.name: PKS 1509+022
gmw_00007552.overview: PKS 1509+022 is a low-redshift radio source [arXiv:1702.04788]. A search for associated 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in the galaxy yielded no detections of either transition [arXiv:1702.04788].

sources.summary: The paper targets this low-redshift radio source for associated 21-cm and OH absorption, reports the GMRT setup and imaging quality, and finds no absorption detection.

quotes.quote: PKS 1509+022 : Was searched in the \ band for a total of 0.73 hours. After flagging the non-functioning antennas (4, 22, 23 and 26), phase calibration, using 3C 327.1, failed to produce a quality image even, after the removal of poorly performing antennas (17, 24, 28 and 30), which left 231 baseline pairs. This was probably due to the fact that this calibrator is over one hour in distance from the target source. Self calibration of the source required removal of the aforementioned antennas before the phases could be calibrated. Even so, no good image could be produced and so the spectrum was obtained by averaging visibilities of the remaining 231 baselines.

We have undertaken a survey for associated \ 21-cm and OH 18-cm absorption in six z 0.2-0.4 radio sources with the GMRT. Despite selecting targets which have ionising photon rates lower than the critical value, above which the neutral gas is believed to be completely ionised, and reaching the sensitivities of previous detections, we do not detect either transition. Given that the OH absorption strength is expected to be 10^ 4 times that of the \ 21-cm absorption cdbw07 , the non-detection of this radical is not surprising, especially in the knowledge that our targets are not as reddened as originally believed.

gmw_00007553.name: IRAS 19154+2704
gmw_00007553.overview: IRAS 19154+2704 is an OH absorber galaxy that exhibits a disturbed morphology indicative of a past or ongoing interaction [arXiv:2312.14286]. Its inner region is excited by an active galactic nucleus that drives a broad ionized-gas outflow, while outer regions show contributions from star formation [arXiv:2312.14286].

sources.summary: The paper presents IRAS 19154+2704 as an OH absorber galaxy with disturbed morphology, AGN-dominated inner excitation, two ionized-gas kinematic components, and a broad AGN-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: We present a two-dimensional study of the gas distribution, excitation and kinematics of the OH absorber galaxy IRAS 19154+2704 using Gemini GMOS-IFU observations. Its continuum image shows a disturbed morphology indicative of a past or on-going interaction. The ionised gas emission presents two kinematic components: a narrow ( 300 km s ^ 1 ) component that may be tracing the gas orbiting in the galaxy potential and a broad ( 500 km s ^ 1 ) component which is produced by an Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) driven outflow, with velocities reaching 500 km s ^ 1 which may exceed the escape velocity of the galaxy. The emission-line ratios and BPT diagrams confirm that the gas excitation in the inner 2 kpc is mainly due the AGN, while in regions farther away, a contribution from star formation is observed. We estimate a mass outflow rate of M _ out =4.0 2.6 M _ yr ^ 1 at a distance of 850 pc from the nucleus.

gmw_00007554.name: AGC 198606
gmw_00007554.overview: AGC 198606 is a dwarf galaxy nicknamed the "Friend of Leo T" [arXiv:1908.00438]. It contains a stellar overdensity located at a distance of 880 kpc that overlaps with its neutral hydrogen half-flux ellipse [arXiv:1908.00438].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes AGC 198606, still finding a significant stellar overdensity, now at 880 kpc and overlapping the H I half-flux ellipse.

quotes.quote: As explained in Section sec:intro , we also previously reported the detection of a stellar population associated with AGC 198606 in janesh15 , and nicknamed this object ``Friend of Leo T''. At the time, our search algorithm detected the highest significance overdensity ( = 92 ) at 380 kpc. In this work, we have applied a different algorithm to remove extended sources from our photometric catalog and reanalyzed the data (see the description of this method in Section sec:methods ).

Using the different extended source cut algorithm, we identify a different overdensity as the most significant in the field, located 9 \ to the West of the previously reported overdensity at a distance of 880 kpc. At a significance of = 97.9 this overdensity is still potentially associated with the \ distribution, overlapping the half-flux ellipse. This stellar overdensity is now approximately twice the distance of Leo T, though our argument in janesh15 that AGC 198606 could be associated with Leo T based on the kinematics of the gas still applies, with the line of sight separation between the gas distributions remaining the same.

gmw_00007555.name: AGC 215417
gmw_00007555.overview: AGC 215417 is an extremely faint, low-mass dwarf galaxy candidate located at a distance of 350 kpc [arXiv:1908.00438]. It is identified as a robust stellar overdensity and is unlikely to be associated with any known nearby galaxies of similar radial velocity [arXiv:1908.00438].

sources.summary: AGC 215417 is identified as a robust, very faint stellar overdensity at 350 kpc, with no likely nearby galaxy association at similar velocity.

quotes.quote: AGC 215417 is located at 350 kpc with a significance of = 98.2 . At this distance it is the lowest mass object among the robust detections and is also extremely faint. It is also unlikely to be associated with any of the known galaxies with similar radial velocities ( cz = 215 ) within 10 \ on the sky since they are all located at much greater distances (at least 1 Mpc). AGC 215417 was observed in bellazzini15a as their object ``L''. Though their analysis did not consider it to be an optical counterpart, their smoothing algorithm detected a region of higher point source density at approximately the same sky position as our overdensity.

gmw_00007556.name: AGC 219656
gmw_00007556.overview: AGC 219656 is a dwarf galaxy candidate located at a distance of 900 kpc [arXiv:1908.00438]. It has a recessional velocity of 192 km/s and has no obvious velocity-matched neighbors within 10 degrees of its position [arXiv:1908.00438].

sources.summary: AGC 219656 is one of the highest-significance detections, placed at 900 kpc with no obvious close velocity-matched neighbors, though WSRT follow-up had not favored it.

quotes.quote: AGC 219656 is the other highest significance detection in the sample ( = 99.9 ), and is located at a distance of 900 kpc. It has a recessional velocity of 192 , and no obvious neighbors within 10 \ of its position that also have a recessional velocity cz < 500 . AGC 219656 is included in the sample of UCHVCs observed with WSRT in adams16 , but was not selected as a good candidate for follow-up based on the higher-resolution WSRT data.

gmw_00007557.name: AGC 249525
gmw_00007557.overview: AGC 249525 is a dwarf galaxy with an associated stellar population located at an estimated distance of 1.64 Mpc [arXiv:1908.00438]. It exhibits a large gas-to-stellar mass ratio and has no clearly associated neighboring galaxies [arXiv:1908.00438].

sources.summary: AGC 249525 has a higher-significance stellar counterpart than AGC 198606, estimated at 1.64 Mpc with large gas-to-stellar mass ratio and no clear neighbors.

quotes.quote: The second paper in the series, hereafter J17 janesh17 , described the detection of a stellar population associated with the UCHVC AGC 249525 at a higher significance than that of AGC 198606. The stellar counterpart in this case had an estimated magnitude M_V between 4.5 and 7.1 and was more distant, at 1.64 0.45 Mpc away. Its to-stellar mass ratio was also large, but with a wide possible range (between 9 and 144, depending on how the optical magnitude was estimated). Though this object has no clearly associated neighbors, the most likely neighbor is the faint irregular galaxy UGC 9128, which has a distance of 2.3 Mpc tully13 and is < 10 degrees away in sky position.

gmw_00007558.name: AGC 268069
gmw_00007558.overview: AGC 268069 is a dwarf galaxy candidate located at a distance of 700 kpc [arXiv:1908.00438]. It has an estimated stellar-mass upper limit of 1.6 × 10⁵ solar masses and lacks cataloged galaxy neighbors with similar systemic radial velocities within 10 degrees [arXiv:1908.00438].

sources.summary: AGC 268069 is a 99.9-significance candidate at 700 kpc, with no cataloged similar-velocity galaxy neighbors and a high estimated stellar-mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: AGC 268069 shares the highest significance in our sample ( = 99.9 ), at a distance of 700 kpc. There are no cataloged galaxy neighbors within 10 degrees that have similar systemic radial velocities cz=131 ; adams13 or distances; however, ten other ALFALFA sources with radial velocities between cz=100 and cz=160 are located less than 6 \ away on the sky. AGC 268069's upper limit estimated stellar mass is the second highest of the UCHVCs in Table tab:optical , at 1.6 10^5 . AGC 268069 was observed in bellazzini15a as their object ``W'', and was not associated with an optical counterpart in that work. If the imaging data from bellazzini15a is centered on the ALFALFA \ centroid of AGC 268069, the overdensity we have identified here is outside of the footprint of those images.

gmw_00007559.name: 1ES 1218+304
gmw_00007559.overview: 1ES 1218+304 is a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2301.00991]. Its optical and ultraviolet fluxes are dominated by emission from its host galaxy, while its multi-wavelength spectral energy distribution is well-fitted by a synchrotron-self Compton model [arXiv:2301.00991].

sources.summary: The paper studies 1ES 1218+304 as a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac, finding no Fermi-LAT change during reported TeV flaring, minute-scale X-ray variability, host-dominated optical/UV, and SSC-modeled SEDs.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: R.A. = 12 21 26.3 (hh mm ss), Dec = +30 11 29 (dd mm ss)

quotes.quote: We report the multi-wavelength study for a high-synchrotron-peaked BL Lac 1ES 1218+304 using near-simultaneous data obtained during the period from January 1, 2018, to May 31, 2021 (MJD 58119-59365) from various instruments including Fermi-LAT, Swift-XRT, AstroSat, and optical from Swift-UVOT TUBITAK observatory in Turkey. The source was reported to be flaring in TeV ray during 2019 but no significant variation in Fermi-LAT is observed. A minute scale variability is seen in SXT light curve suggesting a compact emission region for their variability.

We analyzed the data during these flaring episodes (5-7 January 2019 and 15-20 January 2019). Our findings show that the optical/UV fluxes are dominated by the host galaxy emission while modeling multi-wavelength Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) using the JetSet code. SEDs are best fitted with the synchrotron-self Compton (SSC) model under a one-zone leptonic model.

gmw_00007560.name: NGC 718
gmw_00007560.overview: The galaxy NGC 718 features a Type II.o-OLR profile with a profile break that corresponds to an outer pseudoring [arXiv:0709.3505]. It also hosts an additional, faint ring located at larger radii [arXiv:0709.3505].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 718 as a Type II.o-OLR profile whose break matches an outer pseudoring, with an additional faint ring and a mixed inner/outer scale-length fit.

quotes.quote: The break in the profile at 41 matches the outer pseudoring studied by kennicutt86 . Note that there is an additional, faint ring at larger radii, visible as a bump in the profile at 70 . The scale length (24 ) of the major-axis fit by bba98 is intermediate between our h_ i and h_ o values; this is not surprising, since their fit extends somewhat beyond the break radius and thus includes light from both parts of the profile.

gmw_00007561.name: NGC 4319
gmw_00007561.overview: NGC 4319 possesses an outer one-arm spiral structure that can also be interpreted as a tidal tail [arXiv:0709.3505]. This structure produces a profile bump at 70 arcseconds, obscuring the transition between the inner and outer disks [arXiv:0709.3505].

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4319's outer one-arm spiral or tidal-tail feature, which creates a profile bump and makes the Type III-d classification and break measurement uncertain.

quotes.quote: This galaxy possesses a spectacular outer 1-arm spiral, which could also be interpreted as a tidal tail. This structure produces the dramatic bump in the profile at 70 , obscuring the transition between the inner disk and the outer disk. The III-d classification --- and the break radius measurement -- is therefore somewhat dubious. A combination of bright stars and strong residual structure in the image (including flat-fielding artifacts) prevents us from reliably tracing the profile down to our nominal level.

gmw_00007562.name: NGC 4531
gmw_00007562.overview: NGC 4531 is a barred galaxy with a bar that was first identified in H-band imaging [arXiv:0709.3505]. This bar structure has a position angle of 127 degrees and measured sizes of 8.5 and 14 arcseconds [arXiv:0709.3505].

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 4531 to the barred sample after H-band imaging reveals its bar, then measures bar sizes, position angle, distance, and Type III-s profile status.

quotes.quote: This galaxy was not included in the bar-size study of erwin05 because no bar was visible in the optical images. However, analysis of an H band image from the GOLDMine database gavazzi03 indicates that there is a bar in this galaxy. Using the same approach as erwin05 , we find bar sizes of = 8.5 and = 14 for this galaxy, with the bar having a position angle of 127 . The distance is from tonry01 .

gmw_00007563.name: NGC 4699
gmw_00007563.overview: NGC 4699 is a short-bar disk galaxy that features a composite bulge containing both a primary pseudo-bulge and a small classical bulge [arXiv:1707.03537]. Photometric observations of the galaxy indicate that its outer disk profile exhibits a Type III structure [arXiv:0709.3505].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4699 as an observed short-bar disk galaxy with a composite pseudobulge/classical bulge, explaining why it can occupy the high black-hole mass-ratio region.

quotes.quote: According to the decomposition of Erwin15 , NGC4699 is a galaxy with a composite bulge containing a primary pseudo-bulge (mass 36 M_ ) and a small classical bulge ( 11 M_ ). Thus, NGC4699 should also be classified as a galaxy with a pseudo-bulge.

NGC4699 only harbors a single short bar. The absence of strong outer bars allows short bars to persist for longer because of the lack of interaction between two bars. Thus, it is not surprising that M81 and NGC4699 are present at the cyan shaded region.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 4699's R-band profile is limited by field of view and scattered light, but a public B-band profile supports its basic Type III nature.

quotes.quote: The only R band image we were able to obtain of this galaxy was with the NOT. Due to the small field of view and strong scattered light problems, we are not able to trace the outer disk profile out as far as we might otherwise. We note, however, that the basic Type III nature of the profile is present in a profile profile derived from the publically available B band image of the Ohio State University Bright Spiral Galaxy Survey eskridge02 ; unfortunately, the latter image also has a small field of view, and so the precise nature of the profile beyond 150 remains unknown.

gmw_00007564.name: NGC 5740
gmw_00007564.overview: NGC 5740 is a nearby barred Sb spiral galaxy and a member of a galaxy group [arXiv:0801.1069]. It features a rotating disk spanning approximately 30 kiloparsecs, a Type III-d profile with lopsided asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:0709.3505], and a one-sided extension that may indicate ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:0801.1069].

sources.summary: The paper detects NGC 5740 as a nearby barred Sb group member with a large H I extension, measured disk gas mass and kinematics, and possible ram-pressure stripping.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: a dynamical center of R.A. 14 ^ h 44 ^ m 24.4 ^ s , Decl. 1 ^ 40' 52", with approximate uncertainty 3" in R.A. and 8" in Decl.

quotes.quote: A nearby member of this galaxy group, NGC 5740, is also detected. It shows a prominent one-sided extension which may be the result of ram pressure stripping.

There is a disk of about 30 kpc diameter and a large extension to the NNW. The disk has a mass of about 5.3 10^9 \ and the extension 9.4 10^8 . For comparison, 1986AJ.....92..742S quote a mass in atomic hydrogen of 7.0 10^9 , scaled to our distance.

A ROTCUR analysis of the disk indicates a PA of 339 0.5^ , an inclination of 58 2^ , V_ sys = 1586 6 km s ^ 1 , a dynamical center of R.A. 14 ^ h 44 ^ m 24.4 ^ s , Decl. 1 ^ 40' 52", with approximate uncertainty 3" in R.A. and 8" in Decl. The first-moment map suggests regular rotation continues into the NNW extension. Keeping V_ sys and the dynamical center fixed, and fitting the receding half of the galaxy only, the extension exhibits little change in PA but a continued fall in V_ rot and an increase in inclination to 61^ at 2' and 70^ at 4'.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5740 as Type III-d because its post-break isophotes show lopsided asymmetric spirals and disk-like ellipticity, with a very deep surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: The isophotes outside the break radius show signatures of lopsided, asymmetric spirals, and the overall ellipticity is slightly higher than that of the isophotes inside the break radius. Thus, we consider the the light beyond the break radius to be part of the disk, and classify this as a Type III-d profile. This is one of our deepest profiles, with 28.2 R mag arcsec ^ 2 .

gmw_00007565.name: NGC 7280
gmw_00007565.overview: NGC 7280 is a gas-rich lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261] that hosts a secondary bar [arXiv:0707.4599] and an inner polar gaseous ring [arXiv:1907.07261]. While there is no active star formation in its disk [arXiv:1907.07261], the galaxy exhibits ionized gas in its outer regions that counterrotates relative to its stars [arXiv:1907.07261].

sources.summary: NGC 7280 has a secondary bar, a relatively gentle ellipticity drop, weak-bar MD-like behavior, and a direct-image position-angle transition marking the bar-to-outer-disc boundary.

quotes.quote: Comments on Individual Galaxies Our images of NGC 357, NGC 1211 and NGC 7280 reach the 25 B-band mag arcsec ^ 2 isophote. On the other hand, in the cases of NGC 799 and NGC 1358 our surface photometry analysis goes approximately only half as far. For the former, an SDSS image (Sloan Digital Sky Survey -- http://www.sdss.org) shows that the bar is relatively weak and is embedded in a lens. It is interesting to note that erw03 found a secondary bar in NGC 7280, which is likely the cause of the first peak we find in its ellipticity profile in the nuclear region (at 1 arcsec from the centre -- Fig. res7280 ).

NGC 7280 also has a drop in ellipticity clearly less sharp than those occurring in the MH-type bars, and our images for this galaxy are amongst our deepest ones. Again, the bar is evident in the ellipse fits (see Figs. gals and res7280 ).

In the position angle radial profile of the direct image of NGC 7280 it is easy to notice the transition between the bar and the outer disc starting at 21 arcsec from the centre, but it takes 11 arcsec more for this change to reach the threshold of 10 ^ (see Fig. res7280 ). Hence, in the following, we will use L_ phase =21 arcsec for the direct image of NGC 7280.

sources.summary: The paper uses a deep composite profile for NGC 7280, finds tentative outer excess, clear inner Type II.o structure, and tentatively favors an OLR break over classical truncation.

quotes.quote: The profile is derived from our combined deep INT-WFC images, supplemented by a profile from our WIYN image at < 5 , since the latter was higher resolution and was less saturated in the center. Figure fig:comp compares the individual profiles from both images. The composite profile is the deepest one in our sample, with 29.0 R mag arcsec ^ 2 . The excess flux at > 100 in the INT-WFC profile appears to be real, but this is a region where we have heavily masked extended halos from nearby bright stars, so it is very difficult to derive accurate isophote shapes.

Since the deviation from the outer exponential starts at only 1 mag arcsec ^ 2 above , we consider this a tentative III profile only. The inner part of the profile ( < 100 ) is clearly Type II.o. Whether this is likely an OLR break or a classical truncation is somewhat harder to determine (there is no visible outer ring to help us), because the two estimates of the bar length vary so much.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7280 as a gas-rich lenticular with an inner polar gaseous ring, polar neutral hydrogen, no disk star formation, and outer ionized gas counterrotating relative to stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 7280. It is one more lenticular galaxy which attracts our attention for many years. Once we had found an inner polar gaseous ring in the center of NGC 7280 sil7280 . Later, in the frame of the ATLAS-3D survey, it has been confirmed that the neutral hydrogen distribution in this galaxy is indeed aligned with the polar axis atlas3d_13 . This galaxy was treated as a gas-rich S0 from the beginning chamaraux87 , and star formation was carefully searched for in its disk pogge_esk93 .

But there was no current star formation found in the disk of NGC 7280. Instead, we have discovered counterrotation of the ionized gas with respect to the stars beyond the central region, at R>10^ countersau . Now, with a full map of the ionized-gas velocities (Fig. fig_fpi ), we confirm the central compact polar disk; the outer gas is distributed asymmetrically and counterrotates with respect to the stars (Fig. fig_ls ).

gmw_00007566.overview: The active galaxy 1328+254 is a compact steep-spectrum radio source [arXiv:2202.13119] and a radio-loud quasar [arXiv:2011.03130] at a redshift of 1.055 [arXiv:2202.13119]. It features a complex, curving radio jet [arXiv:2011.03130, arXiv:2202.13119] and exhibits strong optical, ultraviolet, and X-ray variability [arXiv:2205.07497].

sources.summary: The paper treats 3C 287 as a CSS quasar with an 8 kpc projected jet, double-power-law radio emission, multiple radio structures, significant submillimeter non-thermal contribution, and non-thermal X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: -3C 287 is a CSS with a projected radio jet of 8 kpc. Because the jet inclination angle for this object is unknown, we were unable to estimate its de-projected jet length. Also we were unable to find the R_ CD value for this object, therefore adopted the average value of the CSS quasars in our sample (Section sec:radio ) which resulted in 10 core contribution. The radio emission is modeled as a double power-law with a cutoff at submm/FIR wavelengths (see Table tab:best_radio_fit ,) resulting in significant non-thermal contribution at submm wavelengths. The VLBI and MERLIN observations indicates the presence of multiple radio structures including a curving jet Fanti1989 .

The X-ray spectrum fitted by the accretion disk model implies a significant contribution from a non-thermal component. The X-ray data obtained with XMM-Newton and Chandra Salvesen2009,Wilkes2013 find a soft X-ray spectrum ( =1.8) that can be fitted with a simple power-law.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1328+254 as a CSS radio source with complex jet structure and an approximately 130 mas displacement tied to a newly brightened component.

quotes.quote: 1328+254 was classified as a compact steep-spectrum (CSS) radio source with a complex structure and several jet components 1989A&A...217...44F,1990A&A...231..333F at z = 1.055 1985PASP...97..932S . 1989A&A...217...44F suggest that the core is associated to the brightest feature (component A), the jet structure is helical and shaped by precession. However, 1998A&A...332...10M and 1998AJ....115.1295K have doubts about the actual core position. 1998A&A...338..840P found that the core is not well-defined within the complex radio structure, and the jet precession model is questionable.

This radio source yields the largest observed displacement (about 130 mas) over very short observational time (3 yr) between 2014 June and 2017 June. The enormous position shift between the 2014 and 2017 epochs is most probably linked to a brightening of a new component in the north-eastern direction from the original compact structure detected before 2014. The phase centre seems to reach its maximum offset from the 2014 position around the end of 2019 and has started an apparent motion in opposite direction in 2020 (Fig. fig6 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C287 with H-ATLAS J133038.1+250901, concluding its 500 micron flux is blazar-dominated and discussing its synchrotron and variability behavior.

quotes.quote: The blazar FBQS J133037.6+250910 (the core of the bright radio source 3C 287) is located on top of the brightest 350 m and 500 m pixels of H-ATLAS J133038.1+250901, while the 250 m position is 11.16'' away. We therefore concluded that also in this case the 500 m flux density is dominated by the blazar.

The low-frequency excess is particularly conspicuous in the cases of 3C286 (J133108.4+303034) and 3C287 (J133038.1+250901); its contribution at 1.4 GHz makes the spectral index between 1.4 and 5 GHz, conventionally used to discriminate between flat- and steep-spectrum sources, somewhat steeper than the canonical boundary of 0.5 . However, the data show that the synchrotron emission of these sources extends up to IR/optical wavelengths, as is the case for blazars, and straightforwardly account, in particular, for the sub-mm H-ATLAS flux densities. Further evidence of the blazar nature of these sources is the strong variability especially in the optical/UV and X-ray bands. As mentioned above, J133038.1+250901 (the core of the bright radio source 3C 287) is a blazar while neither of the other two sources can be classified as a sub-mm blazar.

sources.summary: 3C 287 is a bright QSO with a compact southwest neighbor, aligned intervening sources, limited optical detections, and a compact radio core.

quotes.quote: 3C 287 (z=1.055) Our IR image of 3C 287 reveals the bright QSO with bright compact neighbor 4 to the southwest. In addition, four sources of varying brightnesses are present along a line separating the target from the bright neighboring source. Of these 4 sources, 2 show some signal in our optical image. The associated radio signal is limited to a compact core on the optical core of the QSO.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ICRF J133037.6+250910 as 3C 287, notes nearby comparable SDSS objects, and reports a 128 mas Gaia optical offset from the radio position.

quotes.quote: ICRF J133037.6+250910 ( X=55 , =128  mas) is 3C 287. On SDSS there are two optical objects of comparable magnitude (around 18) at 3 \ from each other, with the ICRF position roughly coinciding with the brighter object. From the SDSS images it is difficult to determine if this object is extended. The optical position from Gaia is offset from the radio position by 128 mas towards north-east, away from the other object, but it is difficult to see how such a faint object at 3 \ could perturb the Gaia measurements.

gmw_00007567.name: ztf18aanlzzf
gmw_00007567.overview: The galaxy ztf18aanlzzf is a radio-loud, blue Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift of 0.234 [arXiv:2311.16726]. It exhibits merging patterns and tidal features, suggesting it is likely an ultraluminous infrared galaxy triggered by a galaxy merger [arXiv:2311.16726].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ztf18aanlzzf/J1612 as a blue NLS1, likely ULIRG merger host, with repeated TDE-like flares, high star formation, and an approximately million-solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: We present a noteworthy transient event in the optical light curves of ztf18aanlzzf (SDSS J161259.83+421940.3), identified as a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) exhibiting merging patterns in the optical image. The 16-year long-term archived light curve revealed that this target stays in a steady state, while three flares occurred within the past 5 years with time separations ranging from 1 year to 3.5 years. The flare patterns of rapid brightening and slow decline following the peak, coupled with distinctive spectral features with strong He ii and rare appearance of Bowen fluorescence line emissions, indicate at least two Tidal Eruption Event (TDE) flares in ztf18aanlzzf with a time separation of 3.5 years.

Basic information J1612 is an optically blue Narrow Line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) covered by the SDSS survey at redshift 0.234. It is also known as a radio-loud target with the star-forming rate of 10^ 2.35 M_ yr^ 1 2015MNRAS.451.1795C, Jarvel2018, 2018A&A...614A..87B , which is much higher than in the local universe Kennicutt2012 . The combination of the high star formation rate, and the tidal features revealed by Subaru/HSC images see Fig. fig_subaru , depth of 26 AB mag, 2022PASJ...74..247A , J1612 is very likely a ULIRG triggered by a galaxy merger.

gmw_00007568.name: CGRaBS J0733+0456
gmw_00007568.overview: CGRaBS J0733+0456 is a high-redshift blazar located at z = 3.01 [arXiv:1902.09077]. The galaxy exhibits radio, infrared, and gamma-ray flux variations, including violent long-term infrared variability and intraday variations [arXiv:1902.09077].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGRaBS J0733+0456 as a z = 3.01 blazar with strong radio, infrared, and gamma-ray variability; correlated infrared and gamma-ray flares support its Fermi association.

quotes.quote: CGRaBS J0733+0456, first known as a radio emitter 1995ApJS...97..347G and identified as a high redshift quasar at z = 3.01 2008ApJS..175...97H , stands out for its large-amplitude mid-infrared variability during the systematical study of WISE flaring sources (Wang et al in preparation). Considering the significant radio variability 2008ARep...52..278G and the broadband flat radio spectrum spanning from 150 MHz to 15 GHz 2011ApJS..194...29R,2017A&A...598A..78I , it is not surprised that CGRaBS J0733+0456 is suggested as a blazar 2009A&A...495..691M,2014ApJS..213....3M,2018ApJ...869..133F . However, due to the low signal to noise ratio (S/N) of the W3 band detection listed in the ALLWISE catalog, the position of CGRaBS J0733+0456 in the ``WISE blazar strip'' 2011ApJ...740L..48M can not be well constrained. Interestingly, there is a ray source cospatial with CGRaBS J0733+0456, named as FL8Y J0733.8+0455 https://fermi.gsfc.nasa.gov/ssc/data/access/lat/fl8y/ or 4FGL J0733.8+0455 in the fourth Fermi LAT source catalog 2019arXiv190210045T .

In summary, we present a broadband temporal view of the high redshift blazar CGRaBS J0733+0456. Significant radio, IR and ray flux variations are identified. In particular, violent long-term IR variability with amplitude over one order of magnitude and intraday IR variations with variability timescale down to a few hours have been detected. Moreover, the IR and ray light curves share a rather similar shape, providing a decisive proof of the association between CGRaBS J0733+0456 and FL8Y J0733.8+0455.

gmw_00007569.name: DYNAMO D13-5
gmw_00007569.overview: DYNAMO D13-5 is a rotating disk galaxy [arXiv:1405.7410, arXiv:2107.01217] located at a redshift between 0.075 and 0.15 [arXiv:1405.7410]. It exhibits a high star formation rate [arXiv:1405.7410] and contains star-forming clumps that are younger near the center and older toward the edges [arXiv:2107.01217].

sources.summary: The paper observes D 13-5 as a DYNAMO galaxy with z=0.075-0.15 sample context, high star formation, rotating-disk classification, and a measured molecular gas fraction of 0.18.

quotes.quote: The systems observed were D 13-5, G 04-1, G 10-1 and H 10-2. Stellar masses for these systems range from 1-7 10^ 10 M _ and redshifts span the range z=0.075-0.15 . Star formation rates for D 13-5, G 10-1 and H 10-2 are 20 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . The star formation rate of G 04-1 is somewhat higher at 50 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . Galaxies G 04-1 and D 13-5 are classified as rotating disks, while H 10-2 and G 10-1 are classified as a perturbed rotators due to asymmetries in their velocity fields, and may be experiencing merging.
The DYNAMO galaxies have f_ gas =0.18 0.07 , 0.31 0.03 and 0.22 0.21 for D 13-5, G 04-1, and G 10-1 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper studies DYNAMO D13-5 as one of six rotating DYNAMO disks with HST UVIS data; its clumps show outward age increases with little extinction-driven colour evolution.

quotes.quote: In this work, we consider a sub-sample of six DYNAMO galaxies selected to build on a multi-wavelength study of these objects: DYNAMO D13-5, D15-3, G04-1, G08-5, G14-1, and G20-2, which are classified as rotating discs based on H kinematics.

The six DYNAMO galaxies in our sub-sample were observed with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) UVIS channel in three filters: F225W (UV wide), F336W (U), and F467M (Str o mgren b).

Nearly three-quarters (40) of the clumps we select in these six DYNAMO galaxies lie in the third category: clumps with large increases in age. This trend is particularly well illustrated in galaxies D13-5, and D15-3. This suggests that the evolution of these two colours is driven predominantly by age: the clumps are younger at the centers and older toward the edges, and extinction is not a large contributor to the clump colour evolution.

gmw_00007570.name: D15-3
gmw_00007570.overview: D15-3 is a rotating disk galaxy that has been observed with the Hubble Space Telescope [arXiv:2107.01217]. The galaxy contains stellar clumps that are younger near the center and older toward the outer edges, with little contribution from extinction [arXiv:2107.01217].

sources.summary: The paper studies D15-3 as one of six rotating DYNAMO disks with HST UVIS data; its clumps particularly illustrate outward age increases with little extinction contribution.

quotes.quote: In this work, we consider a sub-sample of six DYNAMO galaxies selected to build on a multi-wavelength study of these objects: DYNAMO D13-5, D15-3, G04-1, G08-5, G14-1, and G20-2, which are classified as rotating discs based on H kinematics.

The six DYNAMO galaxies in our sub-sample were observed with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) UVIS channel in three filters: F225W (UV wide), F336W (U), and F467M (Str o mgren b).
