This trend is particularly well illustrated in galaxies D13-5, and D15-3. This suggests that the evolution of these two colours is driven predominantly by age: the clumps are younger at the centers and older toward the edges, and extinction is not a large contributor to the clump colour evolution.

gmw_00007571.name: G08-5
gmw_00007571.overview: G08-5 is a rotating disk galaxy that has been observed with the Hubble Space Telescope's Wide Field Camera 3 [arXiv:2107.01217]. Photometric analysis of its clumps reveals a color profile characterized by a strong increase in age [arXiv:2107.01217].

sources.summary: The paper includes G08-5 among rotating DYNAMO disks with HST UVIS imaging, and cites its clump colour profile as showing a strong age increase.

quotes.quote: In this work, we consider a sub-sample of six DYNAMO galaxies selected to build on a multi-wavelength study of these objects: DYNAMO D13-5, D15-3, G04-1, G08-5, G14-1, and G20-2, which are classified as rotating discs based on H kinematics.

The six DYNAMO galaxies in our sub-sample were observed with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3) UVIS channel in three filters: F225W (UV wide), F336W (U), and F467M (Str o mgren b).

A significant fraction of the clumps in our sample show colour (age/extinction) profiles such as the ones we show for galaxies D13-5 and G08-5 in Figure fig:g04-1-c2 , where there is a strong increase in age, while fewer clumps show profiles such as the ones we show for galaxies G14-1 and G20-2, where there is no strong change in age but a strong change in extinction across the clump.

gmw_00007572.name: G20-2
gmw_00007572.overview: G20-2 is classified as a compact rotating disk galaxy based on its hydrogen-alpha kinematics [arXiv:2107.01217]. Spatially resolved extinction maps of the galaxy show only mild spatial variability and reveal no highly attenuated star-forming clumps [arXiv:2107.01217].

sources.summary: The paper treats G20-2 as a compact rotating DYNAMO disk and reports extinction mapping with mild variability and no highly attenuated star-forming clumps.

quotes.quote: In this work, we consider a sub-sample of six DYNAMO galaxies selected to build on a multi-wavelength study of these objects: DYNAMO D13-5, D15-3, G04-1, G08-5, G14-1, and G20-2, which are classified as rotating discs based on H kinematics.
Galaxies G04-1, G14-1, and G20-2 are furthermore classified as ``compact'' rotating discs green14 . These galaxies are ones for whom the SDSS band exponential scale lengths are less than 3 kpc.

Using HST H observations and Keck/OSIRIS Pa observations, bassett17 created spatially resolved ( 0.8-1 kpc) H extinction maps for galaxies D13-5, G04-1, and G20-2, showing that there is only mild spatial variability in the amount of extinction within a given galaxy, and finding no evidence for highly attenuated star-forming clumps.

gmw_00007573.name: src1
gmw_00007573.overview: The galaxy src1 is an X-ray-detected Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1507.04517]. Its spectrum is modeled with an absorbed power-law plus a thermal component, and it has an upper limit for its nuclear star-formation rate of about 20 solar masses per year [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper identifies src1 as an X-ray-detected Seyfert 2 in the compact group, models its absorbed spectrum, and places an upper limit on its nuclear star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Sources src1, src2, src3, src5, and src8 are clearly detected in all bands. src8 and src5 have already been identified in X-rays as Seyfert-like AGN earlier lamassa09,baumgartner13 . src6 and src7 are not detected (3 confidence level), while the X-ray emission visible in the \ composite image at a distance of 13 arcsec from src4 is likely due to a background quasar at z=0.831.

The 0.3 10 keV spectra of the two Seyfert 2s (src1 and src5) are modelled with an absorbed power-law plus a thermal component with temperature of kT 0.1-0.6 keV ( mekal in Xspec) emerging below 2 keV. The cold absorption gas has column densities in the range N_ H 1-20 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .
Using the relation from ranalli03 , we obtain an upper limit for the nuclear SFR of about 20 and 10M _ yr ^ 1 for src1 and src5, respectively.

gmw_00007574.name: src5
gmw_00007574.overview: The galaxy src5 is a Compton-thin, Seyfert-like active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1507.04517]. It exhibits an absorbed Seyfert 2 spectrum in X-rays and an unusually low [NII]/H ratio, which may indicate a recent galaxy interaction or the accretion of low-metallicity gas [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper treats src5 as an X-ray Seyfert-like, Compton-thin AGN with absorbed Seyfert 2 spectrum, low [NII]/H ratio, and possible interaction or low-metallicity accretion origin.

quotes.quote: Sources src1, src2, src3, src5, and src8 are clearly detected in all bands. src8 and src5 have already been identified in X-rays as Seyfert-like AGN earlier lamassa09,baumgartner13 . src6 and src7 are not detected (3 confidence level), while the X-ray emission visible in the \ composite image at a distance of 13 arcsec from src4 is likely due to a background quasar at z=0.831.

The 0.3 10 keV spectra of the two Seyfert 2s (src1 and src5) are modelled with an absorbed power-law plus a thermal component with temperature of kT 0.1-0.6 keV ( mekal in Xspec) emerging below 2 keV. The cold absorption gas has column densities in the range N_ H 1-20 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 .

Furthermore, src5 exhibits an extraordinary low [ NII ] /H ratio (the error on this ratio for type 2 AGN is much lower than the spectral offset found, see Fig. bpt ), but is clearly a Compton-thin AGN. This is very rare in the local Universe groves06 , and it could be due to a recent galaxy interaction or due to accretion of low-metallicity gas from the intragroup environment.

gmw_00007575.name: src8
gmw_00007575.overview: The galaxy src8 is a Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.035 [arXiv:1507.04517]. Its broadband X-ray spectrum is well fitted by a partial-covering absorption model [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper includes src8, NGC 3080, as a close-redshift group source detected in X-rays and modeled as a type-1 Seyfert with partial-covering absorption.

quotes.quote: We also find another source in this region (src8), which is close in redshift (z=0.035) but at larger projected separation, and include it in this study.

Sources src1, src2, src3, src5, and src8 are clearly detected in all bands. src8 and src5 have already been identified in X-rays as Seyfert-like AGN earlier lamassa09,baumgartner13 . src6 and src7 are not detected (3 confidence level), while the X-ray emission visible in the \ composite image at a distance of 13 arcsec from src4 is likely due to a background quasar at z=0.831.

In the Seyfert 1 source (src8) the broadband spectrum is well fitted with an absorption component with partial covering fraction f _ =0.41 0.07. The partial covering model is favoured with respect to a fully covering cold absorption with an F-test probability larger than 99.9 per cent.

gmw_00007576.name: src2
gmw_00007576.overview: The galaxy src2 is classified as a LINER [arXiv:1507.04517]. It exhibits an unabsorbed X-ray power-law spectrum with a high 2–10 keV luminosity, suggesting its nuclear activity is likely accretion-driven [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper classifies src2 as one of two LINER galaxies with unabsorbed X-ray power-law spectra, high 2-10 keV luminosity, and likely accretion-driven activity.

quotes.quote: Sources src1, src2, src3, src5, and src8 are clearly detected in all bands. src8 and src5 have already been identified in X-rays as Seyfert-like AGN earlier lamassa09,baumgartner13 . src6 and src7 are not detected (3 confidence level), while the X-ray emission visible in the \ composite image at a distance of 13 arcsec from src4 is likely due to a background quasar at z=0.831.

The two LINERs (src2 and src3) are modelled with a power-law continuum in the X-ray band. Both LINERs are unabsorbed and show no detectable Fe line, although the S/N is poor above 5 keV due to their X-ray flux (less than 10 net counts in the pn). Their 2--10 keV luminosity is nevertheless high. At 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 it is above the mean value found in the systematic X-ray study of the largest sample of LINERs gonzalez09 .

In terms of spectral components and shape, the X-ray behaviour of LINERs (src2 and src3) is in very good agreement with the global properties of the sample of LINERs investigated in X-rays by gonzalez09 . However, in terms of 2--10 keV luminosity, both src2 and src3 show high values ( 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 ) that is within the average value found for the AGN candidates. We conclude that our LINERs are likely accretion driven, which increase the fraction of AGNs in this CG to from 40 to 60 (from 3 to 5 out of 8).

gmw_00007577.name: src3
gmw_00007577.overview: The galaxy src3 is classified as a LINER [arXiv:1507.04517]. Its nuclear activity is likely accretion-driven, exhibiting an unabsorbed X-ray power-law spectrum with a high 2–10 keV luminosity [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper classifies src3 as one of two LINER galaxies with unabsorbed X-ray power-law spectra, high 2-10 keV luminosity, and likely accretion-driven activity.

gmw_00007578.name: src6
gmw_00007578.overview: The galaxy src6 is classified as a star-forming galaxy rather than a broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1507.04517]. It is not detected in X-rays and exhibits enhanced star formation relative to galaxies in the local Universe [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies src6 as a star-forming galaxy rather than a broad-line AGN, reports no X-ray detection, and finds elevated SFR relative to local star-forming galaxies.

quotes.quote: Sources src1, src2, src3, src5, and src8 are clearly detected in all bands. src8 and src5 have already been identified in X-rays as Seyfert-like AGN earlier lamassa09,baumgartner13 . src6 and src7 are not detected (3 confidence level), while the X-ray emission visible in the \ composite image at a distance of 13 arcsec from src4 is likely due to a background quasar at z=0.831.

It is worth noting that our classification of src6 and src7 departs from that by liu11 , who report them as broad-line AGN. In order to examine the spectra for presence of the broad lines we fit the spectra with the narrow-line components and inspect the residuals. These show no evidence for broad lines. Moreover, we find that the narrow lines ratios point to a classification of src6 and src7 as SFGs (Fig. bpt ).

Using SFR vs stellar mass relation at z=0, we find the values of SFR for src6 and src7 (see Table busca ) higher than measured in the local Universe elbaz07 . This indicates an enhanced star formation, which is especially evident in src6.

gmw_00007579.name: src7
gmw_00007579.overview: The galaxy src7 is classified as a star-forming galaxy rather than a broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1507.04517]. It is not detected in X-rays and exhibits an enhanced star formation rate compared to similar galaxies in the local Universe [arXiv:1507.04517].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies src7 as a star-forming galaxy rather than a broad-line AGN, reports no X-ray detection, and finds elevated SFR relative to local star-forming galaxies.

quotes.quote: Sources src1, src2, src3, src5, and src8 are clearly detected in all bands. src8 and src5 have already been identified in X-rays as Seyfert-like AGN earlier lamassa09,baumgartner13 . src6 and src7 are not detected (3 confidence level), while the X-ray emission visible in the \ composite image at a distance of 13 arcsec from src4 is likely due to a background quasar at z=0.831.

gmw_00007580.name: MRC 2216-206
gmw_00007580.overview: MRC 2216-206 is a giant radio galaxy located at a redshift of 1.148 [arXiv:0909.4123]. It has a linear size of 730 kiloparsecs and exhibits extended soft X-ray emission that is plausibly due to inverse Compton scattering of cosmic microwave background photons [arXiv:0909.4123].

sources.summary: The paper presents MRC 2216-206 as a giant radio galaxy with apparent ICCMB emission, XMM-Newton counts, soft emission beyond a hotspot, and spectral-analysis constraints.

quotes.quote: Finally, we present X-ray observations of two more giant radio galaxies, MRC 2216-206 and MRC 0947-249, where ICCMB emission seems to be also apparent. 3C 469.1, MRC 2216-206 and MRC 0947-249 are located at redshifts of 1.336, 1.148 and 0.854 respectively, corresponding to linear scales of 8.6 kpc/ , 8.4 kpc/ and 7.8 kpc/ \ in our adopted cosmology of H_0=70 \ km \ s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and _ =0.73 and assuming a flat universe.

We also obtained observations of giant radio galaxies MRC 2216-206 ( z=1.148 , linear size 730 kpc Kapahi1998 ) and MRC 0947-249 ( z=0.854 , linear size 530 kpc Kapahi1998 ) with XMM-Newton on 2008 May 03 and May 10, respectively, with corresponding exposure times of 22.6 ks (8.8 ks) Good time remaining after filtering to remove flares and taking dead-time intervals into account and 17.0 ks (10 ks). In the 0.3-10.0 keV band, there are 361 19 counts (of which 181 6 are background, with 47 19 soft 0.3-1.5 keV photons) for MRC 2216-206 and 195 14 counts (of which 126 6 are background, with 46 11 soft photons) for MRC 0947-249. Whereas at higher energies the X-ray images are more consistent with being point-like sources near the location of the corresponding optical host galaxy, indicated by crosses (Fig. mrc2216_coresplit and mrc0947_coresplit ), both sources show extended soft X-ray emission below 1.5 keV, which is plausibly due to ICCMB. We note that the soft emission in MRC 2216-206 appears to extend beyond the location of the South-East radio hotspot.
Spectral analysis of MRC 2216-206 was carried out exactly as for 3C 469.1, since a single-component power law fit gave an unphysically hard photon index of =0.5 0.35 .

gmw_00007581.name: MRC 0947-249
gmw_00007581.overview: MRC 0947-249 is a giant radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.854 [arXiv:0909.4123]. It has a linear size of 530 kiloparsecs and exhibits apparent inverse Compton cosmic microwave background emission [arXiv:0909.4123].

sources.summary: The paper presents MRC 0947-249 as a giant radio galaxy with apparent ICCMB emission, XMM-Newton counts, and too few counts for spectral fitting.

quotes.quote: Finally, we present X-ray observations of two more giant radio galaxies, MRC 2216-206 and MRC 0947-249, where ICCMB emission seems to be also apparent. 3C 469.1, MRC 2216-206 and MRC 0947-249 are located at redshifts of 1.336, 1.148 and 0.854 respectively, corresponding to linear scales of 8.6 kpc/ , 8.4 kpc/ and 7.8 kpc/ \ in our adopted cosmology of H_0=70 \ km \ s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and _ =0.73 and assuming a flat universe.

We also obtained observations of giant radio galaxies MRC 2216-206 ( z=1.148 , linear size 730 kpc Kapahi1998 ) and MRC 0947-249 ( z=0.854 , linear size 530 kpc Kapahi1998 ) with XMM-Newton on 2008 May 03 and May 10, respectively, with corresponding exposure times of 22.6 ks (8.8 ks) Good time remaining after filtering to remove flares and taking dead-time intervals into account and 17.0 ks (10 ks). In the 0.3-10.0 keV band, there are 361 19 counts (of which 181 6 are background, with 47 19 soft 0.3-1.5 keV photons) for MRC 2216-206 and 195 14 counts (of which 126 6 are background, with 46 11 soft photons) for MRC 0947-249.
There were insufficient counts to fit the spectrum of MRC 0947-249.

gmw_00007582.name: galaxy G
gmw_00007582.overview: Galaxy G is a luminous Sbc-Sc spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.225 [arXiv:1003.0446, arXiv:2306.12284]. It lacks an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1003.0446], and its extended circumgalactic medium contains hot gas detected through absorption line spectroscopy [arXiv:2306.12284].

sources.summary: The paper identifies galaxy G as the nearer luminous Sbc-Sc spiral whose extended halo likely hosts the two z~0.225 absorption systems, and reports its position and emission-line properties.

quotes.quote: Galaxy G is at a substantially smaller impact parameter from these absorbers compared to W. Based on the relative proximity, Savage (1998) attributed the z = 0.22496 and the z = 0.22638 absorption systems to gas in the extended halo of galaxy G. Such an inference is consistent with the statistical results from past absorber-galaxy surveys e.g. lanzetta95 , and also with the more recent survey by wakker09 , where they find the absorbers having an origin within 500 kpc of > 0.25L^* galaxies, with some substantial fraction ( 40 ) associated with the halos of > L^* galaxies. In Figure 4, we display a section of the WIYN R band image of the H 1821+643 field published by Savage (1998) indicating the position of galaxy G with respect to the sight line.

The galaxy [ RA(2000) = 18:21:54.5, DEC(2000) = +64:20:09.0 ] is located at 18^ .2 west and 26^ .7 south of the quasar, and has an angular radius of 6^ .6 that corresponds to 24 kpc. The extended source within 4^ from G is identified as a more distant galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.2986 (Schneider 1992). In Figure 4 we display the only available spectrum of galaxy G from Schneider (1992), obtained at a resolution of 25 . The strong H + [ ] 6548, 6584 emission feature was used to determine the redshift of galaxy G to a precision of 0.0005. The relatively narrow width of the Balmer emission feature and the absence of strong [ ] 4959, 5007 emission lines indicate that galaxy G does not harbor an active galactic nucleus or an exceptionally high star-formation rate. We note that the width of H feature in itself is not a strong indicator of high star formation rate (Knapen James 2009). Based on careful analyses of the imaging and spectroscopic data, Savage (1998) conclude galaxy G to be luminous spiral of morphology Sbc-Sc, consistent with the strength of the H emission line feature kennicutt92a, kennicutt92b .

sources.summary: The paper argues the z 0.225 spiral galaxy has hot CGM detected through O VII absorption, with gas 116 kpc from its center and hot mass exceeding the UV-traced phases.

quotes.quote: Here we report on the detection of the K absorption line at the redshift of a spiral galaxy at z 0.225 using 1.2 Ms of observations. This is a robust detection, clearly showing the presence of the hot gas. The mass in the hot phase is at least an order of magnitude larger than that in the cooler phases detected in the UV. The presence of hot gas 116h^ 1 kpc from the center of this galaxy provides credence to the existence of the extended CGM of the Milky Way.

We analyzed the 1.2Ms of LETG-HRC-S data in the sightline to 18 probing the CGM of an intervening galaxy at z 0.225 , corresponding to known absorption systems. We detect the K absorption at the same redshift with high confidence, tracing the hot CGM of the galaxy. The presence of such hot CGM was predicted by Narayanan2010 based on the properties of the UV absorbers. In addition to the z 0.167 galaxy in the sightline to PKS 0405-123 and the ``average'' galaxy detected by stacking analysis of Lyman limit systems ( 1), this is the only other external galaxy in which the hot CGM is detected with absorption line spectroscopy.

gmw_00007583.name: NGC 307
gmw_00007583.overview: NGC 307 is a fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:2303.01495, arXiv:2309.15911] characterized by a small bulge [arXiv:2309.15911]. It is a lightweight system with a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:2303.01495] and near-solar central line strengths [arXiv:2303.01495].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 0307 as the lowest-dispersion and lightest ETG in the sample, with near-solar central line strengths and MUSE kinematics matching earlier measurements.

quotes.quote: The sole exception is the galaxy NGC 0307, where a sampling between 1500 km/s < v_ los < 1500 km/s or N_ vel = 47 , respectively, is sufficient due to its low velocity dispersion.

The only exception here being NGC 0307, the most lightweight ETG in our sample which is markedly closer to solar (Mgb 4.7 , <Fe> 3.2 and NaD 4.5 ). Furthermore, all galaxies, including NGC 1332 and NGC 0307, follow the same radial trends, moving closer to solar with increasing radius (in the outer parts of the galaxies Mgb 4.2- 4 , <Fe> 2.5- 3.2 and NaD 3- 4.7 ; Parikh et al., submitted).
NGC 0307 : Our measurements for v_ rot , , h_ 3 , h_ 4 matched the measurements of Erwin2018 which were based on VLT-FORS1 data (see top panels of Fig. fig:ngc0307Profile )

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 307 as a fast-rotating power-law ETG whose small bulge prevents explicit gradient modeling, leaving only a weak composite mass-to-light gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 307 : As stated above, the bulge of this galaxy was too small to warrant the implementation of gradients. There is, however, a weak composite () gradient from the superposition of the two constant _ bulge , _ disk . The increase of our composite () within 1 kpc is consistent with the one found by E+18, _ bulge / _ disk = 1.1 (their values). Considering our _ bulge , _ disk best-fit model parameters, our _ bulge value is identical to the one from E+18.

gmw_00007584.name: NGC 5328
gmw_00007584.overview: NGC 5328 is an early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1306.1124, arXiv:astro-ph/0509056] that serves as the bright central member of its galaxy group [arXiv:astro-ph/0509056]. It is a dispersion-dominated system [arXiv:1306.1124] featuring a kinematically decoupled core with central counter-rotation [arXiv:2303.01495].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5328 as dispersion-dominated, high-dispersion, and the most redshifted sample galaxy; its SINFONI kinematics required special handling because of biased line-shape moments.

quotes.quote: Five galaxies are dispersion-dominated with rotational velocity not more than 50 \ and no obvious rotation axis: NGC 1407, NGC 1550, NGC 4472, NGC 5328 and NGC 5516. In this group, NGC 1550, NGC 5328 and NGC 5516 have the highest , although none are higher than NGC 4751. In NGC 1550 and NGC 5516 increases towards the centre, while in NGC 5328 it decreases.

For NGC 5328, the MPL fit yields a negative h_3 throughout the SINFONI FoV. This is atypical for early-type galaxies, regardless of whether they are rotating systems (where h_3 is usually anti-correlated with the velocity) or dispersion dominated systems ( h_3 0 ). With a systemic velocity of 4740 , NGC 5328 has the largest redshift of all the galaxies in our sample. This means that the second CO bandhead is shifted into the red end of the SINFONI spectral range, which is significantly contaminated by residual OH emission.

As described above (Sec. sinfonikinematics ), the shapes of the SINFONI LOSVDs for NGC 5328 might be systematically affected by a faint sky emission line and we consider only the measured V and to be reliable.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5328 has increasing outer wing light and h4, central counter-rotation with angular-momentum maxima, and a decoupled core in its kinematic maps.

quotes.quote: By eye, we see increasing wing-light towards larger radii in NGC 6861, NGC 1407, NGC 5328, NGC 5419 (see Figs. fig:FRellipses and fig:SRellipses ). In these same galaxies we also find an increase of h_4 (see Figs. fig:FRkinmap and fig:SRkinmap ).

Both NGC 5328 and NGC 5419 have local maxima of around /r_e 0.1 . This is due to the fact, that both galaxies exhibit counter rotation in their central regions (cf. the v_ rot maps in Fig.  fig:SRkinmap ).

Similarly striking in the kinematics maps are the decoupled cores which we found particularly for NGC 5328 and NGC 5419, as well as to a lesser extend, NGC 1407 (see Fig. fig:SRkinmap ). They are central regions were the rotation patterns of the galaxies suddenly flip and change signs.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5328 as the bright central E3 elliptical of its group, with a de Vaucouleurs profile, flat color, possible discy residuals, and an advanced evolutionary stage.

quotes.quote: NGC 5328 The surface photometry (Figure fig4 , top left) shows a ^ 1/4 surface brightness profile over a range of 7 mag suggesting that the galaxy is a ``bona fide'' elliptical. The ellipticity distribution increases in the range of 0.2 < < 0.4 up to a radius of 7 \ where it becomes flat. The b_ 4 profile is basically flat although a possible discy structure appears in the residual image in Figure fig2 (top row, right panel). We classified NGC 5328 as E3 in contrast to the classification as E1 by rc3 . The colour profile is almost flat at a value of (B--R) 1.7 mag.
Most of the early-type galaxies in groups are ordinary objects, while NGC 5328 is located in the region of bright galaxies, suggesting an advanced stage in its evolution.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 5328 constant-mass-to-light results average over its gradient model, with the prior measurement matching the main-body value but below the central value.

quotes.quote: NGC 5328 : For this galaxy, the constant- measurement is roughly an average over radius of our gradient model () . At the point where our gradient intersects with the constant model ( 5.8 in V-band, 0.6 kpc ), it is also the most well defined with respect to the uncertainties. Our previously published measurement from R+13 appears to be consistent with our _ main , but a factor 1.3 smaller than _ cen .

gmw_00007585.name: NGC 5516
gmw_00007585.overview: NGC 5516 is classified as an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1306.1124, arXiv:1310.5310] that is dispersion-dominated with no obvious axis of rotation [arXiv:1306.1124]. Its structure is best fit using multiple components [arXiv:1310.5310], and its velocity dispersion increases toward the galactic center [arXiv:1306.1124].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5516 as elliptical from its own data, places it among high-dispersion, dispersion-dominated galaxies, and uses dedicated WiFeS observations for modeling.

quotes.quote: Types are from HyperLeda (http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr), except NGC 5516 which we classify as E based on our kinematics and photometry.

Five galaxies are dispersion-dominated with rotational velocity not more than 50 \ and no obvious rotation axis: NGC 1407, NGC 1550, NGC 4472, NGC 5328 and NGC 5516. In this group, NGC 1550, NGC 5328 and NGC 5516 have the highest , although none are higher than NGC 4751. In NGC 1550 and NGC 5516 increases towards the centre, while in NGC 5328 it decreases.
For NGC 5516 3x1200 sec object exposures plus a 1200 sec sky exposure were taken on 2010 May 19.

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 5516 with NGC 7619 as having an NGC 5328-like gradient, notes Salpeter-level outer IMF compatibility, and reports small central kinematic underprediction.

quotes.quote: NGC 5516 7619 : For both of these galaxies we find gradients of a similar magnitude as for NGC 5328, and for which both our new constant- models and previous measurements from R+13, are roughly averages over radius.

Considering our total mass profiles, at 1 kpc , a local IMF with a Salpeter level bottom-heaviness is inconsistent with the fits at a level between one and two sigma for all galaxies except NGC 5516 and NGC 4751.

Similarly, but less significantly the dynamical models for NGC 5516 underpredict the centermost MUSE value, and h_4 within 3 arcsecond . However, once again, the difference in the nonparametric LOSVDs themselves is small.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5516 as elliptical, fits it with multiple components, and finds the derived deficit roughly doubles under the multicomponent treatment.

quotes.quote: We classify NGC 1550 and NGC 5516 as ellipticals, though literature classifications vary.
In the end we found that approximately one fourth of our sample galaxies were best fit using multiple components: NGC 1399, NGC 1407, NGC 4552, NGC 5516, NGC 5813, and NGC 6086.
For two galaxies (NGC 1399 and NGC 5516), on the other hand, the deficit roughly doubles in size.

gmw_00007586.name: MRC B0319-454
gmw_00007586.overview: MRC B0319-454 is a giant radio galaxy with a megaparsec-scale radio structure spanning 2.5 Mpc [arXiv:0812.2052]. The galaxy exhibits asymmetric radio lobes and a jet backflow that are influenced by gravity and density gradients in the surrounding large-scale structure [arXiv:0812.2052].

sources.summary: The paper studies MRC B0319-454 as a 2.5 Mpc giant radio galaxy whose asymmetric radio lobes and jet/backflow morphology trace density gradients, intergalactic gas, and gravity in surrounding large-scale structure.

quotes.quote: We present an investigation of the relationships between the radio properties of a giant radio galaxy MRC B0319-454 and the surrounding galaxy distribution with the aim of examining the influence of intergalactic gas and gravity associated with the large-scale structure on the evolution in the radio morphology. Our new radio continuum observations of the radio source, with high surface brightness sensitivity, images the asymmetries in the megaparsec-scale radio structure in total intensity and polarization.

We compare these with the 3-D galaxy distribution derived from galaxy redshift surveys. Galaxy density gradients are observed along and perpendicular to the radio axis: the large-scale structure is consistent with a model wherein the galaxies trace the ambient intergalactic gas and the evolution of the radio structures are ram-pressure limited by this associated gas. Additionally, we have modeled the off-axis evolution of the south-west radio lobe as deflection of a buoyant jet backflow by a transverse gravitational field: the model is plausible if entrainment is small.

gmw_00007587.name: CXOXB J143009.8+351957
gmw_00007587.overview: CXOXB J143009.8+351957 is a giant radio galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.629 [arXiv:2103.06280]. It features a radio structure extending 1.66 Mpc, with no clear evidence of restarted activity in its radio morphology [arXiv:2103.06280].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CXOXB J143009.8+351957 as a distant medium-X-ray-selected giant radio galaxy with a 1.66 Mpc radio structure, no clear restarted activity, and X-ray properties like lower-redshift hard-X-ray GRGs.

quotes.quote: Despite being previously detected in the shallow XBOOTES survey with just 5 photons murray05,kenter05 , the nuclear source (also known as CXOXB J143009.8+351957 - hereafter) is now firmly detected with 74 counts over the 0.5-7 keV band, and coincides with the geometric center of a spectacular radio structure, extended for 4' , seen in the LOFAR image at 150 MHz (Figure fig:grg_images ). Given its spectroscopic redshift z=0.629 , kochanek12 , 's extension reaches 1.66 Mpc.

The radio morphology of the GRG presented in this work does not show evidence for restarted activity, and the nuclear radio core spectrum does not appear to be GPS-like. On the other hand, the X-ray properties of the new GRG are perfectly consistent with the ones previously studied with Swift/BAT and INTEGRAL at lower redshift. In particular, the bolometric luminosity measured from the X-ray spectrum is a factor of six larger than the one derived from the radio lobes, although the large uncertainties make them formally consistent at 1 .

gmw_00007588.name: Arp 221
gmw_00007588.overview: Arp 221 is a merger remnant galaxy that appears to lack a preferred disc plane [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251]. The coordinates of its center remain stable in the inner 4 arcseconds but change further outward, while its lopsidedness peaks at 8 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251].

sources.summary: The paper finds Arp 221 has a stable inner center that changes outward, small higher-order asymmetries, and a lopsided A1 maximum near 8 arcseconds.

quotes.quote: This could be used to explain the high values of A _2 seen in many galaxies in our sample. In fact this effect is seen in all the galaxies except Arp 221 (Class I), Arp 212 (Class II) , and Arp 209 (Class III). Thus, in most cases studied, the merger remnants seem to indicate a preferred disc plane.

Arp 221: The coordinates of the centre remain constant in the inner 4" and change beyond that. The non-axisymmetric analysis shows that the A _2 , A _3 and A _4 values are small while A _1 shows a maximum of 0.25 at 8".

gmw_00007589.name: AM 0612-373
gmw_00007589.overview: AM 0612-373 is a galaxy that exhibits no substantial change in its center coordinates [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251]. It features a low A1 value, fairly high A3 and A4 values, and a high A2 value that peaks at 15 arcseconds [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251].

sources.summary: The paper finds AM 0612-373 has little center variation, low A1, high A2 peaking at 15 arcseconds, and fairly high A3 and A4.

quotes.quote: AM 0612-373: There is no substantial change in the coordinates of the center, with a total change of 1 pixel for x0 and y0. The value of A _1 is low, while A _2 is quite high, peaking at 0.6 at 15". The A _3 and A _4 values are fairly high.

gmw_00007590.name: Arp 163
gmw_00007590.overview: Arp 163 is a barred galaxy characterized by a strongly asymmetric structure and a continually changing center [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251]. Also known as NGC 4670, it is a nearby star-forming galaxy containing a bright optical source with He II emission and negligible dust attenuation [arXiv:2311.16950].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4670 as a nearby star-forming target where only source A has significant He II emission and negligible dust attenuation by all methods.

quotes.quote: For this study, we selected star-forming regions in eight nearby galaxies ( 0.0010 z 0.0095 , at distances of 3-37 Mpc) with existing He II 1640 HST-STIS UV observations from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). These UV spectra were previously obtained for He 2-10, NGC 3125, Mrk 33, NGC 4214, NGC 4670, and Tol 1924-416 as part of program 9036 (PI: C. Leitherer) taken in 2001–2003, and for NGC 3049 and Tol 89 as part of program 7513 (PI: C. Leitherer) observed in 1999-2000. Additional optical HST-STIS observations, providing He II 4686 line measurements were obtained from the HST program GO-15846 (PI: C. Leitherer), executed in 2020-21. These optical observations were carried out at the exact same location, same orientation, and with the same aperture sizes as the UV observations.

NGC 4670   Even though three sources were found in NGC 4670, only Source A has significant He II emission. The SDSS spectroscopic fiber is centered on Source A, which is the brightest optical source in this region presumably dominating the SDSS spectrum. This source is also characterized by a negligibly small dust attenuation estimate whose values agree within the errors for the three methods.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 163 as barred and strongly asymmetric, with a continually changing center, the strongest m=2 amplitude, and A1 and A4 peaks at small radii.

quotes.quote: Wozniak et al. 1995) is seen in only three cases, which we conclude have well-defined bars. These are: Arp 160, Arp 162, and Arp 163.

Arp 163: This also shows a constantly changing centre (x0 and y0) for the nearby isophotes. The value of the m=2 amplitude A _2 is the strongest, with a maximum of 0.4 between 10"-14". The value of A _1 peaks at 0.3 at 6", and A _4 peaks at 0.3 at 14". The value of A _3 is small at all radii.

gmw_00007591.name: Arp 254
gmw_00007591.overview: Arp 254 is classified as a non-barred, no-fit galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251]. It exhibits a galactic plane despite having highly disturbed outer regions, and it displays changing coordinates for its center along with large A2 and A4 structural amplitudes [arXiv:astro-ph/0605251].

sources.summary: The paper interprets Arp 254 as a non-barred no-fit galaxy with evidence for a galactic plane, changing center coordinates, and large A2 and A4 amplitudes.

quotes.quote: The high values of A _2 if taken as an indication of an inclined thick disc- in no-fit galaxies, which do not have a bar (such as Arp 254 and AM 1025-433), indicates the existence of a galactic plane even if the outer regions are highly disturbed.
Arp 254: This shows changing co-ordinates for the centre. The values of A _2 and A _4 are large as compared to the values of A _1 and A _3 .

gmw_00007592.name: UVISTA-Y3
gmw_00007592.overview: The high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy UVISTA-Y3, also known as REBELS-25, resolves into three distinct components under high-resolution imaging [arXiv:1902.10713, arXiv:2202.11118]. It is characterized by a high stellar mass [arXiv:2311.16071], cold dust [arXiv:2301.09659], a high infrared excess [arXiv:2309.17386], and attenuated ultraviolet star formation [arXiv:2202.11118].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UVISTA-Y3 as the exception in DASH imaging: its UltraVISTA footprint resolves into three labelled components, while blended photometry still favors a robust z~8 source.

quotes.quote: Inspection of the DASH mosaic at the locations of the candidate LBGs discussed in this work resulted in single, isolated sources (for the five sources that are detected at 4 ) with the important exception of one candidate, UVISTA-Y3. In Figure fig:Y3 we present an image stamp extracted from DASH with overplotted the contour of the combined J , H and K_ s imaging data. A SExtractor run identified three individual objects (with S/N 4.5, 2.9 and 2.2 ) overlapping with the UltraVISTA footprint of UVISTA-Y3, that we label as UVISTA-Y3a, UVISTA-Y3b and UVISTA-Y3c, for the three components in order of increasing declination, respectively (see Figure fig:Y3 ). The three sources are found to have relative distances of 0 5 .

Finally, in Figure fig:Y3_blended we show the SED of UVISTA-Y3 when we do not deblend its photometry using the information from the DASH imaging. This SED is best-fitted by a z 8 solution, consistent with our initial selection.
When considered as a single object, UVISTA-Y3 has a p(z>7)=0.99 , suggesting a fairly robust redshift for this source as well.

sources.summary: The paper flags REBELS-25 as an outlier with unusually attenuated UV star formation despite dust mass matching the fiducial relation, suggesting clumped dust near young stars.

quotes.quote: While, within error bars, the bulk of the REBELS galaxies lie close to the fiducial model, there are a number of outliers (namely REBELS-19 and 25) that show _ UV values that a factor 2-3 smaller for a given value. These galaxies are more dust attenuated as compared to our fiducial model predictions which is in accord with the (up to 18 ) higher dust masses shown by some of the REBELS galaxies (specially REBELS-19) when compared to the fiducial model as detailed in Sec.  dust_mass .
The only two outliers in this case are also REBELS-19 and 25 as might be expected from the discussion above.

REBELS-25 is an outlier that shows a larger attenuation of its UV SFR despite its dust mass being in perfect agreement with the fiducial relation. This might hint at dust being preferentially clumped around sites of young star formation.

sources.summary: REBELS-25 is detected in Band 8 continuum and [OIII], has cold dust, low [OIII]/[CII], and inferred steady rather than bursty star formation.

quotes.quote: We detect the dust continuum emission of two of our targets in Band 8 (REBELS-25 and REBELS-38), while REBELS-12 remains undetected.

In addition, we target and detect [ OIII ] 88 m emission in both REBELS-12 and REBELS-25, and find L_ [ OIII ] / L_ [ CII ] ratios of approximately unity, low compared to the L_ [ OIII ] / L_ [ CII ] 2 - 10 observed in the known z 6 population thus far.

For REBELS-25, we only find the Band 6 continuum to be resolved inami2022 . Based on a 2D Gaussian fit, we determine r_ IR = 1.8 0.5 kpc, and we adopt an identical size for the unresolved [ OIII ] emission. The [ CII ] emission is similarly not resolved, such that the beam size translates to a robust upper limit on its [ CII ] size of r_ [ CII ] < 3.7 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper singles out REBELS-25 as an unusually far-infrared-bright high-redshift source with high IRX; its placement may reflect a spatial offset between UV and FIR emitting regions.

quotes.quote: We see one source that is significantly in excess of the others, with IRX = 1.2 0.2 . This is the unusually FIR bright object REBELS-25 that is discussed further in  Hygate23 . This source is included in our stacks, however our results are unchanged if it is removed.
There is a weak trend that the FIR brightest galaxies tend to be above the relation, such as REBELS-25 (as has been seen for ULIRGS; see discussion below).

The fact that A1689-zD1, as well as REBELS-25 in Fig.  fig:irx , appear in the upper left region of the   diagram could be due to a spatial offset between the regions emitting in the rest-frame UV and FIR.

sources.summary: The paper treats REBELS-25 as a high-mass REBELS galaxy with extra FIR continuum constraints, a recurrent high-mass SFH, and metal-rich dust-to-gas agreement when using molecular gas alone.

quotes.quote: It is worth mentioning that for two REBELS galaxies at the high-mass end of the sample (REBELS-25 and REBELS-38) additional FIR continuum measurements around 90 m rest-frame are available ( Algera23 ), allowing for more robust constraints on the dust SED and thus on dust temperature and mass. We comment on the effects of these new measurements throughout the paper.

The choice of comparing the three individual galaxy models with binned data is justified by the fact that REBELS-14 show a SFH that is quite typical of the low stellar mass bin, while REBELS-29 and 25 have two SFHs that are recurrent in the high stellar mass bin. In addition, this choice allows us to better show the typical evolution of an individual galaxy for different dust evolutionary setups.

A relatively good agreement between data and model DtGs for the metal-rich case can be recovered for REBELS-25 by assuming molecular gas mass alone, whereas by adding HI masses the models still largely overestimate the dust-to-gas ratios.

gmw_00007593.name: ESO 0291-G009
aliases_local: the cD galaxy of S e rsic 159-03
gmw_00007593.overview: ESO 0291-G009 is a cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0002403] and brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1002.3297] located at the center of a cooling flow in the Sersic 159-03 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0002403]. It hosts a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1002.3297] and exhibits extended star formation [arXiv:1411.2604] associated with optical filaments [arXiv:astro-ph/0002403].

sources.original_name: the cD galaxy of S e rsic 159-03
sources.summary: The paper identifies a central far-infrared source with the cD galaxy of S e rsic 159-03, describes its cooling-flow and nebular context, and links its flux to cooling-flow dust and star formation.

quotes.quote: The S e rsic 159-03 cluster (Abell S1101, z=0.0564) is of richness class 0, Bautz-Morgan type iii with a central dominant cD galaxy (Abell et al. ). A cooling flow is present, and Allen and Fabian ( ) found a mass deposition rate of M = 231^ +11 _ 10 M_ yr^ 1 from ROSAT PSPC data. The cooling flow is centered on the cD galaxy which exhibits nebular line emission.

The cD galaxy The central infrared source, C100-1, is detected in all maps except at 100 m . The measured fluxes in the two independent observations also agree within the limits. We therefore regard the source as real.

A central source, C100-1, is attributed to the cD galaxy which contains optical filaments, but our optical images do not reveal significant evidence of dust lanes. The fluxes measured for C100-1 are of the same order of magnitude as expected from dust related to star formation in the cooling flow.

sources.summary: The paper studies the central galaxy of S ersic 159-03, finding 1-3 solar masses per year of extended star formation, displaced cooling-flow activity, and cold dust north of the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Star Formation in Condensing, Low-Entropy ICM In 3 and previous works mcdonald10,mcdonald12a,werner11,oonk10 we show significant evidence for star formation in the central galaxy of S ersic 159-03. The measured star formation rate of 1--3 M _ yr ^ 1 (derived from UV, H , far-IR, and [ C ii ] ) is concentrated in the center of the galaxy, but extends in all directions for 10--15 kpc and to the north for 35 kpc.

The most recently formed stars, indicated by the peak H /UV ratio, are spatially coincident with the lowest entropy ICM, that has been displaced by the AGN 7.5 kpc north of the center of the cD galaxy. This suggests that the displacement of the cooling core from the direct vicinity of the central AGN can temporarily break the feedback cycle and lead to cooling and star formation that is offset from the center of the galaxy. Extended FIR continuum emission is observed north to north-east of the central galaxy. If this FIR emission is located at the distance of the cluster then this implies that large amounts ( 10 ^8 M _ ) of cold (12 K) dust may be associated with the H filament 30 kpc north of the central galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 0291-G009 as a LINER-like brightest cluster galaxy in Sersic 159-03, with soft ionising radiation and a radio-loud active nucleus.

quotes.quote: In the work presented here we focus on two LINER-like BCGs from our previous samples, PGC 071390 in Abell 2597 (hereafter A2597) and ESO 0291-G009 in Sersic 159-03 (hereafter S159). Optical ( [ O III ] /H and [ O I ] /H , VD97; BTh ) and mid-infrared spectra ( [ Ne III ] / [ Ne II ] and [ Ne V ] / [ Ne II ] , Jaffe Bremer in prep.) of these BCGs indicate that their ionising spectrum is very soft i.e. they are extreme LINERs VD97; BTh . However, these BCGs do contain radio-loud AGN. Their 1.4 GHz radio specific luminosity is, 29.3 10 ^ 31 and 1.6 10 ^ 31 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 for A2597 and S159 Bi08 respectively, which are typical for BCGs in cool core galaxy clusters Q08 .

gmw_00007594.name: UGC 6817
gmw_00007594.overview: UGC 6817 is a low-central-surface-brightness galaxy characterized by a knotty, irregular surface and a tail extending to the southwest [arXiv:astro-ph/9910178]. It features a southern star formation region with a possible shell structure and enhanced velocity dispersion around the peripheries of its H II regions [arXiv:1203.6213].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 6817 as a low-central-surface-brightness galaxy with an exponential profile, smooth reddening color gradient, irregular knotty surface, tail, and possible supernova disruption.

quotes.quote: UGC 6817 : has a low central surface brightness and a large scale length. The SBP is close to a pure exponential, and the color gradient is smooth, showing a reddening with increasing radius. The aspect of the galaxy is peculiar, with a knotty irregular surface, and tail extending to the south-west. It could have been disrupted by supernova events. The isophotes also seem twisted, although not examined in detail in this work.

sources.summary: The paper finds that DDO 99 has a relatively simple I-sigma diagram, with only a weak possible shell and enhanced dispersion mostly around HII-region peripheries.

quotes.quote: DDO 99 Unlike the previous case, the I- diagram for DDO 99 has a simpler shape -- there are no pronounced features, except for the horizontal lane and a broad triangular region with a high . Some kind of a hint for a possible shell is a tiny cloud of points in the diagram with I>-15.7 , >21 (marked in green). On the map of the galaxy, these points are grouped mainly on the edge of the southern star formation region, the size of this possible shell is comparable to the spatial resolution of 50 pc. The remaining regions of increased (plotted in orange in the figure) are distributed on the periphery of HII regions.

gmw_00007595.name: UGC 7335
gmw_00007595.overview: UGC 7335 is an elongated, elliptical-shaped irregular galaxy featuring a bright central lane [arXiv:astro-ph/9910178]. It exhibits a near-exponential surface-brightness profile and a shallow color profile that becomes slightly redder at larger radii [arXiv:astro-ph/9910178].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7335 as an elongated irregular galaxy with a bright central lane, near-exponential surface-brightness profile, and shallow color profile that reddens outward.

quotes.quote: UGC 7335 : elongated irregular galaxy with a bright central lane. The shape of the galaxy as a whole is elongated and elliptical. Its SBP is close to a pure exponential. The color profile is shallow, getting slightly redder with radius, and showing a bluing close to the center.

gmw_00007596.name: UGC 7356
gmw_00007596.overview: UGC 7356 is a classical nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9910178]. It is located close in projection to NGC 4258 and features a flat color profile with a slightly bluer nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9910178].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 7356 as a classical nucleated dwarf elliptical, notes its nearby velocity, unresolved brightest stars, and flat color profile with a slightly bluer nucleus.

quotes.quote: UGC 7356 : classical nucleated dwarf elliptical. It has a published heliocentric radial velocity suggesting it is close-by (but it is close in projection to NGC 4258). The brightest stars were not resolved by Makarova et al. \ 1998A&AS..128..459M , confirming the dwarf elliptical nature. Its color profile is flat, with a slightly bluer nucleus.

gmw_00007597.name: UGC 7639
gmw_00007597.overview: UGC 7639 is an irregular dwarf galaxy with a knotty inner surface and a purely exponential stellar distribution [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:astro-ph/9910178]. It contains very little neutral hydrogen, which is distributed off-center and exhibits a disorganized velocity field [arXiv:1605.01638].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 07639 as an irregular dwarf with little off-center HI, disorganized velocity structure, no SparsePak observations, and a redder color consistent with little recent star formation.

quotes.quote: UGC 07639 UGC 07639 is an irregular dwarf galaxy with very little HI detected in new VLA observations (Fig.  fig:u07639sum ). The HI is actually distributed off-centre and nearly perpendicular to the stellar distribution in the 3.6 m image. Furthermore, the HI velocity field appears disorganized with only a hint of circular rotation. UGC 07639 was not targeted for SparsePak ionized gas observations due to the lack of visible H emission in the narrowband image. The stellar distribution at 3.6 m is purely exponential, which is typical for dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7639 as an irregular galaxy with inner excess over an exponential profile, knotty surface, northwest luminosity excess, and reddening color profile.

quotes.quote: UGC 7639 : irregular with a slight excess above an exponential in the inner part of its SBP. Knotty inner surface, and a slight luminosity excess to the north-west. Smooth color profile, showing a reddening with increasing radius.

gmw_00007598.name: BCG
gmw_00007598.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in the Abell 611 cluster is modeled with a dual pseudo elliptical isothermal profile [arXiv:1602.08491]. This stellar mass profile is characterized by a truncation radius of 46.2 kiloparsecs and a central velocity dispersion of 164 kilometers per second [arXiv:1602.08491].

sources.summary: The paper models the A611 brightest cluster galaxy stellar mass with a dPIE profile, using literature constraints on its truncation radius and central velocity dispersion in the lens model.

quotes.quote: BCG Newman2009,Newman2013a,Newman2013b perform a detailed analysis of the stellar mass profile of the BCG in A611 by combining SL and kinematic analyses. They model the BCG stellar mass profile using a dual pseudo elliptical isothermal profile dPIE, see Elisa2007 to fit the surface brightness profile of the BCG. The effective radius of the BCG is consistent with the truncation radius of the dPIE profile ( r_ tr,BCG =46.2 3.4 kpc Newman2013a,Newman2013b ). By combining lensing and kinematic analysis they estimate the amplitude of the dPIE profile describing the BCG stellar mass.
This amplitude is characterized by a central velocity dispersion _ 0,BCG ^*=164 33 km/s. In our lensing model, we also describe the BCG stellar mass component by using a dPIE profile.

gmw_00007599.name: source E
gmw_00007599.overview: Source E is a high-redshift galaxy located approximately 11 kiloparsecs east of source C [arXiv:1908.07777]. The galaxy exhibits a strong velocity gradient and is currently undergoing a major merger with source C [arXiv:1908.07777].

sources.summary: The paper describes source E as an eastern high-redshift component near source C, with a strong velocity gradient, dust-indicating colors, and major-merger evidence.

quotes.quote: The second source (E), which is 1.6'' ( 11 kpc) to the east of source C, shows an integrated flux density (2.6 0.2) Jy km s ^ 1 , a peak flux density (1.9 0.1) Jy beam ^ 1 km s ^ 1 , and a deconvolved size (0.8 0.1)'' (0.3 0.2)'' at a position angle (9 169)^ .

While the two brightest sources (i.e., C and E) are only separated by 100 km s ^ 1 , source W is 300 km s ^ 1 offset from source C. Sources C and W show nearly constant velocity, while source E shows a strong gradient ( 50 200 km s ^ 1 ).

The two dominant sources (C and E) are closely associated, both spatially ( 1.6'' 11 kpc) and in velocity ( 100 km s ^ 1 ), while the third source (W) is separate ( 2.8'' 18 kpc, 300 km s ^ 1 ). Source E features a strong velocity gradient, which may either suggest a rotating galaxy or a tidally disrupted galaxy, while the others exhibit nearly constant velocity. All three show velocity dispersion peaks coincident with the peak of [ CII ] emission. Due to their kinematical properties, we conclude that the three sources in this field are separate objects, not members of the same galaxy. The close spatial separation, low velocity offset, and similar luminosities (L _ CII ,C / L _ CII ,E =1.86<4 ) of sources C and E suggest an ongoing major merger lotz11 .

gmw_00007600.name: source W
gmw_00007600.overview: Source W is an unresolved, optically faint galaxy located approximately 18 kiloparsecs west of a central target [arXiv:1908.07777, arXiv:2006.04837]. It exhibits a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:1908.07777] and is likely a gas-rich or highly dusty companion that will eventually coalesce with the central source in a minor merger [arXiv:1908.07777, arXiv:2006.04837].

sources.summary: The paper describes source W as the weakest western component, offset from source C, with low velocity dispersion, unresolved continuum, and a likely future minor merger.

quotes.quote: Lastly, the weakest source, (W) which is 2.8'' ( 18 kpc) west of source C, has an integrated flux density (0.8 0.2) Jy km s ^ 1 , a peak flux density (0.9 0.1) Jy beam ^ 1 km s ^ 1 , and is unresolved.

While the two brightest sources (i.e., C and E) are only separated by 100 km s ^ 1 , source W is 300 km s ^ 1 offset from source C. Sources C and W show nearly constant velocity, while source E shows a strong gradient ( 50 200 km s ^ 1 ). The immoments task may also be used to create a velocity dispersion (moment two) map (bottom panel color of Figure mom0 ). Source W shows a relatively low velocity dispersion ( _ v,W,pk 40 km s ^ 1 ), while sources C and E exhibit strong peaks in velocity dispersion ( _ v,C,pk 70 km s ^ 1 , _ v,E,pk 80 km s ^ 1 ).

On the other hand, sources C and W are spatially and kinematically separate, but only by 18 kpc and 300 km s ^ 1 . This close separation and their large luminosity ratio (L _ CII ,C / L _ CII ,W =6.03>4 ) suggests that they will coalesce in a minor merger at a later time.

sources.summary: The paper treats S818760 as an ambiguous ALPINE line source, likely a high-redshift dusty or gas-rich companion interacting with the central UV-selected target.

quotes.quote: In addition to these eight detections we found four lines whose identification based on the available photometry is ambiguous. Two of them (S818760 and S859732) do not present any counterpart in the available catalogs and also in the multi--wavelenght images (from UV to MIR). The lack of counterparts suggests that these emissions are produced from highly dusty/high- z sources or from gas--rich galaxies with low stellar masses. The most likely associations are thus \ at 4 < z < 6 or CO transitions at lower redshifts. However, S818760 is located within 3" and has a velocity separation < 300 \ from the central target in the same pointing (see Figure fig:cutouts ).
As a consequence, it is produced very likely by a companion/interacting source with the UV--target emitting also \ but optically faint (see also 2020MNRAS.491L..18J ).

gmw_00007601.name: NGC 4152
gmw_00007601.overview: NGC 4152 is a late-type galaxy located at a distance of 30.6 Mpc [arXiv:2104.05736]. It exhibits ongoing star formation and contains ionized extraplanar gas characterized by lagging rotation, radial inflow, and vertical inflow [arXiv:2104.05736].

sources.summary: The paper selects NGC 4152 for ionised extraplanar-gas modeling and finds lagging rotation plus radial and vertical inflow in this late-type star-forming galaxy.

quotes.quote: In this work we focus on two galaxies from the DMS sample, NGC 3982 and NGC 4152. We select these two galaxies because they have sufficient S/N to reveal the relatively weak EPG emission, and their structures are well-resolved under the SparsePak spatial resolution. Besides, their position--velocity (pv) diagrams along their major axes have emission features called `beard' patterns Schaap00,Fraternali01 that deviate from the bulk rotation and extended towards the systemic velocity.
NGC 4152 is a late-type galaxy with on-going star formation. It lies at a distance of 30.6 Mpc Tully09 , with an inclination angle of 38 1 .

Best-fit values are shown in Table tab:result . The best-fit values for NGC 4152 suggest that this galaxy also has ionised EPG with lagging rotation, radial and vertical inflow, and large velocity dispersion.

gmw_00007602.name: ESO 533-4
gmw_00007602.overview: ESO 533-4 is a nearby, almost bulgeless Sc spiral galaxy viewed nearly edge-on [arXiv:1509.03841, arXiv:2311.13700]. The galaxy features distinct thin and thick stellar disks [arXiv:2311.13700], with the thick disk being approximately 10 billion years old [arXiv:2311.13700] and containing little to no counter-rotating material [arXiv:1509.03841].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 533-4 with VIMOS, finding an almost bulgeless edge-on galaxy whose thick disc has little counter-rotation and differentiated old, metal-poor stellar populations.

quotes.quote: Here we adopt a galactic arch ae ology approach to study the thick disc of ESO 533-4, i.e. we study the kinematics and the stellar populations of this galaxy in detail. ESO 533-4 is a Southern, nearby, and almost bulgeless galaxy. We present the first ever Integral Field Unit spectroscopy of an edge-on galaxy with enough depth and quality to study the thick disc.

The apparent rotational lag of the thick disc of ESO 533-4 is compatible with that expected from the combinations of two effects: differential asymmetric drift and the projection effects arising from studying a disc a few degrees ( 2-3^ o ) away from edge-on. Thus, ESO 533-4 contains little or no counter-rotating material. This is compatible with three formation scenarios: the secular dynamical heating of an initially thin disc, the formation of the thick disc at high redshift in an early turbulent disc phase, and the creation of a thick disc in a major merger event.

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO 533-4 as an observed edge-on Sc spiral comparison, noting its distinct thin and thick disks, 10 Gyr thick disk, metallicity within the simulated range, and unavailable [Mg/Fe] value.

quotes.quote: In particular, we compare qualitatively our sample to the only two edge-on spiral galaxies with clearly distinct thick and thin disks, with published two-dimensional stellar-population analyses from IFS data. One is NGC 5746, an Sb galaxy of similar mass to the most massive AURIGA galaxies, with a 12 Gyr-old thick disk Martig2021 . Another is the Sc galaxy ESO 533-4, of similar mass to the least massive AURIGA galaxies, and with a 10 Gyr-old thick disk Comeron2015 . Both thick disks are older than all AURIGA thick disks, and the difference is larger than the estimated uncertainties in observations ( 1 Gyr in Martig2021 ).

However, the two thick disks observed in NGC 5746 and ESO 533-4 show [ M/H ] values in the range covered by our simulation sample. While Comeron2015 did not provide [ Mg/Fe ] values for ESO 533-4, NGC 5746 shows higher values than our thick-disk sample.

sources.summary: The paper revises ESO 533-4 thick-disc fits after accounting for extended PSF wings, finding thin-disc dominance at faint levels and changed fitted density and scale-height ratios.

quotes.quote: For ESO 533-4 this happens at or just below surface brightness levels of 26 mag arcsec^ 2 . For this particular galaxy, the fitted _ T _ z=0 / _ t _ z=0 values are larger than in CO15 - where the PSF treatment was incorrect and thus the effect of the PSF wings was largely underestimated -- and the fitted _ T / _ t are smaller. This results in a similar thin to thick surface-density ratio, _ T / _ t . The same happens when we compare the ESO 533-4 fits here with those in CO12 , where the PSF was treated as a Gaussian core.

gmw_00007603.name: Candidate 0956+2848 986
gmw_00007603.overview: Candidate 0956+2848 986 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:2205.15388]. It is the faintest candidate in its sample and may experience modest gravitational lensing by a foreground galaxy located 3 arcseconds away [arXiv:2205.15388].

sources.summary: The paper flags Candidate 0956+2848 986 as the only SuperBoRG dropout likely to be modestly lensed, but says the correction does not alter the UV luminosity function.

quotes.quote: Candidate 0956+2848 986 is the only candidate that may experience some gravitational lensing with a magnification of =1.5 1.3 by a z 1.3 foreground galaxy located 3 arcsec away. This candidate happens to be the faintest in our sample. Its UV magnitude before magnification correction is 20.5 mag, and correcting the flux of this galaxy with the derived magnification does not significantly change the derived UVLFs.

gmw_00007604.name: LBQS1009 025
gmw_00007604.overview: LBQS1009 025 is a doubly imaged quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.74 [arXiv:1711.07492, arXiv:astro-ph/0510465]. The system is gravitationally lensed by an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of 0.871 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465].

sources.summary: The paper identifies A1012-0307 with LBQS1009-0252, describes its two deblended quasar spectra, and reports wavelength-dependent flux ratios consistent with lensing effects.

quotes.quote: A1012-0307, a known lens with power-law flux-ratios A1012-0307 is the known lens LBQS1009-0252 hew94 . The NTT data can be well deblended into two z_ s =2.745 quasar spectra, showing the same lines with the same shape (fig.  fig:confspec ). The redshift from NTT spectra is slightly higher than the one ( z_ s =2.739 ) quoted in its original discovery paper. Differently from the other NIQs, whose flux ratios are almost constant with wavelength, in this case the flux ratio is well fitted over the whole spectral range by f_ =3.0( /9000 )^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies LBQS1009 025 as a doubly imaged quasar whose lensing galaxy spectrum matches an E template at z=0.871.

quotes.quote: LBQS1009 025 : A doubly imaged quasar, with 1 54 image separation, discovered by Hewett et al. (1994). The quasar is at a redshift of 2.74 . The lensing galaxy spectrum, shown in Fig. LBQS1009 , is matched by an E galaxy template with a redshift of 0.871 , very close to the fundamental-plane redshift ( 0.88 ) found by Kochanek et al. (2000).

gmw_00007605.name: PMNJ1632 003
gmw_00007605.overview: PMNJ1632 003 is a radio-discovered quasar located at a redshift of 3.424 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465]. It is gravitationally lensed by a galaxy with a tentative redshift of 1.165, producing a doubly imaged system with a securely detected central image [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:astro-ph/0507522].

sources.summary: The paper describes PMNJ1632 003 as a doubly imaged radio-discovered quasar lensed by a galaxy, with a central image and a tentative lens redshift of z=1.165.

quotes.quote: PMNJ1632 003 : A doubly imaged quasar, with 1 47 image separation, discovered in radio frequencies by Winn et al. (2002b). The quasar is at a redshift of 3.424 , and is the only quasar lensed by a galaxy for which the demagnified central image has been securely detected (Winn et al. 2004). The existence of the central image sets a lower limit on the galaxy's surface density at the location of the central image.

The spectrum of the lensing galaxy is noisy and we cannot confidently identify the redshift. However, as seen in Fig. PMNJ1632_Gal_QSO a, we detect a jump in the spectrum at 8600 40 . If attributed to the Balmer discontinuity, then an absorption line at 6060 in the QSO spectrum (Fig. PMNJ1632_Gal_QSO b) may be Mg II 2800 , and the galaxy redshift would be 1.165 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PMN J1632 0033 as a known central-image gravitational lens and prototype, using mass-model convergence and shear estimates for simulations of microlensing near its central image.

quotes.quote: winn03,winn04 detected a central image at radio wavelengths in the asymmetric 2-image lens PMN J1632 0033, while inada detected a central image at optical wavelengths in the unusual 4-image cluster lens SDSS J1004+4112. Our goal in this paper is to combine the two phenomena and study microlensing of central images. The issue is timely since central images are now being observed, and compelling since the images naturally form in regions where the density of stars is high and microlensing seems inevitable. We customize our calculations to the lens PMN J1632 0033, because it is both a known central image system and also a prototype for the sorts of systems that are expected to yield the most central images in the future centers,bowman .

winn03,winn04 recently studied a wide range of mass models for PMN J1632 0033, which can be used to estimate the total convergence and shear at the positions of the images. The lens data, including the central image, are consistent with a simple power law surface mass density R^ with = 0.91 0.02 , plus a small external tidal shear. Table tbl:kapgam gives the values of and for such a model, which we adopt as inputs for our microlensing simulations.

gmw_00007606.name: PHL 1092
gmw_00007606.overview: PHL 1092 is a high-luminosity narrow-line quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0404550] classified as an extreme Population A active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01811]. It exhibits rapid X-ray variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0404550] and is characterized by a high accretion rate [arXiv:2004.05201] near the Eddington limit [arXiv:astro-ph/0404550].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PHL1092 as an extreme Population A NLS1, models its broad-line region features, and compares its black-hole mass estimates with prior optical, X-ray, and disk-fitting work.

quotes.quote: Table tab:lineratio provides UV line ratios relevant to the classification (PHL 1092 meets the ``extreme Population A'' criteria of marziani14 ), and to the tentative definition of the BLR physical conditions (Sect. physblr ). We fit \ and \ with Lorentzian profiles, representing the BLR contribution. In addition, we noticed a blue asymmetry in these lines, which was fit with a Gaussian component, following the approach of negrete18 . Moreover, we employed one Gaussian to model each of the 4959,5007 doublet.

Other works in the literature report different values of M_ BH for PHL1092. For instance, dasgupta04 using optical scaling relation for the BLR radius and single epoch method found log(M_ BH )=8.20M_ . czerny01 found log(M_ BH )=6.09 \ using X-ray variability and log(M_ BH )=8.26 \ from accretion disk fitting method. nikolajuk09 found log(M_ BH )=8.46M_ using more recent scaling relations for the optical single epoch recipe. By the same method, miniutti12 found log(M_ BH ) 8.48M_ .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes XMM-Newton observations of PHL 1092, finding a featureless dominant soft X-ray excess, rapid 35% variability, and emission consistent with an accreting black hole near the Eddington limit.

quotes.quote: We present results based on an XMM-Newton observation of the high luminosity narrow-line QSO PHL 1092 performed in 2003 January. The 0.3-10 keV spectrum is well described by a model which includes a power-law ( 2.1 ) and two blackbody components ( kT 130 eV and kT 50 eV). The soft X-ray excess emission is featureless and contributes 80 to the total X-ray emission in the 0.3-10 keV band. The most remarkable feature of the present observation is the detection of X-ray variability at very short time scale: the X-ray emission varied by 35 in about 5000 s.

Considering the high intrinsic luminosity ( 2 10 ^ 45 erg s ^ 1 ) and the large inferred mass of the putative black hole ( 1.6 10^8 M_ ), the observed time scale of variability indicates emission at close to Eddington luminosity arising from very close to the black hole. We suggest that PHL 1092 in particular (and narrow line Seyfert galaxies in general) is a fast rotating black hole emitting close to its Eddington luminosity and the X-ray emission corresponds to the high-soft state seen in Galactic black hole sources.

sources.summary: The paper describes PHL1092 as a high-emission, high-accretion AGN outlier whose observed line ratios may require higher column density, a tailored SED, and a larger dustless BLR.

quotes.quote: We expect that, on top of the effect of metallicity, sources like the PHL1092 -- which is the highest emitting source in our sample and also accreting at a very high rate =1.24, murilo2020 , can be modelled with an appropriate incident continuum, one that has a relatively higher number of energetic photons ( 1 Ryd).

For the , the maximum value ( 0.374) went up by a similar factor ( 53 ) although even with this model, we are unable to account for the and measurements for the highest emitting sources in our sample, i.e., PHL1092 (shown with a magenta diamond), I Zw 1 estimate from murilo2016 and Mrk231 from persson1988 . There is a clear U-turn feature observed in the higher ionization cases (log = -1.5 and -1.75), where the maximum values corresponds to a singular cloud density, log = 11.75 and drops when going in either direction in terms of the density. With an additional increase in the column density ( 10^ 25.5 N_H 10^ 26 cm ^ 2 ), we can extend the correlation to values consistent with the ones of PHL1092 and others, but then the optically-thin approximation ( 1 ) assumed in this work is no longer applicable.

For example, in the case of PHL1092, with L_ 5100 7.638 10^ 44 erg s^ 1 murilo2020 gives a R _ sub = 1.138 pc. This is already a 40 increase in the size of the dustless BLR as compared to what has been assumed in this paper. With the inclusion of a proper SED shape mimicking the PHL1092's emission and this increased size for the dustless BLR, the effective and values may be achievable.

gmw_00007607.name: NGC 5846cE
gmw_00007607.overview: NGC 5846cE is a compact elliptical galaxy located in the nearby NGC 5846 group [arXiv:1003.1663]. It exhibits a two-component light profile and shows structural, dynamical, and stellar population properties that suggest it was heavily tidally stripped by NGC 5846 [arXiv:1003.1663].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 5846cE as a compact elliptical in the NGC 5846 group, with two-component structure, measured stellar kinematics/populations, and properties suggesting tidal stripping by NGC 5846.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of the second compact elliptical (cE) galaxy SDSS J150634.27+013331.6 in the nearby NGC 5846 group by the Virtual Observatory (VO) workflow . This object ( M_B = -15.98 mag, R_e = 0.24 kpc) becomes the fifth cE where the spatially-resolved kinematics and stellar populations can be obtained. We used archival HST WFPC2 images to demonstrate that its light profile has a two-component structure, and integrated photometry from GALEX, SDSS, UKIDSS, and Spitzer to build the multi-wavelength SED to constraint the star formation history (SFH). We observed this galaxy with the PMAS IFU spectrograph at the Calar-Alto 3.5m telescope and obtained two-dimensional maps of its kinematics and stellar population properties using the full-spectral fitting technique. Its structural, dynamical and stellar population properties suggest that it had a massive progenitor heavily tidally stripped by NGC 5846.

The structural properties and low luminosity place NGC 5846cE between M 32 and A496cE Chilingarian+07 on the FP and its projections. At the same time, its stellar population properties, in particular very Mg/Fe overabundance, make it resembling extreme cases like A496g1 and NGC 4486B. Indeed, NGC 5846cE exhibits a two-component light profile.

gmw_00007608.name: J142230
gmw_00007608.overview: J142230 is a post-starburst galaxy that hosts a type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.06928]. Its hydrogen-alpha kinematics depart from simple rotation, and it exhibits three oxygen III components consistent with an active galactic nucleus-driven wind quenching scenario [arXiv:2310.06928].

sources.summary: J142230 is described as a post-starburst type 1 AGN whose H kinematics depart from simple rotation, with an H wing and three [O III] components tied to an AGN-wind quenching scenario.

quotes.quote: Similarly, most H velocity maps follow rotation by gravity except for several targets, such as [ 1 ] J142230, [ 2 ] J163324 and [ 13 ] J083132. Note that the wing component of H was not detected in the majority of the type 1 AGNs except [ 1 ] J142230 and [ 2 ] J163324.

For example, [ 1 ] J142230, the only PSB in our sample, shows that the [ ] profile has three Gaussian components. Moreover, the most blueshifted Gaussian component among the three has 1046 km s ^ 1 of velocity dispersion. This is consistent with a quenching scenario by the energetic AGN-driven winds e.g., Baron18 .

gmw_00007609.name: J083132
gmw_00007609.overview: J083132 is a barred galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2310.06928]. It displays elongated hydrogen-alpha emission likely associated with star-forming regions along its bar, anomalous hydrogen-alpha velocity behavior, and a radial ionization transition from active galactic nucleus to composite, star-forming, or LINER classifications [arXiv:2310.06928].

sources.summary: J083132 is identified in the paper as a barred AGN host with elongated H emission, anomalous H velocity behavior, and a radial BPT transition from AGN to composite/star-forming or LINER ionization.

quotes.quote: Barred galaxies (e.g., [ 13 ] J083132 and [ 16 ] J211334) show elongated features in the H flux maps, probably due to star-forming regions along the galactic bar.
Similarly, most H velocity maps follow rotation by gravity except for several targets, such as [ 1 ] J142230, [ 2 ] J163324 and [ 13 ] J083132.
For [ 7 ] J021708, [ 9 ] J085632, [ 11 ] J234933 and [ 13 ] J083132, there are radial trends of BPT classification, from AGN to composite and star formation (or LINER).

gmw_00007610.name: 2MFGC 04711
gmw_00007610.overview: 2MFGC 04711 is an early-type galaxy dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:1206.0719]. It is gravitationally coupled to the S galaxy, which has induced a perturbed, U-shaped rotation profile through tidal friction [arXiv:1206.0719].

sources.summary: The paper treats 2MFGC 04711 as an early-type member of the AM 0546-324 system, measuring its redshift, old stellar population, U-shaped rotation profile, and tidal coupling to the S galaxy.

quotes.quote: The spectra of 2MFGC 04711, NED02, S, and the C galaxy resemble those of early-type galaxies and no emission lines were detected. No star-forming regions and no nuclear ionization sources were detected in the observed regions of the four main galaxies. The calculated heliocentric radial velocity for the S galaxy is 20 141 10 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0696), which agrees with the radial velocity of the Abell S0546 cluster (cz = 20 893 km s ^ 1 ) ; for 2MFGC 04711, it is 20 057 10 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0693); and for NED02, it is 20 754 10 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0718) , both in agreement with quoted values in NED.

The rotation profiles of 2MFGC 04711 and NED02 are typical of tidal coupling in ellipticals with no net rotation, which results in a U-shaped rotation profile with the galaxy core at the base of the U. Both galaxies are gravitationally coupled directly with the proposed central object of the cluster, the S galaxy. The U-shaped structure is a direct observational signature of tidal friction with the extended material of the S galaxy. Internally, the no-net rotation core in the U-shaped rotation profile of both 2MFGC 04711 and NED02 seems to be slightly perturbed by the tidal interaction with the S galaxy, which lies in the center of the local gravitational potential-well of this system. 2MFGC 04711 and AM 0546-324 (NED02) are completely dominated by an old stellar population with age between 2 10^ 9 < t 13 10^ 9 yr.

gmw_00007611.name: AM 0546-324
gmw_00007611.overview: AM 0546-324 is an early-type galaxy dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:1206.0719]. It is gravitationally coupled with the central S galaxy of its cluster, which drives tidal deformation and a perturbed, U-shaped rotation profile [arXiv:1206.0719].

sources.summary: AM 0546-324, also called NED02, is analyzed as an early-type system member with measured velocity, old stellar population, tidal coupling, and deformation driven by the S galaxy.

quotes.quote: The spectra of 2MFGC 04711, NED02, S, and the C galaxy resemble those of early-type galaxies and no emission lines were detected. No star-forming regions and no nuclear ionization sources were detected in the observed regions of the four main galaxies. The calculated heliocentric radial velocity for the S galaxy is 20 141 10 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0696), which agrees with the radial velocity of the Abell S0546 cluster (cz = 20 893 km s ^ 1 ) ; for 2MFGC 04711, it is 20 057 10 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0693); and for NED02, it is 20 754 10 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0718) , both in agreement with quoted values in NED.

The rotation profiles of 2MFGC 04711 and NED02 are typical of tidal coupling in ellipticals with no net rotation, which results in a U-shaped rotation profile with the galaxy core at the base of the U. Both galaxies are gravitationally coupled directly with the proposed central object of the cluster, the S galaxy. The U-shaped structure is a direct observational signature of tidal friction with the extended material of the S galaxy. Internally, the no-net rotation core in the U-shaped rotation profile of both 2MFGC 04711 and NED02 seems to be slightly perturbed by the tidal interaction with the S galaxy, which lies in the center of the local gravitational potential-well of this system. 2MFGC 04711 and AM 0546-324 (NED02) are completely dominated by an old stellar population with age between 2 10^ 9 < t 13 10^ 9 yr.

gmw_00007612.name: S galaxy
gmw_00007612.overview: The S galaxy is an early-type galaxy that serves as the central object of the gravitationally bound AM 0546-324 system [arXiv:1206.0719]. It features a smooth distribution of extended material and is perturbed by tidal interactions with its principal companion galaxies [arXiv:1206.0719].
