sources.summary: The paper identifies the S galaxy, or Shadowy galaxy, as the central object of the AM 0546-324 system, with smooth extended material, measured velocity, tidal rims, and perturbation by companion interactions.

quotes.quote: Adopting the S galaxy as the center of this gravitationally bound system, the radial velocity differences between the different quoted members vary from 43 to 646 km s^ 1 . Within 1.2 arcmin of AM 0546-324 there are a few relevant field companions such as the C galaxy in the SE direction and a new Polar Ring galaxy candidate in the SW. Several dwarf objects in and surrounding this system are close enough to be candidate members of this system, but no quoted redshift for these objects was found in the literature. The S galaxy seems to be large enough to wrap up all principal companions with its smooth distribution of material. The spectra of 2MFGC 04711, NED02, S, and the C galaxy resemble those of early-type galaxies and no emission lines were detected.

Both galaxies are gravitationally coupled directly with the proposed central object of the cluster, the S galaxy. The U-shaped structure is a direct observational signature of tidal friction with the extended material of the S galaxy. Internally, the no-net rotation core in the U-shaped rotation profile of both 2MFGC 04711 and NED02 seems to be slightly perturbed by the tidal interaction with the S galaxy, which lies in the center of the local gravitational potential-well of this system.

Simultaneously, the S galaxy is perturbed by the whole interaction with all principal objects of the system. Two questions still remain to be answered: (1) is the S galaxy environment the starting point for the birth of a future cD galaxy? and (2) what is the origin of the S galaxy?

gmw_00007613.name: C galaxy
gmw_00007613.overview: The C galaxy is a compact companion and bound member of the AM 0546-324 system [arXiv:1206.0719]. It exhibits an early-type spectrum with no detected emission lines [arXiv:1206.0719].

sources.summary: The C galaxy is described as a compact companion in the AM 0546-324 system, with an early-type spectrum, measured velocity, bound membership, and a small mass relative to the S galaxy.

quotes.quote: The nearby C galaxy was partially inside the GMOS-S slit and the estimated redshift z=0.0685 corresponds to a heliocentric radial velocity of V = 19 834 30 km s ^ 1 , with a calculated distance of 289.2 Mpc and a dynamical mass of 3.74 10^ 10 M _ .
The spectra of 2MFGC 04711, NED02, S, and the C galaxy resemble those of early-type galaxies and no emission lines were detected.

The C galaxy, cz = 19 834 40 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0685), and the K galaxy, cz = 20 197 ( z = 0.0698), are both bound members of the AM 0546-324 system. From the calculated mass lower limit, both 2MFGC 04711 and NED02 have 31 of the mass of the S galaxy, and the C galaxy, almost 7 .

gmw_00007614.name: K galaxy
gmw_00007614.overview: The K galaxy is an early-type galaxy with a radial velocity of approximately 20,923 km/s [arXiv:1206.0719]. It is identified as one of the four main bound member galaxies within the interacting AM 0546-324 system [arXiv:1206.0719].

sources.summary: The K galaxy is one of the four main galaxies in the AM 0546-324 system; the authors give its velocity and identify it as a bound system member.

quotes.quote: The radial velocity of the K galaxy (20 923 km s ^ 1 ) was extracted from Quintana Ram i rez ( ).
Below is a summary of our main results: The AM 0546-324 system is composed of four main galaxies: 2MFGC 04711, AM 0546-324 (NED02), the K galaxy, and the one named S galaxy by us.
The C galaxy, cz = 19 834 40 km s^ 1 ( z = 0.0685), and the K galaxy, cz = 20 197 ( z = 0.0698), are both bound members of the AM 0546-324 system.

gmw_00007615.name: IRAS F01364 1042
gmw_00007615.overview: IRAS F01364 1042 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1502.06617, arXiv:1811.09281] featuring a rotating gas disk [arXiv:1811.09281] and a central radio spectral index that steepens outward [arXiv:1408.4177]. The galaxy is classified as hosting an active galactic nucleus or starburst [arXiv:1408.4177], with excited molecular gas potentially tracing molecular outflows [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRASF01364-1042 as one of the strongest black-hole scaling-relation offsets and notes its unusually large inferred sphere of influence from the nuclear disk fit.

quotes.quote: We also note that the two galaxies which fall furthest above the relation, IRASF01364-1042 and IRASF17207-0014, do not show atypically large residuals in the GALFIT bulge fits (indeed, the residuals of the former appear smaller than average for this sample), which suggests that their offset is not due to errors in the bulge luminosities of Haan11 . Again, our sample of merging galaxies falls above this relation, suggesting that either stars have not been forming in the bulge as quickly as the black holes have been growing, or that a large mass of stars is still strewn about in tidal tails and unrelaxed features that will eventually become part of the bulge.

Two galaxies appear to drive the offset in the M_ BH _ * relation: IRASF01364-1042 and NGC 2623. Although we note that these are not outliers in a statistical sense, we must be certain that they are not unfairly biasing our results.

In one case (IRASF01364-1042), the black hole mass is comparable or larger than the entire nuclear disk. The calculated radius of influence is thus artificially large, because presumably the mass profile of the galaxy changes beyond our field of view.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS F01364-1042 as having a flat central radio spectral index that steepens outward and classifies it as AGN/SB because either AGN or starburst cooling could explain the map.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01364-1042 From the map of this object we see that the centre of radio emission is associated with a very flat index value, which is slightly steepening outwards. Based on our classification, this steepening might be due to the AGN, but since it does not exceed much, within the uncertainties, our theoretical limit it can also be due to a SB located in an HII region. Then the CR electrons as they escape the SB are losing energy which is shown as steepening in the radio spectral index. We classify this object as AGN/SB.

sources.summary: IRAS F01364 1042 is presented as a LIRG with a rotating gas disk; high-resolution OSIRIS data show extended emission and excited molecular gas possibly tracing molecular outflows.

quotes.quote: IRAS F01364 1042 IRAS F01364 1042 is a LIRG with a clear rotating gas disk detected in and as detailed in Paper I. In the 100mas scale data, extended to the south between the major and minor axis has been seen, lending potential support to an outflowing structure traced by . In the 35mas scale data, the continuum is resolved into east-west extended emission with the enhanced / spaxels originating from the center. The spatial correlation of this excited molecular gas with prominent non-Keplerian motion and elevated velocity dispersion as mentioned in the text lends support that there could be molecular outflows present, to be confirmed by follow-up high angular resolution observations.

gmw_00007616.name: NGC 6670E
gmw_00007616.overview: NGC 6670E is the eastern component of a merging galaxy pair [arXiv:1811.09281]. Its gas peaks are displaced from the main continuum peak, residing instead at an off-nucleus star-forming clump that displays high velocity dispersion [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: NGC 6670E is the eastern component of a merging pair; despite calibration limitations, OSIRIS maps show off-nuclear gas peaks and high velocity dispersion near a star-forming clump.

quotes.quote: NGC 6670E NGC 6670E is the eastern component of a merging galaxy pair with a companion 17 kpc away , but the measured separation between the two nuclei as detected in H band HST NICMOS images within the eastern galaxy is 1 kpc . There are two caveats regarding the analysis of this galaxy: 1. the OSIRIS data analyzed in this work was taken after Paper I was published, and thus we do not yet have the quantitative properties of the nuclear disks; 2. this was one of three nuclei for which we did not have proper calibration frames of our standard stars and thus we were unable to flux calibrate this galaxy in the consistent manner we did the others. For this reason, the units on the continuum, , and maps are not properly displayed, and the intensity of the line and continuum should be treated in relative units.

We were also unable to produce a SFR map since we did not have a properly calibrated map of the luminosity. Nonetheless, it is interesting that the gas peaks for both and are displaced from the main continuum peak, residing instead at an off-nucleus star-forming clump 200 pc west of the K band continuum peak. The does not appear to be excited, though it displays high velocity dispersion that may be traced to the gas peak and could be a candidate for star-formation driven outflows in future studies.

gmw_00007617.name: IRAS F18090+0130
gmw_00007617.overview: IRAS F18090+0130 is an early-stage galaxy merger containing two progenitor galaxies separated by 49 kpc [arXiv:1811.09281]. Both nuclei feature extended continuum resolved into spiral-arm-like structures, with extended molecular and ionized gas that shows no signs of heating or nuclear activity [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: IRAS F18090+0130 is an early-stage merger with two widely separated progenitors; OSIRIS resolves spiral-arm-like continuum in both nuclei and finds extended, unheated gas.

quotes.quote: IRAS F18090+0130 E / W IRAS F18090+0130 hosts two progenitor galaxies 49 kpc apart . At the early stage of merging, this system was observed but not detected in Swift BAT . The OSIRIS observations here provide a baseline of what early-stage galaxy mergers may feature before they become morphologically disturbed: the extended continuum is resolved into what appear like spiral arms well traced by the gas in both nuclei. Both the molecular and ionized gas appears extended but not heated, given the lack of significant nuclear activity in the respective regions. Like NGC 6670E, we lacked proper calibration frames for these galaxies and thus were unable to flux calibrate the OSIRIS data.

gmw_00007618.name: IRAS F20351+2521
gmw_00007618.overview: IRAS F20351+2521 is an early-stage luminous infrared galaxy characterized by galactic-scale spiral arms [arXiv:1811.09281]. The galaxy shows no signs of outflowing or shock-heated gas, and its nuclear region exhibits weak hydrogen recombination line flux [arXiv:1811.09281].

sources.summary: IRAS F20351+2521 is described as an early-stage LIRG with galactic-scale spiral arms and similar gas kinematics, lacking signs of outflowing or shock-heated gas.

quotes.quote: IRAS F20351+2521 IRAS F20351+2521 is a LIRG with galactic-scale spiral arms that feed into the clumpy nature as sampled within the OSIRIS FOV. As was found in Paper I, its and emission show similar kinematics, as this early-stage LIRG shows no sign of outflowing or shock-heated gas.
For CGCG 436 030, IRAS F20351+2521, and NGC 6090, the flux is weak but cospatial relative to that of , resulting in low / values across the nuclear region.

gmw_00007619.name: J1154+513
gmw_00007619.overview: J1154+513 is a hybrid morphology radio source [arXiv:1307.8255]. Its core resolves into a core-jet structure directed toward the Fanaroff-Riley I-like side of the source, with no additional diffuse nuclear emission detected on scales of 1 to 10 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1307.8255].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1154+513 as a hybrid morphology radio source whose VLBA core resolves into a core-jet structure directed toward the FR I-like side, with no nearby diffuse nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: J1154+513 (Fig. hybrids_1 ) Both 1.7 GHz and 5 GHz VLBA images show core-jet structures pointed towards FR I-like large scale jet. Component C with a spectral index of =- 0.01 visible on both maps is a radio core. The components C1 - C6 are parts of the radio jet.

The core flux density of J1154+513 measured on the VLA scale is higher than measured on VLBA images, and yet in this case the core has been clearly resolved into a core-jet structure. We conclude that there is no additional diffuse radio emission in the proximity of the nucleus of these sources on 1-10 kpc scale, which could be anticipated in the case of FR I radio galaxies.
In the case of J1154+513 detected jet is clearly directed towards an FR I-like part of the source (Fig. hybrids_1 ).

gmw_00007620.name: J1206+503
gmw_00007620.overview: J1206+503 is a hybrid radio galaxy [arXiv:1307.8255]. Very Long Baseline Array observations of the galaxy reveal a point-like central radio core with a flat spectral index, as well as a compact hotspot-like structure on its Fanaroff-Riley class I side [arXiv:1307.8255].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1206+503 as a hybrid radio source with a point-like VLBA core, flux densities comparable to VLA measurements, and compact FR I-side hotspot-like structure.

quotes.quote: J1206+503 (Fig. hybrids_1 ) A single component is visible in both 1.7 GHz and 5 GHz VLBA images. Flat spectral index = 0.0 indicates it is a radio core.

For J1206+503 we have only detected a point-like central component. We analyzed the flux densities of the central components of all five sources measured on VLA and VLBA images (Tables 1 2). The summarized flux densities on VLBA scales of four hybrid sources (J1206+503, J1313+507, J1315+516, J1348+286) are comparable to those measured on VLA maps, although for J1348+286 the slightly resolved structure suggests the presence of weak radio jet.

These compact, bright features are visible in the inner part of the fuzzy FR I side in all cases (components W1 in the case of J1206+503 and J1313+507, E1 in J1154+513, J1315+516, and J1348+286, Figs. hybrids_1 hybrids_3 in this paper).

gmw_00007621.name: J1313+507
gmw_00007621.overview: J1313+507 is a Type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.09315] that is classified as a hybrid radio source [arXiv:1307.8255]. It features a flat-spectrum core with a probable jet [arXiv:1307.8255], while its host galaxy exhibits typical star-forming activity [arXiv:2210.09315].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1313+507 as a hybrid radio source with a flat-spectrum core, probable jet toward the FR II-like side, unresolved lower-frequency core-jet structure, and FR I-side compact feature.

quotes.quote: J1313+507 (Fig. hybrids_2 ) The 5 GHz image shows flat spectrum core C and an additional feature, probably a jet C1 directed towards FR II-like part of source. The core-jet structure is unresolved in 1.7 GHz observations.

Here we present higher resolution VLBA observations of five hybrid objects discovered by G06. Two of them revealed a core-jet structure (J1154+513 J1348+286), and the other two show the probable presence of a weak jet (J1313+507 J1315+516).

These compact, bright features are visible in the inner part of the fuzzy FR I side in all cases (components W1 in the case of J1206+503 and J1313+507, E1 in J1154+513, J1315+516, and J1348+286, Figs. hybrids_1 hybrids_3 in this paper). They lie closer to the radio core than the hotspots from the FR II side and possess steep spectra.

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1313+507 as a Type 2 AGN source with extremely broad blue-shifted emission, strong AGN line-ratio constraints, and host-galaxy star formation typical for its redshift.

quotes.quote: J1313+507 A Type 2 source, J1313+507 has extremely broad, blue-shifted emission, with v_ FWHM >20,000 km s ^ 1 (Figure bigfig:spectra , Table tab:fwhm ). The presence of emission also confirms the AGN nature. The high S/N 10 detection and no detection enable us to place a lower limit on the / ratio indicating strong AGN contributions (Figure fig:bpt ). The host galaxy SFR ( 13.9 4.2 M_ yr^ 1 ) is consistent with typical star-forming galaxies at its redshift 11.3 M_ yr^ 1 2014MNRAS.437.3516S .

gmw_00007622.name: J1315+516
gmw_00007622.overview: J1315+516 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy classified as a hybrid radio source [arXiv:1307.8255, arXiv:2210.09315]. It features a radio core with a probable weak southward jet [arXiv:1307.8255], alongside Seyfert narrow-line ratios, blue-shifted outflows, and star formation activity [arXiv:2210.09315].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1315+516 as a hybrid radio source with a probable weak southward jet, a radio core, and VLBA flux densities comparable to VLA measurements.

quotes.quote: J1315+516 (Fig. hybrids_2 ) The 1.7 GHz image revealed the probable presence of weak jet C1 directed towards the south; however, the lack of C1 detection at C-band may indicate that this feature has a very steep spectrum. The component C visible in both images is a radio core.

Here we present higher resolution VLBA observations of five hybrid objects discovered by G06. Two of them revealed a core-jet structure (J1154+513 J1348+286), and the other two show the probable presence of a weak jet (J1313+507 J1315+516).

The summarized flux densities on VLBA scales of four hybrid sources (J1206+503, J1313+507, J1315+516, J1348+286) are comparable to those measured on VLA maps, although for J1348+286 the slightly resolved structure suggests the presence of weak radio jet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1315+516 as an AGN-dominated HyMoRS host with Seyfert narrow-line ratios, broad components, high-ionization emission, blue-shifted outflows, and significant star formation.

quotes.quote: J1315+516 Both the narrow and broad emission lines confirm this source as AGN-dominated. The narrow line ratios firmly place the source in the Seyfert part of the BPT diagram (Figure fig:bpt ). The AGN nature of the source is further supported by the broad and components, with velocity FWHM of over 5000 (Table tab:fwhm ).

The source also has a significant detection of the very high ionization potential line. The source also has blue-shifted outflows as indicated by the winged 5008.24 line. While the host galaxy is optically red, the galaxy has significant SF activity as traced by the emission, in line with typical star-forming galaxies at its redshift cf. 2014MNRAS.437.3516S .

gmw_00007623.name: J1348+286
gmw_00007623.overview: J1348+286 is a hybrid radio source quasar [arXiv:1307.8255] with a point-like optical morphology [arXiv:2210.09315]. It features a complicated radio jet [arXiv:1307.8255], an inverted-spectrum core [arXiv:1307.8255], and emission lines indicating both broad and narrow regions alongside active galactic outflows [arXiv:2210.09315].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1348+286 as a quasar hybrid radio source whose VLBA images show a complicated FR II-side jet, an inverted-spectrum core, and low beaming.

quotes.quote: J1348+286 (Fig. hybrids_3 ) Both VLBA images show the presence of a radio jet pointed towards an FR II-like part of the source. The structure of the jet is complicated, and in the 5 GHz image, seven components could be distinguished. The flat spectrum core C has inverted spectral index = 0.29 . This source is classified as a quasar with a measured spectroscopic redshift of z =0.74 mun03 .

The summarized flux densities on VLBA scales of four hybrid sources (J1206+503, J1313+507, J1315+516, J1348+286) are comparable to those measured on VLA maps, although for J1348+286 the slightly resolved structure suggests the presence of weak radio jet.
The Doppler factor for quasar J1348+286 amounts to 2.2 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1348+286 as the brightest source in the HyMoRS sample, with quasar-like optical morphology, broad and narrow emission components, Seyfert placement, and outflow-like line wings.

quotes.quote: J1348+286 J1348+286 is the brightest source in our sample, with emission line luminosities >10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 . It has a point-like, quasar morphology in the optical imaging. The high S/N BOSS spectrum has a power-law continuum and strong and line detections, which contain both broad (including v_ FWHM >11,000 , at over 3,000 , and and at over 5,000 , Table tab:fwhm ) and narrow components, consistent with an orientation in which both the narrow and the broad line regions are visible. Given its extreme / ratio and the likely / ratio ranges, the source is securely placed in the Seyfert section of the BPT diagram (Figure fig:bpt ). The 5008.24 emission has a Lorentzian shape with wings on either side, indicating blue- and redshifted outflows.

gmw_00007624.name: J161446.9+374607
gmw_00007624.overview: J161446.9+374607 is a compact active galactic nucleus classified as a blazar [arXiv:0711.0862]. Its 5-GHz VLBI flux density is more than double its GB6 catalog flux density, suggesting that the source is highly variable [arXiv:0711.0862].

sources.summary: The paper reports this source as a compact blazar-like radio object whose 5-GHz VLBI flux exceeds the GB6 total flux, suggesting strong variability.

quotes.quote: J161446.9+374607 The compact structure observed is consistent with the blazar classification (March a et al. ). The 5-GHz flux density we detected is more than twice as high as the total flux density found in the GB6 catalogue. This source (Fig. images b) might therefore be highly variable.

gmw_00007625.name: J162111.2+374604
gmw_00007625.overview: J162111.2+374604 is a compact steep spectrum radio source classified as a quasar [arXiv:0711.0862]. It exhibits an asymmetric core-jet-lobe structure, featuring an inner jet extending to the northeast and marginal diffuse emission from its radio lobe [arXiv:0711.0862].

sources.summary: The paper describes this quasar as an asymmetric core-jet-lobe CSS source and reports VLBI detection of its northeast inner jet plus marginal lobe emission.

quotes.quote: J162111.2+374604 Marecki et al. ( ) describe this quasar (known also as 4C +37.46) as an asymmetric core-jet-lobe source. They imaged it with MERLIN at 5 GHz, and with VLBI at 1.7 and 5 GHz as part of a subarcsecond-size Compact Steep Spectrum (CSS) radio source sample. Our image in Fig. images k shows the inner jet structure extended to the north-east. Guided by the lower resolution images of Marecki et al. ( ), we were also able to marginally detect a small fraction ( 1 mJy/beam) of the diffuse radio emission originating from the lobe at around 368 mas east and 376 mas north of the core (out of the field displayed in Fig. images k).

gmw_00007626.name: J162422.0+392440
gmw_00007626.overview: J162422.0+392440 is a double-lobed FR-II radio quasar [arXiv:0711.0862]. It serves as the brightest central member of a nearly linear three-source structure, featuring a compact core imaged with VLBI [arXiv:0711.0862].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this object as the bright central member of a nearly linear three-source structure, a double-lobed FR-II radio quasar with a compact VLBI core.

quotes.quote: J162422.0+392440 Two other compact FIRST radio sources are found within 13 of this object. These three form a nearly linear structure with an angular extent of 21 , the central object being the brightest. This is a double-lobed FR-II radio quasar (de Vries et al. ) of which we imaged the core with VLBI (Fig. images ). It appears compact with a hint on an extension to the N-NE which might have been resolved with longer north--south baselines.

gmw_00007627.name: J163302.1+392427
gmw_00007627.overview: J163302.1+392427 is a quasar that exhibits a milliarcsecond-scale extension in VLBI imaging [arXiv:0711.0862]. This active galactic nucleus has also demonstrated intra-night optical variability during at least one epoch [arXiv:0711.0862].

sources.summary: The paper notes a nearby FIRST source aligned with this quasar’s mas-scale extension and reports previous intra-night optical variability.

quotes.quote: J163302.1+392427 Another FIRST source is found within 9 to the south-east of our object. Its direction is close to the position angle of the mas-scale extension seen in our VLBI image (Fig. images v). Intra-night optical variability was detected in this quasar at one epoch by Stalin et al. ( ).

gmw_00007628.name: AGC 208457
gmw_00007628.overview: AGC 208457 is a tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located near the NGC 3166/9 galaxy pair [arXiv:1807.10404]. It is characterized by a lack of dark matter [arXiv:1807.10404, arXiv:1605.04386], high metallicity [arXiv:1807.10404], and young, blue stellar clumps [arXiv:1807.10404, arXiv:1605.04386].

sources.summary: The paper confirms AGC 208457 as a strong tidal dwarf galaxy candidate with substantial H I, young stellar clumps, high metallicity, and likely origin in NGC 3166/9 debris.

quotes.quote: The most promising TDG candidate identified by our campaign thus far is AGC 208457, located around the NGC 3166/9 galaxy pair. Our GMRT H i measurements indicate that AGC 208457 has sufficient mass ( M_ H_I = 2.3 0.3 10^8 M _ ; lee2012 ) to become a long-lived TDG bou2006 . Assuming that this object is self-gravitating and virialized, it has a dynamical-to-gas mass ratio close to unity denoting a lack of dark matter lee2012 .

Deep optical photometry in lee2016 shows two stellar clumps with a combined mass of M_ stellar 2 10^7 M _ spatially located within the highest H i column density region of AGC 208457 (see Figure fig:TDG ). These stars appear to be fairly blue and are bright in the ultraviolet (UV) suggesting that they are quite young ( lee2012 , lee2016 ). AGC 208457 therefore has many hallmarks of a TDG.

fig:sbg ), the NW stellar clump of AGC 208457 has line ratios that are consistent with metal-rich SBGs, which are located in the bottom right portion of the plot. While the metallicity values alone do not unambiguously distinguish AGC 208457 as a TDG, this object is relatively metal-rich when compared to typical dwarf irregular galaxies (see van2006 , cro2009 , ber2012 ). The enhanced metallicity of AGC 208457 is likely inherited from its parent galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents AGC 208457 as a self-gravitating, dark-matter-poor TDG candidate with a blue, young optical component relative to NGC 3166 and NGC 3169.

quotes.quote: The NGC 3166/9 group contains a TDG candidate, AGC 208457, at least three dIrrs and four H i knots. Deep CFHT imaging confirms that the optical component of AGC 208457 is bluer --- with a 0.28 mag g colour --- and a few Gyr younger than its purported parent galaxies.

The GMRT data for the NGC 3166/9 group indicate that AGC 208457 has sufficient mass to be self-gravitating and has a dynamical to gas mass ratio close to unity, which denotes a lack of dark matter and is one of the hallmarks of a TDG.
AGC 208457 is a putative TDG that has the hallmarks of a self-gravitating, dark matter poor, second-generation galaxy.

gmw_00007629.name: NGC 4747
gmw_00007629.overview: NGC 4747 is a spiral galaxy that has experienced gravitational interactions with its neighbor, NGC 4725 [arXiv:1807.10404]. This interaction produced a gas-rich eastern tidal tail containing young, metal-rich stars and potential early-stage tidal dwarf galaxies [arXiv:1807.10404].

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 4747 to a metal-rich gas and stellar tidal tail containing star clusters, H I peaks, and possible early-stage tidal dwarf galaxy formation.

quotes.quote: Two other interesting TDG candidates are found in the eastern tidal tail of NGC 4747 lee2016 . Gravitational interactions between neighbouring spiral galaxies, NGC 4725 and NGC 4747, likely resulted in gas-rich tidal tails. The most prominent tail extends about 8 arcmin ( 30 kpc) to the north-east of NGC 4747 and appears to contain two distinguishable star clusters (SCs) with masses of M_ stellar 6 10^6 and 9 10^6 M _ lee2016 . As shown in Figure fig:knots , the H i tail is oriented at a slight angle from a bent stellar tail and contains two H i peaks (referred to as NE-1 and NE-2) each with a mass of M _ H_I 10^8 M_ lee2016 .

As expected, the stars in the NGC 4747 tail are fairly young (< 2.5 Gyr; see lee2016 ) and metal-rich, as shown in Fig. fig:sbg , thereby substantiating tidal origins. This system is in a much earlier evolutionary stage than AGC 208457 and turbulent motions, which can inhibit star-formation in high density H i regions (see sen2015 ), are still present within the tail. Nevertheless, NE-1 and NE-2 appear to have sufficient mass -- especially if they continue to accrete content from the tidal tail (see lee2016 ) -- to eventually decouple from their host and become self-gravitating TDGs.

gmw_00007630.name: SDSSJ144317.83+510721.0
gmw_00007630.overview: SDSSJ144317.83+510721.0 is a gravitationally lensed system containing a background Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:2210.07249]. The foreground lensing source is located at a redshift of z = 0.5501, while the background galaxy lies at z = 2.9448 [arXiv:2210.07249].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ144317.83+510721.0 as a proof-of-concept lensed LAE, recovering its background redshift and measuring a higher HETDEX Ly-alpha flux.

quotes.quote: We found a single BELLS GALLERY LAE candidate that fell on a HETDEX fiber, which is not surprising given the relatively small fraction of sky HETDEX has covered so far (compared to SDSS), the 1/4.5 filling factor of HETDEX IFUs (the IFUs, even after dithering to cover the inter-fiber gaps, only cover 1/4.5 of the HET focal plane; see Figure 3 of and Figure 3 of , and the low surface density of strong gravitationally lensed systems. We present the extracted HETDEX spectrum for this source (SDSSJ144317.83+510721.0) in Figure fig:fig1 . From , the redshift of the foreground source is z = 0.5501 and the redshift of the background source is z=2.9443 with a Ly flux of 1.69 0.148 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

From the extracted HETDEX spectrum, we found a background source redshift of z = 2.9448 with a Ly flux of 3.60 0.40 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . While our flux is higher, a direct comparison between Ly fluxes reported from using BOSS and the current work using HETDEX is difficult. BOSS uses single fibers in a plug plate ( 2 ) Drory_2015 , while HETDEX observations consist of 3-dither sequences with IFUs, thus collecting more of the total light. In particular, while a single fiber can be aperture corrected, this typically assumes a point-source profile, while the HETDEX IFU can be sensitive to more extended emission which has been observed in LAEs e.g, Steidel_2011 , which is consistent with our 2 higher flux measurement.

gmw_00007631.name: host of GRB 980613
gmw_00007631.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 980613 is an infrared-detected galaxy [arXiv:1402.4006] characterized by an asymmetric, chaotic, and highly peculiar structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197]. This complex system consists of at least five distinct galaxies or galaxy fragments [arXiv:1402.4006] and is classified within the merger region of the concentration-asymmetry diagram [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197].

sources.summary: The paper discusses the host of GRB 980613 as an infrared-detected GRB host whose stellar-mass discrepancy may reflect a complex configuration of several galaxies or fragments.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:herschel shows the PACS 100 \ images for the 7 detections overlaid on IRAC images (3.6 \ except for the host of GRB 980613 which is 4.5 ).

The biggest discrepancy is for the host of GRB 980613, for which the estimate by castro10 is roughly 40 times larger than ours, and 200 times larger than the one given by perley13 . This could be due to the complex configuration of this host, which consists of at least five galaxies, or galaxy fragments djor03 .
Of 15 GRBHs around z 1 , only three were detected at 24 \ with \ GRB 970828, GRB 980613, GRB 990705: lefloch06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 980613 host as asymmetric, chaotic, and very peculiar, placing it in the peculiar or merger region of the concentration-asymmetry diagram.

quotes.quote: The host of GRB 980613 was previously studied by Hjorth et al. (2002), who find an asymmetric and chaotic structure, while Gorosabel et al. (2003) argue that the host of GRB 000418 is compact and smooth, both in agreement with our results (Table 1).

In total, we find that the hosts of GRB 980613, GRB 010222, GRB 020405 and GRB 020903 are all located in the peculiar section/merger area of the C-A diagram. For GRB 980613, GRB 020405 and GRB 020903 this is not unexpected, since these all appear very peculiar.

gmw_00007632.name: UDG--B1
gmw_00007632.overview: UDG--B1 is a blue ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the outskirts of the Hickson Compact Group 25 [arXiv:2102.04564]. It has an effective radius of 3.7 kpc and displays morphological and kinematic signatures consistent with a recent tidal interaction [arXiv:2102.04564].

sources.summary: The paper presents UDG--B1 as a blue UDG near HCG 25 with resolved H I, possible tidal interaction signatures, likely dark-matter dominance, and outskirts-group membership.

quotes.quote: The targets are from contrasting non--cluster environments: UDG--B1 is projected in the outskirts of Hickson Compact Group 25 and Secco--dI--2 (SdI--2) is an isolated UDG. These UDGs also have contrasting effective radii with \ of 3.7 kpc (similar to the Milky Way) and 1.3 kpc respectively. SdI--2 has an unusually large M_ HI M_* ratio =28.9, confirming a previous single dish \ observation. Both galaxies display \ morphological and kinematic signatures consistent with a recent tidal interaction, which is also supported by observations from other wavelengths, including optical spectroscopy. Within the limits of the observations' resolution our analysis indicates that SdI--2 is dark matter-dominated within its \ radius and this is also likely to be the case for UDG--B1.Our study highlights the importance of high spatial and spectral resolution \ observations for the study of the dark matter properties of UDGs.

HI morphology and mass UDG--B1 is projected 9 arcmin (225 kpc) SW of the Hickson Compact Group, HCG 25 centre, see Figure fig1 . black The UDG's black heliocentric radial velocity (V _ opt ) is 89 \ higher than the mean velocity black for HCG 25, V _ opt = 6356 \ (NED) with a dispersion ( _v ) = 61.3 \ Tovmassian99 . UDG-B1 is projected within the typical group R _ 200 of 500 kpc roman17 black , where R _ 200 is the radius enclosing an overdensity of > 200 with respect to the critical density of the universe. Its projected position and velocity are therefore consistent with UDG--B1 being an outskirts member of HCG 25 black so past interactions between the UDG--B1 and HCG 25 group members cannot be ruled out .

gmw_00007633.name: Secco--dI--2
gmw_00007633.overview: Secco-dI-2 is an isolated blue ultra-diffuse galaxy with an effective radius of 1.3 kpc [arXiv:2102.04564]. It is dominated by dark matter within its neutral hydrogen radius and exhibits morphological and kinematic signatures of a recent tidal interaction [arXiv:2102.04564].

sources.summary: The paper presents Secco--dI--2 as an isolated blue UDG with a high H I-to-stellar mass ratio, tidal-interaction signatures, and dark-matter dominance within its H I radius.

quotes.quote: The targets are from contrasting non--cluster environments: UDG--B1 is projected in the outskirts of Hickson Compact Group 25 and Secco--dI--2 (SdI--2) is an isolated UDG. These UDGs also have contrasting effective radii with \ of 3.7 kpc (similar to the Milky Way) and 1.3 kpc respectively. SdI--2 has an unusually large M_ HI M_* ratio =28.9, confirming a previous single dish \ observation. Both galaxies display \ morphological and kinematic signatures consistent with a recent tidal interaction, which is also supported by observations from other wavelengths, including optical spectroscopy. Within the limits of the observations' resolution our analysis indicates that SdI--2 is dark matter-dominated within its \ radius and this is also likely to be the case for UDG--B1.Our study highlights the importance of high spatial and spectral resolution \ observations for the study of the dark matter properties of UDGs.

gmw_00007634.name: UGC 2690
gmw_00007634.overview: UGC 2690 is an SAc spiral galaxy [arXiv:2102.04564]. It exhibits an asymmetric morphology in both its optical structure and neutral hydrogen distribution, which likely stems from a recent interaction with another group member [arXiv:2102.04564].

sources.summary: The paper reports H I detection of the SAc galaxy UGC 2690 in the UDG--B1 field, noting contamination of earlier GBT data, asymmetric H I morphology, and fitted rotation.

quotes.quote: At first sight this may seem like some flux loss for the interferometric observations. However, while the GBT pointing was at the position UDG--B1 the GBT FWHP beam is 9 arcmin and thus the spectrum was contaminated by emission from UGC 2690. UGC 2690 is projected 6.6 arcmin from UDG--B1, so lies just beyond the radius of GBT FWHP beam. black Combining the GMRT \ spectra of UDG--B1 and UGC 2690, attenuated to 40 of its actual flux, produces a spectrum consistent with the GBT spectrum.

APPENDIX A UGC 2690 \ was also detected in the SAc galaxy UGC 2690, within GMRT UDG B1 black field of view , at V _ HI = 6275 4 \ with W _ 20 340 8 . The \ morphology from the medium resolution cube is shown in Figure fig2690 and displays an asymmetric increase in minor axis diameter NE of the optical centre. A similar asymmetry is seen in optical image. It seems likely both the \ and optical features are due to a recent interaction with another group member.

gmw_00007635.name: SDSS J125942.80+121312.6
gmw_00007635.overview: SDSS J125942.80+121312.6 is a quasar at a redshift of z=0.751 [arXiv:1603.00134]. It is classified as a broad-absorption line quasar that exhibits variable Balmer absorption lines and optical Fe II absorption [arXiv:1603.00134].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J125942.80+121312.6 as a BAL QSO with variable Balmer absorption, dense outflowing gas, optical Fe II absorption, and transverse absorber motion.

quotes.quote: We report on the discovery of broad Balmer absorption lines variability in the QSO SDSS J125942.80+121312.6, based on the optical and near-infrared spectra taken from the SDSS-I, SDSS-III Baryon Oscillation Spectroscopic Survey (BOSS), and TripleSpec observations over a timescale of 5.8 years in the QSO's rest-frame. The blueshifted absorption profile of H shows a variation of more than 5 at a high velocity portion ( >3000 \ km \ s ^ 1 ) of the trough.

In this work, we report on the variability of BALs in the H I Balmer series in the QSO SDSS J125942.80+121312.6 (hereafter as J1259+1213). This object also shows overlapping UV Fe II troughs. For the first time, we identify excited optical Fe II absorption lines in the QSO spectra, which allow for an accurate measurement for Fe ^+ .
J1259+1213 is a z=0.751 QSO with strong broad H I Balmer absorption lines reported by Hall (2007) according to its SDSS DR5 spectrum.

gmw_00007636.name: Swift J1112.2-8238
gmw_00007636.overview: Swift J1112.2-8238 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.89 [arXiv:1605.01724]. It hosted a tidal disruption event candidate that exhibited high X-ray flux variability and a post-peak decay consistent with a power-law profile [arXiv:1605.01724].

sources.summary: The paper includes Swift J1112.2-8238 among MAXI TDE candidates, giving its redshift, X-ray luminosity, flux variability, peak timing, and post-peak power-law light-curve behavior.

quotes.quote: On the basis of positional coincidence, we identify 3 TDE black candidates in the MAXI transient source catalog from the literature, Swift J1112.2-8238 (hereafter Swift J1112-82; ), Swift J1644+57 Bur11 , and Swift J2058.4+0516 (hereafter Swift J2058+05; ). They are located at z= 0.89 , z= 0.354 , and z= 1.1853 , and the (isotropic) luminosities in the 4--10 keV band are estimated to be 10 ^ 47.1 erg s ^ 1 , 10 ^ 46.6 erg s ^ 1 , and 10 ^ 47.5 erg s ^ 1 , respectively.

As the reference, we analyze the light-curve pattern of the four identified TDEs, Swift J1112-82, Swift J1644+57, Swift J2058+05, and NGC 4845. We find that all of them show the following two characteristics. The first one is high variability amplitudes; the ratio between the highest flux and the one of the previous bin in the 30-day (90-day) averaged light curves is 7.0 (1.6), 16.3 (7.7), 8.2 (2.4), and 1.4 (6.8) for Swift J1112-82, Swift J1644+57, Swift J2058+05, and NGC 4845, respectively. Here, we assign a flux of 5 10^ 12 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 (4--10 keV) for each bin when the source is undetected, which is the sensitivity limit of the 2nd MAXI catalog Hiroi13 .

The second characteristic is that the decay light curves are consistent with a power-law profile of t^ 5/3 , where t is time since the onset time of each TDE. We note that the time of the flux peak, t_ p , is delayed from the TDE onset time by approximately 80 days for NGC 4845, 20 days for Swift J1644+57 and Swift J2058+05, and 5 days for Swift J1112-82. Hence, we set the central day of the bin showing the highest flux as t_ p , and estimate the TDE onset time by correcting for these offsets. We confirm that the 10-days light curves in the 3--10 keV band follow power-law profiles, as shown in Figure tde_lc . A power-law fit to the light curve over 90 days after the peak flux is found to be acceptable in terms of a ^2 test, yielding the best-fit index of 1.86^ +0.45 _ 1.55 , 1.94^ +0.39 _ 0.48 , 1.92^ +0.58 _ 0.73 , and 2.00^ +1.14 _ 1.25 for Swift J1112-82, Swift J1644+57, Swift J2058+05, and NGC 4845, respectively.

gmw_00007637.name: Swift J2058.4+0516
gmw_00007637.overview: Swift J2058.4+0516 is a galaxy nucleus located at a redshift of z = 1.1853 [arXiv:1605.01724]. It hosted a tidal disruption event candidate that exhibited high variability amplitudes and a decay light curve consistent with a power-law profile [arXiv:1605.01724].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Swift J2058.4+0516 as a MAXI TDE candidate, reporting its redshift, hard X-ray luminosity, variability amplitude, peak delay, and acceptable power-law decay fit.

quotes.quote: On the basis of positional coincidence, we identify 3 TDE black candidates in the MAXI transient source catalog from the literature, Swift J1112.2-8238 (hereafter Swift J1112-82; ), Swift J1644+57 Bur11 , and Swift J2058.4+0516 (hereafter Swift J2058+05; ). gmw_00007638.name: the BCG in 0102
gmw_00007638.overview: The BCG in 0102 is an extremely luminous brightest cluster galaxy with a compact Sersic profile [arXiv:1109.0953]. It exhibits the spectral signature of an E+A+[O II] galaxy and is likely the result of a recent galaxy merger [arXiv:1109.0953].

sources.summary: The paper describes the BCG in 0102 as extremely luminous and unusually blue, with E+A+[O II] spectral features, a compact Sersic profile, and a likely merger origin.

quotes.quote: For clarity the BCG for 0102 \ is shown as the yellow star, with a luminosity of 22L^* in the i- band. This is clearly an extremely luminous object and it is the brightest BCG in the ACT southern sample.

The VLT/FORS2 spectrum of the BCG in 0102 has the signature of an E+A+ [ O 2 ] galaxy. This galaxy is a possible high-redshift analog of NGC 1275, the blue and radio-loud cD galaxy in the Perseus cluster at z=0.018 McNamara1996 . Blue BCGs like this one usually live in the centers of cool core clusters e.g. Santos2011 , and in this case the BCG is located within the cool, low entropy, merging core in 0102 revealed by the \ data. We also obtain the physical size of the BCG from the structural parameters estimated using GALFIT in section sec:cmd . The galaxy has an effective radius R_e=10.4 h_ 70 ^ 1 kpc for a S ersic index n=1.3 . It is likely that this BCG is the result of a recent galaxy merger; we speculate that this extreme galaxy resulted from a merger of two BCGs during the cluster collision.

gmw_00007639.name: COSMOS-11982
gmw_00007639.overview: COSMOS-11982 is a high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of z=2.089 [arXiv:1412.1835]. Its spectral energy distribution indicates a quiescent stellar population, and it has no detected emission lines [arXiv:1412.1835].

sources.summary: The MOSDEF survey obtained two spectra of COSMOS-11982 with the same redshift, describes its SED as quiescent, and reports no detected emission lines in the object.

quotes.quote: Two of the three spectra for which we measure a robust redshift target the same galaxy (COSMOS-11982). The spectrum in mask co2 03 has a S/N of 9.4 per pixel in the H-band and the spectrum in mask co3 01 has a S/N of 4.1. By fitting the spectra independently, we find the same spectroscopic redshift of z=2.089 .
COSMOS-13577 and COSMOS-11982 both have spectral energy distributions indicative of a quiescent stellar population.
For COSMOS-11982 no emission lines are detected, and thus we zoom in on the absorption lines.

gmw_00007640.name: PGC 087327
gmw_00007640.overview: PGC 087327 is an intermediate-mass lenticular galaxy located in the Hydra I galaxy cluster [arXiv:2205.09124]. It lies at a distance of approximately 56.7 Mpc and hosts a robust population of globular clusters that exhibits a clear color bimodality [arXiv:2205.09124].

sources.summary: The paper studies PGC 087327’s globular cluster system, finding a robust GC overdensity, color bimodality, a specific frequency of 1.8, and a GCLF distance of 56.7 Mpc.

quotes.quote: We take advantage of exquisitely deep optical imaging data from HST/ACS in the F475W ( ) and F606W ( ) bands, to study the properties of the globular cluster (GC) population in the intermediate mass lenticular galaxy , in the Hydra I galaxy cluster. We inspect the photometric (magnitudes and color) and morphometric (compactness, elongation, etc.) properties of sources lying in an area of 19 19 kpc centered on and compare them with four neighbouring fields over the same HST/ACS mosaic. This allowed us to identify a list of GC candidates and to inspect their properties using a background decontamination method. Relative to four comparison fields, shows a robust overdensity of GCs, N_ GC =82 9 .

In spite of the short wavelength interval available with the and passbands, the color distribution shows a clear bimodality with a blue peak at g_ F475W V_ F606W =0.47 0.05 mag and a red peak at g_ F475W V_ F606W =0.62 0.03 mag. We also observe the typical steeper slope of the radial distribution of red GCs relative to blue ones. Thanks to the unique depth of the available data, we characterize the GC luminosity function (GCLF) well beyond the expected GCLF turn-over. We find g^ TOM _ F475W = 26.54 0.10 mag and V^ TOM _ F606W = 26.08 0.09 mag, which after calibration yields a distance of D_ GCLF = 56.7 4.3(statistical) 5.2(systematic) Mpc.

gmw_00007641.name: NGC 7771A
gmw_00007641.overview: NGC 7771A is a galaxy classified as the bluest member of its quartet, which suggests a high rate of star formation [arXiv:1510.00193]. It is linked to NGC 7771 by a neutral hydrogen envelope and a bridge extending from the southern arm of NGC 7771 [arXiv:1510.00193].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7771A as the bluest quartet member, linked by an H I envelope and bridge to NGC 7771, with a late-type Sm rotation curve.

quotes.quote: The system of NGC 7771, 7770 and 7771A is embedded in a common envelope of neutral hydrogen ( 2005A&A...442..137N ).

Although NGC 7771A has not been included in the list of Kazarian galaxies (obviously, because of low surface brightness), however, it is the bluest object in the quartet (see colors in Table tab ), which evidently suggests that it also has a UV excess caused by a high rate of star formation.

Radio observations in 1997AJ....114...77N and 2005A&A...442..137N showed that the southern arm of NGC 7771 extends much further than what is visible in the optical image, making a bridge to NGC 7771A to the West and ending with a region of bright radio emission, see Figure syste .

gmw_00007642.name: J0144
gmw_00007642.overview: J0144 is a spectroscopically confirmed, heavily reddened quasar located at a high redshift of z > 2 [arXiv:1910.00602]. It has a weak X-ray detection, which is significantly detected in the 0.5–3 keV sub-band but not at higher energies [arXiv:1910.00602].

sources.summary: The paper says J0144 is a spectroscopically confirmed z>2 heavily reddened quasar with weak, off-axis X-ray detection and limited physical constraints.

quotes.quote: J0144 was covered as part of the Stripe 82 X-ray survey ( LaMassa16a ), J1216 was detected off-axis in an archival observation (PI Ponman), and J1539 was targetted (PI Zappacosta) and published in Martocchia17 .
Of the remaining three out of ten sources, two (J1122 and J2205) are undetected at all energies, and one (J0144) has a weak detection.
J0144 is confirmed to be significantly detected in the 0.5 - 3 keV sub-band ( P_ B 1 ), but not at higher energies.

gmw_00007643.name: J1122
gmw_00007643.overview: J1122 is a heavily reddened quasar located at a high redshift of z > 2 [arXiv:1910.00602]. The active galactic nucleus was undetected in X-rays during observations, which may be due to high obscuration [arXiv:1910.00602].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1122 as a new XMM-targeted z>2 heavily reddened quasar that was undetected in X-rays, possibly due to high obscuration.

quotes.quote: For five of these sources (J1122, J2200, J2205, J2243, and J2314), the quasars were targetted as part of a new program (PI Lansbury; program ID 082409), which targetted sources with luminous line detections ( L_ OIII >10^ 43 ) from Temple19 .
Of the remaining three out of ten sources, two (J1122 and J2205) are undetected at all energies, and one (J0144) has a weak detection.
Alternatively, these sources (J1122 and J2205 in particular) could be outlying highly obscured ( N_ H 10^ 23 ) HRQs with anomalous low dust to gas ratios.

gmw_00007644.name: J2205
gmw_00007644.overview: J2205 is a newly observed, heavily reddened quasar [arXiv:1910.00602]. The source was undetected at all energies by XMM-Newton, suggesting it may be a highly obscured active galactic nucleus with an anomalously low dust-to-gas ratio [arXiv:1910.00602].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2205 as a newly observed heavily reddened quasar that was undetected by XMM and may be highly obscured.

quotes.quote: For five of these sources (J1122, J2200, J2205, J2243, and J2314), the quasars were targetted as part of a new program (PI Lansbury; program ID 082409), which targetted sources with luminous line detections ( L_ OIII >10^ 43 ) from Temple19 .
Of the remaining three out of ten sources, two (J1122 and J2205) are undetected at all energies, and one (J0144) has a weak detection.
Alternatively, these sources (J1122 and J2205 in particular) could be outlying highly obscured ( N_ H 10^ 23 ) HRQs with anomalous low dust to gas ratios.

gmw_00007645.name: J2243
gmw_00007645.overview: J2243 is a heavily reddened quasar that exhibits luminous [O III] line detections [arXiv:1910.00602]. X-ray observations of the source over the 0.2–4.5 keV energy range reveal an unusually steep photon index [arXiv:1910.00602].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2243 as a newly observed heavily reddened quasar modeled over 0.2-4.5 keV and singled out for an unusually steep photon index.

quotes.quote: For five of these sources (J1122, J2200, J2205, J2243, and J2314), the quasars were targetted as part of a new program (PI Lansbury; program ID 082409), which targetted sources with luminous line detections ( L_ OIII >10^ 43 ) from Temple19 .

For subsequent analyses of these sources, we limit to the energy ranges where there is significant signal above the background: 0.2 - 10 keV for J1234, J2200, and J2315; 0.2 - 4.5 keV for J2243; 0.5 - 10 keV for J1216 and J1539; and 0.5 - 4.5 keV for J2314.
The most significant outlier is J2243, with = 2.5 0.3 .

gmw_00007646.name: J2314
gmw_00007646.overview: J2314 is a newly observed, heavily reddened quasar [arXiv:1910.00602]. The active galaxy was targeted for observation due to its luminous oxygen line detections [arXiv:1910.00602].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2314 as a newly observed heavily reddened quasar modeled over 0.5-4.5 keV, with its photon index fixed to the sample average.

quotes.quote: For five of these sources (J1122, J2200, J2205, J2243, and J2314), the quasars were targetted as part of a new program (PI Lansbury; program ID 082409), which targetted sources with luminous line detections ( L_ OIII >10^ 43 ) from Temple19 .

For subsequent analyses of these sources, we limit to the energy ranges where there is significant signal above the background: 0.2 - 10 keV for J1234, J2200, and J2315; 0.2 - 4.5 keV for J2243; 0.5 - 10 keV for J1216 and J1539; and 0.5 - 4.5 keV for J2314.
In the seventh case (J2314) was fixed to 1.9 , consistent with the average for the sample.

gmw_00007647.name: 3C 314.1
gmw_00007647.overview: 3C 314.1 is an abnormally radio-loud Fanaroff-Riley class I radio source hosted by a faint elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1003.2467]. The host galaxy is unusually blue with no major extinction features, and it has been identified as a potential relic radio galaxy that is no longer actively powered by its jet [arXiv:1003.2467].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes 3C 314.1 as an unusually powerful FR I source in a faint, blue host without major extinction and possibly no longer jet-powered.

quotes.quote: 3C 314.1 is an abnormally radio-powerful FR I radio source ( _ 10 L_ 178 =25.42 ) in a faint host galaxy ( M_H=-22.96 ). It is apparently not in any cluster allington-smith+93 , exhibiting an under-density of nearby galaxies relative to an average elliptical. It shows no major extinction features in the optical-IR color-map, and is unusually blue for an elliptical galaxy ( R-H 1.5 ). We note that this object is flagged by buttiglione+09 as a relic radio galaxy which is no longer being actively powered by the jet.

gmw_00007648.name: UGC 5740
gmw_00007648.overview: UGC 5740 is a low-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.002 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It acts as an absorbing galaxy toward the sightlines of the quasars 1ES 1028+511 and 1SAX J1032.3+5051 [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper discusses UGC 5740 as a likely low-redshift absorbing galaxy toward two QSO sightlines, excluded from the SDSS catalog but favored over formal photometric-redshift matches.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1028+511 found absorbing galaxies UGC 5740 and SDSS J103108.88+504708.7 with (z_ gal , )=(0.002, 90  kpc) and (0.003, 25  kpc), respectively. UGC 5740 is also matched by to an absorber in the spectrum of 1SAX J1032.3+5051. These galaxies are not in our galaxy catalog due to their very low redshifts and the photometric redshift error cut we applied.

1SAX J1032.3+5051 reported an absorbing galaxy, UGC 5740, very close to the sightline at = 65  kpc, with z_ gal = 0.00216 . UGC 5740 is also matched by to an absorber in the spectrum of 1ES 1028+511. As reported above, UGC 5740 is not in our SDSS galaxy catalog.

gmw_00007649.name: SDSS J103110.35+505211.0
gmw_00007649.overview: SDSS J103110.35+505211.0 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.137274 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is positioned at an impact parameter of 278.6 kpc from a quasar sightline and is associated with multiple absorption features [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper recovers SDSS J103110.35+505211.0 as a top absorber match near z=0.137, while noting its uniqueness assignment changes to a nearby z=0.1406 absorber.

quotes.quote: As tabulated in Table  tab:lit , associate a \ absorption feature with a z = 0.137274 galaxy SDSS J103110.35+505211.0 at = 278.6  kpc from the QSO sightline. We recover this pair as the top galaxy match for this absorber, which we associate with additional \ and O 6 components. However, our uniqueness criteria pair this galaxy with a different absorber, listed in Tables  tab:rvir and tab:mstar , for which it is also the top match: a \ line with z_ abs =0.1406 .

gmw_00007650.name: SDSS J083607.41+250645.7
gmw_00007650.overview: SDSS J083607.41+250645.7 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.023 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is identified as the closest known galaxy to a z = 0.023 absorber, with an impact parameter of 283 kpc [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper favors SDSS J083607.41+250645.7 as the z=0.023 absorber galaxy because its photometric and spectroscopic redshifts agree, despite alternate algorithmic pairings.

quotes.quote: pair a z_ abs =0.0233 absorber with SDSS J083607.41+250645.7 ( =283  kpc), and flag this galaxy as the closest known galaxy to the absorber. The SDSS photometric redshift of this galaxy is in good agreement with the spectroscopic value and with z_ abs . Both methods find this to be the second best galaxy match.

Our methods instead pair SDSS J083607.41+250645.7 with a double \ component system with z_ abs =0.028 and =343  kpc. Given the spectroscopic redshift, we favor the galaxy-absorber pair for z_ abs =0.023 found by . Our methods identify 16 additional galaxy-absorber pairs with the two methods.

gmw_00007651.name: NGC 3649
gmw_00007651.overview: NGC 3649 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.0212 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is identified as the most likely association for a circumgalactic medium absorber at a redshift of 0.0163 [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3649 as the more likely association for a z=0.0163 absorber after rejecting an alternate SDSS match on spectroscopic-redshift grounds.

quotes.quote: z_ abs =0.0163 , NGC 3649 WakkerSavage2009 : This galaxy is excluded from our SDSS galaxy catalog due to the photometric redshift error cut. Instead, we find a match with SDSS J111844.37+213351.0, with (z_ phot , )=(0.017, 313  kpc). The spectroscopic redshift, z_ gal =0.0212 , calls this into question, however, so NGC 3649 is a more likely association.

gmw_00007652.name: SDSS J111843.28+212723.0
gmw_00007652.overview: SDSS J111843.28+212723.0 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.0323 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is identified as the most plausible galaxy match for a circumgalactic medium absorber at a redshift of 0.0322 [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper recovers SDSS J111843.28+212723.0 as a top absorber match, then prefers the literature association because SDSS spectroscopic redshifts support it over the uniqueness reassignment.

quotes.quote: z_ abs =0.0322 , SDSS J111843.28+212723.0 Liang2014 : Both methods recover this galaxy-absorber pair as the top galaxy match for this absorber. However, after the uniqueness criteria are applied, the galaxy paired with this absorber is SDSS J111833.63+211300.4, (z_ phot , )=(0.039, 400  kpc) and SDSS J111843.28+212723.0 is instead paired with z_ abs =0.041 ( =495  kpc). Since the SDSS spectroscopic redshifts of SDSS J111843.28+212723.0 and SDSS J111833.63+211300.4, are z_ gal =0.0323 and z_ gal =0.0601 , respectively, we consider the match reported by Liang2014 to be the most plausible.
The galaxy found to be the most probable match to this absorber by both methods is SDSS J111843.28+212723.0, uniquely identified with z_ abs =0.0322 as noted above.

gmw_00007653.name: SDSS J122815.96+014944.1
gmw_00007653.overview: SDSS J122815.96+014944.1 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.00303 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is plausibly associated with a circumgalactic medium absorber at a redshift of 0.0034 [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper recovers SDSS J122815.96+014944.1 as an absorber match but notes photometric-redshift tension and ultimately favors the spectroscopic z=0.0034 association.

quotes.quote: associate this absorber with SDSS J122815.96+014944.1, (z_ gal , )=(0.00303, 69  kpc). We do recover this match with both methods. However, because the photometric redshift for this galaxy, z_ phot =0.013 , is not in good agreement with the spectroscopic value, we find P(r_ vir )=P(M_*)=0.13 , and its unique match is to a different \ absorber: z_ abs =0.020 , =472  kpc, with P(r_ vir ), P(M_*)= 0.26, 0.29. Our statistics match the z_ abs =0.0034 absorber instead to SDSS J122806.95+032056.2, (z_ phot , )=(0.012, 377  kpc), with a probability close to our 10 threshold value. Given the spectroscopic redshift information available for SDSS J122815.96+014944.1 and used in the analysis of , we consider its association with the z_ abs =0.0034 absorber to be most plausible.

gmw_00007654.name: SDSS J123034.42+011624.4
gmw_00007654.overview: SDSS J123034.42+011624.4 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.031 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is associated with a z = 0.031 carbon IV absorber system at an impact parameter of 151 kpc [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J123034.42+011624.4 as a more promising z=0.031 absorber association because the stellar-mass method and spectroscopic redshift agree.

quotes.quote: A more promising association found by the stellar mass method between a z_ abs =0.031 \ and C 4 system and SDSS J123034.42+011624.4, with (z_ phot , )=(0.031, 151  kpc). The spectroscopic redshift is in good agreement in this case, z_ gal =0.031 , and this pair is shown in Figure  fig:q1230 . The unique galaxy match to the absorber found by the virial radius method, SDSS J123141.38+011821.5, (z_ phot , )=(0.048, 495  kpc), is less plausible, both because of the large impact parameter and because SDSS reports z_ gal =0.0234 .

gmw_00007655.name: WISEA J111125.60+554435.4
gmw_00007655.overview: WISEA J111125.60+554435.4 is a galaxy identified as a potential match for a z=0.138 absorber pair [arXiv:2111.04256]. However, another nearby galaxy, SDSS J111116.84+554812.2, provides a closer spectroscopic redshift match of z=0.13813 to the absorber [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper discusses WISEA J111125.60+554435.4 as a reported z=0.138 absorber pair and top match by both methods, though uniqueness favors another galaxy.

quotes.quote: report that the galaxy WISEA J111125.60+554435.4 is a pair with z_ abs =0.138 . This galaxy is our top match to the absorber with both methods. However, the after imposing the uniqueness criteria on the stellar mass method results, the unique galaxy paired with this absorber and reported in Table  tab:mstar is SDSS J111116.84+554812.2 (z_ phot , )=(0.142, 339  kpc), and report its spectroscopic redshift to be z_ gal =0.13813 , an even better redshift match with z_ abs . Between the two methods, we report 22 other possible galaxy-absorber pairs for this sightline.

gmw_00007656.name: SDSS J111141.44+554424.3
gmw_00007656.overview: SDSS J111141.44+554424.3 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.05493 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is identified as a possible absorber pair at redshift 0.0548, though another galaxy lies closer to the absorber [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J111141.44+554424.3 as a possible z=0.0548 absorber pair supported by its spectroscopic redshift, while noting another galaxy lies closer.

quotes.quote: One possible pair identified by both methods is z_ abs =0.0548 and SDSS J111141.44+554424.3, (z_ phot , )= (0.057, 212  kpc). The spectroscopic redshift reported by , z_ gal =0.05493 , supports this association and the pair is shown in Figure  fig:sbs1108 . These authors report that another SDSS galaxy lies closer than SDSS J111141.44+554424.3 to the absorber.

gmw_00007657.name: SDSS J111132.78+554817.9
gmw_00007657.overview: SDSS J111132.78+554817.9 is a galaxy with a photometric redshift of approximately 0.33 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is associated with a candidate O VI absorber pair at a projected distance of 253 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper lists SDSS J111132.78+554817.9 as a candidate z=0.33 O VI absorber pair, top-ranked by stellar mass but reassigned by uniqueness criteria.

quotes.quote: The candidate pairs also include a z_ abs =0.33 O 6 absorber, uniquely paired with SDSS J111132.78+554817.9, (z_ phot , )=(0.33, 253  kpc) by the virial radius method. This galaxy is the top stellar mass method match to the absorber also, but the uniqueness criteria match it with two z_ abs =0.285 \ absorbers, and no unique match is listed for z_ abs =0.33 . This pair is shown in Figure  fig:sbs1108 .

gmw_00007658.name: SDSS J111132.33+554712.8
gmw_00007658.overview: SDSS J111132.33+554712.8 is a galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.462 [arXiv:2111.04256]. It is associated with a z=0.46 absorber system containing O VI, Si III, and C III [arXiv:2111.04256].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J111132.33+554712.8 associated with a z=0.46 absorber, supported by its spectroscopic redshift, although uniqueness criteria choose a different absorber.

quotes.quote: The stellar mass method yields a match of a z_ abs =0.46 system with O 6 , Si 3 , and C 3 and SDSS J111132.33+554712.8, (z_ phot , )=(0.43, 78  kpc). The galaxy's spectroscopic redshift, z_ gal =0.462 , supports this association. This pair is also shown in Figure  fig:sbs1108 . The virial radius method finds this galaxy as the top match to the z_ abs =0.46 system also, but the uniqueness criteria match it with an O 6 only z_ abs =0.4358 absorber.

gmw_00007659.name: UGC 11557
gmw_00007659.overview: UGC 11557 is classified as a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0212102]. Studies of its dark matter halo have analyzed its inner density slope, though the exact value remains subject to different interpretations of its break radius [arXiv:astro-ph/0212102].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 11557 as the one overlapping galaxy whose inferred inner density slope differs between studies, attributing the discrepancy to an ambiguous break-radius choice.

quotes.quote: For one galaxy (UGC 11557) the slopes differ significantly: SMBB find =-0.84 0.27 , while dBMBR find =-0.08 0.23 . An inspection of the density profiles in Fig. 1 of dBMBR and Fig. 5 in SMBB shows that this difference in slope is entirely due to a different choice of break radius, where SMBB have chosen a larger radius than dBMBR, resulting in a much steeper slope. Choosing a smaller break radius will give a value entirely consistent with that in dBMBR. An inspection of the break radii of the 8 other high-resolution SMBB profiles shows that UGC 11557 is the only case where we would claim that the choice in break radius is ambiguous.

gmw_00007660.name: VCC 562
gmw_00007660.overview: VCC 562 is a compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1005.3058]. Classified as a blue compact dwarf, it exhibits a modest star formation rate that is lower than typical values for galaxies of its class [arXiv:1005.3058].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VCC 562 as one of three compact Virgo blue compact dwarfs detected by Herschel, with modest star formation, dwarf-scale mass, and no 500 micron detection.

quotes.quote: Three of the BCDs have been detected with PACS and SPIRE and are analysed in this work: VCC 562, VCC 1179, and VCC 1356. They have B magnitudes between 15.5 and 16.7 ( M_B between 14.5 and 15.6 ), and \ masses of 10 ^7 10 ^8 assuming a distance to Virgo of 16.5 Mpc 2007ApJ...655..144M . We inferred star-formation rates (SFRs) of between 10 ^ 1 and 10 ^ 2 yr ^ 1 from extinction-corrected and UV fluxes 2004A&A...417..499G, 2009ApJ...706.1527B . Although these dwarfs are compact, thus classified as BCDs by 1985AJ.....90.1681B , their SFRs are lower than the typical values for BCDs 2004AJ....128.2170H,2009MNRAS.399..924Z .
Figure fig:sdssspire compares PACS and SPIRE data of the three dwarfs to the SDSS images. VCC 1179 is not detected at 100 m, nor VCC 562 at 500 m.

gmw_00007661.name: VCC 1179
gmw_00007661.overview: VCC 1179 is a compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1005.3058]. Classified as a blue compact dwarf, it exhibits a modest star-formation rate and features an additional structure to the north that extends beyond its optical disc [arXiv:1005.3058].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VCC 1179 as a compact Virgo blue compact dwarf with modest star formation, Herschel detections except at 100 microns, a possible northern feature, and uncertain cold-dust fits.

quotes.quote: Three of the BCDs have been detected with PACS and SPIRE and are analysed in this work: VCC 562, VCC 1179, and VCC 1356. They have B magnitudes between 15.5 and 16.7 ( M_B between 14.5 and 15.6 ), and \ masses of 10 ^7 10 ^8 assuming a distance to Virgo of 16.5 Mpc 2007ApJ...655..144M . We inferred star-formation rates (SFRs) of between 10 ^ 1 and 10 ^ 2 yr ^ 1 from extinction-corrected and UV fluxes 2004A&A...417..499G, 2009ApJ...706.1527B . Although these dwarfs are compact, thus classified as BCDs by 1985AJ.....90.1681B , their SFRs are lower than the typical values for BCDs 2004AJ....128.2170H,2009MNRAS.399..924Z .

VCC 1179 is not detected at 100 m, nor VCC 562 at 500 m. The other galaxies are marginally detected at 500 \ with a low signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) (4 and 5 for VCC 1179 and VCC 1356, respectively). The VCC 1179 images show an additional feature to the north extending beyond the edge of the optical disc. It is not clear whether this feature is associated with the galaxy or not, but it also does not seem to be related to any background source in the SDSS images.

gmw_00007662.name: VCC 1356
gmw_00007662.overview: VCC 1356 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1005.3058]. It exhibits a lower star formation rate than is typical for galaxies of its classification [arXiv:1005.3058].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes VCC 1356 as a Virgo blue compact dwarf whose 500 micron flux is underestimated by single-temperature dust fits and whose two-component cold-dust solutions imply very high dust masses.

quotes.quote: Three of the BCDs have been detected with PACS and SPIRE and are analysed in this work: VCC 562, VCC 1179, and VCC 1356. They have B magnitudes between 15.5 and 16.7 ( M_B between 14.5 and 15.6 ), and \ masses of 10 ^7 10 ^8 assuming a distance to Virgo of 16.5 Mpc 2007ApJ...655..144M . We inferred star-formation rates (SFRs) of between 10 ^ 1 and 10 ^ 2 yr ^ 1 from extinction-corrected and UV fluxes 2004A&A...417..499G, 2009ApJ...706.1527B . Although these dwarfs are compact, thus classified as BCDs by 1985AJ.....90.1681B , their SFRs are lower than the typical values for BCDs 2004AJ....128.2170H,2009MNRAS.399..924Z .

However, Fig. 2 shows that 500 \ fluxes of VCC 1179 and VCC 1356 tend to be underestimated by the single-temperature fits. This difference could be due to either thermal or non-thermal radio emission 1992ARA&A..30..575C,2005A&A...434..849H , an additional cold ( 10 K) dust component 2003A&A...407..159G,2009A&A...508..645G , an enhanced abundance of small grains 2002A&A...382..860L , or the different optical properties of the amorphous dust grains 2007A&A...468..171M .

Since we have four points in the SED of VCC 1179 and the uncertainties in the flux densities are large, the fit could not be tightly constrained, and we discuss only the results obtained for VCC 1356. More than one set of parameters were able to provide a reasonable fit to the SED of this galaxy.

gmw_00007663.overview: The galaxy 9091700038 is a lenticular galaxy that features a relatively round central bulge component [arXiv:2011.04873]. It exhibits stellar population gradients, with outer regions that are older and more metal-poor, and inner regions that are younger and more metal-rich [arXiv:2011.04873].

sources.summary: The paper uses galaxy 9091700038 as a worked example for SAMI bulge-disk decomposition, showing binning choices and reproducing age/metallicity gradients from older, metal-poor outer regions to younger, metal-rich inner regions.

quotes.quote: Figure DataModelResidual shows an example of the binning for the galaxy 9091700038. In this case, a fixed elliptical annulus would not follow the relatively round central bulge component.

For the 9091700038 galaxy Figure DataModelResidual shows the fitting results: the data representing the whole galaxy age/metallicity maps, the galaxy model obtained from the fitting of Equations (1) and (2), and the residuals measured with respect to the map estimated from the Monte Carlo simulations at step (6) of Section sec:Galaxy age and metallicity maps . Without any complicated modelling we are able to reproduce in this galaxy the gradients from outer regions being older and more metal-poor to inner regions being younger and more metal-rich. On average the residuals agree within 2 , however for few spatial bins the agreement is only at 5 .

gmw_00007664.overview: The galaxy 9091700076 is characterized by a distinct bulge and disk structure [arXiv:2011.04873]. Its bulge is older and more metal-rich than its surrounding disk [arXiv:2011.04873].

sources.summary: The paper uses galaxy 9091700076 as a contrasting worked example, finding an older, more metal-rich bulge than disk and acceptable age and metallicity residuals despite only three spatial bins.

quotes.quote: In Figure DataModelResidual2 we show another example of fitting results for the 9091700076 galaxy. As in Figure DataModelResidual , we show the data representing the whole galaxy age/metallicity maps, the galaxy model obtained from the fitting of Equations (1) and (2), and the residuals. Oppositely to the 9091700038 galaxy, the 9091700076 galaxy has an older and more metal-rich bulge when compared to its disk. Due to the low S/N per pixel, only three spatial bins are obtained with S/N=20. The residuals agree within 3 for age and within 2 for metallicity.

gmw_00007665.name: 2MASX J21341775-5338101
gmw_00007665.overview: 2MASX J21341775-5338101 is the brightest cluster galaxy of Abell 3785 [arXiv:2108.00569]. It features a flat-spectrum core that feeds bent-tail radio jets and lobes [arXiv:2108.00569].

sources.summary: Host 2 is the southeast and brightest-cluster-galaxy host in the Dancing Ghosts, with a flat-spectrum core, steep lobe and tail structures, and bent jets.

quotes.quote: `Host 2', identified as WISEA J213417.69-533811.1, is located in the southeast at a photometric redshift of z=0.076 ( D_c 320 Mpc) abell_members .
Examination of the southern host shows a sudden change in the spectral index. Similar to the northern Host 1, it has a flat spectral index core and steeper spectral index lobe and tail structures.

Relatively close (15 arcsec) to the core of the Host 2 (H2C), we see the eastern jet of Host 2 forming a lobe (H2L-E), which bends as it propagates towards Host 1 (H2WT-E). The Host 2 western jet propagates a relatively short distance before forming a circular lobe (H2L-W). The distance to the lobe appears shorter than in the eastern jet. There is some indication of the jet propagation after the lobe, but it is surrounded by the wide tail from the Host 1 (H1WT-E).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J21341775-5338101 as Host 1 of PKS 2130-538, the brightest Abell 3785 galaxy, with flat-spectrum core emission feeding bent-tail radio structures.

quotes.quote: It consists of the radio lobes of two host galaxies, one of which ( ``Host 1'': 2MASX J21341775-5338101) is the bright galaxy at the centre of the curved northern radio bridge, at a redshift of 0.0781. This is the brightest galaxy of the cluster Abell 3785. The other host galaxy ( ``Host 2'': 2MASX J21340666-5334186) is the bright galaxy near the southeast end, at a redshift of 0.0763. The spectral index image helps to isolate the contributions from these two hosts.

In the north, there is a very flat spectrum region ( 0 ) at the position of Host 1 , connecting to relatively flat 0.4 - -0.5 jets (``1''). These then connect to the large bright regions of 0.6 - -0.7 , steepening sharply down the tails to the south to at least -1.5, beyond which the spectra become more uncertain.

gmw_00007666.name: 2MASX J21340666-5334186
gmw_00007666.overview: The galaxy 2MASX J21340666-5334186 is a radio galaxy host located at a redshift of 0.0763 [arXiv:2108.00569]. It features its own flat radio core along with steeper lobe and tail structures [arXiv:2108.00569].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J21340666-5334186 as Host 2 of PKS 2130-538, with a flat radio core, steeper lobe/tail structures, and uncertain interaction with Host 1.

quotes.quote: The other host galaxy ( ``Host 2'': 2MASX J21340666-5334186) is the bright galaxy near the southeast end, at a redshift of 0.0763. The spectral index image helps to isolate the contributions from these two hosts. In the north, there is a very flat spectrum region ( 0 ) at the position of Host 1 , connecting to relatively flat 0.4 - -0.5 jets (``1''). These then connect to the large bright regions of 0.6 - -0.7 , steepening sharply down the tails to the south to at least -1.5, beyond which the spectra become more uncertain. All of this is consistent with the behavior of bent-tail galaxies. In the eastern half, there is a dramatic change in spectral index at the position of the emission associated with the second host; it has its own flat core and steeper lobe/tail structures. Although the overall emission comes from two distinct hosts, it is unclear whether there is an interaction between them, or merely a superposition.

sources.summary: Host 1 is the northern AGN host in the Dancing Ghosts; its jets form large lobes, low-surface-brightness wide tails, and multiple northeast filaments.

quotes.quote: The northern `Host 1', also identified as WISEA J213406.70-533418.7, sits in the centre of the northern radio bridge between two lobes at a photometric redshift of z=0.078 (with a comoving distance of D_c 328 Mpc).

After the initial linear flow, jets of the northern host (Host 1) form large lobes (H1L-E and H1L-W). We see this effect in a number of other AGN RG_review . Lobes associated with the northern source extend southwards, forming a low surface brightness structure and wide tails that are marked in Fig. figure:i_img_legend as H1WT-E and H1WT-W. We also note that both lobes and right wide tail (H1L-E, H1L-W, H1WT-W) show multiple low surface brightness filaments (or wisps; see Fig. figure:i_img_legend marked as H1Wisp1 to H1Wisp4) extending in the northeast direction.

gmw_00007667.name: PMN J2041-5256
gmw_00007667.overview: PMN J2041-5256 is a double-lobed radio active galactic nucleus associated with a host galaxy at a redshift of z=0.048 [arXiv:2108.00569]. Its complex, bent jets are likely shaped by intra-cluster winds, and the entire source could span a projected length of 750 kpc [arXiv:2108.00569].

sources.summary: The paper describes PMN J2041-5256 as a double-lobed radio AGN with complex bent jets, diffuse western emission, and a projected size that could reach 750 kpc.

quotes.quote: The source PMN J2041-5256, shown in Figure fig:doubletail , is a double lobed radio AGN, associated with the host galaxy WISEA J204112.05-525737.7 at a redshift of z=0.048. Previous radio data sumss,gregory94 show only an indistinct extended source corresponding to the nucleus. Its jets (shown with numbers in the Figure to indicate their putative host) are presumably being bent by intra-cluster winds, but the morphology is much more complex than normal bent-tail galaxies.

The eastern jet (``2'') is bifurcated, whilst the western jet (``1'') breaks down into a number of blobs, accompanied by a large diffuse area of emission to the west of the source. One possibility is that its relative motion with respect to the intracluster medium has a large component along the line of sight; the bifurcated tail and western diffuse extensions would then be more typical of structures seen in bent-tail galaxies, but seen here in projection.
