If the direction of motion were 20 degrees from the line of sight, the entire source length would be 750 kpc, among the larger bent-tail sources.

gmw_00007668.name: NGC 7090
gmw_00007668.overview: NGC 7090 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:2011.08870] characterized by active, widespread star formation [arXiv:0804.3819, arXiv:astro-ph/0607102] and an asymmetric, slightly disturbed neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:2108.00569]. The galaxy hosts transient ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:1904.13044, arXiv:2011.08870] and features a highly asymmetric radio halo [arXiv:2403.05709].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 7090 as an asymmetric edge-on spiral with a filamentary dust lane and H structures, including loops and filaments tied to active disk star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 7090's disk is similar to that of NGC 4700, with a slightly more prominent dust lane and an apparently wider, more amorphous overall appearance. The dust lane is very filamentary. Most parts of the dust lane are located in the regions above the midplane.

Images of individual regions in H reveal the presence of a multitude of conspicuous, resolved individual structures, including partial loops and filaments perpendicular to the disk plane. A few of the extended filaments are detected in the southern part of the galaxy. These connect visibly to the bright star forming regions in the disk (see middle right panel of Figure f:n7090sixpanel ). It is obvious that the H emission distribution is apparently widest above regions with the most active star formation in the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7090 as a nearby galaxy with previously detected radio, HI, dust, and H emission; this work finds a prominent asymmetric halo and 0.7 kpc scale height.

quotes.quote: NGC 7090: Several authors have also examined this nearby galaxy; detected for the first time a radio halo with an average scale height of 1.77 kpc. They used VLA observations at 1.43 GHz, with an angular resolution of 34.5", and considered a galaxy distance of 11.7 Mpc. Later, found extraplanar HI emission. observed evidence of extraplanar H _ emission, and recently found extraplanar dust and H _ emission.

As evidenced by both the z profile and the radio contours, this galaxy exhibits a prominent and highly asymmetric radio halo. To achieve the best fit, we utilized a two-component function with an exponential disk. We obtained an average halo scale height of 0.7 kpc, considering the distance listed in Table table:general .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7090 as a nearby star-forming galaxy at 6.6 Mpc hosting two transient ULXs observed with XMM-Newton, Chandra, Swift, and Hubble.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we report the X-ray properties of two transient ULXs (Fig. fig:srcimg , hereafter X1, X2) found in the nearby star-forming galaxy NGC 7090. X1 is classified as an ULX candidate in lin_etal12 based on XMM-Newton observations. X2 was detected in the 2012 Swift/XRT observations and included in the first Swift XRT point source catalogue 1SXPS evans_etal14 .

In this work we identify it as an ULX with peak 0.3--10 keV X-ray luminosity higher than 3 10^ 39 . We adopted a distance to NGC 7090 of 6.6 Mpc tully_etal92 throughout this work . NGC 7090 was observed by XMM-Newton , Chandra , Swift and Hubble in the past decades.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7090 as a widespread-star-formation spiral with radio and H imaging, where disk radio-continuum and star-forming radii differ significantly.

quotes.quote: The 1.4 GHz maps of NGC 4700 and NGC 7090 are from Dahlem et al. (2001).
The H images of NGC 4700 and NGC 7090 originate from Rossa and Dettmar (2003a,b).

Galaxies with widespread SF (in the present sample NGC 4666, NGC 4700 and NGC 7090; NGC 891 and NGC 4631 from DLG95) exhibit a significant difference between r_ SF and r_ CR (see Table tab:data ). This suggests either radial electron diffusion in the disks or low-level SF in the outer parts of their disks, or a combination of both.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7090 as the edge-on spiral host of a newly discovered transient ULX, with low Galactic foreground extinction, local dust-lane extinction, and roughly half-solar oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We report on the discovery of a new, transient ultraluminous X-ray source (ULX) in the galaxy NGC 7090.

The Galactic foreground extinction in the direction of NGC 7090 is low ( A_V = 0.063 , Schlafly11 ), but given that it is an edge-on spiral galaxy and that the ULX appears to be located on one of the dust lanes, there must be local extinction as well.

Although there is not much information regarding the metallicity of NGC 7090 available in the literature, we note that the majority of oxygen abundance estimates compiled by would imply an abundance of A_ O /solar 0.5 (or alternatively 12 + O/H 8.4 ).

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 7090 as an interesting edge-on spiral with a radio halo, extraplanar H emission, and an asymmetric, slightly disturbed H I disk.

quotes.quote: The most interesting galaxy in Fig. fig:nearbygalaxies is the edge-on spiral NGC 7090. Using the ATCA, heesen16, heesen18 obtained detailed radio continuum maps, finding a radio halo with polarised emission up to 6 kpc above the disk correlating with extraplanar H emission. ATCA H i images dahlem05 reveal an asymmetric, slightly disturbed disk matching the stellar extent.

gmw_00007669.name: EMU PS J205139.8--570434
gmw_00007669.overview: EMU PS J205139.8--570434 is a giant Fanaroff-Riley I radio galaxy hosted by the brightest cluster galaxy 2MASX J20513976--5704334 [arXiv:2108.00569]. Its radio emission spans a projected linear size of 1.53 Mpc, featuring North-South oriented jets with wiggles that terminate in diffuse lobes [arXiv:2108.00569].

sources.summary: The paper presents EMU PS J205139.8--570434 as a newly revealed giant FR I radio galaxy hosted by 2MASX J20513976--5704334, with 1.53 Mpc radio emission, jets, wiggles, and diffuse lobes.

quotes.quote: The GRG EMU PS J205139.8--570434 (hereafter GRG J2051--5704), shown in Figure GRG1 , is hosted by 2MASX J20513976--5704334 at z_ sp =0.0602 jones2009 and its radio emission can be traced over a Largest Angular Size 22.1 \ and thus a linear projected size of 1.53 Mpc. It has an FR I radio morphology whose jets are oriented roughly North-South, feature several wiggles, and terminate in diffuse lobes at both extremes of the source.

The strong (S _ 944 =2.12 Jy) source 3.1 arcmin due E, surrounded by diffraction rings, is the well-studied galaxy IC 5063 at z_ sp =0.01135. The central region of the new GRG can be recognized in the SUMSS bock99 images and was even detected at mm wavelengths by the South Pole Telescope mocanu13 . However, the full extended structure shown here has not been previously detected, despite extensive imaging of the neighboring source IC 5063 ( murphy10 and references therein), probably because of the brightness sensitivity and dynamic range limitations. The host galaxy of GRG J2051--5704 is the brightest galaxy of cluster 400d J2051--5704 aka MCXC J2051.6--5704, burenin07,piffaretti11 at a redshift of 0.0599. The radio morphology is reminiscent of archetypal FR I sources such as 3C 31 and Hydra A, with wiggles consistent with either the presence of jet instabilities due to interaction with the surrounding gas, or jet precession e.g. nawaz16 .

gmw_00007670.name: host of GRB 110709B
gmw_00007670.overview: The host of GRB 110709B is a faint galaxy identified in Hubble Space Telescope imaging [arXiv:1209.4654]. Its detection in the F606W filter limits its redshift to below 4, and it exhibits a modest star formation rate of approximately 1 solar mass per year [arXiv:1209.4654].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a faint HST galaxy as the host of GRB 110709B, argues the association is robust, limits the redshift to below 4, and infers modest unobscured UV star formation.

quotes.quote: Within the uncertainty regions we find a single galaxy in both HST images (Figure fig:110709host ), which we identify as the host of GRB 110709B. Photometry of this source using the tabulated WFC3 zeropoints gives m_ F606W =26.78 0.17 AB mag and m_ F160W =25.13 0.10 mag, corrected for Galactic extinction.

Thus, the sub-arcsecond radio and X-ray positions enable a robust association with the galaxy, while the XRT position alone would have led to marginal confidence. This is essential since the detection of the host galaxy in the F606W filter limits the redshift to z 4 , indicating that GRB 110709B is dark due to extinction and not a z 18 origin. At z 2 , the observed F606W band samples the rest-frame UV emission from the host galaxy. Based on the observed flux density we infer a star formation rate of SFR 1 M _ yr ^ 1 ken98 , not corrected for possible galactic-scale extinction.

gmw_00007671.name: host of GRB 111215A
gmw_00007671.overview: The host of GRB 111215A is a galaxy located at an estimated redshift of z ~ 1.8 to 2.7 [arXiv:1209.4654]. It is expected to be an actively star-forming galaxy, with an observed g-i color that indicates the presence of extinction [arXiv:1209.4654].

sources.summary: The paper associates GRB 111215A with a detected galaxy, measures optical brightness, constrains the host to z 1.8-2.7, and interprets its color as evidence for extinction.

quotes.quote: Photometry of the galaxy relative to the SDSS catalog results in a brightness of m_g=24.50 0.15 mag and m_i=23.64 0.05 mag corrected for Galactic extinction. The probability of chance coincidence for this galaxy within the XRT error circle is P_ cc 0.01 , with a slightly lower probability of 7 10^ 3 using the radio position, dominated by the physical extent of the galaxy of about 1'' . Given the low chance coincidence probability we consider this galaxy to be the host of GRB 111215A.

We do not identify any emission lines from the host galaxy. Given that the galaxy is reasonably bright in g and i band and is expected to be actively star-forming, the lack of emission lines indicates that z 1.8 (from the non-detection of [ O 2 ] 3727 to 10030 ) and z 2.7 (from the absence of a break due to the Lyman limit to 3400 ). At z 1.8-2.7 , the observed i and g band fluxes trace the host galaxy rest-frame UV emission. The observed color of g-i 0.85 0.20 mag is indicative of extinction; for example, it is well-matched to the observed color of Arp 220, a local ULIRG.

gmw_00007672.name: J021659-044920
gmw_00007672.overview: J021659-044920 is a relic giant radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.3 [arXiv:1508.00030]. It features a very red host galaxy situated between two large-scale radio lobes that span a projected linear size of 1.2 Mpc [arXiv:1508.00030].

sources.summary: The paper reports J021659-044920 as a rare dying giant radio galaxy at z about 1.3, with 1.2 Mpc relic lobes, steep radio spectra, co-spatial X-rays, and a red host galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a relic Giant Radio Galaxy (GRG) J021659-044920 at redshift z 1.3 that exhibits large-scale extended, nearly co-spatial, radio and X-ray emission from radio lobes, but no detection of Active Galactic Nuclei core, jets and hotspots. The total angular extent of the GRG at the observed frame 0.325 GHz, using Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope observations is found to be 2.4 arcmin, that corresponds to a total projected linear size of 1.2 Mpc. The integrated radio spectrum between 0.240 and 1.4 GHz shows high spectral curvature ( _ 0.610 GHz ^ 1.4 GHz _ 0.240 GHz ^ 0.325 GHz > 1.19) with sharp steepening above 0.325 GHz, consistent with relic radio emission that is 8 10 ^ 6 yr old. The radio spectral index map between observed frame 0.325 and 1.4 GHz for the two lobes varies from 1.4 to 2.5 with the steepening trend from outer-end to inner-end, indicating backflow of plasma in the lobes. The extended X-ray emission characterized by an absorbed power-law with photon index 1.86 favours inverse-Compton scattering of the Cosmic Microwave Background (ICCMB) photons as the plausible origin.

The relic GRG without a radio core presented in this study has been assigned a reliable optical counterpart at optical position RA = 02 ^ h 16 ^ m 59 ^ s .064, Dec. = 04^ 49 ^ 20 ^ .85 with spectroscopically measured redshift z_ spec 1.325 see table 3 in simpson06 . The identified host galaxy of the relic GRG is a very red galaxy ( R z ' = 2.0) and resides almost exactly midway between the radio lobes.

gmw_00007673.name: component W
gmw_00007673.overview: Component W is an unlensed hyper-luminous infrared starburst galaxy [arXiv:1806.01951, arXiv:1908.03199] that contains an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1806.01951, arXiv:1908.03199]. It is structured as a regularly rotating disk [arXiv:2303.16227] with rotation-dominated molecular gas [arXiv:1806.01951] and single-temperature dust emission [arXiv:1806.01951, arXiv:1908.03199].

sources.summary: Component W is one of HATLAS J084933's two HyLIRGs, with rotation-dominated molecular gas, single-temperature dust SEDs despite an AGN, and long resolved gas exhaustion times.

quotes.quote: They determined that HATLAS J084933 comprises at least four starburst galaxies scattered across a 100 kpc region at z=2.41 . The two brightest galaxies, dubbed W and T, both HyLIRGs in their own right, are separated by 85 kpc on the sky. Of these, T is amplified modestly by a foreground galaxy with a lensing magnification of 2 . The molecular gas reservoirs of W and T, each 3 kpc in size, are rotation-dominated, and counter-rotate.

Spatially resolved sub-mm spectral energy distributions (SEDs) reveal that component W is well fit with greybody emission from dust at a single temperature over the full extent of the galaxy, despite it containing a powerful AGN, while component T requires an additional component of hotter nuclear dust and additional sources of emission in the mm. We confirm that \ can be used as a rough tracer of warm/dense molecular gas in extreme systems, though the /CO luminosity ratio increases sub-linearly. We obtain an exquisite and unprecedented resolved (2.5-kpc-scale) 'warm/dense molecular gas' Schmidt-Kennicutt (SK) relationship for components W and T. Gas exhaustion times for all apertures in W (T) are 1--4 Gyr (0.5--2 Gyr).

sources.summary: ID18 is identified with unlensed galaxy W in the HATLAS J084933.4+021443 protocluster; the CO(4-3) data show it is a regularly rotating disk.

quotes.quote: ID18 ID18 lies in a well-studied protocluster. This structure was originally found in the largest extragalactic Hershel survey (H-ATLAS). Ivison_2013, Ivison_2019 find that HATLAS J084933.4+021443 hosts an exceptionally high SFR 7000 M _ yr ^ 1 , spread over 5 galaxies (W, T, C, M, E), confined within a scale of 100 kpc . The ALMA data set used here maps galaxy W and T at high resolution and SNR, while the data quality is not sufficient for performing a kinematic analysis on galaxies C, M, and E. Further, galaxy T is not included in the ALPAKA sample as it is a strongly gravitationally lensed source, while galaxy W, that is, ID18 is not lensed Ivison_2013 .
The CO(4-3) data analyzed in this paper indicates that ID18 is a regularly rotating disk.

sources.summary: Component W is described as an unlensed HyLIRG with a broad CO line, single-temperature far-infrared dust emission, and X-ray emission dominated by a Type-1 AGN.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: A point-like X-ray source was found at = 08h 49m 33.59s , = +02^ 14' 44.''6 (J2000) with a 1- statistical uncertainty of 0.4 arcsec

quotes.quote: W is an unlensed HyLIRG. M and C are somewhat less luminous (still \ >10^ 12 L _ , so ULIRGs, and unlensed) yet gas-rich galaxies. An unusually high intrinsic IR luminosity was first suspected for \ because of its broad CO J=1 -0 line, 1,180 km s ^ 1 FWHM, as detected by the Greenbank Telescope (GBT), which harris12 argued was consistent with no gravitational amplification. However, although W does have an unusually broad CO line, 825 115 km s ^ 1 FWHM, and is unlensed, the overall line width was shown by ivison13 to owe much to the velocity dispersion of the aforementioned group of luminous starbursts found within the GBT beam.

Later ALMA observations suggested that the far-IR emission of the most luminous component, W, corresponds to greybody emission from dust at a single temperature, 40 K, throughout the full extent of the galaxy gomez18 . In all of these long-wavelength studies, and also with regard to its rest-frame optical--through--radio SED, \ resembles a starburst, with no obvious sign of any influence from an AGN, though --- like mergers and interactions --- accreting black holes are often difficult to identify in dusty starbursts with anything but the deepest and most complete of datasets.
Nevertheless, the precise positional coincidence of the X-ray source with component W implies that the X-ray emission is dominated by this component.

gmw_00007674.name: component T
gmw_00007674.overview: Component T is a bright, rotation-dominated starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.41 [arXiv:1806.01951]. It is modestly amplified by a foreground gravitational lens and contains a reservoir of hotter nuclear dust [arXiv:1806.01951].

sources.summary: Component T is a bright HyLIRG in HATLAS J084933, showing rotation-dominated molecular gas, lensing amplification, hotter nuclear dust, and a resolved warm-gas relation.

quotes.quote: They determined that HATLAS J084933 comprises at least four starburst galaxies scattered across a 100 kpc region at z=2.41 . The two brightest galaxies, dubbed W and T, both HyLIRGs in their own right, are separated by 85 kpc on the sky. Of these, T is amplified modestly by a foreground galaxy with a lensing magnification of 2 . The molecular gas reservoirs of W and T, each 3 kpc in size, are rotation-dominated, and counter-rotate.

Spatially resolved sub-mm spectral energy distributions (SEDs) reveal that component W is well fit with greybody emission from dust at a single temperature over the full extent of the galaxy, despite it containing a powerful AGN, while component T requires an additional component of hotter nuclear dust and additional sources of emission in the mm. We confirm that \ can be used as a rough tracer of warm/dense molecular gas in extreme systems, though the /CO luminosity ratio increases sub-linearly. We obtain an exquisite and unprecedented resolved (2.5-kpc-scale) 'warm/dense molecular gas' Schmidt-Kennicutt (SK) relationship for components W and T. Gas exhaustion times for all apertures in W (T) are 1--4 Gyr (0.5--2 Gyr).

gmw_00007675.name: component C
gmw_00007675.overview: Component C is a fainter galaxy within the HATLAS J084933 system [arXiv:1806.01951]. It exhibits weak but reliable continuum and line emissions, and its newly resolved internal kinematics are linked to component M by a continuous bridge [arXiv:1806.01951].

sources.summary: Component C is a fainter HATLAS J084933 galaxy with weak but reliable continuum and line detections; its internal kinematics are newly resolved and linked to M by a bridge.

quotes.quote: Continuum emission at all observed frequencies is strongly detected and resolved towards galaxy components W and T and relatively weakly detected in components C and M. The emission lines CO J:3--2, CO J:7--6, and \ are also strongly detected and resolved in W an T and weakly (but reliably) detected and resolved in components C and M.

The emission lines of CO J:3--2, CO J:7--6, \ and 2ofull \ are detected in the four previously identified galaxies of the HATLAS J084933 system (W, T, M and C); of these, the latter has the weakest lines.

Components M and C have smaller velocity gradients, with v_ max = 132 km s ^ 1 (264 km s ^ 1 peak to peak) and v_ max = 164 km s ^ 1 (327 km s ^ 1 peak to peak), respectively. Note that the internal kinematics of components C and M are resolved for the first time in our CO J:7--6 and \ maps. Further, the relatively low spatial resolution CO J:3--2 maps show an almost continuous (in flux and velocity) bridge between C and M.

gmw_00007676.name: component M
gmw_00007676.overview: Component M is a galaxy within the HATLAS J084933 system that exhibits weak but reliable continuum and line emission [arXiv:1806.01951]. Its newly resolved internal kinematics show a small velocity gradient, and a continuous bridge of gas connects it to component C [arXiv:1806.01951].

sources.summary: Component M is a fainter HATLAS J084933 galaxy with weak but reliable continuum and line detections; its internal kinematics are newly resolved and linked to C by a bridge.

quotes.quote: Continuum emission at all observed frequencies is strongly detected and resolved towards galaxy components W and T and relatively weakly detected in components C and M. The emission lines CO J:3--2, CO J:7--6, and \ are also strongly detected and resolved in W an T and weakly (but reliably) detected and resolved in components C and M.

The emission lines of CO J:3--2, CO J:7--6, \ and 2ofull \ are detected in the four previously identified galaxies of the HATLAS J084933 system (W, T, M and C); of these, the latter has the weakest lines.

Components M and C have smaller velocity gradients, with v_ max = 132 km s ^ 1 (264 km s ^ 1 peak to peak) and v_ max = 164 km s ^ 1 (327 km s ^ 1 peak to peak), respectively. Note that the internal kinematics of components C and M are resolved for the first time in our CO J:7--6 and \ maps. Further, the relatively low spatial resolution CO J:3--2 maps show an almost continuous (in flux and velocity) bridge between C and M.

gmw_00007677.name: BCG2
gmw_00007677.overview: BCG2 is the second-ranked brightest cluster galaxy situated at the center of a southwestern subcluster in the Abell 1763 system [arXiv:1812.02645]. This subcluster is dominated by a gaseous secondary system and hosts a wide-angle tail radio galaxy [arXiv:1812.02645].

sources.summary: The paper describes BCG2 as the second-ranked galaxy centered in a southwestern WAT-hosting subcluster, whose motion and orientation make it a likely sloshing perturber.

quotes.quote: Situated at its center is the second-ranked galaxy in the Abell 1763 system ( _ sdss =16.63), also host to a WAT (see Section sec:disc ). Henceforth, we will refer to the brightest galaxies in the primary cluster and subcluster as BCG1 and BCG2, respectively.

The BCG2 subcluster can be seen in Figure fig:excess and Figure fig:excessSDSS in the southwestern quadrant of each image. Except for the emission comprising the G3 extension, the gas distribution is relatively symmetrical, with the second brightest galaxy in the system (BCG2) lying at its center.

To the southwest of the primary cluster lies a gaseous secondary system, dominated by BCG2. Dynamical analysis shows that the two systems are moving with a relative velocity of 1812 km s ^ 1 . Given the location of the BCG2 subcluster and its orientation with respect to the spiral, it is identified as a likely candidate for initiating spiral formation. Within this scenario, the BCG2 system is on a counterclockwise path, having recently passed south of the primary cluster.

gmw_00007678.name: G3
gmw_00007678.overview: G3 is the third-ranked galaxy in the Abell 1763 complex [arXiv:1812.02645]. It is likely the dominant galaxy of an infalling subcluster whose trajectory and velocity may contribute to core sloshing and spiral formation within the system [arXiv:1812.02645].

sources.summary: The paper defines G3 as Abell 1763's third-ranked galaxy, associated with an elongated X-ray feature and a possible infalling subcluster contributing to core sloshing.

quotes.quote: Located within this emission feature, roughly halfway between BCG1 and BCG2, lies the third-ranked galaxy in the Abell 1763 complex (hereafter referred to as G3). A recent study presented in Haines18 probes XMM and Subaru observations of the LoCUSS galaxy cluster sample in search of infalling subsystems. They identify both the BCG2 and G3 systems as subclusters infalling on Abell 1763. They calculate X-ray luminosity derived subcluster masses on the order of 10 ^ 14 M _ for each.
This elongated feature is coincident with G3, the third-ranked galaxy in the system.

In Section sec:discG3 we discuss the third-ranked galaxy (G3) in the system which is likely the dominant galaxy in an additional infalling subcluster. It is located at an intermediate distance between BCG1 and BCG2 (distance to BCG1 750 kpc). Its trajectory and velocity relative to the primary mean v 1500 km s ^ 1 , allow for the conclusion that the system's ICM halo and associated DM structure might be contributing prominently to core sloshing and spiral formation. It may also be playing a substantial role in generating the large peculiar velocity of BCG1, imparting upon it significant gravitational acceleration in an already complex dynamical environment.

gmw_00007679.name: NGC 1516
gmw_00007679.overview: NGC 1516 is an interacting system containing two galaxies [arXiv:2101.06242]. It is also known as the host galaxy of the supernova SN 2018ezx [arXiv:2101.06242].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1516, the SN 2018ezx host, to test its SFH-grouping algorithm, noting high-SNR MUSE data and an interacting two-galaxy field.

quotes.quote: We choose NGC 1516, the host galaxy of SN 2018ezx, to test the algorithm. The SNR of the MUSE observations of NGC 1516 is high, and there are 8682 spatial pixels satisfying our spectral selection limit ( sec:data ). It is an interacting galaxy with both galaxies inside the FoV of MUSE, which can potentially have two or more groups of SFHs in the data cube.

gmw_00007680.name: XBSJ120359.1+443715
gmw_00007680.overview: XBSJ120359.1+443715 is a candidate narrow-line quasar located at a redshift of z=0.541 [arXiv:0710.0955]. The galaxy exhibits a blue spectrum and displays strong iron emission [arXiv:0710.0955].

sources.summary: The survey describes XBSJ120359.1+443715 as a z=0.541 source with a blue spectrum and strong Fe II emission, classifying it as an NLQSO candidate.

quotes.quote: The second source (XBSJ120359.1+443715, z=0.541) has a blue spectrum and a quite strong Fe II4570 \ hump which is usually considered as the signature of a NLSy1. Unfortunately we cannot further quantify the strength of this hump in respect to the H line since this latter line falls outside the observed spectrum. We classify this object as NLQSO candidate.

gmw_00007681.name: XBSJ124214.1--112512
gmw_00007681.overview: XBSJ124214.1--112512 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a type 1 AGN [arXiv:0710.0955]. Its optical spectrum exhibits a magnesium II emission line with a narrow core and a broad wing, and its continuum emission shows modest extinction [arXiv:0710.0955].

sources.summary: The survey reports MgII emission with a narrow core and broad wing in XBSJ124214.1--112512, fits its continuum with modest extinction, and classifies it as type 1 AGN.

quotes.quote: Finally, in the third object (XBSJ124214.1--112512, z=0.82) we have detected the MgII 2798 \ emission line with a relatively narrow (FWHM 1900 km s ^ 1 ) core plus a broad wing. Both the FeII 4570 \ and the HeII line fall outside the observed range and we cannot apply the diagnostic criteria discussed above. Using the spectral model described in Section 8 we have successfully fitted the observed continuum emission using a value of A _V 0.5 mag i.e. below the 2 mag limit that corresponds to our classification criteria (see Section 8). We thus classify this object as type 1 AGN.

gmw_00007682.name: Source 1
gmw_00007682.overview: Source 1 is a compact, nonthermal X-ray and radio source that is interpreted as the active nucleus of a one-sided radio galaxy [arXiv:1510.06351]. It is physically associated with an aligned, elongated diffuse emission source that represents its radio lobe [arXiv:1510.06351].

sources.summary: The paper treats Source 1 as a compact nonthermal X-ray/radio source and favors interpreting it as the active nucleus of a one-sided radio galaxy with Source 2 as its lobe.

quotes.quote: Observations reveal a point-like source aligned with elongated diffuse emission. The X-ray spectra of these sources is best-fitted by an absorbed power law with photon index 1.7 for the point-like source and 2.0 for the extended source. Both sources show nonthermal radio-continuum counterparts that might indicate a physical association. In addition, from the available data, we did not detect variability on the point-like source in several timescales.

Second, an extra-Galactic nature is discussed, where the point-like source might be the core of a radio galaxy and the extended source its lobe. In this case, we compare derived fluxes, spectral indices, and spatial correlation with those typical from the radio galaxy population, showing the feasibility of this alternative astrophysical scenario. From the available observational evidence, we suggest that the most promising scenario to explain the nature of these sources is a system consisting of a one-sided radio galaxy, where the point-like source is an active galactic nucleus and the extended source corresponds to the emission from its lobe.

gmw_00007683.name: IRAS 05024--1941
gmw_00007683.overview: IRAS 05024--1941 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy that exhibits starburst-like systemic neon excitation [arXiv:0907.4370]. It displays strong evidence for an obscured active galactic nucleus and shows outflow signatures in its neon lines [arXiv:0907.4370].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 05024--1941 among sources with starburst-like systemic neon excitation, high radio luminosity, outflow signatures, and evidence for a powerful obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: In pure starburst galaxies the excitation of the nuclear starburst as measured from the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] line ratio is usually low and in the range of 0.05--0.2 thornley00,bernard-salas09 A notable exception may be the pure starburst galaxy NGC 7714, which has a measured [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio of 0.75 bernard-salas09 . In our sample of sixteen excitation conditions like these are met for the systemic components of IRAS 05024--1941, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 12127--1412NE, IRAS 15462--0450 and IRAS 15225+2350. Among these IRAS 15225+2350 is the only source optically classified as a starburst.

Given the high radio luminosities for IRAS 13451+1232, IRAS F00183--7111, IRAS 11119+3257, IRAS 12127--1412NE, and IRAS 05024--1941, the apparent correlation between radio luminosity and [ Ne iii ] line width in Fig. fig4 may indicate that for these sources interaction of expanding radio jets with the dense ISM surrounding the AGN may explain the observed neon line kinematics. Four of the sources in Fig. fig2 which show outflow signatures in their neon lines do not show the tell-tale mid-infrared signature of AGN activity, the presence of a 14.32 m [ Ne v ] line, in their spectrum. While this may indicate their outflows to be starburst-driven, three of these sources, IRAS F00183--7111, IRAS 05024--1941 and IRAS 12127--1412NE, display strong evidence for the presence of a powerful AGN -- e.g. 1.4 GHz radio fluxes 4--10 times in excess of the value expected for star forming galaxies. In addition, IRAS F00183--7111 and IRAS 12127--1412NE have silicate strengths S _ sil ; spoon07 below -2.5, indicating an apparent silicate optical depth of at least 2.5 toward the nucleus.

gmw_00007684.name: IRAS 15462--0450
gmw_00007684.overview: IRAS 15462--0450 is an active galaxy that exhibits starburst-like systemic excitation [arXiv:0907.4370]. It features an extreme neon outflow signature [arXiv:0907.4370] and is classified as a narrow-line object with a large oxygen emission line width [arXiv:astro-ph/0107528].

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 15462--0450 as having distinct systemic and blue-shifted neon components, starburst-like systemic excitation, and an extreme [Ne v] outflow signature.

quotes.quote: For other sources two gaussian components can be discerned, one at systemic velocity, the other at blue shifted velocities. Examples are the [ Ne ii ] line of IRAS 07598+6508 and the [ Ne iii ] line of IRAS 15462--0450.

In pure starburst galaxies the excitation of the nuclear starburst as measured from the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] line ratio is usually low and in the range of 0.05--0.2 thornley00,bernard-salas09 A notable exception may be the pure starburst galaxy NGC 7714, which has a measured [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] ratio of 0.75 bernard-salas09 . In our sample of sixteen excitation conditions like these are met for the systemic components of IRAS 05024--1941, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 12127--1412NE, IRAS 15462--0450 and IRAS 15225+2350. Among these IRAS 15225+2350 is the only source optically classified as a starburst.

The most extreme sources in this subsample do not even exhibit a [ Ne v ] component at systemic velocity. Examples are IZw1, IRAS 05189--2524, IRAS 07598+6508, IRAS 11119+3257 and IRAS 15462--0450. This strongly suggests that at least in these sources the observed [ Ne v ] emission cannot arise in NLR gas in keplerian rotation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 15462-0450 as a narrow-line outlier with a large [OIII] width; it says HII subtraction and spectral resolution can make the NLR width significantly overestimated, so it excludes the object.

quotes.quote: It is clearly shown in Figures 1 and 2 that five NL objects, Mrk 507, Mrk 684, IRAS 15462-0450, MS 22102+1827, and PG 1404+226, having large [ OIII ] widths, deviate from other NL objects. Note that Mrk 507, Mrk 684, IRAS 15462-0450 are the three objects for which a narrow HII region contribution has been subtracted in the VVG sample. For spectra with a resolution of 3.4 used by VVG, the HII component may not be reliably separated from a narrow component of NLR with width 200---500 km s ^ 1 if an additional broad wing is present. Thus the widths of NLR in these three objects are likely to be significantly over-estimated.

gmw_00007685.name: J1214+0940
gmw_00007685.overview: J1214+0940 is a low-metallicity blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1609.02358]. It is relatively gas-poor compared to similar objects, with only 27 percent of its baryonic mass in gaseous form [arXiv:1609.02358].

sources.summary: The paper notes that J1214+0940 has no nearby H I contamination, is unusually gas-poor relative to most lower-metallicity GBT objects, and shows no obvious distinguishing characteristic.

quotes.quote: For J1214+0940 with v = 1702 km s ^ 1 , there is a faint diffuse yellow galaxy at 4 5 of the BCD in the NW direction, but its velocity is 1245 km s ^ 1 .

It is seen that while the high-metallicity ( [ O/H ] 8.0) SINGS galaxies span the range 0 f_ gas 0.95, the lower-metallicity GBT objects (with the exception of one galaxy, the BCD J1214+0940 with f_ gas = 0.27) span the more restricted range 0.65 f_ gas 0.99, i.e. the vast majority of low-metallicity ( [ O/H ] 7.8) have more than 65 of their baryonic mass in gaseous form.
We have examined J1214+0940 to see if it possesses any particular characteristic that would make it relatively gas-poor, but found none.

gmw_00007686.name: J0944+0936
gmw_00007686.overview: J0944+0936 is classified as a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1609.02358]. It exhibits a multi-knot structure, and its star formation rate and stellar mass are excluded from statistical studies due to highly uncertain aperture corrections [arXiv:1609.02358].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0944+0936 as a multi-knot BCD and excludes its star formation rate and stellar mass from statistical analyses because its aperture corrections are highly uncertain.

quotes.quote: Using the classification terminology of San16 , we find 3 galaxies (J0133+1343, J1055+5111 and J1201+0211) or 10 of the sample to be symmetric, with a centrally concentrated emission, 3 galaxies (J1044+0353, J1202+5415 and J2238+1400) possessing 2 knots (10 ), 5 galaxies (J0908+0517, J0944+0936, J1148+5400, J1150+5627 and J1335+4910) having multiple knots (17 ) and the remaining 18 galaxies (63 ) having a cometary structure.

The two galaxies J0944+0936 and J1148+5400 have a large difference between m_r(tot) and m_r(3 ) (more than 4.5 mag), and hence large uncertain aperture corrections. We have thus decided not to include their SFRs in our statistical studies.
Like the SFRs, the M_* of the galaxies J0944+0936 and J1148+5400 are not included in our statistical studies because of their large uncertain aperture corrections.

gmw_00007687.name: J1148+5400
gmw_00007687.overview: J1148+5400 is classified as a blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1609.02358]. The galaxy exhibits a multi-knot structure, though its star formation rate and stellar mass remain highly uncertain due to large aperture corrections [arXiv:1609.02358].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J1148+5400 as a multi-knot BCD and excludes its star formation rate and stellar mass from statistical analyses because its aperture corrections are highly uncertain.

quotes.quote: Using the classification terminology of San16 , we find 3 galaxies (J0133+1343, J1055+5111 and J1201+0211) or 10 of the sample to be symmetric, with a centrally concentrated emission, 3 galaxies (J1044+0353, J1202+5415 and J2238+1400) possessing 2 knots (10 ), 5 galaxies (J0908+0517, J0944+0936, J1148+5400, J1150+5627 and J1335+4910) having multiple knots (17 ) and the remaining 18 galaxies (63 ) having a cometary structure.

The two galaxies J0944+0936 and J1148+5400 have a large difference between m_r(tot) and m_r(3 ) (more than 4.5 mag), and hence large uncertain aperture corrections. We have thus decided not to include their SFRs in our statistical studies.
Like the SFRs, the M_* of the galaxies J0944+0936 and J1148+5400 are not included in our statistical studies because of their large uncertain aperture corrections.

gmw_00007688.name: object C
gmw_00007688.overview: Object C is an H I dwarf galaxy located to the east of VIRGOHI 21 [arXiv:astro-ph/0508153]. It shows no signs of interaction and is too undermassive to explain the dynamics of its neighbor [arXiv:astro-ph/0508153].

sources.summary: Object C is described as an H I galaxy east of VIRGOHI 21, but its velocity distribution, size, luminosity, and undisturbed appearance make it too small to explain the dynamics.

quotes.quote: (d) If object C, the H i galaxy just to the east (left) of VIRGOHI 21 (Fig. 2) were involved its mass, by (a), must be 10^ 11 M_ . However its measured H i velocity distribution and size suggest a probable mass 10^9 M_ , while it has a luminosity of only 10^8 L_ and a M_ HI of 4 10^6 M_ . It is two orders of magnitude too undermassive and underluminous to explain the dynamics of VIRGOHI 21, while there is no sign of its interacting.

gmw_00007689.name: GALEXASC J115117.64-293638.0
gmw_00007689.overview: GALEXASC J115117.64-293638.0 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=0.063 [arXiv:1511.01718]. Its spectrum displays several narrow emission lines, which indicate a near-solar oxygen abundance [arXiv:1511.01718].

sources.summary: The presumed host galaxy was observed in the slit; its spectrum gives a redshift consistent with LSQ13fn and a near-solar oxygen abundance from narrow emission lines.

quotes.quote: The edge of the presumed host galaxy (GALEXASC J115117.64-293638.0; Fig. fig_local_image ) was fortuitously included in the slit during six of the ten spectroscopic epochs. The range in slit position angles used among the epochs was between 75 90 ^ (subtended from N to E), and the galaxy spectrum was located on average 5 '' (or at a projected distance of 6.4 kpc) from the SN and 3 9 (or 4.9 kpc) from the galaxy nucleus. Several narrow emission lines were apparent in the galaxy spectrum that could easily be identified (see Fig. fig_host_spec ). We used these lines to calculate a redshift of z=0.063 0.001 by measuring the central wavelengths and averaging over each epoch. This redshift corresponds to a distance modulus of =37.1 0.1 mag ( d=264 Mpc), with H_ 0 =75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ M =0.27 and _ =0.73 , and is completely consistent with that derived from the SN classification spectrum z=0.064 ; Sollerman_2013 .

We estimated the oxygen abundance of the host galaxy spectra using the N2 calibration of pettini_pagel_2004 . The calibration is based on the flux ratio of emission lines that are close in wavelength: [ N ii ] 6583 and H 6563 - thus the sensitivity to precise flux calibration and extinction is much reduced. Taking the mean over all six extracted spectra results in an oxygen abundance of 12+ log(O/H) =8.6 0.2 , which is close to the solar value Z_ =8.69 ; Asplund_2009 .

gmw_00007690.name: ERS 8290
gmw_00007690.overview: ERS 8290 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as a z-band dropout [arXiv:1107.1261]. Spectroscopic observations detected an emission line with an asymmetric profile characteristic of Lyman-alpha, suggesting a redshift of z = 5.286 [arXiv:1107.1261].

sources.summary: The paper treats ERS 8290 as a z-band dropout with an apparent Ly-alpha line, but notes the inferred redshift conflicts with photometry and may involve a nearby V-drop candidate.

quotes.quote: Figure 2 (bottom panel) shows a 2-D spectroscopic montage of 4 sources for which line emission may be present, of which one case (HUDF09 1596) is marginal (2 ) and the other (ERS 8290) lies outside the expected redshift range if it is Ly . The emission line apparently seen in ERS 8290 (a z' band dropout) is detected at > 5 with a flux of 6.7 0.8 10^ 18 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 at 7644 2 . It also exhibits the asymmetric profile characteristic of Ly in the 1D extraction, but this would place the object at z=5.286 , quite discrepant from our photometric estimate of z = 6.52. However, upon examining the positioning of our LRIS slits more carefully (bottom panel of Figure 2), we find there is a faint V drop candidate with i' = 27.5 and z' = 27.9 only 0.4^ away which would have been at least partially visible through our slit during the exposure. After subtracting the line flux from the i' band photometry, we determine a photometric redshift of z = 4.91, in reasonable agreement with the Keck spectroscopy, particularly given a greater line flux (as is likely given the object's poor positioning in the slit) would increase the photometric redshift estimate.

gmw_00007691.name: ERS 8496
gmw_00007691.overview: ERS 8496 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 6.441 [arXiv:1107.1261]. Its Lyman-alpha emission line displays an asymmetric profile characterized by a steeply rising blue edge and a slowly decaying red tail [arXiv:1107.1261].

sources.summary: The paper reports ERS 8496 as a Keck-detected high-redshift Ly-alpha emitter, measuring its asymmetric line profile, spectroscopic redshift, and rest-frame equivalent width.

quotes.quote: The two satisfactory detections refer to emission at 9045 2 \ for ERS 8496 in the LRIS mask and emission at 9780 \ 4 \ in the NIRSPEC exposure of the lensed source Abell 1703 zD6. Both objects are detected at 5 in our final exposures. In our 1D extraction of ERS 8496, the emission line has a FWHM of 9 1 , and displays an asymmetric profile with a steeply rising blue edge and slowly decaying red tail, characteristic features of Ly at high redshift.

If both lines are Ly , the implied spectroscopic redshifts for ERS 8496 and Abell 1703 zD6 are z=6.441 0.002 and 7.045 0.004 , respectively, in excellent agreement with our photometric predictions of 6.52 and that of 7.0 derived by .
Under this assumption, taking the magnitude from the first filter in which the object is detected ( Y_ 098M for ERS 8496, and J_ 125 for 1703 zD6), we calculate EWs of 69 10 and 65 12 , respectively.

gmw_00007692.overview: 013815+00 is a quasar at a redshift of z=0.94 that recently transitioned from a radio-quiet to a radio-loud state [arXiv:2007.01590]. This transition was triggered by enhanced accretion onto its central black hole, which ignited a compact, expanding radio jet [arXiv:2007.01590].

sources.summary: The paper presents 013815+00 as a quasar rapidly transitioning from radio quiet to radio loud, with accretion-disk brightening, compact radio morphology, and a newborn jet.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The radio emission of quasar 013815+00 (RA 01h38m15.061s, Dec 00d29m14.07s) was detected on 2013 December 20 at 3 GHz by the Caltech-NRAO Stripe 82 Survey (CNSS), a multi-epoch radio transient survey carried out between December 2012 and May 2015 with the Jansky VLA Mooley .

quotes.quote: Here we present a detection of a rapid transition from the radio-quiet to the radio-loud mode in quasar 013815+00 (z=0.94) which coincides with changes of its UV-optical continuum and the low ionization MgII broadline. We interpret this as an enhancement of accretion onto a central black hole of mass about 10^9 solar masses. As a consequence a new radio-loud AGN was born. Its spectral and morphological properties indicate that it went through the short gigahertz-peaked spectrum (GPS) phase at the beginning of its activity and has now stabilized its flux density at the level of a few mJy. The radio morphology of 013815+00 is very compact and we predict that with such short-term jet activity its development will be very slow.

The burst of radio activity in quasar 013815+00 detected on 2013 December 20 changed the source status from radio-quiet to radio-loud. The expanding new radio jet is responsible for the convex radio spectrum peaking at 5 GHz in the early stage of activity but also for the rapid changes observed in this spectrum during the next few years. Ignition of radio emission coincides with a significant increase in the brightness of the accretion disk and emission lines.

gmw_00007693.name: G1
gmw_00007693.overview: G1 is a cluster-member galaxy located near a giant arc [arXiv:2401.10168]. Its individual galaxy-scale halo contribution is required and optimized in the lens model to accurately reproduce the lensing configuration of the arc's star-forming clumps [arXiv:2401.10168].

sources.summary: The paper treats G1 as a cluster-member galaxy near the giant arc whose galaxy-scale halo contribution is required and individually optimized in the lens model.

quotes.quote: These cluster-member galaxies were the BCG, which is not expected to follow the same scaling relations as other cluster-member galaxies; and the galaxy labeled G1 in fig:generalmodel , because of its proximity to the giant arc.

Contribution to the lensing potential from galaxy G1 was required in order to reproduce the lensing configuration of the star forming clumps observed in the giant arc. The normalization parameter of G1 was optimized individually, in order to add flexibility to the lens model and avoid biasing the scaling relation parameters towards a solution that is primarily driven by one galaxy.

gmw_00007694.name: ESO 306-017
gmw_00007694.overview: ESO 306-017 is a massive fossil-group elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.036 [arXiv:1208.4060]. The galaxy hosts an abundant population of globular clusters and exhibits a tidal tail that indicates a recent galactic interaction [arXiv:1208.4060].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 306-017 as the most massive fossil-group galaxy in the sample, with abundant globular clusters, high specific frequency, and tidal features suggesting recent interaction.

quotes.quote: In order to have a representative sample we choose: the nearest, NGC 6482 ( z=0.013 ), the prototype, NGC 1132 ( z=0.023 ), and the most massive known to date, ESO 306-017 ( z=0.036 ), shown in Fig. pretty_pictures .

For ESO 306-017 we can see a feature around _ 10 () 1.7 , that might be stripped material from the second brightest galaxy in the image, resulting in an excess of light. Furthermore, the residual images (Fig. sersic_residuals ) show evidence of galactic interaction: apparent shells in NGC 1132, a tidal tail in ESO 306-017 and an inner disk in NGC 6482.

The main results of this study are: We detected 369, 1410 and 1918 GCs down to magnitude z =24.7, 26.2 and 26.2 in NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively; after completeness correction and assuming a Gaussian GCLF with expected M^0 _g=-7.2 and _ GCLF =1.4 , the number of GCs in each FOV are: 1140 87, 3613 295 and 11577 1046, respectively. The derived values of S_N are: 1.9 0.1 , 3.1 0.3 and 6.3 0.6 for NGC 6482, NGC 1132 and ESO 306-017, respectively. From analysis of the surface brightness distributions, we found evidence of recent interactions, particularly in ESO 306-017, which shows a tidal feature coincident with the finger reported previously in X-rays, and NGC 1132, which has shell-like structure.

gmw_00007695.name: SDSS J 142947
gmw_00007695.overview: SDSS J 142947 is a Green Pea galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.17 [arXiv:2003.07187]. It exhibits strong, double-peak emission [arXiv:2003.07187] and a double-horned spatial profile caused by central absorption [arXiv:1610.05767].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J 142947 as a z~0.17 Green Pea galaxy, fitting narrow and blue-shifted emission components, finding dense enriched outflowing gas and possible low-density LyC escape channels.

quotes.quote: We investigate the ionised gas kinematics, physical properties and chemical abundances of SDSS J 142947 , a Green Pea galaxy at redshift z 0.17 with strong, double-peak \ emission and indirect evidence of Lyman continuum (LyC) leakage. Using high-dispersion spectroscopy, we perform a multi-component analysis of emission-line profiles. Our model consistently fits all lines as a narrow component with intrinsic velocity dispersion 40 , and two broader blue-shifted components with 90 \ and 250 .

The blue-shifted broader components are consistent with highly turbulent, possibly clumpy ionised gas at the base of a strong photoionised outflow, which accounts for 50 of the integrated emission-line fluxes. The outflow is dense and metal-enriched compared to the \ regions, with expansion velocities larger than those obtained from UV interstellar absorption lines under standard assumptions. Some of these metals may be able to escape, with outflows loading factors comparable to those found in high- z galaxies of similar SFR/Area. Our findings depict a two-stage starburst picture; hard radiation fields from young star clusters illuminate a turbulent and clumpy ISM that has been eroded by SNe feedback. While UV data suggest an extended \ halo with high average \ column density, LyC photons could only escape from SDSS J 142947 through low \ density channels or filaments in the ISM approaching density-bounded conditions, traced by outflowing gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes GP1429 + 0643 as having central absorption that produces a double-horned spatial profile, unusually large emission size, similar blue/red profiles, and residual near-zero-velocity emission.

quotes.quote: In GP1429 + 0643, a large fraction of the \ emission in the galactic center is absorbed, resulting in a double horned spatial profile.
In GP1429 + 0643 which has a double horned spatial profile, the deconvolved \ FWHM is about 7 times the UV FWHM.

In the other four cases (GP1244 + 0216, GP1133 + 6514, GP1429 + 0643, and GP1219 + 1526), the blue-part and red-part \ emission are very similar. We also noticed that the \ spatial profiles show a relation with the \ velocity profiles -- the objects with weaker blue peak in \ velocity profile (i.e. small flux ratio of blue-part to red-part \ emission), such as GP1137 + 3524, GP0911 + 1831, and GP0926 + 4428, also have broader blue-part spatial profiles. On the other hand, GP1133 + 6514, which has the strongest blue peak in \ velocity profile, seems to show slightly more compact blue-part \ emission than the red-part \ emission. In four Green Peas (GP1219 + 1526, GP1133 + 6514, GP0926 + 4428, and GP1429 + 0643), the \ velocity profiles show large residual emission at velocity near zero.

gmw_00007696.name: NGC 2935
gmw_00007696.overview: NGC 2935 is a barred spiral galaxy classified as a transition object that is not a Seyfert or LINER [arXiv:1204.6212]. It features circumnuclear, inner, and outer rings [arXiv:2111.08846], along with gas-emission knots and a positive abundance gradient in its bar region [arXiv:1204.6212].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 2935 as a photometric frequency-curve example, finding an ILR similar to previous work, larger CR and OLR within uncertainty, and rings near resonance locations.

quotes.quote: Figure Fig1 shows examples of the frequency curves (right panels) for IC 1438 and NGC 2935 obtained from our photometric approach with our adopted dynamical M/L ratio.

The pattern speeds _ bar of these two galaxies were estimated by 2019Schmidt from photometric methods mentioned above. _ bar is displayed by a horizontal line of orange color. R_ ILR (green), R_ UHR (gray), R_ CR (blue), and R_ OLR (red) are presented with uncertainties on the horizontal line of _ bar .

In the case of NGC 2935 (bottom row), our estimate of R_ ILR is similar to that of 2019Schmidt , whereas R_ CR and R_ OLR are larger than theirs (but still similar considering the uncertainty). The right panels of Figure Fig1 show that IC 1438 and NGC 2935 have all kinds of resonances, including ILR, UHR, CR, and OLR. In the left panels, their color index maps show blue features appearing the circumnuclear, inner, and outer rings.

sources.summary: The paper finds larger-radius gas-emission knots in NGC 2935, treats it as a transition object, and reports a positive bar-region abundance gradient.

quotes.quote: For seven galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 1530, NGC 1832, NGC 2665, NGC 2935, NGC 3081 and NGC 4314) we have also found knots of gas emission at larger radii, corresponding to one or several crowded \ regions.
The run1 galaxies that are not classified as Seyfert or LINER, NGC 2665 and NGC 2935, are transition objects.

If we consider all the detected emission in the bar region at a 3- significance, for the star formation galaxies, we detect a positive gradient for three galaxies (NGC 1169, NGC 2523 and NGC 2935) and a negative gradient is observed for two galaxies (NGC 1832 and NGC 2962).

gmw_00007697.name: Sagittarius dwarf galaxy
gmw_00007697.overview: The Sagittarius dwarf galaxy is a prolate, tidally disrupted system with a total mass of approximately 4e8 solar masses [arXiv:2006.02929]. It is currently out of equilibrium and is expected to be completely disrupted within the next gigayear [arXiv:2006.02929].

sources.summary: The paper models the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy remnant using Gaia astrometry, photometry, velocities, and N-body simulations, finding a prolate, tidally disrupted system of about 4e8 solar masses likely to dissolve within a Gyr.

quotes.quote: We use the astrometric and photometric data from Data Release 2 and line-of-sight velocities from various other surveys to study the 3d structure and kinematics of the Sagittarius dwarf galaxy. The combination of photometric and astrometric data makes it possible to obtain a very clean separation of Sgr member stars from the Milky Way foreground; our final catalogue contains 2.6 10^5 candidate members with magnitudes G<18 , more than half of them being red clump stars. We construct and analyze maps of the mean proper motion and its dispersion over the region 30 12 degrees, which show a number of interesting features. The intrinsic 3d density distribution (orientation, thickness) is strongly constrained by kinematics; we find that the remnant is a prolate structure with the major axis pointing at 45^ from the orbital velocity and extending up to 5 kpc, where it transitions into the stream. We perform a large suite of N body simulations of a disrupting Sgr galaxy as it orbits the Milky Way over the past 2.5 Gyr, which are tailored to reproduce the observed properties of the remnant (not the stream).

The total mass of the remnant is 4 10^8 M_ , of which roughly a quarter resides in stars. The galaxy is significantly out of equilibrium, and even its central density is below the limit required to withstand tidal forces. We conclude that the Sgr galaxy will likely be disrupted over the next Gyr.

gmw_00007698.name: IC 356
gmw_00007698.overview: IC 356 is a spiral galaxy classified as SA(s)ab pec [arXiv:1108.3662]. Its inner disk exhibits multiple spiral arms and dust lanes, while its outer profile is characterized by tightly wrapped spirals [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 356's outer profile as III-d and describes bar evidence, spiral structure, dust features shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: IC 356 (III-d; SA(s)ab pec) : The disk of this galaxy dominates the light at > 30 (2 kpc) from the center of the galaxy. The inner disk clearly shows spiral structure with multiple arms and dust lanes, and the bulge also shows dust lanes. Tightly wrapped spirals are clearly visible outside the antitruncation break, and the ellipticity is roughly constant out to 300 , so this is a clear III-d profile. The profile is similar in some respects to that of NGC 4612 (Paper I), though there is no bar in IC 356.

gmw_00007699.name: IC 499
gmw_00007699.overview: IC 499 is an Sa-type galaxy that contains an unclassified inner bar measuring 1.5 kiloparsecs in length [arXiv:1108.3662]. Its outer profile exhibits a classical Type II truncation break, followed further out by a Type III-s antitruncation [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 499's outer profile as II.o-CT + III-s and describes bar evidence, antitruncation, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: IC 499 (II.o-CT + III-s; Sa) : Though this galaxy is not classified as barred, we find an inner bar of length 10 (1.5 kpc) (Section sec:bars ). The profile has a clear Type II break at 48.5 (6.8 kpc); since this is more than four times the bar radius, we consider this a ``classical truncation'' rather than an OLR break. Further outside, we find an antitruncation at a radius of 93 (13 kpc). The ellipticity declines from this point outwards, which leads to our classification of Type III-s.

gmw_00007700.name: NGC 949
gmw_00007700.overview: NGC 949 is an unbarred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0312268] classified as SA(rs)b [arXiv:1108.3662]. Its structure features a disky pseudobulge with strong dust streaks [arXiv:1108.3662], a warped disk [arXiv:1909.04048], and kinematically decoupled off-plane gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0312268].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 949 as an unbarred spiral with a zero central major-axis gas gradient, a plateau-like gas rotation curve, and non-zero minor-axis velocities attributed to decoupled off-plane gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 949, NGC 2460, NGC 2541, and NGC 6810 are unbarred spiral galaxies which belong to a sample of 16 bright ( B_T 13.5 ) and nearby ( V_ 3500 ) spirals studied by Coccato et al. (2004) to search for a circumnuclear Keplerian disk (Bertola et al. 1998; Funes et al. 2002). The main properties of the sample galaxies are summarized in Table tab:sample . The major-axis rotation curve of their gaseous component is characterized by a remarkable zero-velocity plateau.

The central velocity gradient along the major axis is zero (NGC 949, NGC 2541, NGC 6810) or at least less steep (NGC 2460) than that we measured for radii larger than 3'' . Further out the gas velocity increases to reach a maximum and then remains constant out to the last observed radius. Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis of all the sample galaxies, in spite of which would be expected if the gas traced the circular velocity in the disk plane.
We attribute the minor-axis velocity gradients to the presence of kinematically-decoupled component which is not rotating in the plane of the stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 949's outer profile as III-d and describes spiral structure, dust features, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 949 (III-d; SA(rs)b) : The bulge shows strong streaks of dust, and is really bright out to 15.6 (830 pc). Further out the disk is smooth and shows no real evidence of spiral structure. The isophotes beyond the break radius appear to have approximately the same shape as those inside (though there may be a slight position-angle twist), so we consider this a III-d profile. As the ellipse fits show a roughly constant ellipticity inwards as far as some 10 arcseconds from the center, it is probable that nominal photometric bulge ( > 0 arcsec) is in fact a ``disky pseudobulge'' see kormendy04,erwin03b .

sources.summary: The paper identifies a warp in NGC 0949, fixes median geometry for modeling, and finds its EPG fraction reaches the sample maximum.

quotes.quote: NGC 0949. The tilted ring fit with \ indicates clearly the presence of a warp ( >10 ) in both inclination and position angle. Given the limited number of resolution elements (7 per side), we have decided to fix the INC and PA to median values for both the rotation curve and the EPG modelling. We have verified that the EPG mass and kinematics do not change significantly if we use a rotation curve that accounts for the warp.
The EPG fraction, f_ EPG , varies from 9 (NGC 3198) to 27 (NGC 0949) with a mean value of 14 , and is fairly consistent with the values typically quoted in the literature e.g. Fraternali10 .

gmw_00007701.name: NGC 972
gmw_00007701.overview: NGC 972 is classified as an Sab galaxy with a complex, dust-rich bulge and a Type III-s outer profile [arXiv:1108.3662]. Near-infrared observations reveal a small central bar and a two-armed spiral pattern, while ionized gas within the central region shows non-circular streams [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 972's outer profile as III-s and describes spiral structure, dust features, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 972 (III-s; Sab) : This has a complex bulge with an obvious high dust content, irregularly distributed. The spiral form can just be discerned in the disk. Beyond 43 (4.5 kpc) the disk is smooth, and gives way to two lobes further out (more visible to the North) which may be arm fragments, or an external pseudoring at 90 (9.5 kpc). It is at this point where the antitruncation begins. The ellipticity starts to fall systematically from the break, which accounts for our classification as Type III-s.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 972 as morphologically ambiguous, with a pseudo-ring, NIR bar, strong central non-circular gas streams, and star-formation-related residual velocities.

quotes.quote: The near-H continuum image (Fig. ngc972 a) lacks a spiral structure, but on the H image (Fig. ngc972 b) the HII regions concentrate toward a pseudo-ring with the radius of about 30 . Several works which analyze CCD-data on NGC 972 (Ravindranath \ Prabnu 1998, Zasov \ Moiseev 1998) also contain remarks about the absence of a noticeable spiral structure on the broad-band optical images and about a large amount of dust within the ring of HII regions. However, near-infrared images obtained through JHK filters reveal a prominent two-armed spiral pattern and a small bar with the size of 10 \ elongated in PA=175 180 (Mayya et al. 1998; Zasov \ Moiseev 1998).

The isovelocities of the ionized-gas velocity field within the central 10 \ have a characteristic S-shape (Fig. ngc972 ), evidently induced by a presence of the bar seen in NIR. A turn of the kinematic major axis in the center exceeds 30 (Fig. ngc972 d); the non-circular (radial?) gas streams in the bar are so significant that a cross-section of the velocity field in PA 30 gives an impression of counterrotation.

gmw_00007702.name: NGC 1161
gmw_00007702.overview: NGC 1161 is classified as an S0^0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1108.3662]. Although previously suggested to be a barred galaxy, photometric analysis shows no evidence of a bar and reveals a weak dust lane located 1.1 kiloparsecs from its center [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1161's outer profile as I and describes bar evidence, dust features, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 1161 (I; S0 ^0 ) : franco03 suggested for this object the classification SAB0 pec. However, we find no evidence for a bar in this galaxy. Their suggestion might be due to the presence of a weak dust lane at 8 (1.1 kpc) from the center of the galaxy. No break in the profile is seen out to at least 4 scalelengths.

gmw_00007703.name: NGC 2460
gmw_00007703.overview: NGC 2460 is an unbarred, intermediate-inclination spiral galaxy classified as SA(s)a [arXiv:astro-ph/0312268, arXiv:1108.3662]. It features a tightly wound, multi-armed spiral structure, with non-zero minor-axis gas velocities that suggest a kinematically decoupled component [arXiv:astro-ph/0312268, arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2460 as an unbarred spiral whose central major-axis gas gradient is less steep than farther out, with non-zero minor-axis velocities attributed to decoupled off-plane gas.

quotes.quote: NGC 949, NGC 2460, NGC 2541, and NGC 6810 are unbarred spiral galaxies which belong to a sample of 16 bright ( B_T 13.5 ) and nearby ( V_ 3500 ) spirals studied by Coccato et al. (2004) to search for a circumnuclear Keplerian disk (Bertola et al. 1998; Funes et al. 2002). The main properties of the sample galaxies are summarized in Table tab:sample . The major-axis rotation curve of their gaseous component is characterized by a remarkable zero-velocity plateau.

The central velocity gradient along the major axis is zero (NGC 949, NGC 2541, NGC 6810) or at least less steep (NGC 2460) than that we measured for radii larger than 3'' . Further out the gas velocity increases to reach a maximum and then remains constant out to the last observed radius. Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis of all the sample galaxies, in spite of which would be expected if the gas traced the circular velocity in the disk plane.
We attribute the minor-axis velocity gradients to the presence of kinematically-decoupled component which is not rotating in the plane of the stellar disk.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2460's outer profile as II-CT and describes bar evidence, spiral structure, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 2460 (II-CT; SA(s)a) : This galaxy has a tightly wound multi-armed spiral structure starting deep inside the galaxy. Even in what should be the genuine bulge there is a lot of structure. In the outer zone the two arms join to form a single arm, yielding an unusual appearance. This seems to be associated with the surface brightness truncation. There are no signs of a bar or a ring.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2460 among four nearby intermediate-inclination spiral galaxies observed with major- and minor-axis long-slit spectroscopy for stellar disk kinematic modeling.

quotes.quote: We observed four galaxies: NGC 1068, NGC 2460, NGC 2775, and NGC 4030. All four are nearby, spiral galaxies of intermediate inclination ranging in Hubble type from Sa to Sbc. The main characteristics of each galaxy are listed in Table t:sample . Long-slit spectra were obtained along the photometric major and minor axes of each galaxy, in multiple exposures of 30 minutes each.

gmw_00007704.name: NGC 3455
gmw_00007704.overview: NGC 3455 is a spiral galaxy classified as (R')SAB(rs)b [arXiv:1108.3662] that forms an interacting pair with NGC 3454 [arXiv:0912.4266]. This tidal interaction has distorted the outer eastern arms of NGC 3455 [arXiv:0912.4266] and triggered recent star formation, which is indicated by blue color profiles and extended ultraviolet emission [arXiv:0912.4266].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3455 as paired with NGC 3454, having tidally distorted outer eastern arms, extended UV emission, and blue color profiles suggesting recent star formation triggered by interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 3454 and NGC 3455 are close both in projection and in redshift space (Table 1). The first galaxy displays a clean edge-on spiral morphology while the outer eastern arms of NGC 3455 appear tidally distorted. Summarizing, although cases of morphological distortion/asymmetries and multiple arms are present in all groups, only in LGG 225 there is a direct evidence of tidal distortion, as in the cases of pairs NGC 3447/NGC 3447A and NGC 3454/NGC 3455. Furthermore the UV emission in NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, in NGC 3455 and UGC 6035 extends much farther out than the respective optical images (Figure 225 ).

Blue color profiles of the interacting pair NGC 3454/NGC 3455, and NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, suggest that recent star formation events could be triggered by tidal interaction. Some LGG 225 members display unambiguously interacting (NGC 3447/NGC 3447A, NGC 3454/NGC 3455) and distorted morphologies (UGC 6035) particularly evident in the UV imaging. There is evidence that such episodes are triggered by on-going interaction as in the case of NGC 3447A or NGC 3454/NGC 3455.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3455's outer profile as II-CT + III-d and describes bar evidence, spiral structure, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3455 (II-CT + III-d; (R')SAB(rs)b) : Although this galaxy is classified as SAB, we were unable to find any convincing evidence for a bar. The ellipticity of the isophotes stays roughly constant out to at least 100 (7.5 kpc), well beyond the antitruncation break ( R_ brk = 40 ); in addition, two spiral arms extend out to 70 (5.4 kpc). Thus, we are confident that the outer profile is III-d. NCG 3599 (I; SA0 ^0 ) : This galaxy, almost face-on, has a weak ring between 45 (4.3 kpc) and 71 (6.8 kpc) and a small bar 11 (1.05 kpc) long (see Figure fig:n3599 ). No break in the profile is seen out to at least 6 scalelengths.

gmw_00007705.name: NGC 3619
gmw_00007705.overview: NGC 3619 is a peculiar lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.07261] belonging to a group of at least five systems [arXiv:0908.3564]. It features a system of outer stellar shells [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:1907.07261], a UV-bright inner ring [arXiv:1907.07261], and asymmetric dust lanes [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:1907.07261] that suggest a recent minor merger or accretion event [arXiv:0908.3564].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3619 as an S0 group galaxy whose concentrated gas, central star formation, dust lanes, and stellar shells point to a recent minor merger or accretion event.

quotes.quote: NGC3619 is a S0 galaxy, member of a group composed of at least five systems garcia03 . The distribution is asymmetric and much concentrated in the center vandriel89 , where star formation is taking place and dust lanes are clearly visible. The extent of the roughly matches the UV star-forming disk, i.e., half the optical radius. In optical, the galaxy shows prominent outer stellar shells, suggesting a recent minor-merging/accretion event. Numerical simulations predict that the stars from a satellite make a system of shells several 10 ^ 8 yr after the end of the merging event which can last for more than 1 Gyr kojima97 .
Thus, all the observational evidence is consistent with an external origin for the atomic hydrogen and residual star formation observed in NGC3619.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3619 as a peculiar gas-rich lenticular with shells, compact neutral hydrogen, asymmetric dust and emission, a UV-bright ring, and differently oriented stellar and gaseous disks.

quotes.quote: NGC 3619. It is another peculiar lenticular galaxy, settling in a rich group and demonstrating a system of outer shells ripples - possible results of close interaction or even merging. It is rather gas-rich, and the distribution of the neutral hydrogen is unusually compact for S0s n3619hi . In the optical bands, spiral-like dust lanes are projected against the stellar body to the south from the nucleus implying a circumnuclear gaseous disk settled asymmetrically with respect to the stellar one. A UV-bright inner ring has been detected in the GALEX survey see also our Fig. galex cortese09 .

The distribution of the emission is also very asymmetric and extended (Fig. fig_fpi ); however the ionized gas demonstrates regular rotation with a possible small kinematical major-axis turn in the very center. The rotation velocities of the gas and star components reveal very different projections over the full extension of our measurements (Fig. fig_ls ), so we may suppose different spatial orientations of the stellar and gaseous disks in NGC 3619.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3619's outer profile as III and describes profile breaks, shells shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3619 (III; (R)SA(s)0 ^+ ) : This is probably a nearly face-on shell galaxy. Four shells, which show up as arcs, can be seen, two at 129 (14.9 kpc) and two weaker arcs at 185 (21.3 kpc) and 200 (23.1 kpc). One of the two inner arcs might be the extension of an arm. This structure makes it difficult to determine the galaxy's orientation, though the inner isophotes are very round and the galaxy is probably close to face-on. The surface brightness profile is clearly of Type III, with a gradual change of gradient around the break; the outer slope is the region where the shells/arcs show up.

gmw_00007706.name: NGC 3675
gmw_00007706.overview: NGC 3675 is an SAb spiral galaxy featuring a very bright nucleus and a spiral disk [arXiv:1605.01638, arXiv:1108.3662]. Its main neutral hydrogen disk contains two dense clumps north and south of the nucleus, surrounded by a larger, patchy diffuse outer gas ring [arXiv:1605.01638].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3675 as an SAb galaxy with a large HI disc, patchy diffuse outer emission, dense central clumps, and molecular gas dominating the inner radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 3675 Archival VLA observations of this SAb galaxy shown in Fig.  fig:n3675sum reveal an HI disc 10 arcmin in diameter measured at 10 ^ 20 atoms cm ^ 2 with patchy diffuse emission that extends out to a diameter of 21.5 arcmin. There appears to be a gap between the main HI disc and this outer diffuse neutral gas ring. There is no obvious extended diffuse stellar light at 3.6 m to match the extended component of the HI disc. The main HI disc features two dense clumps of HI emission directly north and south of the galaxy's nucleus. NGC 3675 was observed by with the WSRT, but the observations were low signal-to-noise and did not detect the diffuse HI ring.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3675's outer profile as III-s and describes bar evidence, spiral structure, dust features shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3675 (III-s; SA(s)b) : Although eskridge00 say that this galaxy shows a strong bar in the IR, mollenhoff01 find no indication of a bar in their JHK observations, and we find no signs of a bar in either the SDSS images or any of the publically available near-IR images. A very bright nucleus is surrounded by a spiral disk, with a dust structure which shows up more strongly in the East, suggesting a possible warp. The profile is Type III with a break at 154 (9.6 kpc); since the ellipticity decreases steadily out to the limits of our free-ellipse fitting (ellipticity = 0.3 at 270 ), we classify this as III-s.

gmw_00007707.name: NGC 3813
gmw_00007707.overview: NGC 3813 is a small, irregularly formed spiral galaxy classified as SA(rs)b [arXiv:1108.3662]. It features a bright nucleus, bright knots within its spiral structure, and a complex brightness profile that transitions from a sharp downward break to a shallower outer slope [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3813's outer profile as II-CT+III-s and describes spiral structure, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3813 (II-CT+III-s; SA(rs)b) : This is a small spiral, of irregular form with a bright nucleus. In the spiral structure there are bright knots. The brightness profile is quite noisy beyond 160 (17 kpc). There is a sharp downward break in the profile slope at 43 (4.6 kpc), making for a Type II profile. However, beyond 70 (7.5 kpc), still used for the fit, the profile becomes shallower, accompanied by much rounder isophotes (the ellipticity drops from > 0.6 to 0.25 between 50 and 65 ), so the outer profile is pretty clearly III-s.

gmw_00007708.name: NGC 3898
gmw_00007708.overview: NGC 3898 is an early-type spiral galaxy in the Ursa Major group [arXiv:0908.3564] that features a classical bulge [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:0705.0973] and a multi-armed spiral structure [arXiv:1108.3662]. Active star formation is primarily concentrated in a ring within its optical radius [arXiv:0908.3564], while its extended atomic hydrogen disk displays a warped inner region [arXiv:0908.3564].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC3898 as a prototype Sa galaxy with a classical bulge, UV ring star formation, an extended low-density H I disk, and possible interaction or accretion signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC3898 is a Sa galaxy in the Ursa major group. In the Hubble Atlas it is described as the prototype of Sa galaxy: i.e., with a prominent bulge component and multiple spiral arms. HST observations reveal that the bulge is a `classical bulge' drory07 , consistent with a major merger/accretion event in the past. UV observations show that active star formation is mainly segregated in a ring well within the optical radius, although star-forming regions are also found at the edge of the optical disk.

The atomic hydrogen is distributed in a low column density ( 1-2 M _ pc ^ 2 ) disk extending up to 4 times the radius containing 80 of the B-band light noordermeer05 . The highest column density is observed in correspondence with the UV star-forming regions, suggesting that only in these few regions the can condensate into molecular hydrogen and initiate the star formation cycle krumholz09 . The disk appears to be warped in the inner parts and shows some `wiggles' with an amplitude of 30-50 noordermeer07 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3898's outer profile as III-d and describes spiral structure, profile breaks shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3898 (III-d; SA(s)ab) : A bright nucleus sits within a bulge well defined out to 27 (2.5 kpc) on the SDSS image. From this point outwards we see a clear disk, with a multi-armed spiral structure out to some 90 (8 kpc). The surface brightness is very low beyond this radius, but there are clear signs of an arm going out to beyond 220 (20 kpc). This outer zone retains the ellipticity of the inner ( 0.4 ). So we consider it an extended part of the disk; thus, this is a Type III-d profile.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3898 as a classical-bulge example, noting its smooth regular stellar light profile, lack of pseudobulge evidence, and high-Sersic classical profile.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:bulges illustrates typical examples of what we identify as classical bulges (left panels) and a pseudobulges (right panels). Notice first, that the classical bulge (NGC 3898) has a smooth and regular stellar light profile as seen in the WFPC2 F547M image in the middle panel of Fig. fig:bulges . There is no reason evident in the image to think that this galaxy harbors a pseudobulge.
Also notice that the classical bulge has a () ^ 1/3.4 profile, while the pseudobulge is nearly exponential.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC06787 as an illustrative SPARC galaxy whose oscillatory rotation curve favors the HIDM gas-scaling model and is poorly fit by SIDM.
