quotes.quote: UGC06787: The rotation curve contains oscillations that the baryonic components cannot explain alone. As these oscillations come from the gas contribution, the model that best fits this galaxy is the HIDM gas-scaling model. The very peaked inner star contribution and the rotation curve oscillations make the SIDM fits particularly bad. The gas contribution is too small to explain the oscillations in a SIDM context.

gmw_00007709.name: NGC 4223
gmw_00007709.overview: NGC 4223 is classified as an SA(s)0^+ galaxy with a bulge encircled by a very tight spiral pseudo-ring [arXiv:1108.3662]. Further out, the galaxy features two extended arms in an elongated S shape that causes its outer isophotes to become more elliptical [arXiv:1108.3662].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4223's outer profile as III-s(?) and describes spiral structure, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4223 (III-s(?); SA(s)0 ^+ ) : The bulge is encircled by a very tight spiral forming a pseudo-ring between 35 and 46 (2.8 and 3.7 kpc) in radius. Further out, at 80 (6.5 kpc), we see two extended arms in an elongated S shape, which cause the isophotes to become more elliptical. The isophotes appear to become rounder for > 100 (8 kpc) (though we cannot measure isophote shapes at 190 ), which leads us to tentatively classify this profile as III-s.

gmw_00007710.name: NGC 4281
gmw_00007710.overview: NGC 4281 is a fast-rotating early-type galaxy [arXiv:1902.10175] classified as an S0^+ lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1108.3662]. It features an inclined disk embedded in a rounder spheroid [arXiv:1108.3662], a small dust ring [arXiv:1108.3662], and a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1902.10175].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4281 as one of six nearby fast-rotating early-type galaxies with new SINFONI-based dynamical modeling and reports a Schwarzschild black-hole mass of (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_.

quotes.quote: We add six new black hole mass (M _ BH ) measurements of nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies to the known black hole mass sample, namely NGC 584, NGC 2784, NGC 3640, NGC 4570, NGC 4281 and NGC 7049. Our target galaxies have effective velocity dispersions ( _ e ) between 170 and 245 km s ^ 1 , and thus this work provides additional insight into the black hole properties of intermediate-mass early-type galaxies. We combine high-resolution adaptive-optics SINFONI data with large-scale MUSE, VIMOS and SAURON data from ATLAS ^ 3D to derive two-dimensional stellar kinematics maps. We then build both Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild models to measure the central black hole masses. Our Schwarzschild models provide black hole masses of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_ for NGC 584, (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_ for NGC 2784, (7.7 5) 10^7 M_ for NGC 3640, (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 4281, (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_ for NGC 4570 and (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 7049 at 3 confidence level, which are consistent with recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4281 as a disk-galaxy bulge in its NIR spheroid sample, excluding it from some fits because it stands out in spectral-index relations.

quotes.quote: Six of our galaxies are giant ellipticals (NGC 4472, NGC 4621, NGC 4649, NGC 4697, NGC 6909, IC 4296), five are intermediate-size ellipticals (NGC 4478, NGC 4564, NGC 4742, NGC 5077, NGC 5576), one is a bright dwarf elliptical (NGC 3641), and two are bulges of disk galaxies (NGC 4281, NGC 4594; our spectra are dominated by their spheroids).

The correlations between the NIR indices and velocity dispersion for the sample galaxies are plotted in Fig fig:VelDips ; the fit do not consider NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) because of the reasons explained further on. Three galaxies -- NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) -- are above the relation of S08 for the cluster stars, which hints at different chemical enrichment history with respect to the rest of the sample. Figure fig:IR_FeH shows the EW of the NIR spectral indices versus the Mg _2 measurements from ben93 . Na I , and to a lesser extent Ca I and CO, do show trends with respect to Mg _2 , with NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) standing out.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 4281's outer profile as III-s and describes dust features, rings shaping its measured surface-brightness structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4281 (III-s; S0 ^+ ) : The ellipticity of this galaxy rises to a relatively constant plateau value (the latter covering the range 22 - 55 ), and then falls smoothly at larger radii. This suggests an inclined disk embedded in a rounder, luminous spheroid, making this a clear if somewhat extreme example of a Type III-s profile. There is a small dust ring with radius 9 (1 kpc), which produces a dip in the ellipticity profile.

gmw_00007711.name: J1209 2032
gmw_00007711.overview: J1209 2032 is a red elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2009.12592] hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2009.12592] with an old stellar population and no signs of variability [arXiv:2009.12592]. It is a compact radio source with an inverted spectrum [arXiv:2009.12592] and shows no signs of interaction with nearby galaxies [arXiv:2009.12592].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1209 2032 as an inverted-spectrum compact radio source and red elliptical AGN with strong associated H I absorption supporting free-free absorption in its steeply inverted radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: The strong associated HI absorption detected against the source J1209 2032 ( z =0.4040) implies an optical depth of 0.178 0.02 corresponding to an HI column density of 34.8 2.9 10 ^ 20 cm ^ 2 , for an assumed HI spin temperature of 100 K and covering factor of 1. This is among the highest known optical depth and HI column densities found for compact radio sources of GPC/CSS type and supports the free-free absorption model for the steeply inverted radio spectrum of this source.

The sources J1209 2032 and J1549 + 5038 are inverted spectrum source with spectra peaking near the rest-frame frequencies of 430 MHz and 1407 MHz, respectively. The spectral index (150-325 MHz) below the turnover frequency is > +2.64 for J1209 2032 and +2.42 0.22 for J1549 + 5038 Mhaskey2019a,Mhaskey2019b , whereas the theoretical SSA limit for a homogeneous synchrotron radio source is + 2.5 . The AT20G catalogue reports fractional polarisation of 1 at 20 GHz, 8 GHz and 5 GHz, for J1209 2032 Murphy2010 .

J1209 2032 (z=0.404 0.002) is classified as a red elliptical galaxy with no sign of interaction with the nearby galaxies Jackson2002 . Its low-resolution spectrum obtained with the ESO 3.6m ESO reveals an early-type galaxy with old stellar population and weak emission lines [ OII, OIII ] , indicating an AGN Hook2003 . There is no sign of variability seen in the two SDSS spectra taken 11 years apart.

gmw_00007712.name: J1549 + 5038
gmw_00007712.overview: J1549 + 5038 is an inverted-spectrum compact radio source [arXiv:2009.12592] that exhibits strong flux variability at centimeter wavelengths [arXiv:2009.12592, arXiv:1807.05441]. Very Long Baseline Array imaging resolves the source into a dominant flat-spectrum core and a curved, knotty jet extending to the southwest [arXiv:2009.12592, arXiv:1807.05441].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1549 + 5038 as an inverted-spectrum compact radio source with a high rotation measure, strong centimeter variability, and a VLBA-resolved core plus curved jet structure.

quotes.quote: The sources J1209 2032 and J1549 + 5038 are inverted spectrum source with spectra peaking near the rest-frame frequencies of 430 MHz and 1407 MHz, respectively. The spectral index (150-325 MHz) below the turnover frequency is > +2.64 for J1209 2032 and +2.42 0.22 for J1549 + 5038 Mhaskey2019a,Mhaskey2019b , whereas the theoretical SSA limit for a homogeneous synchrotron radio source is + 2.5 . The AT20G catalogue reports fractional polarisation of 1 at 20 GHz, 8 GHz and 5 GHz, for J1209 2032 Murphy2010 . For the source J1549 + 5038 Pasetto2016 have inferred a large (rest-frame) rotation measure (RM = 1400 500 rad m ^ 2 ). The available radio continuum data for J1549 + 5038 confirms a strong flux variability at centimetre wavelengths, as cited in Mhaskey2019b .

In the VLBA images of the source J1549 + 5038 at 5 GHz Xu1995,Helmboldt2007 and at 2 and 8 GHz Fey2000 , the source appears resolved into a dominant flat-spectrum core and a fairly bright curved jet which is 20 mas (180 pc) long and extends towards the south-west. The jet is itself resolved into a couple of knots. The core contributes nearly 67 of the total flux at 5 GHz as measured with the single dish Green Bank telescope 87GB, Gregory1991 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1549+5038 as a resolved core-jet radio source with high-frequency opacity, variability, and a high rotation measure indicating dense magneto-ionic plasma.

quotes.quote: J1549+5038 The VLBA images at 5 GHz Xu1995,Helmboldt2007 and at 2 8 GHz Fey2000 have resolved this source into a dominant flat-spectrum core and a fairly bright curved jet 10 mas long and extending to the south-west. The jet is resolved into 2-3 knots. The source is also detected at 15 GHz (810 03 mJy, Richards2011 ) and at 30 GHz (738 39 mJy, Lowe2007 ) which is the highest frequency up to the spectrum of any source in our sample is seen to remain opaque (Figs fig:spec_all_detected fig:spec_all_undetected ). This is also in accord with the observed strong variability at 15 GHz (modulation index 9 , see Richards2014 ). Pasetto2016 have inferred a high (rest-frame) rotation measure (RM = 1400 500 rad m^ 2 ) for this source, revealing the presence of dense magneto-ionic plasma.

gmw_00007713.name: BCM--A
gmw_00007713.overview: BCM--A, also known as 3C28, is a low-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034, arXiv:astro-ph/0603090] located at the center of the northern subcluster of Abell 115 [arXiv:0705.1444]. It features a relaxed double radio structure with 100-kiloparsec lobes [arXiv:1803.02357, arXiv:astro-ph/9907034] but lacks a detected radio or X-ray core [arXiv:astro-ph/9907034, arXiv:astro-ph/0603090].

sources.summary: 3C28 is used as a redshifted radio-galaxy model: an unusual FRII-like, 100-kpc-lobed AGN whose extended structure tests ngVLA imaging performance.

quotes.quote: The simulations shown in Figure  fig:3C28_sim explore prospects for studies of classical radio galaxies with properties similar to 3C28, an example of an AGN with prominent lobes with an extent on the order of 100 kpc that is located at the center of a galaxy cluster at z 0.2 . Radio galaxies like 3C28 are commonly classified according to the historical fanaroff+74 type I (FRI; ``edge darkened'') and II (FRII; ``edge brightened'') categories, with FRI sources typically associated with lower radio powers and inefficient MBH accretion compared to the FRII class. While the radio morphology of 3C28 is somewhat unusual for a radio galaxy, harwood+15 argue on the basis of their spatially-resolved spectral aging analysis that 3C28 is consistent with an FRII radio galaxy produced by an AGN that shut down 6-9 Myrs ago. The complex, extended morphology of 3C28 spans a wide range of spatial scales and required multi-configuration VLA observations. 3C28 thus serves as an excellent test case for simulating the performance of the ngVLA antenna configuration.

sources.summary: 3C 28 is described as a low-excitation radio galaxy in Abell 115, with relaxed double radio structure and significantly extended ROSAT emission near the cluster peak.

quotes.quote: 3C 28 This low-excitation radio galaxy lies in the double X-ray cluster Abell 115 (Feretti \ 1984; Worrall, Birkinshaw Cameron 1995). It has a relaxed double radio structure with no detected radio core, although thin radio filaments emerging from the host galaxy may suggest that it is not an inactive object (Leahy, Bridle Strom 1998). 3C 28 lies in the northern component of the X-ray cluster, with its lobes straddling (within the ROSAT pointing accuracy) the peak in the X-ray surface brightness. Radial profile fitting shows the source to be significantly extended (Table profile ). A point-like component is not significant on an F-test.

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCM--A (3C28) as the second brightest cluster galaxy at A115N, coincident with the northern X-ray peak and assigned to the high-velocity KMM3 structure.

quotes.quote: The northern, high velocity subcluster (A115N) is likely centred on the second brightest cluster galaxy (BCM--A, coincident with radio source 3C28) and the northern X--ray peak. The southern, low velocity subcluster (A115S) is likely centred on the first brightest cluster galaxy (BCM--B) and the southern X--ray peak.

The projected clustercentric distance vs. the rest-frame velocity of the 88 galaxies is shown in Fig. figvd where we also show the three brightest galaxies in our sample, each of them corresponding to a different galaxy density clumps as found by Beers et al. ( ), i.e. IDs 54, 21(=3C28) and 104 (hereafter BCM--B, BCM--A, and BCM--C) corresponding to B (A115S), A (A115N), and C clumps, respectively. The position of BCM--B is very close to that of the southern X--ray peak [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 58 81 , Dec.= +26 19 58 9 (J2000.0) as recovered from X--ray Chandra data, see Fig. figisofote ] . BCM--A=3C28 is coincident with the northern X--ray peak and is very close to the position of the X--ray centroid when masking the X--ray emission of the point source [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 53 2 , Dec.= +26 24 59 (J2000.0) by Govoni et al. ] . We also consider the bright galaxy ID 81 (hereafter BCM--D, see Sect. sec:2D ) located in the middle of the above three galaxies.

KMM1 group is sparse in the sky, but well distinct in velocity from the other two groups. Several of its galaxies were already detected by the weighted gap analysis as belonging to WGAP1 and WGAP2 (see Sect. sec:vel ). Moreover, KMM1 contains BCM--D. KMM2 and KMM3 groups are well distinct in the sky. KMM2 contains both BCM--B and BCM--C, while KMM3 contains BCM--A.

sources.summary: The paper reports that 3C 28 is a low-excitation source undetected in radio and X-rays, requiring upper limits derived from local cluster-contaminated counts.

quotes.quote: 3C 28 The core of this low-excitation source is undetected in both radio and X-ray. We derive X-ray upper limits from the local count density, which is high as the source lies in the centre of one component of the merging cluster Abell 115 (e.g. Gutierrez Krawczynski 2005). Limits on luminosities in absorbed and unabsorbed components were derived assuming photon indices of 1.7 and 2.0 respectively.

gmw_00007714.name: BCM--B
gmw_00007714.overview: BCM--B is identified as the first brightest cluster galaxy in its region [arXiv:0705.1444]. It is located near the southern X-ray peak of the A115S subcluster and is associated with the KMM2 structure [arXiv:0705.1444].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BCM--B as the first brightest cluster galaxy near the southern X-ray peak, corresponding to A115S and included in the KMM2 structure.

quotes.quote: The northern, high velocity subcluster (A115N) is likely centred on the second brightest cluster galaxy (BCM--A, coincident with radio source 3C28) and the northern X--ray peak. The southern, low velocity subcluster (A115S) is likely centred on the first brightest cluster galaxy (BCM--B) and the southern X--ray peak.

The projected clustercentric distance vs. the rest-frame velocity of the 88 galaxies is shown in Fig. figvd where we also show the three brightest galaxies in our sample, each of them corresponding to a different galaxy density clumps as found by Beers et al. ( ), i.e. IDs 54, 21(=3C28) and 104 (hereafter BCM--B, BCM--A, and BCM--C) corresponding to B (A115S), A (A115N), and C clumps, respectively. The position of BCM--B is very close to that of the southern X--ray peak [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 58 81 , Dec.= +26 19 58 9 (J2000.0) as recovered from X--ray Chandra data, see Fig. figisofote ] . BCM--A=3C28 is coincident with the northern X--ray peak and is very close to the position of the X--ray centroid when masking the X--ray emission of the point source [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 53 2 , Dec.= +26 24 59 (J2000.0) by Govoni et al. ] . We also consider the bright galaxy ID 81 (hereafter BCM--D, see Sect. sec:2D ) located in the middle of the above three galaxies.

KMM1 group is sparse in the sky, but well distinct in velocity from the other two groups. Several of its galaxies were already detected by the weighted gap analysis as belonging to WGAP1 and WGAP2 (see Sect. sec:vel ). Moreover, KMM1 contains BCM--D. KMM2 and KMM3 groups are well distinct in the sky. KMM2 contains both BCM--B and BCM--C, while KMM3 contains BCM--A.

gmw_00007715.name: BCM--C
gmw_00007715.overview: BCM--C is a bright cluster member galaxy associated with the C clump of the Abell 115 cluster [arXiv:0705.1444]. It is located within the KMM2 group, and a galaxy substructure has been detected around it [arXiv:0705.1444].

sources.summary: The paper treats BCM--C as a bright cluster member tied to the C clump, included in KMM2, and associated with a detected substructure.

quotes.quote: The projected clustercentric distance vs. the rest-frame velocity of the 88 galaxies is shown in Fig. figvd where we also show the three brightest galaxies in our sample, each of them corresponding to a different galaxy density clumps as found by Beers et al. ( ), i.e. IDs 54, 21(=3C28) and 104 (hereafter BCM--B, BCM--A, and BCM--C) corresponding to B (A115S), A (A115N), and C clumps, respectively. The position of BCM--B is very close to that of the southern X--ray peak [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 58 81 , Dec.= +26 19 58 9 (J2000.0) as recovered from X--ray Chandra data, see Fig. figisofote ] . BCM--A=3C28 is coincident with the northern X--ray peak and is very close to the position of the X--ray centroid when masking the X--ray emission of the point source [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 53 2 , Dec.= +26 24 59 (J2000.0) by Govoni et al. ] . We also consider the bright galaxy ID 81 (hereafter BCM--D, see Sect. sec:2D ) located in the middle of the above three galaxies.

KMM1 group is sparse in the sky, but well distinct in velocity from the other two groups. Several of its galaxies were already detected by the weighted gap analysis as belonging to WGAP1 and WGAP2 (see Sect. sec:vel ). Moreover, KMM1 contains BCM--D. KMM2 and KMM3 groups are well distinct in the sky. KMM2 contains both BCM--B and BCM--C, while KMM3 contains BCM--A.

On the basis of the above section we conclude that A115 is formed by two subclusters well distinct in the sky and centred around BCM--A and --B, hereafter A115N and A115S, with the addition of several low velocity galaxies, above all in the eastern cluster region, likely organised in small groups (see Fig. figgrad and the PA of the velocity gradient). As for the low velocity groups we detect two 2D galaxy concentrations around the two bright, low velocity galaxies BCM--C and --D. Moreover, we find two possible groups in the low velocity tail of the velocity distribution (WGAP1 containing BCM--D and WGAP2). The Dressler--Schectman test detects a substructure around the BCM--C, too. The presence of a group around BCM--D finds some support in the faint X--ray emission shown there (see Gutierrez Krawczynski and Fig. figisofote ).

gmw_00007716.name: BCM--D
gmw_00007716.overview: BCM--D is a bright, low-velocity galaxy located between three main galaxy density clumps [arXiv:0705.1444]. It is contained within the WGAP1 and KMM1 groups, and its surrounding group structure is supported by faint X-ray emission [arXiv:0705.1444].

sources.summary: The paper describes BCM--D as a bright, low-velocity galaxy between the main clumps, contained in WGAP1/KMM1 and supported by faint X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The projected clustercentric distance vs. the rest-frame velocity of the 88 galaxies is shown in Fig. figvd where we also show the three brightest galaxies in our sample, each of them corresponding to a different galaxy density clumps as found by Beers et al. ( ), i.e. IDs 54, 21(=3C28) and 104 (hereafter BCM--B, BCM--A, and BCM--C) corresponding to B (A115S), A (A115N), and C clumps, respectively. The position of BCM--B is very close to that of the southern X--ray peak [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 58 81 , Dec.= +26 19 58 9 (J2000.0) as recovered from X--ray Chandra data, see Fig. figisofote ] . BCM--A=3C28 is coincident with the northern X--ray peak and is very close to the position of the X--ray centroid when masking the X--ray emission of the point source [ R.A.= 00^ h 55^ m 53 2 , Dec.= +26 24 59 (J2000.0) by Govoni et al. ] . We also consider the bright galaxy ID 81 (hereafter BCM--D, see Sect. sec:2D ) located in the middle of the above three galaxies.

KMM1 group is sparse in the sky, but well distinct in velocity from the other two groups. Several of its galaxies were already detected by the weighted gap analysis as belonging to WGAP1 and WGAP2 (see Sect. sec:vel ). Moreover, KMM1 contains BCM--D. KMM2 and KMM3 groups are well distinct in the sky. KMM2 contains both BCM--B and BCM--C, while KMM3 contains BCM--A.

On the basis of the above section we conclude that A115 is formed by two subclusters well distinct in the sky and centred around BCM--A and --B, hereafter A115N and A115S, with the addition of several low velocity galaxies, above all in the eastern cluster region, likely organised in small groups (see Fig. figgrad and the PA of the velocity gradient). As for the low velocity groups we detect two 2D galaxy concentrations around the two bright, low velocity galaxies BCM--C and --D. Moreover, we find two possible groups in the low velocity tail of the velocity distribution (WGAP1 containing BCM--D and WGAP2). The Dressler--Schectman test detects a substructure around the BCM--C, too. The presence of a group around BCM--D finds some support in the faint X--ray emission shown there (see Gutierrez Krawczynski and Fig. figisofote ).

gmw_00007717.name: ID 3324
gmw_00007717.overview: ID 3324 is a galaxy that exhibits a spatial profile with two distinct peaks separated by 1.5 arcseconds [arXiv:2204.06937]. Spectra extracted from these peak locations show comparable fluxes and velocity centroids, though the second peak may be spurious [arXiv:2204.06937].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID 3324 as the only detected source with a two-peak spatial profile; separate spectra are similar, but the second peak may be spurious.

quotes.quote: ID 3324 presents an unsual spatial profile with two peaks separated by 1 beam width (1.5 ). Spectra extracted separately at each peak location yield 1D lines with roughly comparable fluxes and velocity centroids that are consistent with one another and the rest-optical redshift at less than the 1 level. Given the relatively small area probed by our observations, the probability of a S/N > 5 noise fluctuation located spatially and spectrally close to our targets is small. However, we cannot rule out the possibility that the second peak of ID 3324 is in fact spurious.

We proceed by extracting the total line flux for ID 3324 using a spatial profile of two point sources aligned with the two peak locations, but note that our results in Section sec:results do not significantly change if we exclude ID 3324 entirely or only include the \ flux from the peak that is more spatially coincident with the HST imaging centroid.

gmw_00007718.name: 4FGL 2022.7+4216
gmw_00007718.overview: 4FGL 2022.7+4216 is a BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z = 0.069 [arXiv:2303.03619]. The source exhibits quasi-periodic gamma-ray fluctuations with a period of 100 days, which may be driven by jet precession or helical plasma motion [arXiv:2303.03619].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Fermi-LAT gamma-ray variability of the BL Lac object 4FGL 2022.7+4216, reporting a first 100-day QPO and possible jet-precession or helical-blob origins.

quotes.quote: In this study, we carry out a comprehensive variability analysis of the BL Lac object 4FGL 2022.7+4216 detected by the Fermi- LAT, over a period of more than three years, from April 27, 2019 to August 09, 2022. By utilizing multiple widely-used methods of time-series analyses, we detect the presence of quasi-periodic fluctuations with a period of 100 days with a confidence level exceeding 4 . This is the first time such a variability feature pertaining to this source is being reported. We propose that the observed QPO may be related to the precession of the blazar jet with a high Lorentz factor or to the motion of a plasma blob through the helical structure of the jet.

The BL Lacerate object 4FGL J2202.7+4216 at redshift z = 0.069 was detected in a high flux state on May 1, 2019, with a daily averaged flux reaching (1.5 0.2) 10^ 6 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 by Fermi- LAT 2019ATelGarrappa . Subsequently, on August 19, 2020, Fermi- LAT has captured the source with daily averaged flux (2.8 0.2) 10^ 6 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 2020ATelOjha . The ray flaring activity has coincided with the optical brightening where R magnitude < 12 has been registered by several optical monitoring campaigns 2020ATelOpticalGrishina,2020ATelATOM,2020ATelSteineke .

gmw_00007719.name: NGC 4984
gmw_00007719.overview: NGC 4984 is an early-type galaxy classified as either an Sa spiral or a weakly-barred S0/a galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382]. It features a complex inner structure with a double bar in its central region [arXiv:astro-ph/0504646, arXiv:astro-ph/0306382] and an outer warped gaseous disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382].

sources.summary: The paper reaches no firm bar-age conclusion for NGC 4984, citing one-sided low dispersion, missing minor-axis spectra, and complex inner structure.

quotes.quote: Finally, the case of NGC 4984 is the only one in that we could not arrive to a definite conclusion. The dispersion reach relatively low values but only in one side of the bar. The lack of minor axis spectra also prevents us to reach a conclusion. Note also that the inner structure of this galaxy is complex (see Fig. 1), and that jun97 suggest that this galaxy has a secondary bar possibly almost aligned with the primary. This complexity certainly is reflected in our results.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4984 among low-inclination spirals whose minor-axis gas velocities indicate an inner gaseous warp, and describes it as an early-type system with double-bar structure plus an outer warped disk.

quotes.quote: (i) NGC 4984, NGC 7213, and NGC 7377 show an overall velocity curve along the minor axis without zero-velocity points, out to the last measured radius, which is interpreted as due to the warped structure of the gaseous disk.

Warped gaseous disks An inner warp of the gaseous disk can account for the gas velocity curve observed along the minor axes in the low-inclined galaxies NGC 4984, NGC 7213, and NGC 7377. NGC 4984 is classified as a very early-type Sa spiral in CAG and as a weakly-barred S0/a in RC3.
Therefore NGC 4984 has a peculiar structure with a double bar in the central 30'' and a warped disk at larger radii.

gmw_00007720.name: NGC 7377
gmw_00007720.overview: NGC 7377 is an S0 galaxy with a normal disk that shows no evidence of recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382]. Its disk is threaded by a multi-armed spiral pattern composed entirely of dust lanes, and its outer regions exhibit a reversal of gas rotation [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382].

sources.summary: The paper groups NGC 7377 with warped gaseous disks, noting outer gas-rotation reversal, unusual morphology, and kinematics similar to NGC 7213 that imply non-coplanar gas and stellar disks.

quotes.quote: Major-axis kinematics The only noteworthy features observed in the major-axis kinematics of the ionized-gas and stellar component are the counterrotation of gas with respect to stars found in NGC 7213, and the reversal of gas rotation measured in the outermost regions of NGC 7377.

According to Sandage Bedke (1994) NGC 7377 is one of the most unusual galaxies in their atlas. They state that there is no evidence of recent star formation over an otherwise normal S0 disk, yet the entire disk is threaded by a multi-armed spiral pattern composed of dust lanes only. These lanes are more visible to the south-west (Panel 75, CAG). The morphology and the kinematics of NGC 7377 (Fig. fig:kinematics ) are similar to those of NGC 7213. In particular the major and minor-axis gas kinematics of NGC 7377 behave very similarly to the minor and major-axis gas kinematics of NGC 7213, respectively.

We interpret the counterrotation of gas and stars observed along the galaxy major axis and the gas velocity decrease along the minor axis (which is steeper than Keplerian) as due to a geometrical decoupling between the gaseous and stellar disks, which are not coplanar.

gmw_00007721.name: NGC 3885
gmw_00007721.overview: NGC 3885 is a galaxy featuring dust lanes that thread through its smooth disk and cross near the center of its bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382]. The galaxy exhibits non-circular gas motions in its nuclear region, which are attributed to a triaxial bulge potential or the presence of a bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382].

sources.summary: The paper explains NGC 3885's central gas velocity gradients as non-circular motion from a triaxial bulge or bar, supported by bulge-disk misalignment and isophotal twist.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586 the gas velocity rises almost linearly in the inner regions of both the major (where gas rotates faster than stars) and minor axes. Non-zero gas velocities along the minor axis are confined in the nuclear regions. Such gas kinematics have been explained as being due to non-circular motion induced by a triaxial potential.

Non-circular gas motions in a triaxial bulge or in a bar Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis in the bulge-dominated region of NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586, and a velocity gradient is observed along their major axis too. We interpret the observed kinematics as due to non-circular gas motion in the principal plane of the disk caused by bulge triaxiality or due to the action of a bar.

The triaxiality of the bulge of NGC 3885 is suggested by the significant Lindblad's misalignment (Lindblad 1956) between the apparent major axes of bulge and disk ( P.A. 10^ ). The bulge-to-disk misalignment measured on the photographic plates of NGC 3885 available in CAG (Panel 76 and 87) is consistent with the isophotal twist found in the near infrared by Jungwiert et al. (1997) in spite of the presence of the dust lanes threading through the smooth disk and crossing the bulge close to its center. Therefore the twist between the isophotes of bulge and disk of NGC 3885 cannot be attributed to the bulge obscuration by dust.

gmw_00007722.name: NGC 4224
gmw_00007722.overview: NGC 4224 features a boxy, peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382]. The galaxy exhibits non-zero gas velocities along its minor axis and a velocity gradient along its major axis, which are interpreted as non-circular gas motions driven by a bar or a triaxial bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382].

sources.summary: The paper interprets NGC 4224's central gas velocity gradients on both axes as non-circular disk-plane motion driven by a triaxial bulge or bar, and notes its boxy bulge.

quotes.quote: In NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586 the gas velocity rises almost linearly in the inner regions of both the major (where gas rotates faster than stars) and minor axes. Non-zero gas velocities along the minor axis are confined in the nuclear regions. Such gas kinematics have been explained as being due to non-circular motion induced by a triaxial potential.

Non-circular gas motions in a triaxial bulge or in a bar Non-zero gas velocities are measured along the minor axis in the bulge-dominated region of NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586, and a velocity gradient is observed along their major axis too. We interpret the observed kinematics as due to non-circular gas motion in the principal plane of the disk caused by bulge triaxiality or due to the action of a bar.
Both NGC 4224 and NGC 4586 have a boxy/peanut-shape bulge (Panel 76, CAG).

gmw_00007723.name: NGC 2855
gmw_00007723.overview: NGC 2855 features a kinematically-decoupled component of ionized gas that rotates orthogonally with respect to its stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382]. This inner polar disk is surrounded by a faint ring-like structure, which suggests an ongoing acquisition process [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2855 as an inner-polar-disk case, citing orthogonally rotating ionized gas, a shallow central major-axis gradient, minor-axis gas velocities, and a surrounding ring-like structure suggesting acquisition.

quotes.quote: (iii) NGC 2855 and NGC 7049 show a change of slope of the velocity gradient measured along the major axis (which is shallower in the center and steeper away from the nucleus), as well as non-zero gas velocities in the central regions of the minor axis. This has been attributed to the presence of a kinematically-decoupled gaseous component in orthogonal rotation with respect to the galaxy disk, namely an inner polar disk.

In a previous paper (Corsini, Pizzella Bertola 2002) we reported on the special case of NGC 2855, which is characterized by the presence of a kinematically-decoupled component of ionized gas in orthogonal rotation with respect to the stellar disk as well as of a faint ring-like structure surrounding the galaxy. These features are indicative of an on-going acquisition process.

In NGC 2855 and NGC 7049 the major-axis gas velocity gradient in the innermost 500 pc (where gas rotates slower than stars) is shallower than that measured farther out (where gas rotates faster than stars). Moreover the non-zero gas velocities observed along the minor axis are confined in the nuclear regions.

gmw_00007724.name: NGC 7049
gmw_00007724.overview: NGC 7049 is a massive, fast-rotating lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1808.05761] that contains a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1902.10175]. The galaxy hosts a dusty disk of cool gas [arXiv:1808.05761] and a decoupled gaseous component rotating orthogonally to the main disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0306382].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7049 as a massive fast-rotating lenticular with a dusty cool-gas disk, extended hot X-ray atmosphere, and rotation-shaped multiphase condensation.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the results of the analysis of XMM-Newton data for the massive, lenticular galaxy NGC 7049. The galaxy harbours a dusty disc of cool gas and is surrounded by an extended hot X-ray emitting gaseous atmosphere with unusually high central entropy. The hot gas in the plane of rotation of the cool dusty disc has a multi-temperature structure, consistent with ongoing cooling.

Our analysis of the XMM-Newton observation of the massive fast-rotating lenticular galaxy NGC 7049 has led to the following results. The X-ray spectral modelling properties are: emission-weighted temperature k_ B T = 0.43^ +0.02 _ 0.01 keV ; emission-weighted metallicity: Z = 0.7^ +0.2 _ 0.1 Z_ ; ellipticity _ X = (0.126 0.004) ; and central density n(0) = (0.031 0.001) cm^ 3 . The hot gas has an unusually high central entropy and a temperature peak. While the hot gas in the rotational plane of the cool dusty disc has a multi-temperature structure, the thermal structure along the rotation axis is single-phase.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7049 as an inner-polar-disk candidate whose ionized gas has decoupled orthogonal rotation; it rejects a barred-potential explanation and emphasizes its disk-galaxy nature.

quotes.quote: (iii) NGC 2855 and NGC 7049 show a change of slope of the velocity gradient measured along the major axis (which is shallower in the center and steeper away from the nucleus), as well as non-zero gas velocities in the central regions of the minor axis. This has been attributed to the presence of a kinematically-decoupled gaseous component in orthogonal rotation with respect to the galaxy disk, namely an inner polar disk.

The case of inner polar gaseous disks The gas velocity field of NGC 2855 and NGC 7049 differs from that of NGC 3885, NGC 4224, and NGC 4586 since the velocity gradient observed along the minor axis is associated with a change in the slope of the velocity gradient measured along the major axis (which is shallower in the center and steeper away from the nucleus). This is the kinematic signature of the presence of two decoupled gaseous components rotating around two roughly orthogonal axes.

The same is true for NGC 7049 according to the color map by Veron-Cetty Veron (1988). In spite of looking more like to an elliptical than any of the other sample galaxies, NGC 7049 is a disk galaxy as it results from the photometric decomposition of the band surface photometry by Bertola, Vietri Zeilinger (1990, 1991). table 2 ht NOTES. -- Col.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7049 as one of six nearby fast-rotating early-type galaxies with new SINFONI-based dynamical modeling and reports a Schwarzschild black-hole mass of (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_.

quotes.quote: We add six new black hole mass (M _ BH ) measurements of nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies to the known black hole mass sample, namely NGC 584, NGC 2784, NGC 3640, NGC 4570, NGC 4281 and NGC 7049. Our target galaxies have effective velocity dispersions ( _ e ) between 170 and 245 km s ^ 1 , and thus this work provides additional insight into the black hole properties of intermediate-mass early-type galaxies. We combine high-resolution adaptive-optics SINFONI data with large-scale MUSE, VIMOS and SAURON data from ATLAS ^ 3D to derive two-dimensional stellar kinematics maps. We then build both Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild models to measure the central black hole masses. Our Schwarzschild models provide black hole masses of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_ for NGC 584, (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_ for NGC 2784, (7.7 5) 10^7 M_ for NGC 3640, (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 4281, (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_ for NGC 4570 and (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 7049 at 3 confidence level, which are consistent with recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations.

gmw_00007725.name: J 1454
gmw_00007725.overview: J 1454 is an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.13 that is classified as a merger [arXiv:2107.11631]. It features extended [FeVII] emission reaching 23 kpc, though it is not considered a strong active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2107.11631].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 1454 as an elliptical merger with [FeVII] emission extending 23 kpc, the largest CLR in the sample, but not a strong AGN candidate by bolometric scaling.

quotes.quote: We measure [ FeVII ] emission in four MPL-8 galaxies: J 1454 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.13; elliptical; merger), J 1349 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.02; no GZ2 classification), J 0906 ( [ FeVII ] 3586 ; z = 0.11; spiral; merger), and J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ; z = 0.03; spiral).
Here, we measure [ FeVII ] 3760 flux in J 1454 out to 23.0 2.9 kpc (4.4 above the mean of the CLD distribution; the largest in our sample).

We measure R _ f values for J 1454 and J 0906 to be 8.1 and 1.7 above the mean of the R_ f distribution, respectively - the two highest in our sample. This suggests that J 1454 is not a strong AGN candidate, and that J 0906 requires further analysis to determine if it hosts an AGN.

gmw_00007726.name: J 0906
gmw_00007726.overview: J 0906 is a spiral galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.11 that is classified as a merger [arXiv:2107.11631]. The galaxy exhibits substantial [FeVII] 3586 emission extending out to 10.0 kpc from its center, and it requires further analysis to determine if it hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.11631].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 0906 as a spiral merger with [FeVII] emission to 10 kpc, no MaNGA AGN-catalog classification, and an ambiguous AGN-candidate status requiring further analysis.

quotes.quote: We measure [ FeVII ] emission in four MPL-8 galaxies: J 1454 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.13; elliptical; merger), J 1349 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.02; no GZ2 classification), J 0906 ( [ FeVII ] 3586 ; z = 0.11; spiral; merger), and J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ; z = 0.03; spiral).
For J 0906 , we identify substantial [ FeVII ] 3586 emission out to 10.0 2.5 kpc from the galactic center (1.9 above the mean of the CLD distribution; the second largest in our sample).

We measure R _ f values for J 1454 and J 0906 to be 8.1 and 1.7 above the mean of the R_ f distribution, respectively - the two highest in our sample. This suggests that J 1454 is not a strong AGN candidate, and that J 0906 requires further analysis to determine if it hosts an AGN.

gmw_00007727.name: J 1349
gmw_00007727.overview: J 1349 is a galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.02 with an unclassified morphology [arXiv:2107.11631]. It is considered an active galactic nucleus candidate and exhibits [FeVII] emission extending out to 7.0 kiloparsecs from its galactic center [arXiv:2107.11631].

sources.summary: The paper reports [FeVII] emission in J 1349 out to 7 kpc, notes its unclassified morphology, and classifies it as an AGN candidate from the bolometric-luminosity relation.

quotes.quote: We measure [ FeVII ] emission in four MPL-8 galaxies: J 1454 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.13; elliptical; merger), J 1349 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.02; no GZ2 classification), J 0906 ( [ FeVII ] 3586 ; z = 0.11; spiral; merger), and J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ; z = 0.03; spiral).
We measure [ FeVII ] 3760 emission in J 1349 out to 7.0 0.6 kpc from the galactic center.
On the other hand, J 1349 ( R_ f = 1.5 ) is within 1 of the mean of the distribution. We consider this galaxy to be an AGN candidate due to its proximity to the regression line.

gmw_00007728.name: J 1714
gmw_00007728.overview: J 1714 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2107.11631] classified as an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2107.11631]. It is a coronal-line galaxy that exhibits [NeV] emission extending up to approximately 4 kiloparsecs from its center, though it lacks continuous coronal-line emission within its central region [arXiv:2107.11631].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J 1714 as an elliptical [NeV] CL galaxy with emission to about 4 kpc, limited central continuity, and MaNGA AGN-catalog status through the [NeV] sample.

quotes.quote: The remaining four CL galaxies, J 0752 ( [ NeV ] 3427 ), J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ), J 1714 ( [ NeV ] 3347 ), and J 2051 ( [ NeV ] 3427 ) lack continuous CL emission within a 2. ^ 5 x 2. ^ 5 FoV.

We measure [ NeV ] 3427 emission in six galaxies from MPL-8: J 1614 , J 1714 , J 0736 , J 0752 , J 2051 , and J 2116 . In two of these galaxies, J 1614 and J 1714 , we also measure [ NeV ] 3347 emission. We present [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 flux maps for J 1614 ( z = 0.13; no GZ2 classification) and J 1714 ( z = 0.09; elliptical) in Figure nevmaps . For J 1614 , we identify extended [ NeV ] 3347 emission out to 5.9 2.9 kpc from the galactic center; 8.3 3.9 kpc for [ NeV ] 3427 . For J 1714 , we measure [ NeV ] 3347 emission out to 4.3 2.2 kpc from the galactic center; 3.7 2.2 kpc for [ NeV ] 3427 .

We cross-match these galaxies with the largest existing MaNGA AGN catalog (``MaNGA AGN catalog''; 2020arXiv200811210C ; AGN classifications determined using WISE mid-infrared color cuts, Swift /BAT hard X-ray observations, NVSS/ FIRST 1.4 GHz radio observations, and SDSS broad emission lines; Section tab:mangaagn ), and determine that all [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 galaxies are classified as AGNs.

gmw_00007729.name: J 0736
gmw_00007729.overview: J 0736 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z = 0.12 [arXiv:2107.11631] that is classified as a quasar [arXiv:1702.08609]. The galaxy exhibits [NeV] 3427 emission [arXiv:2107.11631] and contains an extended [O III] component with a second moment exceeding 2000 km/s [arXiv:1702.08609].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS 0432-51884-0246 to test whether [O iii] extended wings are cloud emission, citing its >2000 km/s component, shifted broad-H central wavelength, and preference for cloud-origin extended components.

quotes.quote: Fig. line2 shows the emission lines and the fitted results in the object SDSS 0432-51884-0246, which has the second moment of the extended component of the [ O iii ] 5007 \ larger than 2000 km/s . Then, based on the results shown in Fig. line2 , we can find that if the extended components were from the broad H clouds, there could be much different line profiles of the broad H and the broad H : the solid line in yellow, the dotted line in red and the dashed line in red shown in the bottom panel of Fig. line2 .

Through the determined broad line profile without the contributions of the extended component, the central wavelength of the broad H is about 4866 . However, with considerations of the extended components to the broad H , the central wavelength should be about 4887 . Meanwhile, the central wavelength of the broad H is about 6566 , leading to the expected central wavelength of the broad H of about 4865 . Here, the central wavelengths are calculated by the definition shown in the following Equation (1). Therefore, under the accepted criterion that there are similar line profiles of the broad Balmer lines, the extended components are preferred from the clouds in SDSS 0432-51884-0246.

sources.summary: The paper lists J 0736 as a [NeV] 3427-emitting CL galaxy at z=0.12 with CLD 5.4 kpc and AGN classification through the [NeV] sample.

quotes.quote: We measure [ NeV ] 3427 emission in six galaxies from MPL-8: J 1614 , J 1714 , J 0736 , J 0752 , J 2051 , and J 2116 .

We show the [ NeV ] 3427 flux maps for J 0736 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 5.4 2.7 kpc), J 0752 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 7.1 2.7), J 2051 ( z = 0.11; spiral; CLD = 2.5 2.5 kpc), and J 2116 ( z = 0.08; spiral; CLD = 4.3 1.9 kpc) in Figure fevmaps .

We cross-match these galaxies with the largest existing MaNGA AGN catalog (``MaNGA AGN catalog''; 2020arXiv200811210C ; AGN classifications determined using WISE mid-infrared color cuts, Swift /BAT hard X-ray observations, NVSS/ FIRST 1.4 GHz radio observations, and SDSS broad emission lines; Section tab:mangaagn ), and determine that all [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 galaxies are classified as AGNs.

gmw_00007730.name: J 2051
gmw_00007730.overview: J 2051 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.11 [arXiv:2107.11631]. It is classified as an active galactic nucleus host, exhibiting [NeV] 3427 coronal line emission that is concentrated within its central region [arXiv:2107.11631].

sources.summary: The paper lists J 2051 as a spiral [NeV] 3427-emitting CL galaxy at z=0.11 with CLD 2.5 kpc, limited central continuity, and AGN classification through the [NeV] sample.

quotes.quote: The remaining four CL galaxies, J 0752 ( [ NeV ] 3427 ), J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ), J 1714 ( [ NeV ] 3347 ), and J 2051 ( [ NeV ] 3427 ) lack continuous CL emission within a 2. ^ 5 x 2. ^ 5 FoV.
We measure [ NeV ] 3427 emission in six galaxies from MPL-8: J 1614 , J 1714 , J 0736 , J 0752 , J 2051 , and J 2116 .

We show the [ NeV ] 3427 flux maps for J 0736 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 5.4 2.7 kpc), J 0752 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 7.1 2.7), J 2051 ( z = 0.11; spiral; CLD = 2.5 2.5 kpc), and J 2116 ( z = 0.08; spiral; CLD = 4.3 1.9 kpc) in Figure fevmaps . In each galaxy, [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 emission is predominantly concentrated within the galactic interior (within 2.^ 5 throughout the paper), suggesting that a central source governs CL production in these galaxies. We cross-match these galaxies with the largest existing MaNGA AGN catalog (``MaNGA AGN catalog''; 2020arXiv200811210C ; AGN classifications determined using WISE mid-infrared color cuts, Swift /BAT hard X-ray observations, NVSS/ FIRST 1.4 GHz radio observations, and SDSS broad emission lines; Section tab:mangaagn ), and determine that all [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 galaxies are classified as AGNs.

gmw_00007731.name: J 2116
gmw_00007731.overview: J 2116 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.08 [arXiv:2107.11631]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus and exhibits [NeV] 3427 emission concentrated within its interior [arXiv:2107.11631].

sources.summary: The paper lists J 2116 as a spiral [NeV] 3427 CL galaxy at z=0.08 with CLD 4.3 kpc, AGN classification, and recovery in one BPT AGN catalog.

quotes.quote: We measure [ NeV ] 3427 emission in six galaxies from MPL-8: J 1614 , J 1714 , J 0736 , J 0752 , J 2051 , and J 2116 .

We show the [ NeV ] 3427 flux maps for J 0736 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 5.4 2.7 kpc), J 0752 ( z = 0.12; no GZ2 classification; CLD = 7.1 2.7), J 2051 ( z = 0.11; spiral; CLD = 2.5 2.5 kpc), and J 2116 ( z = 0.08; spiral; CLD = 4.3 1.9 kpc) in Figure fevmaps . In each galaxy, [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 emission is predominantly concentrated within the galactic interior (within 2.^ 5 throughout the paper), suggesting that a central source governs CL production in these galaxies. We cross-match these galaxies with the largest existing MaNGA AGN catalog (``MaNGA AGN catalog''; 2020arXiv200811210C ; AGN classifications determined using WISE mid-infrared color cuts, Swift /BAT hard X-ray observations, NVSS/ FIRST 1.4 GHz radio observations, and SDSS broad emission lines; Section tab:mangaagn ), and determine that all [ NeV ] 3347 , 3427 galaxies are classified as AGNs.
We find that 1/4 CL AGN, that are in MPL-5, are in this catalog (J 2116 ).

gmw_00007732.name: NGC 7320a
gmw_00007732.overview: NGC 7320a is a spiral galaxy located near Stephan's Quintet [arXiv:2306.11231]. It has a radial velocity of 6702 km/s and is a candidate source for a nearby diffuse H I feature, which may have been pulled out as a tidal tail [arXiv:2306.11231].

sources.summary: The paper reports FAST detection of NGC 7320a and explores whether it was an earlier intruder or the source of the nearby diffuse H I feature with a similar radial velocity.

quotes.quote: Anon 2 and Anon 4 were detected in previous observations Shostak1980, Williams2002 , and the FAST detections of NGC 7320a and Anon 7 were reported by Xu2022 .

Origin of the diffuse HI feature Given its spatial and kinematical connections to the debris field produced by interactions among core members of SQ and by collisions with numerous intruders, Xu2022 argued that the diffuse feature was most likely due to gas ejected from one of the core galaxies during an interaction with an early intruder (plausibly NGC 7320a or Anon 4) more than 1 Gyr ago.

First of all, it is possible that the diffuse feature is a tidal tail pull out from the neighboring spiral galaxy NGC 7320a instead of from a core member of SQ. The diffuse HI feature is right next to NGC 7320a, and its radial velocity ( v=6633 km s^ 1 ) is very close to that of NGC 7320a ( v=6702 km s^ 1 ).

gmw_00007733.name: Anon 4
gmw_00007733.overview: Anon 4 is a low-mass dwarf galaxy with a radial velocity of approximately 6057 km/s [arXiv:2306.11231]. It is a possible old intruder to the Stephan's Quintet group, though a direct physical connection to the group's 6000 km/s neutral hydrogen component remains unconfirmed [arXiv:2306.11231].

sources.summary: The paper treats Anon 4 as a detected neighboring low-mass galaxy and possible old intruder; its velocity matches the 6000 component, though P-V data do not confirm a direct connection.

quotes.quote: Among them, Anon 8 and Anon 9, named following the convention in the literature Shostak1984, Williams2002 , are new detections of this work. Anon 2 and Anon 4 were detected in previous observations Shostak1980, Williams2002 , and the FAST detections of NGC 7320a and Anon 7 were reported by Xu2022 . For Anon 2 and Anon 4, the HI velocities measured by FAST are consistent with those by VLA within 1- , and the values of M_ HI by FAST are 0.1 dex and 0.2 dex above the VLA measurements, respectively, after the adjustments for the different H_0 adopted ( Williams2002 assumed H_0 = 75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 whereas we assume H_0 = 70 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ).

A candidate for such an intruder could be Anon 4 ( v = 6057 km s ^ 1 ), which has a relative radial velocity of about 600 km s ^ 1 and a projected distance of approximately 0.2 Mpc from the SQ centre. If the relative transverse velocity is about 200 km s ^ 1 , it would have taken approximately 1 Gyr for Anon 4 to move to the current position after the collision. If this scenario is true then it is conceivable that the 6000 component of the HI emission in the debris field of SQ is due to stripped gas from Anon 4 when it passed through the group center.

The plot shows that the 6000 component (the emission around the position of x = 0 and velocity = 6000 km s ^ 1 ) has about the same velocity as Anon 4 (the emission around x = -2 arcmin), but there is no connection between the two emissions. This does not confirm any relationship between the 6000 component and Anon 4.

gmw_00007734.name: KAZ 295
gmw_00007734.overview: KAZ 295 is an early-type galaxy with a stellar mass of approximately 10^9.71 solar masses [arXiv:2306.11231]. It shares a similar redshift to NGC 7320a, and a past encounter between these two galaxies may have pulled a long, diffuse H I stream [arXiv:2306.11231].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KAZ 295 at the far end of the diffuse H I feature, confirms a similar redshift to NGC 7320a, and suggests their encounter may have pulled a long H I stream.

quotes.quote: Indeed there is an early type galaxy, KAZ 295, at the other end of the diffuse feature opposite to NGC 7320a. Our new optical spectroscopy finds a redshift velocity of 6555 km s ^ 1 for the galaxy (see Appendix B), confirming it to be at about the same distance of NGC 7320a. Its stellar mass estimated from the SDSS photometry is M_* = 10^ 9.71 M_ which is slightly lower than that of NGC 7320a ( M_* = 10^ 10.08 M_ ).

In analog to the simulation of Duc2008 , KAZ 295 may have a high transverse velocity relative to NGC 7320a, and the encounter between the two may have pulled a long HI stream from NGC 7320a that is identified by us as the diffuse feature. However there are some inconsistencies which cast doubts on the analogy. The case simulated by Duc2008 are for 2 massive galaxies of M_* > 10^ 11 M_ , and NGC 7320a and KAZ 295 are low mass galaxies of M_* 10^ 10 M_ . Also the simulated HI stream is only 200 kpc whereas the length of the diffuse feature is 500 kpc. Whether the putative interaction between the two low-mass galaxies can trigger the formation of an HI feature as long as 500 kpc needs to be investigated by future studies.

gmw_00007735.name: SDSS J154843.06+220812.6
gmw_00007735.overview: SDSS J154843.06+220812.6 is an S0 lenticular galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.031 [arXiv:2108.12431]. Pre-flare observations of the galaxy show weak or absent active galactic nucleus activity and no mid-infrared variability [arXiv:2108.12431].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J1548 as the S0 host of VT J1548 at z=0.031, with weak or absent pre-flare AGN signatures, no significant MIR variability, and a low-mass SMBH.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1548, the host of VT J1548, is an S0 galaxy at z=0.031 ( d_L = 137 Mpc). Its pre-flare line ratios are consistent with no or weak AGN activity (Figure fig:BPT ). Its pre-flare WISE color was inconsistent with typical AGN colors and it showed no significant MIR variability. It hosts a low mass SMBH with M_ BH /M_ = 6.48 0.33 .

gmw_00007736.name: SDSS J1657+2345
gmw_00007736.overview: SDSS J1657+2345 is an active galactic nucleus or tidal disruption event candidate that was discovered as a mid-infrared flare with no optical counterpart [arXiv:2108.12431]. The flare evolved over a timescale of more than 1000 days and exhibits broad hydrogen-alpha emission but lacks coronal line emission [arXiv:2108.12431].

sources.summary: The paper compares SDSS J1657+2345 to VT J1548 as a MIR-selected candidate TDE or AGN flare with no optical counterpart, slow evolution, Balmer features, and no coronal lines.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the candidate TDE or AGN flare SDSS J1657+2345 was discovered as a MIR flare with no optical counterpart Yang2019 . It evolved over 1000 day timescales, like VT J1548. Broad H is detected in its spectrum, but no broad H is detected. In contrast to VT J1548, no coronal line emission is detected. Follow up radio and X-ray observations would help determine whether this event is analogous to VT J1548.

gmw_00007737.name: J2250-0846.2
gmw_00007737.overview: J2250-0846.2 is a CO-emitting candidate galaxy that exhibits a close, two-component emission line structure [arXiv:2109.09754]. This spectral feature may indicate either a galaxy merger or a single object with a two-peaked emission line [arXiv:2109.09754].

sources.summary: The paper treats J2250-0846.2 as a CO-emitting candidate whose close two-component line structure may indicate either a galaxy merger or a two-peaked emission line from one object.

quotes.quote: One of the sources (J2250-0846.2) is identified by our algorithm as two close ( =0.4 , v 625 km s^ 1 ) sources. This could be either a galaxy merger or a galaxy with a two peaked emission line, with total width FWHM >700 km s^ 1 . In this study, we consider this as a single object, since even in the merger scenario this would likely trace the same dark matter halo. We study this object further in a forthcoming paper (Garc ia-Vergara et al. in preparation).

gmw_00007738.name: G4
gmw_00007738.overview: G4 is an interacting Sc spiral galaxy that dominates the kinematics of its group [arXiv:astro-ph/0410014, arXiv:astro-ph/0509905]. A close encounter with the galaxy G1 approximately 200 million years ago triggered enhanced star formation and produced a 29-kiloparsec tidal tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0410014, arXiv:astro-ph/0509905].

sources.summary: The paper presents Galaxy 4 as the kinematic centerpiece of CG J1720-67.8: a spiral likely launched the strong tidal tail and recently interacted with Galaxy 1.

quotes.quote: As visible in Fig. mosaic_hevel , the entire group appears dominated by the kinematics of Galaxy 4, although, at first glance, the kinematic center seems to be displaced from the photometric center of the galaxy. Galaxy 1 shows the same systemic velocity as Galaxy 4, while the systemic velocity of Galaxy 2 is roughly 200 km s ^ 1 higher.

The most likely progenitor is Galaxy 4, the spiral galaxy to which the tail appears to be connected. As a consequence, we can assume that the direction of the orbital angular momentum vector is concordant with the spin vector of Galaxy 4.

Although other interpretations are possible, as discussed extensively in Section 7.1, we favour a scenario in which Galaxy 4 and Galaxy 1 experienced a prograde-retrograde close encounter within the last 200 Myr. During this encounter, the strong tidal tail and the bridge between the two galaxies were formed, and possibly also the cone-like plume with the apex pointing approximately to the centre of Galaxy 1. The interaction process triggered strong star formation episodes across the bodies and in the centres of both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes G4 as an interacting Sc galaxy with enhanced star formation, a tidal tail, and spectra/SEDs consistent with an interaction-induced burst.

quotes.quote: The enhanced star formation activity in their nuclei and throughout their disks and the clumpy distribution of star forming regions of G4 appear evident on the H map (Paper IV).

The three galaxies appear in mutual interaction and a bright, knotty tidal tail departing from G4 extends for 29 kpc (H _0 = 75 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 ). From the maximum amplitude of the velocity curve of G4 and the projected length of the tidal tail we have estimated for it an age of 200 Myr (Paper IV).

We found that the two late-type group's spirals, G1 and G4, have SEDs and optical spectra in reasonable agreement with a CC-model of an Sc galaxy at an age 40 -- 180 Myr after the onset of an interaction-induced intermediate to strong burst of star formation. In particular the strong and older burst offers the preferred solution for G1, while intermediate ages and strengths between the two proposed models might be more suitable to G4. From the K band luminosity of G4 and the mass-to-light ratio of the preferred model we derived a stellar mass of 1.6 10 ^ 10 M _ and a total (stellar + gaseous) mass of 2 10 ^ 10 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper describes G4 as the more massive interacting spiral, linked to the main tidal tail, an offset kinematic center, shared velocity with G1, and unequal-mass disk modeling.

quotes.quote: The total (stars + gas) masses of the galaxies implied by the best-fit models are 3.4 -- 7 10 ^ 10 M _ (depending on the considered model) for G2, 4 -- 6 10 ^ 9 M _ for G1, and 2 10 ^ 10 M _ for G4.

Since the two spiral galaxies G1 and G4 have similar systemic velocities and appear to be connected by a bridge of ionized gas, we first compared the observations with an encounter between two unequal mass disks. The best-fit evolutionary synthesis models imply for these two galaxies a mass ratio 1:3 or 1:4, hence a 1:3 mass ratio was adopted in the simulations.

The age of the most prominent tidal tail, based on its projected length and on the kinematics of G4, is estimated to be approximately 200 Myr. This is indicative of the age of the latest strong interaction event. The velocity field of the group (Fig. temp:fig3 ) obtained from the H emission line gives evidence of its complex kinematics. The kinematic center is offset from the center of G4 by about 5 kpc. Galaxies G1 and G4, and the bridge of ionized gas between them have similar systemic radial velocities.

gmw_00007739.name: 2MASXJ0903
gmw_00007739.overview: The early-type galaxy 2MASXJ0903 exhibits an asymmetric molecular gas morphology, with low-surface brightness emission extending further from the center on one side of its disc [arXiv:1801.08140]. This asymmetry is reflected in its spectrum, and its gas surface brightness is well-fit by an exponential profile [arXiv:1801.08140].

sources.summary: ALMA observations show 2MASXJ0903 has extended asymmetric molecular emission, an asymmetric spectrum, and an exponential gas surface-brightness fit.

quotes.quote: 2MASXJ1416 has detached gas clouds, the low-surface brightness emission extends further from the centre on one side of the disc in 2MASXJ0903 and CGCG013--092, and NGC4797 and 2MASXJ1301 exhibit a small warp in their centres (best seen by the tilt in the zero velocity contour in the velocity maps).
The spectra of galaxies 2MASXJ0903, 2MASXJ1416, 2MASXJ1301, and CGCG013--092 have an asymmetric profile, reflecting their asymmetric morphologies.
For the galaxies 2MASXJ0903, 2MASXJ1416, NGC4797, and 2MASXJ1301, we find a reasonable fit by using an exponential gas surface brightness profile.

gmw_00007740.name: 2MASXJ1416
gmw_00007740.overview: The early-type galaxy 2MASXJ1416 exhibits detached molecular gas clouds [arXiv:1801.08140]. It also displays an asymmetric spectrum and clear asymmetrical features at large radii [arXiv:1801.08140].

sources.summary: The paper finds detached molecular gas clouds, an asymmetric spectrum, and clear large-radius asymmetrical features in 2MASXJ1416.

quotes.quote: 2MASXJ1416 has detached gas clouds, the low-surface brightness emission extends further from the centre on one side of the disc in 2MASXJ0903 and CGCG013--092, and NGC4797 and 2MASXJ1301 exhibit a small warp in their centres (best seen by the tilt in the zero velocity contour in the velocity maps).
The spectra of galaxies 2MASXJ0903, 2MASXJ1416, 2MASXJ1301, and CGCG013--092 have an asymmetric profile, reflecting their asymmetric morphologies.
4 out of our 6 targets clearly show asymmetrical features at large radii -- galaxies 2MASXJ0903, 2MASXJ1416, NGC4797, and CGCG013--092.

gmw_00007741.name: NGC4797
gmw_00007741.overview: NGC4797 is an early-type galaxy that exhibits clear asymmetrical features at large radii [arXiv:1801.08140]. The galaxy also features a small molecular-gas warp in its center and has a gas surface brightness distribution that fits an exponential profile [arXiv:1801.08140].

sources.summary: NGC4797 is described as having a central molecular-gas warp, clear large-radius asymmetrical features, and a gas surface brightness distribution fit by an exponential profile.

quotes.quote: 2MASXJ1416 has detached gas clouds, the low-surface brightness emission extends further from the centre on one side of the disc in 2MASXJ0903 and CGCG013--092, and NGC4797 and 2MASXJ1301 exhibit a small warp in their centres (best seen by the tilt in the zero velocity contour in the velocity maps).
4 out of our 6 targets clearly show asymmetrical features at large radii -- galaxies 2MASXJ0903, 2MASXJ1416, NGC4797, and CGCG013--092.
For the galaxies 2MASXJ0903, 2MASXJ1416, NGC4797, and 2MASXJ1301, we find a reasonable fit by using an exponential gas surface brightness profile.

gmw_00007742.name: CGCG013--092
gmw_00007742.overview: CGCG013--092 is an early-type galaxy that exhibits a point-source continuum detection at 3 mm [arXiv:1801.08140]. The galaxy features asymmetric, low-surface-brightness molecular gas and an X-shaped position-velocity diagram characteristic of a stellar bar [arXiv:1801.08140].

sources.summary: The paper reports 3 mm continuum, asymmetric low-surface-brightness gas, and an X-shaped position-velocity diagram typical of barred molecular gas in CGCG013--092.

quotes.quote: We detected the continuum for galaxies 2MASXJ0903 and CGCG013--092 ( 300 Jy), which are both point sources at 3 mm.

2MASXJ1416 has detached gas clouds, the low-surface brightness emission extends further from the centre on one side of the disc in 2MASXJ0903 and CGCG013--092, and NGC4797 and 2MASXJ1301 exhibit a small warp in their centres (best seen by the tilt in the zero velocity contour in the velocity maps).
Object CGCG013--092 clearly shows an X-shape in its position-velocity diagram, which is typical for molecular gas within a bar.

gmw_00007743.name: 2MASXJ1301
gmw_00007743.overview: The early-type galaxy 2MASXJ1301 exhibits a subtle structural asymmetry, with its emission extending from -8 kpc to +10 kpc [arXiv:1801.08140]. It also features a small warp in its center and contains a gas disc that is theoretically unstable against gravitational collapse [arXiv:1801.08140].

sources.summary: The ALMA analysis describes 2MASXJ1301 as warped and subtly asymmetric, with a Toomre-Q exception to otherwise stable gas discs.

quotes.quote: 2MASXJ1416 has detached gas clouds, the low-surface brightness emission extends further from the centre on one side of the disc in 2MASXJ0903 and CGCG013--092, and NGC4797 and 2MASXJ1301 exhibit a small warp in their centres (best seen by the tilt in the zero velocity contour in the velocity maps).
The asymmetry of galaxy 2MASXJ1301 is more subtle, but can be identified by noticing that the emission extends from 8 kpc to +10 kpc.
Even when assuming a canonical value of _ gas =8 km s ^ 1 , we find that Q>1 and therefore theoretically stable against gravitational collapse, with the exception of 2MASXJ1301.

gmw_00007744.name: 2MASXJ1333
gmw_00007744.overview: 2MASXJ1333 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1801.08140]. Unlike similar targets, it shows no signs of asymmetries, and its detected molecular gas appears to be part of a regular, relaxed gas disc that requires an outer surface-density cutoff [arXiv:1801.08140].

sources.summary: Unlike the other targets, 2MASXJ1333 shows no disturbed gas structures; its molecular gas appears regular and needs an outer surface-density cutoff.

quotes.quote: Only galaxy 2MASXJ1333 shows no sign of asymmetries.
Galaxy 2MASXJ1333 is the only object for which, at the current resolution, all the detected molecular gas seems to be part of a regular, relaxed gas disc.
For galaxy 2MASXJ1333, however, we find that we additionally need an outer surface density cutoff in order to fit the data.

gmw_00007745.name: NED01
gmw_00007745.overview: NED01 is a ring galaxy viewed almost face-on that resembles the collisional, empty-ring class of galaxies [arXiv:1310.6807]. It exhibits tidal perturbations, including an external ring and an internal loop-ring structure, and is interacting with its elliptical-like companion galaxy, NED02 [arXiv:1310.6807].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NED01 as the ring galaxy in FM 047-02, showing face-on tidal ring structures, old stellar populations, and interaction with elliptical-like companion NED02.

quotes.quote: In the FM 047-02 system, the NED01 galaxy is seen almost face-on and is clearly ringed, with two or three prominent smooth structures on the S and SE sides of the ring. No clearly prominent knots are visible in other regions of the ring, as detected in the Solitaire-candidate galaxies AM 2152-592, AM 2012-282, and AM 1434-783. As can be seen in Fig. fig_01 , the object shows two clear signatures of tidal perturbations: one main external ring and an internal loop-ring-like structure extending to the SW. NED01 resembles the collisional RE class of rings (empty rings) proposed by Theys Spiegel ( ).

The FM 047-02 system is composed of the ring galaxy NED01 and the elliptical-like galaxy NED02, (2MASX J20262950-7236443 and 2MASX J20263396-7236083, in NED, respectively). The projected separation between both galaxies' centers is almost 60 kpc. The velocity difference is close to v= 240 (km s ^ 1 ), confirming that both galaxies are interacting close companions.

gmw_00007746.name: NED02
gmw_00007746.overview: NED02 is an early-type companion galaxy dominated by an old stellar population, though it also contains a small fraction of young stars [arXiv:1310.6807]. The galaxy features a counter-rotating core and is currently undergoing a tidal interaction with its neighbor, NED01 [arXiv:1310.6807].

sources.summary: The paper treats NED02 as the elliptical-like companion in FM 047-02, with mostly old stars, a small young component, counter-rotating core, and tidal interaction with NED01.

quotes.quote: During the whole long-slit signal, the spectra of both galaxies resemble those of an early-type galaxy. Both objects are dominated by an old stellar population of 2 10^ 9 < t 13 10^ 9 yr and a small, but a non-negligible fraction (about 13 ) of young stars of t 5 10^ 7 yr are estimated to contribute to the optical flux for NED02. Both observed small radial-velocity differences, and the structures around NED01 suggest an ongoing tidal interaction of both galaxies. The spectroscopic results and the morphological peculiarities of NED01 can be adequately interpreted as continuing off-center stage of interaction with the companion galaxy NED02. The core of NED01 and NED02 are in counter-rotating motion.

When inspecting Figs. fig_02 and fig_03 with respect to both heliocentric velocity centers, the following is suggested: (a) the core of NED02 is counter-rotating; (b) the external region of NED02 is kinematically decoupled from its central region (while the NE section is approaching us, the SW section is receding from us); and () both sections of the NED01 ring are receding from us, along with the internal loop-ring.

gmw_00007747.name: C1-15182
gmw_00007747.overview: C1-15182 is a high-redshift galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of z = 3.369 [arXiv:1606.05350]. It exhibits a high [OIII] emission line luminosity that is typical of galaxies harboring active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1606.05350].

sources.summary: The paper confirms C1-15182 at z>3 using [OIII] emission, finds high [OIII] luminosity and line width typical of AGN, and models its massive stellar population.

quotes.quote: The [ OIII ] 4009,5007 doublet emission is detected in both NIRSPEC and Xshooter 1D spectra for C1-19536 and C1-15182, however, the lower S/N of the Xshooter spectrum is evident in the panels of Figure fig-lines . This is true especially for C1-15182, where a strong continuum is not detected when fitting the emission lines with the Xshooter spectrum, deeming the calculated equivalent width of [ OIII ] emission highly uncertain. We therefore fixed the continuum level when fitting the [ OIII ] lines in the Xshooter spectrum for C1-15182 to the median flux values of the spectral region ( 450 ) around the [ OIII ] lines and not contaminated by skylines.

[ OIII ] line luminosities are L_ OIII =1.76^ +0.6 _ 0.5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 and 7.9^ +5.9 _ 2.8 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 for C1-19536 and C1-15182, respectively, at the high end of [ OIII ] luminosities found in galaxies harboring AGNs maschietto08, kuiper11, harrison16 . The resulting systemic redshifts for C1-15182 and C1-19536 are z_ spec =3.369 0.002 and z_ spec =3.1373 0.0008, respectively, spectroscopically confirming their redshifts to be at z>3 .

gmw_00007748.name: C1-19536
gmw_00007748.overview: C1-19536 is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of z = 3.1373 [arXiv:1606.05350]. The galaxy is detected in X-rays and exhibits luminous broad [OIII] and weak Lyman-alpha emission lines [arXiv:1606.05350].

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms C1-19536 at z=3.1373, reports luminous broad [OIII] and weak Ly emission, and identifies it as an X-ray-detected powerful AGN host.

quotes.quote: The [ OIII ] doublet was identified in the spectra of C1-19536 and C1-15182, along with a weak Ly detection in the spectrum of C1-19536. A symmetric Gaussian profile plus a local continuum was used to fit these lines in the extracted one-dimensional spectra. Identical redshifts, FWHM and a ratio of 1:3 for the amplitudes of the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet were assumed. Figure fig-lines shows the observed one dimensional spectra around the regions of the considered spectral lines. The regions of the spectra significantly affected by skylines, indicated in gray regions, were excluded from the line profile modeling. The best-fit Gaussian profiles are indicated in red. A Monte Carlo approach was used to measure the uncertainties in the centroid, flux, and width of the fitted emission lines. For each 1D spectrum, 1000 simulated spectra were created by perturbing the flux of the true spectrum at each wavelength by a Gaussian random amount with the standard deviation set by the level of the 1 error at that specific wavelength. Line measurements were obtained from the simulated spectra in the same manner as the actual data. We computed the formal lower and upper confidence limits by integrating the probability distribution of each parameter (centroid, width, continuum, and emission line flux) from the extremes until the integrated probability is equal to 0.1585. We calculated the fluxes of the observed emission lines by integrating the fitted Gaussian profiles, with uncertainties determined using the 1 distribution of integrated fluxes from Monte Carlo simulations as described above. The equivalent widths were calculated individually for the observed and simulated spectra by dividing the integrated line flux by the continuum of the fit, with uncertainties derived as above. The [ OIII ] 4009,5007 doublet emission is detected in both NIRSPEC and Xshooter 1D spectra for C1-19536 and C1-15182, however, the lower S/N of the Xshooter spectrum is evident in the panels of Figure fig-lines . This is true especially for C1-15182, where a strong continuum is not detected when fitting the emission lines with the Xshooter spectrum, deeming the calculated equivalent width of [ OIII ] emission highly uncertain. We therefore fixed the continuum level when fitting the [ OIII ] lines in the Xshooter spectrum for C1-15182 to the median flux values of the spectral region ( 450 ) around the [ OIII ] lines and not contaminated by skylines. We used the [ OIII ] emission line fitting results from the higher S/N NIRSPEC spectrum to analyse the emission line properties of C1-15182 and C1-19536. The best-fit redshifts, line velocities corrected for the instrumental profile (determined by the width of skylines in each spectra) and the observed equivalent widths are listed in Table tab-lines , with the quoted uncertainties corresponding to the 1 errors estimated from the Monte Carlo simulations. We estimated the line luminosities by adopting the redshifts of the [ OIII ] emission lines as the systemic redshifts, listed in Table tab-lines . [ OIII ] line luminosities are L_ OIII =1.76^ +0.6 _ 0.5 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 and 7.9^ +5.9 _ 2.8 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 for C1-19536 and C1-15182, respectively, at the high end of [ OIII ] luminosities found in galaxies harboring AGNs maschietto08, kuiper11, harrison16 . The resulting systemic redshifts for C1-15182 and C1-19536 are z_ spec =3.369 0.002 and z_ spec =3.1373 0.0008, respectively, spectroscopically confirming their redshifts to be at z>3 .

Assuming a power-law photon index = 1.9 nandrapounds94 , the X-ray luminosities L_ 2-10 keV are (6.4 1.7) 10 ^ 45 for C1-15182, (16.6 2.6) 10 ^ 45 for C1-19536 and (9.6 1.5) 10 ^ 45 erg s ^ 1 for A2-15753 (redshift uncertainties are included in quoted errors). We used the empirically derived observed L_ OIII L_ 2-10 keV relation of local AGNs from ueda15 to investigate the expected X-ray flux of the sources with detected [ OIII ] emission (C1-19536 and C1-15182). The estimated L_ 2-10 keV for these galaxies using the observed L_ OIII are an order of magnitude less than the luminosities calculated using X-ray detections (using =1.4 lowers this factor to 6-8 ).

gmw_00007749.name: C1-19764
gmw_00007749.overview: C1-19764 is a massive galaxy with a photometric redshift estimated at 2.72 [arXiv:1606.05350]. Based on its rest-frame colors, it is classified as a quiescent galaxy with low star formation [arXiv:1606.05350].

sources.summary: The paper models C1-19764 as a massive target whose continuum redshift moves below z=3, with low SED-based star formation and quiescent colors.

quotes.quote: The spectroscopic program targeted two additional galaxies, C1-2127 and C1-19764, consistent with being at z 3 but not originally included in the sample presented in marchesini10 because they did not strictly satisfy the completeness limit in stellar mass (though both massive with log(M _ * /M _ ) 11.2-11.3). Table tab-obs also lists C1-21316 from the sample of marchesini10 but not targeted by our spectroscopic follow-up program, since it has a previously confirmed spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =3.971 ( capak10, smolcic12 ; but see also miettinen15 ). C1-21316 is also a sub-millimeter galaxy (SMG) listed as AzTEC 5 in the AzTEC millimeter survey catalog in COSMOS scott08 . In this section we briefly describe the ground-based spectroscopic data obtained from Keck-NIRSPEC and VLT-X-shooter as part of follow-up programs aimed at spectroscopically confirming the existence of very massive galaxies at z>3 . Along with the observational techniques, we provide a summary of the data reduction. We refer the reader to geier13 and marsan15 for a detailed description of the spectroscopic data reduction. Table tab-obs summarizes the wavelength coverage, total exposure time, and seeing of the spectroscopic observations.
